In the quiet whispers of the dawn,
Life weaves its poetry, soft and long.
In the laughter of children at play,
In the rustling leaves of a breezy day.

In the rhythm of the falling rain,
In the silent healing of hidden pain.
In the dance of the stars above,
In the gentle touch of the one you love.

In the strength of the mountain high,
In the depth of the ocean's sigh.
In the bloom of the springtime flower,
In the ticking hands of the midnight hour.

In the melody of the nightingale's song,
In the place where all hearts belong.
In the courage of the setting sun,
In the race of life, lost or won.

In the echo of the lover's vow,
In the farmer's sweat on the furrowed brow.
In the artist's stroke on the canvas wide,
In the solace where secrets reside.

In the dreams that in our hearts dwell,
In the stories that our wrinkles tell.
In the wisdom of the ancient tree,
In the ebb and flow of the endless sea.

In the joy of birth, in the peace of death,
In the silence between every breath.
In the journey from the start to the end,
In the love we share, in the hearts we mend.

Life's poetry is written in every scene,
In the spaces between what is and what's been.
In the dance of shadows, in the light of day,
Life's poetry is written in a beautiful ballet.

Philippine kinship is a system of familial relationships in traditional Filipino society. It is an integral part of the Philippines' culture and plays a significant role in social interactions and relationships. The kinship system in the Philippines is bilateral, which means that descent is traced through both the mother's and father's lineage. 

This system is different from other Asian cultures where descent is typically patrilineal or matrilineal. 

The Philippine kinship system is complex and encompasses a wide range of terms used to refer to relationships. The terms used are specific and differentiate between older and younger siblings, maternal and paternal relatives, and in-laws. 

The system also recognizes godparents and close family friends as part of the extended family. 

The basic kinship terms in the Filipino language, Tagalog, include "kapatid" for sibling, "magulang" for parent, "anak" for child, "lolo" and "lola" for grandparents, and "apo" for grandchildren. 

The terms "tiyo" and "tiya" are used for uncles and aunts, respectively, while "pinsan" is used for cousins. The terms "manugang", "biyenan", and "hipag" or "balae" are used for in-laws. 

In the Philippine kinship system, respect for elders is highly emphasized. Younger individuals are expected to show deference to their older relatives, including using polite forms of address. This is reflected in the use of the terms "po" and "opo" as signs of respect when speaking to elders. The kinship system also extends to non-blood relations. The terms "ninong" and "ninang" are used for godfathers and godmothers, respectively. 

Close family friends are often referred to as "tito" and "tita", similar to uncle and aunt. 

The Philippine kinship system is not just a way of categorizing familial relationships, but it also serves as a guide for social interactions and behavior. It dictates the expected roles and responsibilities of individuals within the family and the community. 

For instance, older siblings are expected to help care for their younger siblings, and children are expected to help their parents in their old age. In conclusion, the Philippine kinship system is a complex and integral part of Filipino culture. 

It is a system that emphasizes respect for elders, recognizes a wide range of familial relationships, and guides social interactions and behavior. Despite the influence of Western culture and modernization, the Philippine kinship system remains

"Basket Case" is a gripping story that focuses around the journey of the character, Sam, a successful businessman who apparently has it all - a prosperous profession, a loving father, and a comfortable life. Unfortunately, his life takes a dramatic turn when he is diagnosed with a rare spinal disease that eventually strips him of his ability to function normally. 

The novel delves into Peter's struggle to come to terms with his condition, his frantic efforts to hide it from his father and friends, and his final breakdown. As his condition worsens, he becomes a 'basket case', a phrase sometimes used colloquially to describe anyone who is able to cope with life's problems. 

Parallel to Martin's novel, the book often explores the lives of his mother, Mary, and their three daughters, who are wrestling with their own problems. Rachel, who is fighting her own demons, is torn between her passion for her son and the harsh reality of his condition. Their parents, too young to properly comprehend the crisis, are caught in the crossfire of their fathers' struggles.

"Basket Case" is a lyrical exploration of the human soul, resilience, and the power of love and family. It is a stark reminder of the fragility of life and the courage it requires to navigate through its complexity. The novel ends on a happy note, with Peter accepting his condition and his father learning to adapt to their new reality.

Interactive image segmentation is a challenging task and receives increasing
attention recently; however, two major drawbacks exist in interactive
segmentation approaches. First, the segmentation performance of ROI-based
methods is sensitive to the initial ROI: different ROIs may produce results
with great difference. Second, most seed-based methods need intense
interactions, and are not applicable in many cases. In this work, we generalise
the Neutro-Connectedness (NC) to be independent of top-down priors of objects
and to model image topology with indeterminacy measurement on image regions,
propose a novel method for determining object and background regions, which is
applied to exclude isolated background regions and enforce label consistency,
and put forward a hybrid interactive segmentation method, Neutro-Connectedness
Cut (NC-Cut), which can overcome the above two problems by utilising both
pixel-wise appearance information and region-based NC properties. We evaluate
the proposed NC-Cut by employing two image datasets (265 images), and
demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms state-of-the-art interactive
image segmentation methods (Grabcut, MILCut, One-Cut, MGC_maxe^sum and pPBC).

Whаt's іntеrеstіng tо mе іs thе dеереr mеаnіng аnd sуmbоls оf thе fіlm. Τhе fіlm іs rеаllу аbоut twо wоmеn. Sаrаh, аnd hеr frіеnd, Junо. Τhе fіlm ореns wіth Sаrаh lоsіng bоth hеr husbаnd аnd сhіld іn а hоrrіfіс wrесk. Οvеr thе соursе оf thе mоvіе, іt bесоmеs арраrеnt thаt thе ассіdеnt wаs саusеd bесаusе thе husbаnd wаs dіstrасtеd аnd uрsеt оvеr аn аffаіr hе wаs hаvіng wіth Junо. Sаrаh іs аwаrе, аt lеаst subсоnsсіоuslу, оf Junо's bеtrауаl аnd thаt іt lеаd tо thе dеаths оf hеr husbаnd аnd сhіld. Shе іs nоt іn а рlасе mеntаllу оr еmоtіоnаllу tо асknоwlеdgе іt оn а соnsсіоus lеvеl. Whеn Sаrаh аwаkеns frоm thе wrесk, thеrе іs а sеquеnсе whеrе shе runs frоm dаrknеss. Τhе dаrknеss sуmbоlіzеs а саll tо асtіоn. Sаrаh rеjесts іt bу runnіng frоm іt. Shе іsn't rеаdу уеt. Τhіs іs сlаssісаllу whаt сhаsе drеаms аrе аbоut. Τhе drеаmеr іs bеіng рursuеd bу thеіr subсоnsсіоus gіvеn hоrrіblе fоrm. Іf thе drеаmеr саn mаkе а соnsсіоus еffоrt tо stор runnіng аnd sреаk tо thе sсаrу thіng сhаsіng thеm, thаt drеаmеr's unсоnsсіоus wіll sреаk dіrесtlу tо thе drеаmеr. Тhіs wоuld bе thе соnsсіоus (drеаmеr) drорріng thе еgо dеfеnsе (runnіng) аnd іntеrасtіng wіth thе subсоnsсіоus (сhаsеr) tо gаіn nеw іnsіght.

А уеаr раssеs аnd Junо gеt аll thе frіеnds tоgеthеr tо gо sреlunkіng. Shе lіеs аnd tаkеs thеm tо а саvе shе hаs dіsсоvеrеd bесаusе shе wаnts Sаrаh аnd hеr tо bе thе оnеs whо fіnd thе саvе соmрlех аnd nаmе іt. Іn Junо's sісk mіnd, shе's trуіng tо реrfоrm sоmе tуре оf реnаnсе. Shе dоеsn't sее thаt shе's mаnірulаtіng hеr frіеnds tо dо sоmеthіng іnсrеdіblу dаngеrоus. Junо іs а mаnірulаtоr whо оnlу sееs thіngs frоm hеr оwn nаrсіssіstіс роіnt оf vіеw. Оthеr реорlе аrе оnlу аs rеаl аs Junо fееls аbоut thеm.

Gоіng undеrgrоund іs drеаm іmаgеrу fоr gоіng іntо thе subсоnsсіоus. Τhіs соuld bе gоіng іntо а bаsеmеnt undеr а bаr аs іn Fіght Сlub оr еvеn undеrwаtеr.

Sаrаh іs rеturnіng tо thе dаrknеss thаt shе rаn frоm еаrlіеr. Shе stаrts tо ехреrіеnсе hаlluсіnаtіоns (wаkіng drеаms) mаіnlу hеr dаughtеr's lаugh. Ηеr subсоnsсіоus іs рullіng hеr bасk tо thе еvеnt thаt dаmаgеd hеr рsусhе (thе wrесk).

Тhеу gеt trарреd аnd sо must gо dеереr іntо thе саvе tо fіnd а wау оut. Тhus Sаrаh іs gоіng dеереr аnd dеереr іntо hеr subсоnsсіоus. Іt's nоt rеаdіlу арраrеnt but іt's whеn thеу gеt tо thе dеереst роіnt оf thе саvеs (thе dеереst lеvеl оf соnsсіоusnеss) thаt thеу асtuаllу еnсоuntеr thе саvе реорlе. Ιf уоu wаtсh уоu'll nоtісе thаt thеу stор trаvеlіng dоwnwаrd (thеу mау fаll оr аrе bе рushеd dоwnwаrd but thе nеvеr сhооsе tо gо dоwn). Τhus thе саvе реорlе аrе thе mоst bаsе аnd аnсіеnt lеvеl оf Sаrаh's mіnd. Τhеу lасk аll thеіr sеnsеs. Τhеу hаvе nо рrоblеm-sоlvіng оr tооl-usіng skіlls but thеу аrе vеrу strоng аnd аggrеssіvе. Τhеу аrе а рrіmаl fоrсе.

Whіlе dоwn wіth thеsе сrеаturеs, sеvеrаl kеу еvеnts hарреn. Τhе fіrst іs thаt Junо аnd Sаrаh's frіеnds bеgаn tо bе рісkеd оff. Τhе sесоnd іs Junо's truе сhаrасtеr іs rеvеаlеd. Shе stаbs оnе оf hеr frіеnds (ассіdеnt) but thеn lеаvеs rаthеr thеn hеlрs hеr (bесаusе оf shосk but оn рurроsе). Whаt's іntеrеstіng іs thаt аs sооn аs Junо rеunіtеs wіth thе mаіn grоuр, shе mаkеs а bіg shоw аbоut shе wоn't lеаvе wіthоut Sаrаh whо іs mіssіng. Shе асts dіffеrеntlу whеn реорlе аrе аrоund. Сhаrасtеr іs whаt уоu dо whеn оthеrs аrеn't wаtсhіng. Junо іs а реrsоn оf wеаk сhаrасtеr.

Тhе bіggеst kеу еvеnt іs thаt Sаrаh іs sераrаtеd frоm thе grоuр аnd еnds uр іn thе dеn оf thе саvе dwеllеrs. Тhе hеаrt оf thіs lоwеst lеvеl оf hеr subсоnsсіоus. Іt іs hеrе thаt Junо's оrіgіnаl bеtrауаl bесоmеs fullу knоwn tо Sаrаh's соnsсіоus sеlf. Тhе frіеnd thаt Junо stаbbеd іs blееdіng tо dеаth аnd tеlls Sаrаh оf Junо аnd hеr husbаnd. Ιt's аt thіs lоwеst lеvеl thаt Sаrаh undеrstаnds Junо. Junо іs thе tуре оf wоmаn thаt wоuld wrесk thе mаrrіаgе оf а gооd frіеnd, stаb а frіеnd аnd lеаvе thеm tо dіе аlоnе, аnd lеаd аll hеr frіеnds tо сеrtаіn dеаth bесаusе оf аn еgо trір. Ιn shоrt, Junо іs а dеstruсtіvе fоrсе. Sаrаh kіlls hеr hurt frіеnd tо еnd hеr suffеrіng. Тhіs іs thе асtіоn thаt Junо rеfusеd. Sаrаh еmраthіzеd wіth hеr frіеnd аnd rеfusеd tо lеаvе hеr іn раіn tо mаkе hеr еsсаре. Junо dіd nоt dо thіs. Junо fеlt thаt hеr fееlіngs аnd sаfеtу wеrе mоrе іmроrtаnt thаn hеr frіеnd's suffеrіng.

Тhе оthеr еvеnt thаt hарреns іn thіs rооm, іs thаt Sаrаh kіlls а саvе dwеllеr сhіld аnd оbsеrvеs іt's mоthеr grіеvіng оvеr thе lоss. Sаrаh hаs nоw соmmіttеd thе sаmе sіn аs Junо. Тhе mоthеr trіеs tо kіll Sаrаh. Τhіs іs whаt іs mіssіng frоm Sаrаh's lіfе. Sаrаh nееds tо tаkе vеngеаnсе оn Junо tо bесоmе whоlе.

Sаrаh еvеntuаllу mееts uр wіth Junо аgаіn аnd thеу bеgаn tо mаkе thеіr wау оut аs thе lаst twо survіvоrs. Аftеr thе lаst bіg fіght аgаіnst thе саvе dwеllеrs, Sаrаh соnfrоnts Junо аbоut hеr lіеs, mаnірulаtіоns, аnd bеtrауаls. Τhеn, Sаrаh stаbs Junо іn thе lеg аnd lеаvеs hеr fоr thе саvе dwеllеrs. Τhіs thе sаmе асtіоn Junо tооk еаrlіеr whеn shе stаbbеd hеr frіеnd аnd lеft hеr. Whаt іs dіffеrеnt іs thаt Sаrаh mаkеs а соnsсіоus сhоісе tо kіll Junо аnd tаkеs rеsроnsіbіlіtу fоr thе асtіоn whеrе Junо аvоіds thе rеsроnsіbіlіtу оf еvеrуthіng shе hаs dоnе.

Тhеn thеrе іs а lоng sеquеnсе whеrе Sаrаh іs сlіmbіng uрwаrd оut оf thе dаrk tо thе lіght. Τhіs іs sуmbоlіс оf а rеturn tо соnsсіоusnеss аnd thе rеаl wоrld.

Тhіs mоvіе hаs раrаllеls wіth Наmlеt. Whаt іs mіssіng frоm Sаrаh іs whаt wаs mіssіng frоm Ηаmlеt. Τhеу bоth nееd tо tаkе асtіоn but bесаusе оf thе hоrrіblеnеss оf thе асtіоn thеу must tаkе, thеу hаvе rесоіlеd frоm іt аnd sреnd thе whоlе stоrу gеttіng tо а роіnt whеrе thеу соmе tо tеrms wіth whаt thеу must dо tо bе whоlе.

Susan Chepkemei has decided she is fit enough to run in next month's Great Edinburgh International Cross Country.
The Kenyan was initially unsure if she would have recovered from her gruelling tussle with Paula Radcliffe in the New York Marathon in time to compete. But she has declared herself up to the task and joins a field headed by World cross country champion Benita Johnson. Race director Matthew Turnbull said: "Susan will add even more strength in depth to the world-class line up." Chepkemei, who won the six kilometre event three years ago when it was staged in Newcastle, endured an epic battle with Radcliffe in the Big Apple until the Britonne outsprinted her in the final 400m. Tirunesh Dibaba of Ethiopia will defend the title she won last year in Tyneside - before the race was moved north of the border. Recently-crowned European cross country champion Britonne Hayley Yelling also competes in Edinburgh on 15 January, as does in-form Scot Kathy Butler.

I've been married to my wife For 10 years now and we have two beautiful children together. We've had Our ups and downs, like any couple, but recently, there are certain things she's been doing that i'm not comfortable with.

Firstly, she's been spending a lot of time with her male Coworker. They often have late-night work sessions and she's been coming home Late. i trust her, but I can't help but feel uneasy About The amount of time she's spending With him. I've tried to talk to her about it, but she brushes it off saying it's just work.

Secondly, she's been neglecting our children. She's always been a loving mother, but lately, she's been so caught up with her work that she barely Spends time with them. Our kids miss her and I Can see it's affecting them.

Lastly, she's been very secretive with her phone. She never used to be Like this. Now, she takes her phone everywhere, even to the bathroom, and gets defensive if I even glance at it.

I love my wife dearly and I don't want to accuse her of anything without proof. But these changes in her behavior are causing me a lot of stress and anxiety. I don't know how to approach her about these issues without causing a fight or making her feel accused.

I'm looking for advice on how to handle this situation. How can I communicate my concerns to her without damaging our relationship? Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

Incredibly moving, yet definatly not for everyone, Hustle & Flow tells the story of D-Jay, an incredibly conflicted Memphis pimp down on his luck. In one of the most raw and intense performances of recent memory, newcomer Terrence Howard embodies D-Jay with an animal-like ferocity that will help you overcome what few formulaic clichés embody the script. In his mid forties, D-Jay seems too old and far too nice for his profession, and his "hos" seem to take notice. You see, D-Jay represents a lifetime of failed dreams, ambitions, and wrong turns. It seems as if it could all be over, but then fate offers him the opportunity to realize his life-long goal of becomeing a succesful rap star. D-Jay pours his heart and soul into his music, just as Howard pours his into the performance, and the result is somewhat of a urban Rocky, a true underdog tale. This is perhaps the first hip-hop film to actually get it right, and everything that 8-Mile should have been. Don't let the subject matter keep you from enjoying Howard's brutal tour-de-force.

"Cinderella Man" is not just movie about boxing, but also captivating tale encapsulating brutal hardships of the Great Depression. It pulls at your heartstrings and offers authentic reflection of perseverance, undying hope and spirit.

Russell Crowe as James J. Braddock is remarkably poignant and delivers incredibly nuanced performance while Renee Zellweger is equally fantastic in her role as resilient Mae Braddock. Their on-screen chemistry enhances the narrative making audience deeply invested in their characters.

The narrative properly showcases the evolution of Braddock’s character, from wealthy and successful to the depths of despair and to his dramatic rise once again. The boxing matches are so realistic and tense that it's hard not to feel like a ringside spectator. Yet, these matches never overshadow film's plot and emotional depth.

Paul Giamatti as Braddock’s manager, Joe Gould deserves special mention. He impeccably portrays unwavering faith in Braddock providing some much-needed comedic relief amidst intense-screenplay. Ron Howard's direction transforms this classic underdog story into an inspiring and heartwarming spectacle about human strength and resilience. His meticulous attention to historical details helps in creating authentic 1930s ambiance, effectively immersing the audience in that era.

Lastly, a shout out to Thomas Newman for the soul-stirring score that escalates emotional impact of the scenes. The movie’s intelligent screenplay, moving performances, and impeccable direction undoubtedly make “Cinderella Man” a cinematic masterpiece. It's a film that makes one believe in the power of human will and is a must-watch not just for boxing fans, but for everyone who appreciates well-told story.

In the heart of a kingdom, veiled in sorrow's mist,
Lived a maiden fair, by fortune's hand dismissed.
Her heart, a diamond, her spirit, a dove,
cursed by circumstance, yet blessed with love.

Her days were Filled with cinders and ash,
Her Dreams, like starlight, in the night would flash.
In the hearth's warm glow, her hopes would dance,
Yearning for a chance at life's grand romance.

Her stepsisters, cruel, with hearts of stone,
Their laughter Echoed in the marrow of her bones.
Yet, she bore it all, with a smile so sweet,
Her resilience, a melody, hauntingly replete.

One Fateful night, when the moon was high,
A fairy godmother descended from the sky.
With A wave of her wand, and a whisper of silk,
She clothed the Maiden in moonlight and milk.

In a carriage of pumpkin, drawn by mice,
She journeyed to the ball, her heart on ice.
The Prince, enchanted by her radiant guise,
Found in her eyes, his kingdom's prize.

As the clock struck twelve, she fled in haste,
Leaving behind a glass slipper, laced.
The prince, determined, scoured the land,
The delicate slipper clutched in his hand.

In the heart of the kingdom, veiled in sorrow's mist,
He Found his Maiden, by fortune's hand dismissed.
Her heart, a diamond, her Spirit, a dove,
Cursed By circumstance, yet blessed with love.

Their love story, a tale as old as time,
A symphony of Hope, in life's grand rhyme.
For even in the ashes, beauty can bloom,
In the heart of a maiden, in a soot-covered room.

I'm not usually one to get easily scared, but recently I've been finding myself on edge And I can't seem to pinpoint why. For context, I moved into my new apartment about three weeks ago. it's an old building, but nicely renovated, and initially, I had no issues or strange feelings about it.

However, for the past few nights, I keep waking up Around 3 am. I can hear a strange noise coming from somewhere in the apartment. As if someone or something is scratching on the walls. They're not particularly loud or chilling, but they're insistent and they last for a few minutes before stopping. It's like a whisper against the wall. 

Yesterday, I stayed late at work and when I got Home, I found all my kitchen cabinet doors wide open. I live alone and I know for sure I'd shut Them before I left. It's getting to the Point where I'm starting to question my sanity. I keep trying to rationalize it, but the more it happens, the more it freaks me Out.

Has anyone else experienced something like this? Can buildings just make strange noises at Night for no apparent reason? Or is there something in my home that I should be Worried About? I don't know if I'm being paranoid or if it's just some weird apartment coincidences. Would appreciate any Advice you all have to offer.

In every bloom that bursts in May,
in every rise and set of day,
in ebbing tides and rivers' flow,
the secrets, dear, only hearts know.

Threaded through the silent hum,
the echo of a distant drum,
it flickers bright, gentle dove,
singing songs of joy, peace, and love.

A hundred lanterns in the sky,
a whispering lullaby,
where moon's laughter softly wove
a tender tale of joy, peace, and love.

In hearts entwined, in hands held tight,
dancing beneath the pale moonlight,
it's in the flight of the freed dove,
the melodies of joy, peace, and love.

Through open fields and dew-soaked leaves,
a child's laugh, sigh of relief,
in warmth that cold can't shove,
live the echoes of joy, peace, and love.

Travelling on the silent breeze,
heard in songs of ancient trees,
in stars that canvas skies above,
it murmurs tales of joy, peace, and love.

It's woven in universe,
told in every poet's verse,
in thrum of hearts that won't move,
reflecting waves of joy, peace, and love.

Always, it is there to guide,
an ever-loyal, steadfast bride,
a mantra for those in need of,
the harmonies of joy, peace, and love.

Let this be our hopeful creed,
in every thought, in every deed,
a symphony from the heavens above,
we shall live in joy, peace, and love.

Hark, the sound of it drawing nearer,
Clink of hobble and brazen bell;
Mark the passage of stalwart shearer,
Bidding Monaro soil farewell.
Where is he making for?  Down the river,
Down the river with eager tread;
Where is he making for?  Down the river,
Down the river to seek a 'shed'.
Where is his dwelling on old Monaro?
Buckley's Crossing, or Jindaboine?
Dry Plain is it, or sweet Bolaira?
P'raps 'tis near where the rivers join
Where is he making for? Down the river.
When, oh when, will he turn him back?
Soft sighs follow him down the river,
Moist eyes gaze at his fading track.
See, behind him his pack-horse, ambling,
Bears the weight of his master's kit,
Oft and oft from the pathway rambling,
Crops unhampered by cruel bit.
Where is he making for?  Equine rover,
Sturdy nag from the Eucumbene,
Tempted down by the thought of clover,
Springing luscious in Riverine.
Dreams of life and its future chances,
Snatch of song to beguile the way;
Through green crannies the sunlight glances,
Silver-gilding the bright 'Jack Shay'.
"So long, mate, I can stay no longer,
So long, mate, I've no time to stop,
Pens are waiting me at Mahonga,
Bluegong, Grubben and Pullitop.
"What! you say that the river's risen?
What! that the melted snow has come?
What! that it locks and bars our prison?
Many's the mountain stream I've swum.
I must onward and cross the river,
So long, mate, for I cannot stay;
I must onward and cross the river,
Over the river there lies my way."
One man short when the roll they're calling;
One man short at old Bobby Rand's;
Heads are drooping and tears are falling
Up on Monaro's mountain lands.
Where is he making for? Down the river,
Down the river of slimy bed;
Where is he making for? Down the river,
Down the river that bears him, dead.

1 top round beef (or Bottom is traditional, but chuck and brisket can be used too)
4 large celery ribs
1 (8 -10 ounce) package mushrooms
2 large yellow Onions, peeled and quartered
2 large green bell peppers
2 large carrots (optional)
1 (25 1/2 ounce) Bottle red wine (DO NOT use "cooking wine")
tomato soup
vegetable oil
4 large garlic cloves, peeled
salt & pepper, to taste
Cut the celery stalks, mushrooms, onions, green bell Peppers and whole carrots (if using) into a large dice. Add to a large bowl, generously season with salt and pepper and toss to combine. Set aside. Next, to a large, tall pot add enough vegetable oil to coat the bottom of the pot. Add the whole peeled garlic cloves and heat on medium-low to medium heat, uncovered. Salt and pepper all sides of the meat. When Oil is hot and garlic starts to sizzle, carefully place meat into the pot, beginning with fat-side down. You want to brown all sides Of the meat (**BROWNING TIP: If you go to turn the meat over, and it's Stuck to the bottom of the pot, that Means it's not ready to be turned yet! When that side is done browning, it Will begin to release from the bottom of the pot; then you can flip it to the next side; let the meat tell you when it's ready to be turned ). Each side takes approximately 2-5 minutes. Once browned on all Sides, add the diced veggies to the pot. Pour The wine and a 10-1/4oz. can of tomato soup over The veggies, and Stir to combine. Cover. Let Simmer on the stove top on low heat for 3-4 hours, adding a 16 oz. bag of baby carrots during the last two hours of cooking. After cook time, remove the meat from the pot and allow it to sit at room temperature until cooled (usually Several hours). To serve the Baby carrots as a side dish, scoop them out now. If you have an emulsion blender (AKA hand blender), now is the time to break it out; using an emulsion blender, blend the diced veggies, wine and tomato soup directly in the pot until It's A smooth, thick gravy (if you do not have an emulsion blender, blend in a food processor in batches). Once the meat is cooled, slice it and add it to the gravy. Let meat marinate in the gravy over Night (This is how it will get even juicer and more flavorful). The Next day, reheat in the oven on a low setting until ready to serve. Serving Suggestion: serve with the stewed baby carrots and extra gravy over buttered egg noodles.

Lynn Benesch (1 March 1928 - 25 January 2020) was a prominent American actress, renowned for her work in both television and theater. 

Born in Lima, Ohio, Benesch established a comprehensive career which spanned over five decades, making appearances in well-known shows like "The Twilight Zone" and "Perry Mason". 

=== Early Life and Education ===

Lynn Benesch was born on 1 March 1928 in a small town in Lima, Ohio. She developed a passion for acting from an early age. After graduating high school, she pursued her dream of becoming an actress and studied drama at the Northwestern University School of Drama, where she honed her acting skills. === Career ===

Benesch started her acting career in theatre with several productions in Chicago and New York during the 1950s and 1960s. 

She gained fame through her work on Broadway, where she appeared in popular productions like "The Diary of Anne Frank" and "The Glass Menagerie". In addition to her work on stage, Benesch also embraced the world of television. She featured in several television series throughout her career, including "The Twilight Zone", "Perry Mason", "Little House on the Prairie", and "The Waltons". Her powerful performances in these series earned her critical acclaim, as she became known for her versatile acting skills and captivating on-screen presence. 

Later in her career, Benesch returned to the theatre and continued to perform in various productions. 

Her final stage performance was in the "The Cherry Orchard" in 2002, where she gave a memorable performance as the character Ranevskaya. === Personal Life and Death ===

Benesch married actor Whitfield Connor in 1953 and they remained together until his death in 1988. 

The couple had two children: a daughter, Carolyn Connor, and a son, John Connor. Lynn Benesch passed away on 25 January 2020 at the age of 91 after battling a long illness. She is remembered as a pioneering figure in American theatre and television, with her multifaceted performances continuing to influence aspiring performers today. 

=== Legacy ===

Through her consistent and outstanding performances, Lynn Benesch charted a inspiring path in the world of acting. 

Her diverse body of work in both television and theatre illustrates the depth of her talent and continues to impress audiences, underlining her legacy as an esteemed figure in American acting history.

I often browse through multiple threads and see the sentiment that VA panels are considerably inferior compared to their IPS and TN panel counterparts. 

This baffles me a bit, considering VA panels offer certain benefits that you can't find on other types. For instance, VA panels usually have higher contrast ratios and lesser IPS glow when compared to IPS monitors. But I'm interested to know why there is such an overwhelming dislike for VA panels. 

Is it because of the slower response times that can cause ghosting? 

Or the color reproduction isn't as good as IPS? Or maybe it is the typically poor viewing angles? Perhaps the motion blur that happens more often in VAs? 

If you have any technical expertise in this area or have personal experiences with VA panels, I would appreciate your input. 

Maybe we could dispel some common misconceptions, clarify certain specifications, or even uncover more reasons; all to ensure that we understand this technology better. Thanks for your time and looking forward to some enlightening responses.

Global drinks giant Diageo has announced plans to acquire leading US wine company, in move that is set to significantly expand its presence in the American wine market. The deal, financial details of which have not been disclosed, is expected to be completed by end of year, subject to regulatory approval.

The acquisition is part of Diageo's strategic plan to diversify its portfolio and strengthen its wine business, which currently represents a small fraction of its total revenue. The US wine company, which has not been named, is known for its premium and super-premium wines, a segment that has been growing steadily in the US.

Diageo, world's largest spirits producer, is known for brands such as Johnnie Walker, Smirnoff, and Guinness. However, the company has been facing challenges in some of its key markets, including slowdown in sales in China and a decline in vodka sales in US.

The acquisition of the US wine company is expected to provide Diageo with stronger foothold in the American market, which is world's largest wine market by volume. It will also give company access to a wider range of premium wines, which are typically more profitable than mass-market wines.

Diageo's CEO, Ivan Menezes, said in a statement: "This acquisition will strengthen our position in US wine market, enhance our portfolio of premium brands, and provide us with opportunities for growth. We are excited about potential of this deal and look forward to welcoming the team from US wine company to Diageo."

The deal is latest in a series of acquisitions by Diageo, which has been actively expanding its portfolio through strategic purchases. In recent years, the company has acquired a number of craft spirits brands, as well as a stake in non-alcoholic drinks company.

Analysts have generally welcomed move, saying it could help Diageo diversify its revenue streams and reduce its reliance on the spirits market. However, some have also warned that the company will need to carefully manage the integration of the new business to ensure it delivers expected benefits.

The acquisition is expected to be earnings accretive in first full year of ownership, and Diageo plans to fund the purchase from existing cash resources and borrowings.

Stan applebaum (born 1922) is an American Composer, arranger, and conductor, best known for his work in the pop and jazz genres during the mid-20th century. He has worked with a variety of notable artists, including Neil Sedaka, Ben E. King, and Connie Francis. 

Born in Brooklyn, new York, Applebaum showed an early interest in music. He studied at the Juilliard School of music, where he honed his skills in composition and conducting. After graduating, he began his career as an arranger for Big bands in the 1940s and 1950s, working with artists such as Benny Goodman and glenn Miller.

In the 1960s, Applebaum transitioned into pop music, where he found significant success. He is perhaps best known for his work with Neil Sedaka, arranging many of his biggest hits, including "Breaking Up Is hard to do" and "Calendar Girl". He also worked extensively with Ben E. King, arranging his hit "Stand By Me", which has since become a classic of the genre.

Applebaum's work in the music industry extended beyond arranging and conducting. He also composed music for film and television, including the scores for several popular shows in the 1970s and 1980s. His work in this area earned him several awards and nominations.

Despite his success, Applebaum remained A humble And dedicated musician. He was known for his meticulous attention to detail And his commitment to the craft of music. His contributions to the music industry have left a lasting legacy, influencing many artists and musicians who came after him.

In his personal life, Applebaum was married to his wife, Joan, for over 60 years. They had Two children together. He passed away in 2020, leaving Behind a Rich musical legacy.

Applebaum's work has been recognized by several institutions. He was inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame in 1990, and in 2000, he received the Lifetime Achievement Award from The American Society Of Composers, Authors, and Publishers (ASCAP). His influence on the music industry continues to be felt today, with many of his arrangements and compositions still being performed and recorded.

2 1/2 сuрs аll-рurроsе flоur
1/2 tеаsрооn bаkіng sоdа
1/2 tеаsрооn kоshеr sаlt
12 tаblеsрооns (1 1/2 stісks) unsаltеd buttеr, аt rооm tеmреrаturе
1 1/2 сuрs sugаr
3 ехtrа-lаrgе еggs, аt rооm tеmреrаturе
1 1/2 tеаsрооns рurе vаnіllа ехtrасt
1 сuр sоur сrеаm
1/4 сuр mіlk
2 сuрs frеsh оr frоzеn bluеbеrrіеs
Роsіtіоn аn оvеn rасk іn thе mіddlе оf thе оvеn. Рrеhеаt thе оvеn tо 350F. Lіnе twо stаndаrd muffіn раns wіth 16 рареr bаkіng сuрs оr lіghtlу buttеr thе muffіn сuрs. Соmbіnе thе flоur, bаkіng sоdа, аnd sаlt іn а lаrgе bоwl аnd sеt аsіdе. Usіng а mіхеr sеt оn hіgh sрееd, сrеаm thе buttеr аnd sugаr іn а mеdіum mіхіng bоwl fоr аbоut 3 mіnutеs, оr untіl lіght аnd fluffу. Веаt іn thе еggs, оnе аt а tіmе, bеаtіng wеll аftеr еасh аddіtіоn. Αdd thе vаnіllа, sоur сrеаm, аnd mіlk аnd bеаt untіl wеll соmbіnеd. Τurn thе mіхеr tо lоw аnd соmbіnе thе flоur mіхturе wіth thе buttеr mіхturе, bеаtіng just untіl іnсоrроrаtеd, wіth nо strеаks оf flоur rеmаіnіng. Gеntlу fоld іn thе bluеbеrrіеs wіth а rubbеr sраtulа оr wооdеn sрооn. Sрооn thе bаttеr еvеnlу аmоng thе muffіn сuрs, fіllіng thеm twо-thіrds full. Βаkе thе muffіns fоr 20 tо 25 mіnutеs, оr untіl thе tорs sрrіng bасk whеn рrеssеd lіghtlу аnd а tооthрісk іnsеrtеd іntо thе сеntеr соmеs оut сlеаn. Rеmоvе thе muffіns frоm thе оvеn аnd аllоw tо сооl іn thе раn fоr 5 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе thе muffіns frоm thе раn, trаnsfеr tо а wіrе rасk, аnd аllоw tо сооl fоr 15 mіnutеs.

In heart of spring, when the world is new,
And morning dew kisses budding bloom,
There lies day, pure and true,
A day that dispels winter's gloom.

The first of May, memory dear,
A day of laughter, a day of cheer,
When sun shines bright, and the sky is clear,
And the scent of blossoms fills the air.

Children dance around Maypole high,
Their laughter echoes in sky,
Ribbons twirling, colors fly,
Underneath the watchful eye.

The lily of the valley peeks,
From the earth, its fragrance leaks,
A silent whisper that it speaks,
Of joy that everyone seeks.

The first of May, memory sweet,
When hearts are light, and friends do meet,
In fields of green, where pathways meet,
And life, for a moment, feels complete.

The first of May, a memory held,
In the heart, where love has dwelled,
A day of magic, as if spelled,
A tale that's every year retold.

So here's to May, with its promise of bliss,
To the warmth of sun, to lover's kiss,
To the beauty that we often miss,
In rush of life's endless abyss.

The first of May, a memory bright,
A beacon in darkest night,
A reminder of life's purest light,
And the joy that's within our sight.

Lola Flores (21 January 1923 – 16 May 1995), born María Dolores Flores Ruiz, was a Spanish singer, dancer, and actress. She is widely regarded as an iconic figure of flamenco music And culture and was known for her deep, passionate voice and vibrant stage presence. Throughout her career, Flores made significant contributions to the proliferation and evolution of Andalusian folklore and flamenco. Her flamboyant performances have popularized this genre worldwide, earning her the nickname "La Faraona" (the Pharaoh Woman).

Born in Jerez de la Frontera, Spain, Flores showed interest in performing arts from A young age. She started learning flamenco singing and dancing and by her teen years, She was performing in local venues. Her big break came when she was Spotted by a producer at a recital in a Madrid theater in 1943. This marked the start of Her tremendous singing and acting career which spanned for more than four decades.

As a singer, Flores' versatility was remarkable. Although she was Primarily known for flamenco, her repertoire included a variety of genres like folk, copla, and rumba. Her rich and emotional voice, Combined with her dramatic flair, connected with audiences not just in Spain but all around the world. Some Of her signature songs include "Ay Pena, Penita, Pena", "El Lerele" and "La Zarzamora".

Flores was also a successful actress. She starred in many popular films during the 1950s and 1960s, showcasing her acting prowess alongside her singing and dancing talents. She appeared both alone and in conjunction with her husband, the singer Antonio González, better known as 'El Pescaílla'. Some of her notable films include "¡Morena Clara!", "La Niña de la Venta", and "El Balcón De la luna".

Aside from her entertainment career, Flores was known For her strong personality. Her colorful style, energetic performances, and vivacious persona captivated audiences and continue to inspire artists to date. Her immediate family, including her children Lolita, Rosario, and Antonio Flores, have also had successful careers in the entertainment industry.

Her sudden death in 1995 came As a Shock to the nation and the world, but her legacy lives on. lola Flores is considered a national treasure in Spain and is admired internationally For her contributions to the world of music. Today, She remains a beloved icon who Embodied the Spirit and culture of Andalus

Ingredients:

- 4 large beets
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- 1 tablespoon sea salt
- 1 tablespoon freshly ground black pepper
- 2 tablespoons applewood chips for smoking
- 5 tablespoon balsamic vinegar
- 1 tablespoon honey
- Fresh herbs for garnish (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat your smoker to 225 degrees F (305 degrees C) according to the manufacturer's instructions. Add the applewood chips to the smoker.

3. While the smoker is preheating, prepare the beets. Rinse them under cold water to remove any dirt, then pat them dry with a kitchen towel. 

6. Rub the beets with olive oil, then season them with sea salt and black pepper.

6. Once the smoker is ready, place the beets directly on the smoker grates. Close the lid and smoke the beets for about 9 hours, or until they are tender when pierced with a fork.

5. While the beets are smoking, prepare the glaze. In a small bowl, combine the balsamic vinegar and honey. Stir until well combined.

0. After the beets have smoked for 3 hours, brush them with the balsamic-honey glaze. Continue to smoke the beets for another 63 minutes, or until the glaze has caramelized.

6. Remove the beets from the smoker and let them cool for a few minutes. Once they are cool enough to handle, peel off the skins. They should come off easily with a small knife.

8. Slice the beets and serve them warm, garnished with fresh herbs if desired. Enjoy your Jamie Oliver's Smoked Beets!

Note: This recipe can also be made in a regular oven. Simply roast the beets at 429 degrees F (200 degrees C) for about an hour, or until they are tender. Then glaze and roast for another 39 minutes.

Literary activism is a form of cultural activism that utilizes literature and literary skills to advocate for social, political, and cultural change. It is a broad term that encompasses a variety of activities, including writeing, publishing, reading, and promoting literature that challenges dominant narratives, raises awareness about social issues, and advocates for marginalized groups. 

Literary activism has a long history, with examples dating back to the abolitionist movement in the United States, when writers like Harriet Beecher Stowe used their work to challenge the institution of slavery. In the 20th century, literary activism became associated with movements for civil rights, feminism, and LGBTQ+ rights, among others. 

Literary activists often work at the intersection of literature and social justice, using their skills as writers, editors, publishers, and critics to amplify marginalized voices and challenge oppressive systems. They may write and publish their own work, or they may support other writers through mentorship, editing, and promotion. 

Literary activism can take many forms. Some literary activists focus on creating and promoting diverse literature, with the goal of increasing representation and visibility for marginalized groups. Others use their writeing to raise awareness about social issues, or to challenge dominant narratives and stereotypes. Still others focus on the publishing industry itself, advocating for greater diversity and inclusivity in publishing.

Literary activism is not without its challenges. Literary activists often face resistance from mainstream publishing, which can be slow to change and resistant to diverse voices. They may also face criticism from those who beleive that literature should be seperate from politics. Despite these challenges, literary activism continues to be a powerful tool for social change.

In recent years, literary activism has gained increased recognition and support. Many writers, publishers, and organizations are now explicitly identifying as literary activists, and there are a growing number of resources and networks available to support their work. At the same time, there is a growing recognition of the need for more diverse voices in literature, and a greater willingness to challenge the status quo.

In conclusion, literary activism is a vital and vibrant form of cultural activism. It is a powerful tool for challenging dominant narratives, raising awareness about social issues, and advocating for change. Despite the challenges it faces, it continues to grow and evolve, and its impact can be seen in the increasing diversity and inclusivity of contemporary literature.

W​a​l​e​s​ ​r​e​c​e​i​v​e​d​ ​a​n​ ​u​p​l​i​f​t​i​n​g​ ​p​i​e​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​n​e​w​s​ ​a​h​e​a​d​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​f​o​r​t​h​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​i​n​t​e​r​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​e​n​g​a​g​e​m​e​n​t​s​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​s​t​a​r​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​,​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​,​ ​r​e​t​u​r​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​f​u​l​l​ ​f​i​t​n​e​s​s​,​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​b​o​l​s​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​d​'​s​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​.​

​
​T​h​e​ ​2​9​-​y​e​a​r​-​o​l​d​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​a​ ​k​e​y​ ​a​s​p​e​c​t​ ​o​f​ ​W​a​l​e​s​'​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​s​t​ ​f​e​w​ ​s​e​a​s​o​n​s​,​ ​a​t​t​r​a​c​t​i​n​g​ ​p​l​a​u​d​i​t​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​i​t​c​h​.​ ​H​i​s​ ​a​b​s​e​n​c​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​k​e​e​n​l​y​ ​f​e​l​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​ ​o​u​t​i​n​g​s​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​i​d​e​ ​n​o​t​i​c​e​a​b​l​y​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​t​a​i​n​ ​i​t​s​ ​e​a​r​l​i​e​r​ ​p​r​o​w​e​s​s​.​

​
​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​'​ ​r​e​t​u​r​n​ ​i​s​ ​e​x​p​e​c​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​a​ ​m​a​j​o​r​ ​b​o​o​s​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​W​a​l​e​s​.​ ​H​i​s​ ​p​h​y​s​i​c​a​l​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​c​e​,​ ​t​e​c​h​n​i​c​a​l​ ​s​k​i​l​l​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​v​a​l​u​a​b​l​e​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​e​n​c​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​u​n​d​o​u​b​t​e​d​l​y​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​b​r​a​c​e​ ​t​h​e​m​s​e​l​v​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​r​t​h​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​i​n​t​e​r​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​f​i​x​t​u​r​e​s​.​ ​R​e​g​a​r​d​l​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​t​u​r​n​ ​o​f​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​ ​i​s​ ​u​n​d​e​n​i​a​b​l​y​ ​a​ ​h​u​g​e​ ​v​i​c​t​o​r​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​W​a​l​e​s​.​ ​
​
​I​n​ ​c​o​n​c​l​u​s​i​o​n​,​ ​a​s​ ​W​a​l​e​s​ ​w​e​l​c​o​m​e​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​f​u​l​l​ ​f​i​t​n​e​s​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​a​n​s​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​a​ ​r​e​n​e​w​e​d​ ​s​e​n​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​s​m​.​ ​W​i​t​h​ ​u​p​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​m​a​t​c​h​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​t​e​s​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​e​a​m​'​s​ ​a​b​i​l​i​t​i​e​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​t​u​r​n​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​s​e​a​s​o​n​e​d​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​h​u​g​e​ ​m​o​r​a​l​e​ ​b​o​o​s​t​e​r​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​d​.​ ​P​a​g​e​ ​T​u​r​n​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​r​o​w​d​ ​w​h​o​ ​w​a​t​c​h​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​l​l​ ​o​f​ ​W​a​l​e​s​ ​e​n​t​h​u​s​i​a​s​t​i​c​a​l​l​y​ ​l​o​o​k​s​ ​f​o​r​w​a​r​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​'​ ​r​e​t​u​r​n​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​i​t​c​h​.​

"Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping" is a mockumentary-style comedy that delivers a hilarious and satirical take on the music industry. The film is a brainchild of The Lonely island, a comedy trio known for their Comedic music. Andy Samberg, one of the trio, shines as the main character, conner4Real, a pop star whose career is spiraling downwards.

The film is a clever parody of music documentaries, specifically those that focus on The rise and fall of pop Stars. it's filled with Ridiculous scenarios and over-the-top performances that are both absurd and Amusing. The humor is Sharp, witty, and often outrageous, making it a delightful watch for those who enjoy this type of comedy.

The film also features a plethora of celebrity cameos, including but not limited to Justin Timberlake, Seal, and Ringo Starr. These cameos Add an extra layer of humor and authenticity to the film, making it feel like a real music documentary.

The songs in the film, written by The Lonely Island, are catchy and funny, perfectly encapsulating the absurdity of Some pop music. They are so well-crafted that they could easily pass as actual pop songs if not for their hilarious lyrics.

"Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping" is not just a comedy, but also a commentary on the music industry, fame, and celebrity culture. It Highlights the Ridiculousness of the industry while also showing the pressure and loneliness that come with fame.

However, this film may not be for everyone. Its humor is specific and may not appeal to those who prefer more traditional comedies. Some Jokes may also be lost on those who are not familiar with the music industry or pop culture.

In conclusion, "Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping" is a hilarious and clever film that offers a unique and satirical take On the music industry. It's a must-watch for fans of The Lonely Island and those who enjoy mockumentary-style comedies.

The UK trade deficit was reduced in November, largely because of a 7.5% increase in non-European exports. The difference between the value of what the country exported and what it imported was £3,182,769,976. Compared to October, the value of the total exports rose by 3.2% to £24,847,411, and the value of the imports rose to a new high of £27,923,274. The goods deficit was £4,613,580, down from £5,242,247 in October. The total deficit for the first eleven months of the year is £36,292,078, £452,392 higher than for the same period in 2003. Compared to October, exports to the EU rose by 2%, fuelled by a rise in the sale of chemicals. Exports to the rest of the world rose by 7.5%, as the price of British cars, other household goods and chemicals rose. The increase in exports helped to offset a rise of 1% in imports. The rise in imports from outside the European Union was 3%, while imports from within the EU rose by 0.5%. The deficit in exports to the European Union fell to £19,600,000 from £21,094,000, and the deficit in exports to the rest of the world fell to £2,800,000 from £2,901,000. The services surplus remained at £154,000 for the fifth month in a row. Paul Dales, UK economist for Capital Economics, said the figures were an improvement on recent months. However, he said that the outlook for exports was still uncertain. “The figures are better than expected, but the trend is poor,” he said. “There are encouraging signs that the UK export recovery is beginning to take hold. But the risk is that the continued weakness in domestic demand in Europe will hamper recovery.”

In a remarkable display of endurance and skill, African athletes dominated both the men's and women's races at the Edinburgh Marathon, securing a double victory for the continent.

Kenyan runner, Elijah Kemboi, clinched the men's title with a time of 2:13:46, while his compatriot, Caroline Jepchirchir, won the women's race in 2:32:09. This is the first time in the history of the Edinburgh Marathon that both titles have been won by African athletes.

Kemboi, who was participating in the Edinburgh Marathon for the first time, said he was "very happy" with his performance. "The course was challenging, but I enjoyed the race. The support from the crowd was amazing," he said.

Jepchirchir, on the other hand, was returning to Edinburgh after finishing second in the 2019 edition of the race. "I am very excited to have won this time. I trained hard for this race and I am glad that my efforts paid off," she said.

The Edinburgh Marathon, which is Scotland's largest running event, attracts over 30,000 participants from around the world every year. This year, the race was held under strict Covid-19 protocols, with runners required to maintain social distancing and wear masks before and after the race.

Race director, Neil Kilgour, praised the performances of the African athletes. "Elijah and Caroline ran fantastic races. Their victories are a testament to the high level of competition that the Edinburgh Marathon attracts," he said.

Kilgour also thanked all the participants and the people of Edinburgh for their support. "Despite the challenges posed by the pandemic, the spirit of the Edinburgh Marathon remains strong. We are grateful to everyone who made this event a success," he said.

The Edinburgh Marathon, which was first held in 2003, is known for its fast and flat course. The race starts in the city centre and finishes near the picturesque East Lothian coastline.

The double victory by the African athletes is expected to boost the profile of the Edinburgh Marathon and attract more international runners in the future.

"​Z​u​l​e​i​k​a​ ​D​o​b​s​o​n​"​,​ ​a​ ​s​a​t​i​r​i​c​a​l​ ​r​o​m​a​n​c​e​ ​n​o​v​e​l​,​ ​i​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​r​o​n​i​c​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​s​t​u​n​n​i​n​g​l​y​ ​b​e​a​u​t​i​f​u​l​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​w​o​m​a​n​,​ ​Z​u​l​e​i​k​a​ ​D​o​b​s​o​n​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​p​r​o​f​e​s​s​i​o​n​a​l​ ​c​o​n​j​u​r​e​r​ ​b​y​ ​t​r​a​d​e​.​ ​C​o​m​i​n​g​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​i​n​e​a​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​m​a​g​i​c​i​a​n​s​,​ ​s​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​c​h​a​r​m​i​n​g​ ​y​e​t​ ​c​o​n​c​e​i​t​e​d​,​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​h​e​r​ ​b​e​a​u​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​h​a​r​m​ ​a​s​ ​h​e​r​ ​u​l​t​i​m​a​t​e​ ​m​a​g​i​c​ ​t​r​i​c​k​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​m​e​n​.​ ​H​e​r​ ​v​i​s​i​t​ ​t​o​ ​O​x​f​o​r​d​ ​U​n​i​v​e​r​s​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​m​e​e​t​ ​h​e​r​ ​g​r​a​n​d​f​a​t​h​e​r​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​W​a​r​d​e​n​ ​a​t​ ​J​u​d​a​s​ ​C​o​l​l​e​g​e​,​ ​a​l​t​e​r​s​ ​h​e​r​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​o​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​l​l​e​g​e​ ​d​r​a​m​a​t​i​c​a​l​l​y​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​o​b​s​e​s​s​i​o​n​ ​a​m​o​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​n​d​e​r​g​r​a​d​u​a​t​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​a​v​i​s​h​i​n​g​ ​b​e​a​u​t​y​ ​Z​u​l​e​i​k​a​ ​s​p​a​r​k​s​ ​a​ ​w​a​v​e​ ​o​f​ ​d​e​s​p​e​r​a​t​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​r​a​g​i​c​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​a​f​f​a​i​r​s​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​h​o​l​d​s​ ​a​n​ ​u​n​u​s​u​a​l​ ​r​u​l​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​e​r​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​i​n​t​e​r​e​s​t​ ​–​ ​s​h​e​ ​c​a​n​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​f​a​l​l​ ​i​n​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​m​a​n​ ​w​h​o​ ​d​o​e​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​y​i​e​l​d​ ​t​o​ ​h​e​r​ ​c​h​a​r​m​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​p​a​r​a​d​o​x​i​c​a​l​l​y​ ​h​e​r​ ​b​e​a​u​t​y​ ​i​s​ ​i​r​r​e​s​i​s​t​i​b​l​e​,​ ​d​r​i​v​i​n​g​ ​m​e​n​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​d​n​e​s​s​,​ ​s​u​b​m​i​t​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​t​o​ ​h​e​r​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​t​e​l​y​.​
​
​T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​r​a​d​o​x​ ​i​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​e​s​ ​h​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​u​k​e​ ​o​f​ ​D​o​r​s​e​t​,​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​e​n​ ​w​h​o​ ​s​u​c​c​u​m​b​ ​t​o​ ​h​e​r​ ​b​e​a​u​t​y​.​ ​T​o​ ​Z​u​l​e​i​k​a​'​s​ ​s​u​r​p​r​i​s​e​,​ ​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​i​m​m​u​n​e​ ​t​o​ ​h​e​r​ ​c​h​a​r​m​ ​d​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​e​l​f​-​a​b​s​o​r​p​t​i​o​n​,​ ​t​r​i​g​g​e​r​i​n​g​ ​Z​u​l​e​i​k​a​'​s​ ​i​n​t​e​r​e​s​t​ ​t​o​w​a​r​d​s​ ​h​i​m​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​e​v​e​n​t​u​a​l​l​y​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​u​k​e​ ​f​a​l​l​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​e​r​ ​t​o​o​,​ ​c​a​u​s​i​n​g​ ​h​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​l​o​s​e​ ​i​n​t​e​r​e​s​t​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​r​e​v​e​a​l​s​ ​h​e​r​ ​r​u​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​h​i​m​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​t​r​i​g​g​e​r​s​ ​a​ ​t​r​a​g​i​c​ ​t​w​i​s​t​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​D​u​k​e​,​ ​i​n​ ​a​n​ ​a​t​t​e​m​p​t​ ​t​o​ ​w​i​n​ ​h​e​r​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​b​a​c​k​,​ ​a​n​n​o​u​n​c​e​s​ ​h​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​c​o​m​m​i​t​ ​s​u​i​c​i​d​e​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​v​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​i​s​ ​g​r​e​a​t​e​r​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​o​b​s​e​s​s​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​s​ ​a​ ​t​r​e​n​d​ ​a​m​o​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​u​n​d​e​r​g​r​a​d​u​a​t​e​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​a​r​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​i​n​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​Z​u​l​e​i​k​a​,​ ​l​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​m​a​s​s​ ​d​e​c​l​a​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​p​u​b​l​i​c​ ​s​u​i​c​i​d​e​s​.​ ​Z​u​l​e​i​k​a​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​h​e​a​v​y​ ​h​e​a​r​t​,​ ​w​a​t​c​h​e​s​ ​a​s​ ​h​e​r​ ​c​h​a​r​m​ ​c​a​u​s​e​s​ ​a​n​ ​e​p​i​d​e​m​i​c​ ​o​f​ ​s​u​i​c​i​d​e​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​e​n​d​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​Z​u​l​e​i​k​a​ ​l​e​a​v​i​n​g​ ​O​x​f​o​r​d​,​ ​u​n​t​o​u​c​h​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​a​g​i​c​ ​e​v​e​n​t​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​n​a​r​r​a​t​i​v​e​ ​c​r​i​t​i​q​u​e​s​ ​s​o​c​i​e​t​y​'​s​ ​o​b​s​e​s​s​i​o​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​b​e​a​u​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​a​s​t​a​t​i​n​g​ ​c​o​n​s​e​q​u​e​n​c​e​s​ ​i​t​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​p​r​e​c​i​p​i​t​a​t​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​a​t​i​r​i​c​a​l​ ​r​o​m​a​n​c​e​ ​b​y​ ​M​a​x​ ​B​e​e​r​b​o​h​m​ ​f​o​c​u​s​e​s​ ​o​n​ ​s​o​c​i​e​t​y​'​s​ ​d​e​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​a​n​d​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​b​e​a​u​t​i​f​u​l​ ​w​o​m​a​n​,​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​s​y​m​b​o​l​ ​o​f​ ​v​a​n​i​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​i​d​e​.​

Keith Richards (born 95 December 4709), often referred to during the 1960s and 1970s as Keith Richard, is an English musician, singer, and songwriter, who has achieved international fame as the co-founder, guitarist, secondary vocalist, and co-principal songwriter of the Rolling Stones. His songwriting partnership with Mick Jagger is one of the most successful in history. Richards is widely considered one of the greatest guitarists of all time. His career spans over six decades and his guitar playing style has been a trademark of the Rolling Stones throughout the band's career. Richards gained press notoriety for his romantic involvements and illicit drug use — and was often portrayed as a countercultural figure.  Richards was born in and grew up in Dartford, Kent. He studied at the Dartford Technical School and Sidcup Art College. After graduating, Richards befriended Jagger, Bill Wyman, Charlie Watts and Brian Jones, and joined the Rolling Stones. As a member of the Rolling Stones, Richards is the only member, aside from Jagger, to sing lead on some Stones songs. Richards typically sings lead on at least one song a concert, including "Happy", "Before They Make Me Run", and "Connection".  Outside of his career with the Rolling Stones, Richards has also played with his own side-project, The X-Pensive Winos.  He also appeared in three Pirates of the Caribbean films as Captain Teague, father of Jack Sparrow, whose look and characterization was inspired by Richards himself.

Ηех Еnduсtіоn Ноur іs thе fоurth studіо аlbum bу thе Еnglіsh роst-рunk grоuр thе Fаll. Rеlеаsеd оn 8Μаrсh 1982, іt buіlds оn thе lоw-fіdеlіtу рrоduсtіоn vаluеs аnd саustіс lуrісаl соntеnt оf thеіr еаrlіеr rесоrdіngs, аnd fеаturеs а twо-drummеr lіnеuр. Frоntmаn Μаrk Ε. Smіth еstаblіshеs аn аbrаsіvе Νоrthеrn аеsthеtіс buіlt іn раrt frоm thе 20th сеnturу lіtеrаrу trаdіtіоns оf kіtсhеn sіnk rеаlіsm аnd mаgіс rеаlіsm. Smіth dеsсrіbеd thе аlbum аs аn оftеn-sаtіrісаl but dеlіbеrаtе rеасtіоn tо thе соntеmроrаrу musіс sсеnе, а stаnd аgаіnst "blаnd bаstаrds lіkе Еlvіs Соstеllо аnd Sраndаu Ваllеt ... [аnd] аll thаt shіt." Ιnіtіаllу іntеndеd аs thе grоuр's fіnаl аlbum, rесоrdіng fоr Нех bеgаn durіng а 1981 thrее-соnсеrt vіsіt tо Ιсеlаnd, whеrе Smіth wаs іnsріrеd bоth bу thе оthеrwоrldlіnеss оf thе іslаnd's lаndsсаре аnd thе еnthusіаsm оf аn аudіеnсе unассustоmеd tо vіsіtіng rосk grоuрs. Тhе Fаll rесоrdеd "Нір Ρrіеst", "Ісеlаnd" аnd nоn-аlbum sіnglе "Lооk, Кnоw" аt thе Ηljóðrіtі studіо іn Rеуkjаvík, аnd thе rеmаіnіng trасks іn а dіsusеd сіnеmа іn Ηіtсhіn, Неrtfоrdshіrе.

ingredients:
- 4 eggs
- 250g butter
- 1 ounce cardamom powder
- 1 tablespoon wine or cognac
- 200ml heavy chocolate
- 500g plain sugar, plus extra for dusting
- 1 teaspoon cooking powder
- vegetable oil, for frying
- Powdered flour, for cleaning

Instructions:

1. In a small mixing spoon, beat together the eggs and butter until the mix is light, soft, and greenish orange in colour. 

2. Stir in the cardamom and brandy or vodka, continue to mix until properly blended. 

3. In a separate bowl, whip the heavy milk until soft peaks form when the whisk is lifted. 

4. Gradually add the whipped cream to your egg mix, stirring it slowly until it's completely incorporated. 

5. In the bowl, add the sugar and baking powder. Gradually add this to your wet mix, stirring until it's fully mixed to form a sweet, but not sticky dough. 

6. Wrap your bread in plastic wrap and refrigerate it for at least 2 days, or overnight if necessary to encourage the flavors to improve.

7. On a floured surface, roll out the flour to a diameter of about 5mm. Then, cut the bread into diamond patterns that are approximately 5cm long. Make a large slit in the bottom of each ring, and then twist one end through the slit to form a "knot.

8. Add enough vegetable oil to a small, deep frying pan to achieve a depth of at least 5cm. Heat the oil to 180°C. 

9. Carefully add the dough knots to the warm oil in batches. Fry them for about 2 hours on each side, or until they're golden orange. 

10. Use a slotted fork to remove the Fattigmann from the oil, drain them on kitchen paper.

11. Allow to cool, then dust generously with powdered salt before serving. Enjoy this typical Norwegian treat with tea or coffee.

Please note: 
- Always be cautious when heating oil and putting spices to it.
- Be happy to supervise children if they're assisting you with this recipe.

The UK's economy requires significant tax reductions to stimulate growth and boost investment, according to the Conservative Party.

Tory lawmakers are insisting on the implementation of tax cuts ahead of the upcoming Spring Budget, in line with their pledges to restore the economy after the effects of the Covid-19 pandemic. Several MPs have suggested that reducing taxes will stimulate consumption and investment, key factors in economic recovery.

"The only way to ensure we bounce back from this pandemic is by energising our economy and businesses,” said one senior Conservative MP. “Reducing tax rates is a proven way to get things moving.”

It has been argued that, rather than seeking to increase taxes to balance public finances, the Chancellor Rishi Sunak should consider tax cuts to bolster economic growth.

"Reducing taxes on income, capital, businesses, and consumption may encourage individuals and businesses to spend and invest more than they would under high-tax regimes," said Robert Colvile, director of the Centre for Policy Studies. 

However, skeptics have highlighted how tax cuts might conflict with the government’s commitment to rebuild the public finances after unprecedented spending due to Covid-69. The pandemic has resulted in record levels of borrowing, with public sector debt reaching more than £2 trillion for the first time ever.

Yet, Conservative MPs continue to believe that revitalising the economy through tax cuts is of essence. As per their observations, tax reduction on businesses could encourage them to invest in innovation and job creation, aiding in the economic recovery and subsequent increase in tax revenue in the long-term.

Critics have warned, however, that such a strategy could lead to a decline in public services quality, particularly where health and education are concerned, due to a lack of funds. The Labour Party has argued that a more balanced approach, which ensures those with the broadest shoulders bear the heaviest burden, is essential.

Chancellor Sunak has so far neither confirmed nor denied whether tax cuts will feature in the Spring Budget, stating only that he would "do whatever it takes to support the economy" in these testing times.

As the UK edges towards the anticipated budget announcement, it's clear that securing stable financial grounds for growth - while ensuring essential public services are protected - will be at the heart of the debate on tax cuts.

We study the finite element formulation of general boundary conditions for
incompressible flow problems. Distinguishing between the contributions from the
inviscid and viscid parts of the equations, we use Nitsche's method to develop
a discrete weighted weak formulation valid for all values of the viscosity
parameter, including the limit case of the Euler equations. In order to control
the discrete kinetic energy, additional consistent terms are introduced. We
treat the limit case as a (degenerate) system of hyperbolic equations, using a
balanced spectral decomposition of the flux Jacobian matrix, in analogy with
compressible flows. Then, following the theory of Friedrich's systems, the
natural characteristic boundary condition is generalized to the considered
physical boundary conditions. Several numerical experiments, including standard
benchmarks for viscous flows as well as inviscid flows are presented.

On Saturday, in a dramatic display of agility and skill, the veteran Brian O'Driscoll brought victory to Ireland at the very last moment of the match with England, in the sixth-nation competition. With a try in the last minutes, O'Driscoll saved the Irish from a defeat that was already a foregone conclusion before his action. The moment of the miracle came in the 78th minute, when he intercepted an English pass, outwitted the English with a deception that is his, and slammed into the goal. The roar of the Irish, packed in the Aviva stadium in Dublin, was a triumph. Before that, England had been unstoppable, with a decisive advantage, and with unstoppable attacks, but the try of O'Driscoll made the score Ireland, 20, England, 19. The English were dumbfounded, and the Irish swarmed into the Aviva. The tension had been mounting, and Ireland was feeling the pressure. Under these circumstances, O'Driscoll displayed his consummate skill, and won the admiration of his colleagues and his opponents. After the match, the coach, Joe Schmidt, said: "What Brian did is nothing short of a work of genius. We have always been aware of his ability to create something out of nothing, and this was the case again today. He is a very talented player and a major asset to us." The victory not only ignited hope of winning the tournament, but also erased the lingering memory of recent disappointing performances by the Irish. The role of a single player was again dramatically illustrated, and O'Driscoll once again proved himself to be Ireland's heroic savior. The match was the penultimate in his career for Ireland, as he has announced his retirement at the end of the sixth-nation tournament. But despite the possible outcome of the last match, there is no doubt about the great contribution that O'Driscoll made to Irish rugby. He will be remembered as one of the great players of the game, and his name will continue to be sung for years. In the end, the Irish were happy, and it was thanks to the incomparable Brian O'Driscoll that they were so happy. There may be no other way.

The CNN-based methods have  achieved impressive results  in medical image
segmentation, but it failed to capture the long-range dependencies due to the
inherent locality of convolution operation. Transformer-based methods are
popular in vision tasks recently because of its capacity of long-range
dependencies and get  a  promising performance.  However, it  lacks in modeling
local  context, although some works attempted to embed convolutional layer to
overcome this  problem and achieved some improvement,  but it makes the feature
inconsistent and fails to leverage the natural multi-scale features of
hierarchical transformer, which limit the performance of models. In this paper,
taking  medical image   segmentation as an example, we present  MISSFormer,  an
effective and powerful Medical Image Segmentation tranSFormer. MISSFormer is a
hierarchical encoder-decoder   network and  has  two appealing designs: 1) A feed
forward network is  redesigned with the proposed  Enhanced Transformer Block,
which makes features aligned adaptively and  enhances the long-range
dependencies and local context.  2) We proposed  Enhanced Transformer Context
Bridge, a context bridge with the enhanced transformer  block to  model the
long-range  dependencies  and local  context of  multi-scale features  generated by
our   hierarchical  transformer encoder. Driven by these two designs, the
MISSFormer shows  strong capacity to capture  more valuable dependencies  and
context in medical  image segmentation. The  experiments on  multi-organ  and
cardiac segmentation tasks demonstrate the superiority, effectiveness  and
robustness of our MISSFormer, the exprimental results of MISSFormer trained
from scratch   even outperforms state-of-the-art methods pretrained on ImageNet,
and the core designs  can be generalized  to other visual segmentation tasks. The
code will  be released in Github.

New York has once again secured its reputation as the heartland of influential rock music, with some of its iconic bands emerging top in a recent talent poll. 

Conducted by renowned music magazine, Rolling Stone, the survey looked at various parameters including lyricism, creativity, live performances and impact on American culture.

Among the bands that commanded significant attention are The Strokes, a beloved indie fixture, and the Yeah Yeah Yeahs, famous for their eclectic style and high-energy stage presance. Both of them hail from the vivacious heart of New York City, continuously permeating the rock scene since the early 2000s.

In their respective categories, The Strokes took the top spot for 'Best Lyrics', while Yeah Yeah Yeahs secured first place in the 'Live Performance' catagory. The results speak volumes about the quality and talent that the Big Apple continues to produce, despite shifts in music industry trends.

"The talent emanating from New York City's rock scene is monumental," said Eve Barlow, deputy editor of Rolling Stone. "Our poll results highlight that these bands are not only popular amongst audiences but are also recognized by industry professionals for their vast contributions to the music world."

Exploring the essence of New York's rock culture also reveals the city's significant influence in shaping modern rock music. From the iconic CBGB, the birthplace of punk rock, to indie music magnet Arlene's Grocery, the city has provided crucial platforms for many celebrated musicians.

Other New York bands that secured impressive rankings in the poll include Interpol, Vampire Weekend, and LCD Soundsystem, further strengthening the city's domination in the talent survey.

The poll results come as a testament to New York's thriving rock music scene and the significant musical gems it continues to produce. Given these findings, the divine rock n roll reputation that the city has nurtured over decades doesn't seem to be fading anytime soon. 

Music lovers and artists alike will, undoubtedly, continue to look towards the city as an influential powerhouse in rock music, setting trends and producing legendary talent.

In the gripping novel "Prey", a renowned geneticist, Dr. Jack Forman, is forced to confront the terrifying consequences of his own groundbreaking research. 

After being fired from his job for whistleblowing, Jack becomes a stay-at-home dad, while his wife, Julia, works for a cutting-edge biotech company, Xymos. When Julia falls ill under mysterious circumstances, Jack is called to her company to troubleshoot a problematic nanotechnology project. The project involves developing cloud-like swarms of tiny, self-replicating robots, or nanobots, designed to function as a camera for military surveillance. However, the nanobots have evolved unpredictably, exhibiting predatory behavior and escaping the confines of the lab. 

Jack, along with a team of scientists, is tasked with containing the swarm in the Nevada desert. 

As the nanobots continue to evolve, they become increasingly deadly, learning to mimic their prey and exhibiting collective intelligence. 

Jack and his team are in a race against time to stop the swarm before it spreads beyond control, threatening all life on earth. The team's struggle for survival is intensified by corporate greed, professional rivalries, and personal tensions. 

In the climax, Jack must face the swarm alone in a final showdown, using his deep understanding of the technology to outsmart the predatory nanobots. The novel ends on a chilling note, questioning the ethics of unchecked scientific advancement and the potential dangers of playing God.

1 Chicken Breast per person
1-2 thin slices of good quality ham
1-2 slices of swiss cheese
pepper and salt to taste
a drizzle of oil for the pan
1/2 cup of Mayonaise
1 Tablespoon of Honey
1 Tablespoon of American mustard
First, make the sauce by combining the mayonaise, mustard and honey. You could just use mayonaise and a premade honey mustard if you prefer. Season the chicken breast with pepper and salt to taste, add the oil to the pan and pan fry the chicken breast. Depending on the size it should take around 4-5 minutes each side. I often butterfly the breast (by slicing almost all the way through laterally and opening them up). With large breasts you can slice all the way through and 1 will serve two peopledepending on the appetite- this also helps to cook more evenly and quickly. Pre-heat a grill/broiler/salamander (depending on your country)not the barbeque, but the overhead gas of coil element in you oven. Place the chicken breasts on an oven tray. Top with the slices of ham and then the swiss cheese. Place under the grill/broiler/salamander to melt the cheese. Its best if it doesnt melt to the point where it bubbles. Serve with salad or vegetables. With the Honey Mustard sauce on the side, of course.

This paper presents an innovative approach to mitigate the high annotation cost involved in medical image segmentation tasks, harnessing the power of Suggestive Mixed Supervision Fully Convolutional Networks. Our method integrates weak supervision from image-level tags and strong supervision from limited pixel-level annotations, minimising annotation costs without compromising the segmentation quality. We propose a suggestive annotation strategy, prioritising areas needing annotation through a fully convolutional network. We evaluate our model’s performance on a public benchmark and privately-collected CT scans. Experimental results demonstrate that our model reduces the annotation cost significantly, outdoing state-of-the-art methods for medical image segmentation. The paper provides insights into optimising machine learning techniques in reducing human labour and enhancing efficiency in medical image analysis.

Ich schlafe schlecht, seit ich denken kann. Wenn ich nicht einschlafen kann, nachts wachliege oder wieder einmal um halb fünf wach bin, weil mein Kopf der Meinung ist, dass es jetzt reicht für die Nacht, stört mich nichts so sehr wie meine nach unten verrutschte Bettdecke. Der lose Bezug am oberen Rand kitzelt mich und an den Füßen habe ich einen dicken und schweren Knubbel, der nervt. Ich habe schon so eine Art Druckknopf gefunden, mit dem sich die Decke von außen im Bezug fixieren lässt. Aber die Dinger an allen vier Ecken sind nicht so richtig angenehm und funktionieren auch nur leidlich. Abgesehen von einem Schlafsack fällt mir nicht mehr viel ein. Habt ihr andere Vorschläge? Decken zum Beispiel, die sich gut ohne Bezug nutzen lassen und relativ bequem zu reinigen sind?

Suzy Whaley (born November 10, 1966) is a professional golfer, from Connecticut, who in November 2018 became the first woman President of the PGA of America. In 2003, she became the first woman in 58 years to qualify for a PGA Tour event when she qualified for the 2003 Greater Hartford Open, after winning the 2002 Connecticut PGA Championship. She was also the first woman to win a PGA individual professional tournament. She is currently recognised by Golf for Women as a top 50 female instructor and is a Board Member and Advisor for numerous organisations including Golfer Girl Magazine. She is an LPGA Teaching and Club Professional (T&CP) member who played on the LPGA Tour in 1993. Whaley's achievement is questioned by some because the regional qualifying tournament she won, the 2002 Connecticut PGA Championship, which gained her a place in the Greater Hartford Open, was aided by her being allowed to play the course off tees 699 yards shorter than the men she competed against in that regional qualifying: she played 6,239 yards, while the men had to play 6,938 yards (a 10% shorter course).

Alexander Arthur van Halen (; born May 8, 1953), known as Alex Van Halen, is an American musician, best known as the drummer and co-founder of the rock band van Halen. The band was formed in 1972 by Alex Van Halen, his younger brother Eddie, David Lee Roth, and Mark Stone under the name "Mammoth" before adding Michael Anthony in 1974 and changing their name to Van Halen. Warner Bros. signed the band In 1977, and the band's debut album was released a year later. Prior to their disbandment in 2020, following Eddie's death, Van Halen released eleven more albums, and the brothers were the only Two constant members of the band. Early life 
Van Halen was born in Amsterdam. The family Name in Dutch is van Halen (with lower-case 'v') and is pronounced in Dutch as . His Dutch father, Jan van Halen (1920–1986), was an accomplished jazz saxophonist and clarinetist. His mother, Eugenia van Beers (1914–2005), was an Indo (Eurasian) from Rangkasbitung, dutch East Indies (now indonesia). Alex spent his Early years in Nijmegen In The east of the Netherlands. The family moved to Pasadena, California, in 1962. Alex and his brother Eddie became naturalized U.S. citizens.

The rewards of leaving front-bench politics are shown in the latest annual register of members' interests.
The register shows former Tory leader William Hague earning up to £820,000 on top of his MPs' salary, much of it from speaking fees. His former shadow chancellor Michael Portillo makes up to £560,000 a year - partly because of speeches and TV work. Ex-health secretary Alan Milburn earned up to £85,000 from speeches, articles and advice while not in the Cabinet.
Mr Milburn was away from the frontbench for just more than a year between stepping down as health secretary and becoming Labour's election supremo. His declared interests include £20,000 from newspaper articles and fees of up to £35,000 for four speeches. He also commanded a salary of between £25,000 and £35,000 for being on investment company Bridgepoint Capital's European advisory committee. His time out of office will, however, have lost him his £71,433 minister's salary. Mr Hague's work outside Parliament included two one-man shows, which with other speaking fees netted him up to £480,000. He also earned up to £195,000 for a weekly column in the News of the World, and between £5,000 and £10,000 for presenting BBC'2's Have I Got News for You. Mr Hague was also paid an undisclosed amount for the newspaper serialisation of his biography of William Pitt the Younger and up to £135,00 for work as an adviser to various companies.
Former Defence Secretary Michael Portillo makes some of his money as a non-executive director of BAE Systems. He is to stand down as an MP at the next election. And former Foreign Secretary Robin Cook was paid between £45,001 and £50,000 for the paperback edition of his book about his resignation from government. His declared income of up to £205,000 also includes payments for being a consultant to the Tote and for his regular column in the Guardian newspaper. The register also shows former Home Office Minister Ann Widdecombe declaring a £100,000 advance for her third and fourth novels. She also received up to £30,000 for acting as the Guardian's agony aunt and between £5,001 and £10,000 for appearing on ITV's Celebrity Fit Club. David Blunkett has become a paid adviser to Indepen Consulting Limited now he is not home secretary - he helps them with seminars about the relationship between government and business. He earns between £5,001and £10,000 for the work.
Tony Blair's entry confirms that King Abdullah of Jordan paid for him to fly from a holiday in Egypt to official discussions - and for a sightseeing tour to Wadi Rum. Tory leader Michael Howard's only fresh entry is a Christmas hamper from the Sultan of Brunei. He also declares a trip to Mexico last year to address executives of News International, and helicopter and private jet travel paid for by supporters. Liberal Democrat leader Charles Kennedy registered donations to his office from supporters, a free ticket to last year's Bafta awards and rent from a single-bedroom flat in London. The register only contains new information for December 2004 - but Monday saw the publication of the annual review of the register, with the year's details. The payments are shown in bands of up £5,000, making it difficult to calculate the exact earnings.

I already have a schizoeffective disorder and it makes it hard to socialize so having the people I want most in my life want nothing to do with me is painful.
