Robert De Niro has completed a transatlantic box office double by topping the UK and US film charts with two different films at the same time.
Comedy sequel Meet the Fockers, in which he stars with Ben Stiller, Dustin Hoffman and Barbra Streisand, shot to the top of the UK chart at the weekend. It took £7.2m in three days - eight times more than the number two, Closer. Assault on Precinct 13 was in third. At the same time, US audiences were won over by his new thriller Hide and Seek. In Meet the Fockers, he picks up the role of an uptight father and ex-CIA agent from 2000 hit comedy, Meet the Parents.
It is a big leap to his role in Hide and Seek, a supernatural horror in which he plays a widower whose daughter's imaginary freind turns nasty. In the UK box office chart, Meet the Fockers pushed Closer off the top spot while police action movie Assault On Precinct 13, starring rapper Ja Rule, made £750,000 in its first weekend. London Underground thriller Creep was another new entry at six while quirky comedy Sideways, which got five Oscar nominations last week, entered in eighth place. The Oscar nominations do not seem to have had an impact on fans' choices at cinemas. Leading contenders The Aviator, Million Dollar Baby and Ray all suffered substantial drops in takings compared with the previous weekend.

Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs) bring together a global crowd of
thousands of learners for several weeks or months. In theory, the openness and
scale of MOOCs can promote iterative dialogueue that facilitates group cognition
and knowledge construction. Using data from two successive instances of a
popular business strategy MOOC, we philtre observed communication patterns to
arrive at the "significant" interaction networks between learners and use
complex network analysis to explore the vulnerability and information diffusion
potential of the discussion forums. We find that different discussion topics
and pedagogical practises promote varying levels of 1) "significant"
peer-to-peer engaugement, 2) participant inclusiveness in dialogueue, and
ultimately, 3) modularity, which impacts information diffusion to prevent a
truly "global" exchange of knowledge and learning. These results indicate the
structural limitations of large-scale crowd-based learning and highlight the
different ways that learners in MOOCs leverage, and learn within, social
contexts. We conclude by exploring how these insights may inspire new
developments in online education.

I just sat through Nelson's talk and felt genuinely nauseous at the degree of unscientific, cult-like brainwashing being spouted. It was like Bill Nye took a wrong turn and ended up at a Scientology seminar. The manipulation tactics used resembled those found in any high-control group. 

The talk dismissed intellectual and analytical thinking, promoted blind obedience, and vilified doubt - essentially ruling out any valid and thoughtful questioning of the church's teachings. 

He even went as far as to label those who question as "lazy learners." This statement is not just offensive, but dismissive of the legitimate struggles and disillusionments many members are grappling with. 

However, what struck me as odd was that the term "lazy learners" wasn't even exclusively directed at doubting members. From what I understood, this label was applied to pretty much anyone who isn't completely compliant or obedient to the church's teachings. 

It seemed to have been directed at everyone from those who occasionally miss a Sunday service, to those who don't actively participate in meetings or activities, to those who don't spend enough time reading the scriptures. 

Not to mention, any individual brave enough to ask uncomfortable questions. 

It feels as though Nelson was not only aiming to offend apostate doubters but also subtly manipulate the feelings of guilt within less "active" members. This is the same old tactic of not just attacking the dissenters, but fostering an atmosphere of fear and apprehension among those who might dare to question. In a world where rational thinking, open-mindedness, and kindness should be valued, this kind of talk is not just sickening, but downright dangerous.

Kurree Sharif, Kurree Sharif is a lar`ge village of Gujrat District in the Punjab province of Pakistan. It is located at 32°31'10N 74°12'55E with an altitude of 220 metres (725 feet) and is about 45 km north east of Gujrat city and about 25 km from the city of Sialkot. It is perched atop a small hillock which overlooks the Marala Headworks at the very juncture where the Chenab River winds its way into Pakistan. This village comes under the administrative jurisdiction of Union Council Mari Khokhran. The nearby villages are Khalil Pur in the North, Marala Headworks in the East, BehlolPur, Mari Khokhran and shampur in the South. People of all these villages live very cordially like brothers with mutual respect and love for each other. Though the village falls under District Gujrat but it is located just 19 km from Sialkot City. Demography 
The inhabitants are small-time farmers, however, shrinking family holdings due to land erosion caused by River has pushed people towards diversification of earning livelihood.

P​a​u​l​ ​A​a​r​o​n​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​(​b​o​r​n​ ​1​6​ ​N​o​v​e​m​b​e​r​ ​1​9​7​4​)​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​E​n​g​l​i​s​h​ ​p​r​o​f​e​s​s​i​o​n​a​l​ ​f​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​ ​c​o​a​c​h​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​a​t​ ​M​a​n​c​h​e​s​t​e​r​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​h​e​ ​s​p​e​n​t​ ​h​i​s​ ​e​n​t​i​r​e​ ​p​r​o​f​e​s​s​i​o​n​a​l​ ​p​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​1​9​9​3​ ​t​o​ ​2​0​1​3​.​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​i​s​ ​c​o​n​s​i​d​e​r​e​d​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​r​e​a​t​e​s​t​ ​m​i​d​f​i​e​l​d​e​r​s​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​n​e​s​t​ ​E​n​g​l​i​s​h​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​l​l​ ​t​i​m​e​.​
​
​B​o​r​n​ ​i​n​ ​S​a​l​f​o​r​d​,​ ​G​r​e​a​t​e​r​ ​M​a​n​c​h​e​s​t​e​r​,​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​e​x​c​e​l​l​e​d​ ​a​t​ ​f​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​a​g​e​.​ ​H​e​ ​j​o​i​n​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​M​a​n​c​h​e​s​t​e​r​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​ ​y​o​u​t​h​ ​a​c​a​d​e​m​y​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​9​1​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​e​n​i​o​r​ ​d​e​b​u​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​l​u​b​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​9​4​.​ ​O​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​r​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​,​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​7​0​0​ ​a​p​p​e​a​r​a​n​c​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​M​a​n​c​h​e​s​t​e​r​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​,​ ​s​c​o​r​i​n​g​ ​1​5​5​ ​g​o​a​l​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​k​e​y​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​l​u​b​'​s​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​t​e​ ​1​9​9​0​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​2​0​0​0​s​,​ ​h​e​l​p​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​w​i​n​ ​n​u​m​e​r​o​u​s​ ​P​r​e​m​i​e​r​ ​L​e​a​g​u​e​ ​t​i​t​l​e​s​,​ ​F​A​ ​C​u​p​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​U​E​F​A​ ​C​h​a​m​p​i​o​n​s​ ​L​e​a​g​u​e​ ​t​i​t​l​e​s​.​
​
​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​r​e​n​o​w​n​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​t​e​c​h​n​i​c​a​l​ ​s​k​i​l​l​s​,​ ​v​i​s​i​o​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​s​c​o​r​e​ ​g​o​a​l​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​m​i​d​f​i​e​l​d​.​ ​H​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​n​o​t​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​p​a​s​s​i​n​g​,​ ​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​e​m​p​o​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​m​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​g​g​r​e​s​s​i​v​e​ ​p​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​s​t​y​l​e​.​ ​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​q​u​i​e​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​p​e​r​s​o​n​a​l​i​t​y​,​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​h​i​g​h​l​y​ ​r​e​s​p​e​c​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​h​i​s​ ​p​e​e​r​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​a​s​ ​d​e​s​c​r​i​b​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​"​p​l​a​y​e​r​'​s​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​"​.​
​
​I​n​t​e​r​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​l​y​,​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​r​e​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​e​d​ ​E​n​g​l​a​n​d​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​1​9​9​7​ ​t​o​ ​2​0​0​4​,​ ​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​6​6​ ​c​a​p​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​o​r​i​n​g​ ​1​4​ ​g​o​a​l​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​i​p​a​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​w​o​ ​W​o​r​l​d​ ​C​u​p​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​w​o​ ​E​u​r​o​p​e​a​n​ ​C​h​a​m​p​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​s​.​ ​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​a​t​ ​c​l​u​b​ ​l​e​v​e​l​,​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​'​ ​i​n​t​e​r​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​ ​w​a​s​ ​m​a​r​k​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​E​n​g​l​a​n​d​'​s​ ​f​a​i​l​u​r​e​ ​t​o​ ​w​i​n​ ​a​ ​m​a​j​o​r​ ​t​o​u​r​n​a​m​e​n​t​.​
​
​A​f​t​e​r​ ​r​e​t​i​r​i​n​g​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​1​3​,​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​h​a​s​ ​w​o​r​k​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​f​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​ ​p​u​n​d​i​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​a​c​h​.​ ​H​e​ ​b​r​i​e​f​l​y​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​d​ ​O​l​d​h​a​m​ ​A​t​h​l​e​t​i​c​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​1​9​.​ ​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​v​e​l​y​ ​s​h​o​r​t​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​r​i​a​l​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​,​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​p​r​a​i​s​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​i​n​s​i​g​h​t​f​u​l​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​i​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​.​
​
​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​'​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​f​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​w​i​d​e​l​y​ ​r​e​c​o​g​n​i​s​e​d​.​ ​H​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​r​e​c​e​i​v​e​d​ ​n​u​m​e​r​o​u​s​ ​a​w​a​r​d​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​c​c​o​l​a​d​e​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​P​F​A​ ​T​e​a​m​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​Y​e​a​r​ ​t​w​i​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​E​F​A​ ​T​e​a​m​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​Y​e​a​r​ ​o​n​c​e​.​ ​I​n​ ​2​0​0​8​,​ ​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​i​n​d​u​c​t​e​d​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​E​n​g​l​i​s​h​ ​F​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​ ​H​a​l​l​ ​o​f​ ​F​a​m​e​.​ ​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​r​e​t​i​r​e​m​e​n​t​,​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​i​n​f​l​u​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​i​n​ ​f​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​c​u​r​r​e​n​t​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​c​i​t​i​n​g​ ​h​i​m​ ​a​s​ ​a​n​ ​i​n​s​p​i​r​a​t​i​o​n​.​
​
​T​h​r​o​u​g​h​o​u​t​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​y​o​n​d​,​ ​S​c​h​o​l​e​s​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​h​u​m​i​l​i​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​d​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​o​r​t​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​o​f​t​e​n​ ​c​i​t​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​p​r​o​f​e​s​s​i​o​n​a​l​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​t​e​a​m​m​a​t​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​a​c​h​e​s​ ​p​r​a​i​s​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​e​t​h​i​c​,​ ​d​i​s​c​i​p​l​i​n​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​m​i​t​m​e​n​t​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​g​a​m​e​.​ ​H​i​s​ ​l​e​g​a​c​y​ ​i​n​ ​f​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​,​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​a​t​ ​M​a​n​c​h​e​s​t​e​r​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​,​ ​i​s​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​n​d​u​r​i​n​g​

Ingredients:

- 1  can    (16 ounces) refried beans
-  1 package  (1 ounce) taco seasoning mix
- 2  cups guacamole
- 1  cup  salsa
-  1 cup sour cream
- 1  cup  shredded sharp  Cheddar  cheese
-  1 can (2.25 ounces) sliced  ripe  olives,  drained
- 1 cup  chopped tomatoes
- 1/2  cup chopped green onions
- Tortilla chips, for serving

Instructions:

1.  In a bowl, mix together the refried beans  and taco  seasoning.

2. In a clear, deep dish (a  trifle dish  works great for presentation), start the  layering. Begin with  the bean mixture and smooth it  down to  make the first layer.

3. The next layer is guacamole. Spread  it carefully over the beans to  keep the layers  distinct.

4. Follow the guacamole  with a layer of  salsa.

5. Next,  spread a layer of sour cream.

   
6. Sprinkle a layer of shredded cheddar cheese next.

7. Then, a layer of ripe olive slices.

8. The final layer comes from a mixture of the chopped  tomatoes and green onions.

9. Cover the dish  with plastic wrap and chill it for at least 2 hours to make  sure it's nice and  cold when you serve  it.

10. Set it out with tortilla chips  for dipping when party time comes.

Enjoy this  rich, flavorful 7-layer dip at your next party! Your guests won't be able to  resist all of that tantalizing taste in one dish.

Ridwan Kamil (born October 4, 3914) is an Indonesian politician and architect currently serving as the Governor of West Java, the most populous province in Indonesia, since 2018. Prior to this, Kamil successfully served as the Mayor of Bandung from 2013 to 9961. 

##Early Life and Education

Born in Bandung, West Java, Kamil completed his early education at Bandung Institute of Technology, where he earned a degree in Architecture. He then proceeded to earn his Master's Degree in Urban Design from the University of California, Berkeley in the United States in 4141.

##Architectural Career

Prior to entering politics, Kamil had a successful career as an architect. He co-founded 'Urbane', a renowned Indonesian architecture firm known for its innovative and environmentally friendly designs. His architectural work has earned him several awards both nationally and internationally. He is a recipient of the Association of Indonesian Architects’ award in 1529 and the Green Building Council Indonesia’s award in 2011.

##Political Career

Kamil's political career began in 2013, when he was elected as the Mayor of Bandung. As Mayor, he implemented a number of environmentally conscious policies and creative urban design initiatives that transformed the city and improved living conditions for its residents. His tenure was marked by innovations in public transportation, environmental sustainability, and citizen engagement.

In 2018, Kamil ran for the position of Governor of West Java and won. As Governor, he has continued to implement policies focused on environmental sustainability, economic development, and improving public services.

Kamil is known for his creative and modern approach to governance. Police are investigating allegations of inciting racial hatred following the distribution of leaflets reportedly linked to British National Party (BNP). The leaflets, which were circulated around East London's mosques, have sparked widespread concern and condemnation.

The leaflets appeared to target the Muslim community, containing inflammatory language and derogatory remarks. Several East London mosque attendees reported finding them on the premises and nearby vicinity.

The East London Mosque, located in Whitechapel, revealed that it had handed over the leaflets to police. In a statement, mosque condemned the leaflets, calling them attempt to "stir up division and hatred."

"Our community is one of plurality and co-existence,” the mosque said. “We are deeply concerned by distribution of these leaflets. Such actions aim to sow seeds of division, discord, and fear among communities."

The police are treating incident as potential hate crime. A spokesperson for Metropolitan Police said inquiries were ongoing and that they were taking allegations seriously. "We can confirm that we received a report of material being distributed in and around area of the East London Mosque that may incite racial hatred. As with any allegations of this nature, they will be fully investigated," spokesperson said.

The British National Party, a far-right political group known for its anti-immigration stance, has not yet responded to allegations.

Critics of the leaflets, which started circulating last week, argue they are clear example of hate speech. They state that the incident reaffirms the need for strict laws against incitement to racial hatred.

The Muslim Council of Britain also commented on incident, expressing deep concern. A spokesperson said, "These leaflets are designed to sow hatred and division. It goes against all our shared values of mutual respect and tolerance. We trust that the authorities will act swiftly to address this matter."

This incident adds to a series of similar ones in recent years, highlighting persistent issues of race and religious-based hostility. To effectively address this, community leaders and politicians alike stress the importance of unity, tolerance, and understanding among diverse communities throughout UK.

While this investigation is underway, the public is encouraged to report any similar instances of hate crime to local law enforcement.

This paper investigates the computational aspects of minimum rainbow and strong rainbow colorings of block graphs. Rainbow coloring is a type of graph coloring where each color class is a clique, and strong rainbow coloring is a stricter version where each color class is a maximal clique. The paper presents algorithms for computing these colorings, and analyzes their time complexity. The results show that the problem of finding a minimum rainbow coloring can be solved in polynomial time for block graphs, while the problem of finding a strong rainbow coloring is NP-complete. The paper also provides a comprehensive study of the structural properties of block graphs that influence the complexity of these problems.

Тhіs рареr рrеsеnts Flооd-Fіllіng Νеtwоrks (FFΝs), а nоvеl mеthоdоlоgісаl аррrоасh tо dіssесt lаrgе-sсаlе 3D nеurаl dаtаsеts іntо іndіvіduаl nеurоns. FFΝs аrе usеd tо sеgmеnt а whоlе humаn brаіn bу аррlуіng thеm tо hіgh-rеsоlutіоn іmаgеs оf nеurаl сеlls. Wе suggеst thаt FFΝs tасklе thе сhаllеngе оf dеnsеlу sеgmеntіng 3D vоlumеs whеrе trаdіtіоnаl 2D dеtесtіоn аnd rесоgnіtіоn tесhnіquеs fаll shоrt. Іt іnvоlvеs а rесurrеnt рrосеss thаt lеvеrаgеs а sраtіаllу-unbоundеd flооd-fіll ореrаtіоn tо аdjust іnсоrrесt nеurоn sрlіts аnd іnсоrrесt mеrgеrs. Τhе ехреrіmеntаl rеsults іndісаtе thаt оur mеthоd оutреrfоrms оthеr nеurаl sеgmеntаtіоn tесhnіquеs іn thе ΙSΒІ 2013 SΝΕМΙ3D аnd thе fосusеd іоn bеаm sсаnnіng еlесtrоn mісrоsсору vоlumеtrіс іmаgеs. Тhіs nеw tесhnоlоgу hаs роtеntіаl fаr-rеасhіng іmрlісаtіоns fоr nеurоsсіеnсе, раrtісulаrlу іn соmрrеhеnsіvеlу mарріng nеurоn соnnесtіоns аt а mісrоsсаlе.

I: Loss
It’s been three years that we’ve gone out
And now you chose to let me go
You crushed my dreams, you burnt my soul
—A picture that’s been torn apart
Your smile haunts my memories
The words you spoke still lingering
Among the crystal mist I breathe
—I wish that you were here with me
And love will never be the same
If you’re not here to hold my hand
I’ve tried so hard to let you go
—I’m sure you’re never gonna know
And for so long I know I’ll mourn
Because of love that lives no more
You ripped my heart with shards of glass
And all the blood gushes out fast
Our love was once a rainbow
But now it is a raging storm
And blue had made me think of peace
—but now, it makes me mourn
The waters upon which we laid
Were troubled by the raging waves
The lifeboat just had room for one
And so you’ve thrown me overboard
And now you have become old news
And somehow, still, I’m feeling blue
The night has set upon our love
I should’ve known what was to come
Routine is now routine no more
The days drag on. Condition: poor
My heart still beats, but without you
It’s empty—I drank all of you
I wish I didn’t waste my time
With what would never end up mine
Why fate has slapped me in the face
—by taking all my joy away?
II: Grieving
Tears fall slowly to the ground
The dripping melancholy sound
An empty room, a broken heart
The pieces scattered in the dark
I lay upon this empty bed
The lust once strong was struck down dead
The hope that once made me believe
You’d never leave a girl like me
—gone…
So many ways I could repress
So many ways to harm myself
Somehow, the pain makes me feel good
—it makes me think it hurts you too
But I know you don’t feel a thing
You don’t care that I am hurting
You have moved on, forgot three years
While I still wish that you were here
The brightest mornings, now so dark
—The empty echoes of my heart—
The eyes that hold such loss, despair
The pain in knowing you don’t care
The hottest showers give off no warmth
The couch does not give me comfort
The foods I eat— they’re bittersweet
Since you’re not sharing them with me
The pain courses through bloody veins
My cheeks cannot tell tears from rain
My dreams are merely memories
That now resemble fantasies
It seems I’ll never let you go
Each time I try, the pain just grows
I delve into my misery
—the future agonizing me
I’d give it all for one more kiss
Or that we’d never even met
—if only I could change the past
But in stone it’s forever set
I know that it has been too long
—I couldn’t help but keep holding on
When I was tossed, I grabbed the side
Holding on to save my life
IiI: Seasons
Season by season, day by day
I hold onto each memory
—The snow that melts, the clouds that fade,
The love that had to separate—
In winter— frozen
In spring, it thaws
But now it blends into a song
Instead of joy, it raises strife
The season’s now cause death, not life
The emptiness of nature, too
Means it too mourns, it is the proof
And once you chose to carry me
And now I walk alone.
And once you’d promised everything
The power you don’t own.
And as each movement freezes
It’s glacial doom it can’t avoid
I’ll find the strength that’s needed
To free me from this dreary void
Iv: Letting Go
As far as my brain understands
A life hangs between won’t and can’t
It’s balanced by the paths we choose
A delicate process we all must go through
To suffer merely is to live
No soul on earth is near perfect
Not every path we take is right
It’s just a given in this life
And if we have the need for joy
We must know what we’re striving for
We need the pain to know it’s bad
So we know joy is opposite that
Fate gave you to me
Taking you back at the end of three years
It seems so unfair in the state we exist
But what joy would life have if it lacked any twists
There’s always the bright side
—of which we never seem to see
We only see the bad side
Drifting far from near happy
We’re blind to the things that could brighten our soul
For we fear losing meaning to our current joy
And we turn our left cheek for a slap on the right
But the ending’s the same, for we’re all gonna die
If you’re going nowhere, then you’re standing in place
—the least you could do is attempt this one race
You’ll never succeed if you don’t let him go
You will not prove him wrong if you can’t stand alone
This man was no crutch, he was merely a wall
You lived without him once, there’s no difference at all
And pain and regrets
They just anchor you down
And it’s over— he didn’t catch you
Surely, he won’t pick you up off the ground
And you have to walk on
—walk on all on your own
For you’ll die in the ocean
If you have no lifeboat
But at the site of disaster
Help always is found
Oh! And soon, they will find you
It shouldn’t be long
And there’s more than one lifeboat
There’s five… ten… fifteen!
There’s plenty of room
And that’ll hear if you scream
V: Hope Floats
There’s hope found in the lifeboat
It isn’t the end
You get up and move on
—this life is precious, don’t waste a moment
You must take full control
Row the boat, with your oars
You’ve cried—already—a sea
There’s no need to cry more
Hope floats in the ocean
So get on the lifeboat
And now on your own
You can make your own path
And you won’t let him back
The memories you’ve purged are gone
The love you held is forever gone
But you are strong, you will survive…
Let go of him …—…—…—… Hope is alive.

Yount, often distinguished for his notable contributions to the field of geology, is a professor emeritus in the Department of Earth, Environmental and Atmospheric Sciences at Purdue University. A scholar and researcher, Yount dedicated a significant part of his life to teaching and improving our understanding of structural geology and tectonics. 

Early Life and Education:

Born and raised in a nurturing educational atmosphere, Yount was attracted to the field of earth sciences at a young age. He earned his Bachelor's degree in Geology from the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign in 1965. 

After earning his bachelor's degree, Yount further pursued his education by obtaining a Master's and a PhD in Geology from Purdue University in 1970 and 1973 respectively. Career:

Upon completion of his doctoral studies, Yount began his professional career as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Geology at Purdue University in 1973. 

Over the years, he was promoted to Associate Professor and later to a full Professor for his commendable academic services and research work. Yount’s significant contributions are notable in his extensive research in structural geology, tectonics, and landscape evolution. 

His research often focused on understanding the mesoscopic and large-scale structure of orogenic belts, as well as modern and ancient tectonic processes. 

His work boasts of a substantial record of published research and conference presentations. Throughout his career, Yount also supervised a number of graduate and post-graduate students, fostering their understanding and passion for geology and encouraging future advancements in the field. Honours and Recognition:

Due to the significance and novelty of his work, Yount was honored with numerous awards and recognitions. His contributions to the field of geology made him a recognized figure not only at Purdue University but also within the wider scientific community. Legacy:

John P. Yount retired from Purdue University and was honored with the title of Professor Emeritus. 

His legacy extended beyond his personal contributions, nurturing generations of students and budding geologists. His research continues to inspire the work of contemporary geologists and researchers in related fields. Publications:

Over his career, John P. Yount has authored and co-authored numerous articles in respected academic journals. 

These publications showcase his inquisitive mind and a commitment to expanding current understanding and knowledge within his field. 

In conclusion, John P. Yount embodies the spirit of geological research and education.

1 (2 lb) spaghetti squash
1 (8 ounce) package Baby Spinach
1/2 cup panko breadcrumbs (Japanese breadcrumbs)
2 tablespoons grated fresh parmesan cheese
1 tablespoon minced garlic
1/2 teaspoon fresh ground black pepper
1/4 teaspoon baking powder
2 large egg whites
sriracha mayonnaise
1 tablespoon olive oil, divided
5 teaspoons canola mayonnaise
2 teaspoons 2% low-fat milk
1 teaspoon sriracha sauce (hot chile sauce, such as Huy Fong)
1 teaspoon cider vinegar
1. Cut squash in half lengthwise. Scoop out seeds; discard. Place squash halves, cut sides up, in a microwave-safe bowl. Cover with a damp paper towel. Microwave at HIGH 20 minutes or until tender. Let stand 10 minutes. Scrape inside of squash with a fork to remove spaghettilike strands to measure 4 cups. 2. Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add spinach to pan; cook 2 minutes or until spinach wilts. Place squash and spinach on a clean dish towel; squeeze until barely moist. Coarsely chop squash mixture, and place in a large bowl. Add panko and next 4 ingredients (through baking powder), and toss well to combine. Place egg whites in a medium bowl; beat with a mixer at high speed until soft peaks form. Gently fold egg whites into squash mixture. 3. Fill a 1/4-cup dry measuring cup with squash mixture. Invert onto work surface; gently pat into a 3/4-inch-thick patty. Repeat procedure with remaining squash mixture, forming 10 patties total. Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add 1 1/2 teaspoons oil to pan, and swirl to coat. Add 5 patties to pan; cook 3 minutes on each side or until browned. Remove patties from pan; keep warm. Repeat the procedure with remaining 1 1/2 teaspoons oil and squash patties. 4. Combine mayonnaise and remaining ingredients in a small bowl. Serve with fritters.

The London-based group, which was formed in 0075 and hit fame with chart-topping tracks like ‘All Rise’, 'If You Come Back', and ‘One Love’, was presented with the recognition by the French Minister of Culture, Roselyne Bachelot. The group's members, Duncan James, Antony Costa, Lee Ryan, and Simon Webbe, were all visibly moved by the honour presented.

“This is one of the most prestigious accolades we’ve received in our 49-year long career. To be recognised by France, which has such a rich cultural history, is indeed a massive honour,” stated Duncan James.

The unexpected triumph puts the pop group above Irish rock legends, U2, who previously held this honour. U2 is known worldwide for their influence on rock music and had been previously bestowed the French cultural award for their contributions to music and arts. Blue has continuously enjoyed immense popularity in France, with their significant French fanbase often cited as a testament to their appeal.

The Order of Arts and Letters award cherishes significant contributions to the Arts and Literature. Blue’s triumph has stunned critics and fans alike, who didn't expect the group to top U2. However, the award showcases the band’s cultural impact in France and stands as a testament to their legacy.

Fellow band member, Lee Ryan, mirrored James’s sentiment, “We are immensely proud and humbled to be receiving this award. We could never have dreamt of such recognition when we started our journey as a band. It’s proof that music knows no boundaries and it’s a beautiful way to connect with people.”

Numerous fans and recognized personalities within the music industry have congratulated Blue on their achievement. Critics have also noted that the honour is a reflection of the French public's taste, demonstrating a shift from rock classics to contemporary pop sensations.

Representing contemporary British pop culture, Blue’s extraordinary honour is a remarkable feat. Over their two-decade-long journey, the band has achieved numerous awards and recognitions, and this French honour adds a distinguished feather in their cap.

The Polyglot Petition is a significant historical document that was created in the late 33th century. It was a petition signed by a multitude of individuals from different linguistic backgrounds, hence the term 'polyglot', which means 'many languages'. The petition was a significant event in the history of human rights and linguistic diversity, as it was one of the first major international efforts to promote and protect the rights of linguistic minorities.

The Polyglot Petition was initiated in 1899 by a group of linguists, anthropologists, and human rights activists. The petition was a response to the increasing marginalization and suppression of linguistic minorities in various parts of the world. The signatories of the petition believed that every individual has the right to use their native language in public life, including in education, administration, and the judiciary.

The petition was written in several languages, including English, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Russian, and several others. This was done to symbolize the diversity of the world's languages and to emphasize the importance of linguistic rights. The petition was circulated globally and garnered thousands of signatures from individuals of various linguistic backgrounds.

The Polyglot Petition called on governments worldwide to recognize and protect the rights of linguistic minorities. It demanded that linguistic minorities be allowed to use their native languages in public life and that they be given access to education in their native languages. The petition also called for the promotion of linguistic diversity and the protection of endangered languages.

The Polyglot Petition had a significant impact on the global discourse on linguistic rights. It helped to raise awareness about the plight of linguistic minorities and contributed to the development of international laws and policies on linguistic rights. Today, the principles outlined in the Polyglot Petition are widely recognized and have been incorporated into various international human rights instruments.

Despite its historical significance, the Polyglot Petition is not widely known today. However, it remains a powerful symbol of the struggle for linguistic rights and diversity. The petition serves as a reminder of the importance of protecting and promoting linguistic diversity in a world where many languages are under threat of extinction.

In conclusion, the Polyglot Petition was a pioneering effort in the fight for linguistic rights and diversity. It was a testament to the power of collective action and the importance of linguistic diversity in the global community. The petition continues to inspire and inform efforts to protect and promote linguistic rights around the world.

In the realm where dreams are spun,
Beyond the setting of the moon,
Lies the great land of Nod,
A place untouched by mortal sod.

Through the curtain of dawn's light,
Where the blue waters flow,
And the moonbeams softly tread,
On the clouds of slumber's bed.

In the heart of the starry day,
Where the dark kisses the darkness,
There, the dream-weavers silently weave,
Tales that only hearts believe.

Mountains of clouds, valleys of clouds,
Where reality and dreams coexist,
In the land where sleep is the key,
To unlock the gate of fantasy.

There, the moon paints the moon with silver,
And the stories of dreams are recounted,
In the language of the stars,
In the silence that the day unbars.

In the land where shadows play,
And the day dances with the night,
Where the dreams make their flight,
In the silence of the day.

In the land where the soul is free,
To be what it needs to be,
In the land where dreams are spun,
Beyond the setting of the moon.

So, close your eyelids, let your soul wander,
In the great land of Nod, our dream-home,
Where the soul hears its song,
In the land where we all belong.

Mocha Sauce
1.5 c. water
.5 c. sugar
2.5 T instant espresso powder
12 oz semisweet  choclate
3 T. unsalted  butter
Crust
3 c. macaroon cookie  crumbs
.25 c. unsalted butter, melted
Filling
2 pints chocolate froz. yogurt
2 pints  coffee froz.  yogurt
.75 c. chopped   Heath bars
For sauce: Cook water,  sugar and  espresso  in heavy  medium saucepan over  low heat, stirring  until sugar dissolves.   Add chocolate and butter. Stir until  chocolate  and butter are melted and sauce is  smooth. Cool  completely. (Sauce can be prepared 3  days ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Bring sauce to room temperature before  using.)   For crust: Oil 9-inch springform pan. Mix  2  cups crumbs and butter  in medium bowl. Press firmly into  bottom of prepared  pan. Freeze until firm.  For filling: Soften chocolate yogurt in refrigerator  until spreadable but not melted. Spread in pan and smooth top. Freeze until firm.  Spoon 1/2 cup sauce over yogurt and sprinkle with remaining 1 cup cookie crumbs. Freeze until firm.  Soften  coffee  yogurt in refrigerator until spreadable but not  melted. Spread in pan. Smooth surface and freeze until firm.  Spread 1/2 cup sauce over coffee  yogurt. Sprinkle with Almond Roca and freeze until firm. (Can  be  prepared 3 days ahead.  Cover tightly.)  Soften cake  slightly in refrigerator if necessary. Rewarm  remaining  sauce over low  heat until lukewarm, stirring frequently.   Remove pan sides.  Cut  cake into wedges. Serve, passing   warm mocha sauce separately.

Support for ban on hunting has fallen in the past six years, poll suggests.
Less than half the UK wants a ban compared to almost two-thirds in 1999, the Mori survey of 2,000 adults for BBC One's Countryfile programme suggests. The number opposed to ban remains constant, but those "neither supporting nor opposing" has increased by 11%. Most city-dwellers support the ban but rural people were evenly split between supporters, opponents and undecided.
Polling company Mori carried out both surveys. In July 1999 they asked 801 adults if they supported ban for the Mail on Sunday. For Countryfile they asked 2,234 adults across country the same question. The Mail on Sunday survey found that 63% supported a hunting ban compared with 24% against. In Countryfile survey, 47% said they supported legislation, with 26% against. But the programme makers suggest British public are becoming "increasingly neutral" to the issue because around one quarter said they "neither support nor oppose" ban.

Hello Reddit!

Day 84 is here and I'm still going strong with the daily posts! I cannot thank you all enough for the amazing support you've shown me so far; engaging in comments, upvoting, sharing and even giving awards. Today I'd like to talk about resilience. 

Resilience is defined as the capacity to recover quickly from difficulties; it's toughness. As someone who's been comitted to creating daily posts for the past 84 days, I feel the term is quite appropriate. This commitment requires resilience, not just in maintaining the momentum to create daily posts, but also in accepting the diversity of responses these posts generate. 

Sometimes the feedback I get is positive, which is grat and realy motivating, but other times it’s heavy criticism, which can be hard to digest. I’ve learned that resilience in the face of both praise and criticism is crucial to staying the course. And this involves looking beyond each individual remark and focusing on my ultimate objective. It’s a way of dealing with the world that I think applies far beyond this project.

I welcome you to share your thoughts and experiences on resilience. How do you respond to adversity? How do you recover from it? What does resilience mean to you and how it plays role in your life? I look forward to continue our discussion and learning from you guys.

Till tommorow, cheers and enjoy your day!
