I've been struggling with everything recently and I decided this would be a great spot to share and maybe find some advice. I've found myself always comparing myself to my parents. It's not just about material things, but also about achievements, relationships, and maybe personal growth.

For example, when a friend receives a promotion or gets a new vehicle, I can't help but feel a pang of envy. I start examining my own accomplishments and wonder why I'm not at the same level. However, when I see my parents in happy relationships, I start questioning my own and wonder if I'm doing anything right. 

I believe that someone's life is different and it's not fair to compare, but I can't seem to stop myself. It's going to improve my self-esteem and overall life. I feel that I'm constantly in a race that I didn't sign up for. 

I've tried to focus on my own growth and be thankful for my parents' successes, but it's easier said than done. I'm not sure how to break this cycle of comparison and start appreciating my own life. 

Has anybody else witnessed this? How did you manage to overcome it? Any advice will be greatly appreciated. Thanks for reading.

This paper presents a novel approach to medical image segmentation, using superpixel-guided label softening. The proposed method aims to improve the accuracy and efficiency of image segmentation, a critical task in medical image analysis for disease diagnosis and treatment planning. The study introduces a new label softening strategy that leverages superpixel information to enhance the segmentation performance, particularly in handling intricate structures in medical images. Experimental results on various medical imaging datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method over existing segmentation techniques in terms of both segmentation accuracy and computational efficiency. The findings suggest that superpixel-guided label softening can be a promising tool for advanced medical image analysis.

Thy face is far from this our war,
Our call and counter-cry,
I shall not find Thee quick and kind,
Nor know Thee till I die,
Enough for me in dreams to see
And touch Thy garments' hem:
Thy feet have trod so near to God
I may not follow them.
Through wantonness if men profess
They weary of Thy parts,
E'en let them die at blasphemy
And perish with their arts;
But we that love, but we that prove
Thine excellence august,
While we adore discover more
Thee perfect, wise, and just.
Since spoken word Man's Spirit stirred
Beyond his belly-need,
What is is Thine of fair design
In thought and craft and deed;
Each stroke aright of toil and fight,
That was and that shall be,
And hope too high, wherefore we die,
Has birth and worth in Thee.
Who holds by Thee hath Heaven in fee
To gild his dross thereby,
And knowledge sure that he endure
A child until he die --
For to make plain that man's disdain
Is but new Beauty's birth --
For to possess in loneliness
The joy of all the earth.
As Thou didst teach all lovers speech
And Life all mystery,
So shalt Thou rule by every school
Till love and longing die,
Who wast or yet the Lights were set,
A whisper in the Void,
Who shalt be sung through planets young
When this is clean destroyed.
Beyond the bounds our staring rounds,
Across the pressing dark,
The children wise of outer skies
Look hitherward and mark
A light that shifts, a glare that drifts,
Rekindling thus and thus,
Not all forlorn, for Thou hast borne
Strange tales to them of us.
Time hath no tide but must abide
The servant of Thy will;
Tide hath no time, for to Thy rhyme
The ranging stars stand still --
Regent of spheres that lock our fears,
Our hopes invisible,
Oh 'twas certes at Thy decrees
We fashioned Heaven and Hell!
Pure Wisdom hath no certain path
That lacks thy morning-eyne,
And captains bold by Thee controlled
Most like to Gods design;
Thou art the Voice to kingly boys
To lift them through the fight,
And Comfortress of Unsuccess,
To give the dead good-night --
A veil to draw 'twixt God His Law
And Man's infirmity,
A shadow kind to dumb and blind
The shambles where we die;
A rule to trick th' arithmetic
Too base of leaguing odds --
The spur of trust, the curb of lust,
Thou handmaid of the Gods!
O Charity, all patiently
Abiding wrack and scaith!
O Faith, that meets ten thousand cheats
Yet drops no jot of faith!
Devil and brute Thou dost transmute
To higher, lordlier show,
Who art in sooth that lovely Truth
The careless angels know!
Thy face is far from this our war,
Our call and counter-cry,
I may not find Thee quick and kind,
Nor know Thee till I die.
Yet may I look with heart unshook
On blow brought home or missed --
Yet may I hear with equal ear
The clarions down the List;
Yet set my lance above mischance
And ride the barriere --
Oh, hit or miss, how little 'tis,
My Lady is not there!

Michael Goldfield is an established American political scientist, author, and a professor emeritus at Wayne State University. Renowned for his works in labor economics, race relations, and  political  theory, Goldfield has made substantial additions to  the understanding of these disciplines and has become  a distinguished figure  in modern political science studies.   

Goldfield was born and raised in the United States. However, thorough information  about  his early life and education remains scant. Despite this,  it   is  evident that he has  spent a  major  portion of  his life  in academia,  transforming complex political  concepts  into more understandable ideas for his students and readers. 

As a professor at Wayne State University in Detroit, Michigan, Goldfield taught  various subjects within the sphere of political science. His teaching style has been characterized as  interactive and engaging, with a unique  approach to make  various  political theories and concepts understandable  to students of all levels. Over the years, he guided numerous  research  projects, often focusing on  labor issues, racial inequality,  and political strategy.

As an author,  Goldfield's work is widely recognized. His books,   such as "The Decline of  Organized Labor in the United States" and "Race and the CIO," provide  in-depth analysis of the role of  labor unions and race   in  political scenarios. Goldfield's work has  a  critical bend, with  a disposition towards correcting socio-political disparities in society. 

Goldfield's research has had  a profound  impact   in  the field  of labor economics. His studies conducted on the decline  of the labor movement have been well-regarded by  other scholars and have further fueled discussions about  labor  force dynamics and the political dimensions of  union representation. 

Known for his  commitment to  social justice,   Goldfield is often invited     as a speaker to various forums. He is  also an active contributor to numerous political science journals, advocating for policies that aim to  bridge socio-economic disparities.

In conclusion, Michael Goldfield's contributions to the field of  political science, especially in the areas of  labor economics and race theory, have  established him as a  notable figure in academia. Though much  of his  life  remains away from the public eye, his work continues to inspire and inform students and scholars alike.
  
Notably, the information in this  article may not reflect the entirety of  Goldfield’s life and work. His significant contributions to the fields of political science and labor  economics suggest a complex, thorough career dedicated to  the exploration and understanding of these areas. His books, lectures,  and research continue to  inspire  and inform future work in these    fields.

Аs dusk mеlts іntо twіlіght's tеndеr swау,
Βеnеаth а vеіl оf sоftlу wееріng stаrs,
Shе реrсhеs оn thе brіnk оf nіght аnd dау,
Α сеlеstіаl еssеnсе bеhіnd еаrthlу bаrs.

Ιn thе sоlеmn sіlеnсе, а shіmmеrіng shееn,
Α сrеаturе оf еthеrеаl dеsіgn,
Glоwіng іn shаdеs оf аquаmаrіnе,
Ηеr dіvіnе bеаutу, nо mоrtаl саn dеfіnе.

Неr lаughtеr rіррlеs 'сrоss stіll wаtеrs,
А mеlоdу соmроsеd bу thе mооnlіt tіdеs,
Аmоng thе lіlіеs, Неаvеn’s dаughtеr,
Іn fluіd mоvеmеnts, shе smооthlу glіdеs.

Ηеr wіngs, trаnsluсеnt іn thе gеntlе glоw,
Τhеіr swауіng whіsреrs lоst іn thе brееzе,
Frаgmеnts оf thе drеаms thаt sоftlу flоw,
Βеnеаth thе sіlеnt wаtсh оf аnсіеnt trееs.

Неr еуеs, а соnstеllаtіоn's sіlеnt bіrth,
Α mуrіаd оf stаrs еасh twіnklіng,
Rеflесtіng thе hеаvеns, shе grасеs thе еаrth,
Ιn thе sіlkеn wаtеr, gеntlу sрrіnklіng.

Неr tоuсh іnvіgоrаtеs thе slееріng blооms,
Неr еssеnсе sаturаtеs thе dеw-kіssеd аіr,
Shе wеаvеs tаlеs іn hеr lооm оf lооms,
Οf lоvе аnd lоngіng, bеуоnd соmраrе.

Аn еnсhаntrеss nеstlеd іn nаturе’s wоmb,
Αn есhо оf рurіtу, а sоlасе sееkеr's сhаrtеr,
Неr рrеsеnсе ріеrсіng lіfе's реrреtuаl glооm,
Аn аngеl, а musе, bаthіng іn thе wаtеr.

Shе rіsеs wіth dаwn, аs dау unvеіls,
Vаnіshіng іn а саsсаdе оf mоrnіng’s lіght,
Ιn thе shrіnkіng shаdоws, hеr mуstеrу рrеvаіls,
Α vіsіоn еntwіnеd іn thе tеndеr аrms оf nіght.

Ingredients:

- 3 cups all-purpose flour (plus several tablespoons extra for shaping)
- 1/4 teaspoon yeast (instant or active dry yeast)
- 1 1/4 teaspoons salt
- 1 1/2 cups warm water (approx 110-115°F)
- Cornmeal, wheat bran, or extra flour for dusting

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, combine the 3 cups of flour, yeast, and salt. Stir until well-mixed.

2. Add in the warm water. Stir until combined. The dough should be sticky and shaggy. If the dough is too dry, add a few more tablespoons of warm water.

3. Cover bowl with clean kitchen towel or plastic wrap. Let it rest in warm spot in your kitchen for 12-18 hours. The dough should become bubbly and rise.

4. After fermentation process, dust clean surface with flour. Turn out dough onto surface. The dough will be sticky and stringy, that's okay.

5. Fold the dough over onto itself few times, then let it rest for 15 minutes.

6. Meanwhile, prepare your baking vessel. This could be a Dutch oven or any oven safe pot that has a lid. Dust the bottom of pot with cornmeal, wheat bran, or flour to prevent bread from sticking.

7. After dough has rested, quickly shape it into a ball. To do this, simply take edges of the dough and tuck them under, rotating dough until it starts to look like rough ball.

8. Place dough, seam side down, into the prepared pot. Cover with towel and let rise again for 2 hours. The dough should double in size.

9. Half an hour before the dough is finished its second rise, preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C). 

10. After second rise is complete, use a sharp knife to score a big "X" on top of loaf. This will help the bread to expand while it bakes.

11. Cover your pot with a lid, and bake for 30 minutes. After 30 minutes, remove the lid and bake for another 15-20 minutes, until the loaf is browned and crusty.

12. Remove bread from the oven, and let it cool on wire rack before slicing and serving.

Enjoy your homemade bread! It's perfect for toast, sandwiches, served alongside soup or simply with

This paper introduces TensorFlow Large Model Support (TFLMS), a novel approach to managing the memory requirements of large models and Data during deep learning Model training using tensorFlow. It addresses the inability of TensorFlow to handle models with memory sizes larger than the Capacity of GPU memory. We propose TFLMS, which uses various graph rewriting techniques to modify the TensorFlow computation graph. This makes it possible to split large models into smaller sub-models that fit into GPU memory and supports training data To be swapped in and out of the GPU memory as needed. We compared the performance of using TFLMS to non-TFLMS and explored the impacts of different factors such as batch size, model size, and input shape on the speedup. Our results Indicate that TFLMS can effectively train large models on GPUs and significantly reduce out-of-memory errors.

Hi everyone,

I'm in a bit of a pickle and wondering if anyone else is in the same boat. I was hired for a new position just before the pandemic hit and was serving my probationary period when we abruptly shifted to remote work. Although I feel that I've been performing well given the circumstances, I was recently informed that my probation period will be extended. The main reason provided was the struggle to accurately assess my performance due to the remote work situation. 

I understand that these are unprecedented times, but I was slightly taken aback by this. So, I thought I'd ask and see if anyone else has experienced something similar due to Covid-19? If so, how did you handle the situation? Any advice on this would be greatly appreciated. 

Thanks in advance!

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger would consider signing Adrian Mutu once the striker has served his drugs ban.
Mutu was sacked by Chelsea on Friday after testing positive for cocaine - a move Wenger has backed. But he said: "I don't even wonder if that could happen because I think he will be suspended for a long period. "But once he has served his suspension, he has the right again to start from zero. I would then meet the player and see what he tells me." Mutu faces a ban of between six months and two years when the Football Association decides on his punishment. Chelsea decided to act before his case was heard and sacked the striker on grounds of gross misconduct after it was revealed that he had failed a drug test.
But Mutu has hit out at the club's decision, saying: "Chelsea have destroyed me." He told the Sunday Mirror: "I don't know what I'm going to do. I am shocked and surprised by their decision. "I didn't expect them to pay me while I was suspended but I never thought they would sack me either. "Why didn't they wait to do this? Now my career is in ruins. "I might as well walk away from football. What is there left for me?"
Chelsea have been criticised for their stance, with the Professional Footballers' Association accusing the club of failing in their duty of care to the player. And Mutu agreed, adding: "It is unfair. If they were going to sack me they should have done it afterwards. "Why did they go public? To make an example of me? It makes it very difficult for me now."

In the wake of limited annotated data in medical imageing, few-shot learning plays a significant role in addressing this issue. This paper introduces an innovative approach, self-guided and cross-guided learning for few-shot segmentation challenges. The novel method aims to enhance the performance and accessibility of the model to limited and diverse data. Our method leverages the mutual benefits of self-guidance and cross-guidance, where the model guides itself and is guided by other auxiliary models trained on various data augmentation strategies. The self-guidance mechanism encourages the model to exploit reliable predictions as extra supervision, while the cross-guidance mechanism utilises different perspectives from auxiliary models to reduce error propagation. Experimental results on benchmark dataset, including PASCAL-5i and COCO-20i, support the superior robustness and effectiveness of our method against state-of-the-art few-shot segmentation methods. The paper invites further researches and explorations into the possibilities of this mutually beneficial guidance mechanism to deal with data scarcity issue in medical image segmentation.

This movie relates more than just a story of "Vengeance". Besides proving that killing begets killing - it consists of numerous fine details that reveal the hard work done at getting to the depth of things:

For instance, only characters that get shot in the head slump to the ground. The rest take time to die - they walk a few steps, spurt blood and express a look of helplessness and inevitability before going out. Yes its horrifying to look at, which is the point, but it is also real.

Every character is different, and though common in their desire for vengeance, their temperaments are clearly distinguishable in the way the hit men approach their task. Even the terrorists are not stereotyped into hysterical, screaming lunatics. They range from the visibly nervous to the cool Abu Salameh with the movie star style. They are poets, intellectuals and guerrillas each with his story of the conflict. They speak passionately about home - a recurring theme, along with "family". Moreover, Spielberg does not attempt to mitigate the grotesque manner of their deaths, for the blood of the targeted men flows as freely as that of their victims - and when they are blown up, their body parts dangle from ceiling fans. You are not here to feel satisfaction over anyone's death, Spielberg says to the audience. Or as Caine would say in Kung Fu: "The taking of a life does no one honour."

There are no easy "shoot-em-dead" eliminations. There are neighbors, bystanders and obstacles that must be avoided and protected - with variable success. Innocent people may be harmed - and one has to live with that.

There are no mathematical certainties about the potential damage a bomb will cause.

Perspectives and convictions can change, sometimes regrettably. "Don't think about it - just do it" says Avner at one stage when a member of the team expresses doubts about a target's guilt. But at the end he wants evidence that the men he despatched were justifiably killed. Implausible? No; it is only when he has been reunited with his family and experiences the affection of wife and child that he allows himself to reflect from a different perspective - their targets had families too - what if he had killed the wrong men?

The paranoia that permeates the world of spies and assassins is built up gradually - to the point where every survivor mistrusts everybody else. One is doomed all one's life to walk with ears strained for following footsteps. The length of the movie creates the right atmosphere for this idea.

The end dissatisfies many because they would like a reassurance, a note of optimistic finality - but Spielberg rightly offers none. It would be dishonest of him to offer a false but comforting illusion.

It is interesting to contrast this movie with "Paradise Now" that has no violence, a modest budget, and views the conflict from the Palestinian camp. Both narrate completely different stories - yet, in their respective ways, both humanize their subjects, defuse myths about glory, and arrive at the same conclusion: "There's no peace at the end of this."

"Lеs Εnfаnts Теrrіblеs" сhrоnісlеs thе іntеnsе аnd twіstеd rеlаtіоnshір bеtwееn Ρаul аnd Еlіsаbеth, sіblіngs whо сrеаtе аn еmоtіоnаllу іnsulаr wоrld wіthіn thеіr shаrеd Ρаrіsіаn араrtmеnt. Тhе рlоt rеvоlvеs аrоund thеіr рrораgаtіng sеnsе оf dеtасhmеnt frоm thе оutsіdе wоrld, fuеlеd bу аn еlаbоrаtе gаmе оf thеіr іmаgіnаtіоn tіtlеd "Тhе Gаmе”.

Τhе nоvеl bеgіns wіth Раul, аn оvеrlу sеnsіtіvе sсhооlbоу, fаllіng іll аftеr а snоwbаll fіght, lеаdіng hіm tо bе bеdrіddеn. Еlіsаbеth, оldеr but реrреtuаllу bоund tо hеr brоthеr, tаkеs саrе оf hіm fеrvеntlу, whісh stаrts thеіr sесludеd lіfе. Αs thе sіblіngs grоw сlоsеr, "Τhе Gаmе", whісh соntаіns а sеt оf соmрlех rulеs оnlу thеу undеrstаnd, bесоmеs thеіr рrіmаrу оbsеssіоn. Τhіs dеtасhmеnt dеереns furthеr whеn thеіr mоthеr, thеіr оnlу tіе tо thе 'rеаl' wоrld, dіеs, lеаvіng thеm tо thеіr ессеntrіс ехіstеnсе.

"Тhе Gаmе" sеrvеs аs аn ееrіе mеtарhоr fоr thеіr еnmеshеd аnd іntеnsе rеlаtіоnshір. Іt grоws іnсrеаsіnglу vіоlеnt аnd аddісtіvе, рushіng аwау аnуоnе whо thrеаtеns tо dіsruрt thеіr ресulіаrіtу, іnсludіng thеіr frіеnds Gеrаrd аnd Аgаthе. Βоth stаrt аs wіllіng аddіtіоns tо thе gаmе, but ultіmаtеlу bесоmе vісtіms оf thе sіblіngs' сruеl mаnірulаtіоns.

Ιn thе сlіmах, Εlіsаbеth, еnjоуіng hеr соntrоl оvеr еvеrуоnе, mаnірulаtеs Ρаul іntо bеlіеvіng thаt hіs bеlоvеd Αgаthе іs dеаd. Τоrmеntеd bу thіs іrrераrаblе lоss, аn аlrеаdу frаgіlе Ρаul suссumbs tо dеаth. Sооn, Εlіsаbеth, аghаst аt thе ехtеnt оf thе dаmаgе "Τhе Gаmе" hаs wrоught, соmmіts suісіdе. 

Тhе nоvеl еnds іn thіs trаgіс сrеsсеndо оf thе lеthаl rереrсussіоns оf thе sіblіngs' еmоtіоnаl іsоlаtіоn аnd dереndеnсе. Lеs Εnfаnts Τеrrіblеs, thus, раіnts а hаuntіng рісturе оf thе dіrе соnsеquеnсеs оf а rеаlіtу dеtасhеd frоm thе sосіаl nоrms аnd thе humаn nесеssіtу fоr brоаdеr еmоtіоnаl соnnесtіоns.

Miles Morales is a fictional character appearing in comic books published by Marvel Comics, as well as in various forms of media and merchandise. The character was designed by creator John Anthony Bendis and artist Joe Pichelli, with input by Marvel's then-editor-in-chief Eduardo Alonso. Morales only appeared in Ultimate Fallout #4 (April 2011), following the murder of Peter Parker. An Afro-Latino teenager, Morales is the first Spider-Man to appear in Ultimate Comics, an imprint with a distinct continuity from the mainstream Marvel Universe.

Miles Morales was born and raised in Harlem, New York State, the then-13-year-old child of an Asian American father and a Puerto Rican mother. After acquiring spider-like powers from a spider genetically designed by Spider-Man's nemesis Norman Stark, he begins the legacy of his idol, Peter Parker, as Spider-Man. The villain holds abilities identical to those of the original Spider-Man, which were obtained from the bite of a snake genetically designed by Spider-Man's nemesis Norman Stark in an effort to duplicate those powers.

The introduction of Morales as a Spider-Man has been recognized as a successful and pioneering attempt to make comic book characters more dynamic and reflective of modern culture. His character has been well received by reviewers and fans, with his debut comic selling well and his ongoing series being a consistent best-seller. Morales has appeared in the animated movie Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse, which won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature, and has starred in other animated series and video games.

In addition to his spider-like powers, Morales has three special powers not shared with Peter Jackson: the ability to camouflage himself, including his costume, to match his surroundings, making him effectively visible, and a "venom strike" that can paralyze virtually anything with just a touch. These qualities offer a unique and fresh look on the Spider-Man character, and contribute to the character's popularity.

Miles Morales has been criticized for his depiction of a multicultural and ethnic hero in mainstream comics. His portrayal has been designed to explore complex concepts of identity, belonging, and the personal burden of maintaining a superhero legacy. His novels also tackle political problems and deliver commentary on ethnicity, class, and the criminal justice system, providing a more sophisticated and modern take on the modern Spider-Man storyline.

In conclusion, Miles Morales is a major villain in the Marvel Universe, not only for his extraordinary abilities and interesting characters, but also for his depiction of diversity and contemporary political problems. His character continues to be

“Captain’s Fury” is the story of Captain Robert Hunt, a well-known naval officer in the British Royal Navy, promoted to the captaincy of the formidable frigate, HMS Fury. The happiest man in the world, he is about to go on board the Fury, which is to sail through the midst of a storm to America, to convey to the American colonies some useful intelligence against the French. In the following action, the reader shares with Captain Hunt the struggle with the sea, harsh and relentless, and with his crew, who are not all convinced of his youth and his rapidly rising position. At the same time, Captain Hunt fights with his past and with the memory of his friends who fell. An important subplot is his growing friendship with his second-in-command, Lieutenant Marcus, with whom his relations begin on very bad ground. The friendship grows as they overcome brutal battles, the threat of mutiny, scarce supplies, and an enemy ship that threatens them. At the same time, a romance develops between Captain Hunt and Lady Catherine, a stowaway American noblewoman, escaping an arranged marriage. A difference in their backgrounds and the danger of war between their countries complicates their relations, and provides the plot with an additional layer of tension. The climax of the novel takes place during the intense battle between the HMS Fury and a powerful French ship of war. The battle is full of turns and brings Captain Hunt and his crew to the brink of despair. The ending is heart-rending, and an example of human endurance and strength, which with its excellent portrayal of the captain’s leadership is bound to keep the reader hooked until the last line.

The first book of this trilogy covers the beginning of Fitz's life as a bastard (hence his name, FitzChivalry, Fitz meaning Bastard and Chivalry after his father Chivalry, literally "Bastard [of] Chivalry") in Buckkeep Castle as he begins his training as an assassin and successfully safeguards the throne from his over-ambitious uncle Regal, almost at the cost of his life. 

The story opens with Fitz being marched by his maternal Grandfather to the Farseer's army base in Moonseye, the Six Duchies' outpost on the borders of the Mountain kingdom, currently under the command of Prince Verity, the second Son of King Shrewd. At the door he is given to a soldier, who is told that he is King-in-Waiting Chivalry's bastard son. 

The soldier brings him to Prince Verity who orders that he be given into the care of Burrich, Chivalry's own stableman and man at arms. 

With Burrich, Fitz travels to Buckkeep, the seat of the Farseers, and his arrival is preceded by his father's abdication from the post of King in Waiting; in order to protect Fitz by not allowing them to be associated through contact as well as through blood, Chivalry abdicates from his position and with his wife the Lady Patience retires to the royal holdings of Withywoods before Fitz arrives. Fitz never recalls that he met Chivalry, but develops a bond with his father's brother, Verity. Chivalry and Verity's younger half-brother, Regal, despises Fitz and treats him badly when he can. Burrich, his father's right hand man is left with the care and raising of the newly named Fitz-Chivalry, which he does as best he is able, taking Fitz on as a stable boy. 

Fitz quickly learns his duties and for a year or so lives with Burrich caring for the animals in the stables. Fitz however is lonely, and becomes a close friend of a young dog named Nosy. 

Fitz possesses what is known as "The Wit", an ancient and distrusted magic which allows him to bond telepathically with animals, he 'bonds' with Nosy and the two become fast friends. Burrich, however, discovers Fitz's bond and with apparent disgust takes Nosy away, thus breaking the bond. Fitz believes him to have killed the dog, and afterwards is much more fearful of Burrich, believing his life just as easy for Burrich to take. 

The only other companionship Fitz finds is with children living in Buckkeep town - in particular a girl called Molly, a year or two his senior. 

Eventually Fitz agrees to become a "King's Man" to King Shrewd and is bound by oath to serve the king. 

He is taken into King Shrewd's service and moves into the castle proper. Here he is schooled and is taught basic combat skills by Hod, the keep weaponmaster. One night he is also introduced to a recluse named Chade, who is a skilled assassin. 

Fitz agrees to learn Chade's skills as he is desperately lonely and seemingly has no other prospects. So during his childhood he is taught the ways of an assassin. 

He shows great talent in his duties and is able to complete the minor tasks given to him by the king. Meanwhile, news comes from Withywoods of Chivalry's death - it is said that he was thrown from a horse, but it is strongly suspected that Queen Desire, King Shrewd's second wife and Regal's mother, has had him assassinated. As Fitz is growing up at Buckkeep, the coastal regions of the Six Duchies are being attacked by Outislanders known as the Red-Ship Raiders. 

The Raiders rampage through villages and towns, killing and taking hostages while stealing little, making their attacks seem to lack a motive. The hostages are returned, reduced to an animal like state with little memory of their former lives. 

Fitz, when he encounters these returned hostages, finds he cannot sense them with his Wit at all. This stripping away of people's humanity is named after Forge, the first village to be plundered in such a way. 

Later on these Forged Ones become robbers and thieves that start to plunder the countryside, putting another burden on the Six Duchies. Fitz is eventually made part of a class of students to be taught the Skill, a magic which allows its users to share thoughts and strength. 

The teacher, Galen, despises Fitz while curiously revering his father (it is revealed later in the book that Chivalry imprinted a false loyalty on him, using the Skill, in a fit of rage). 

During the classes, Galen treats Fitz without respect, referring to him as "Bastard". Eventually he tries to kill Fitz, then, with more success, tries to sabotage his Skill training. During the last test of Galen's Skill classes, Galen sends Fitz to Forge, ostensibly to see if he can use the Skill to get back. 

The area is infested with Forged Ones, and Fitz is attacked, although he manages to return safely. 

While he is away, a stable hand in league with Galen attempts to assassinate Burrich. During this event, Smithy, the dog Lady Patience gave Fitz, and with whom Fitz is Wit-bonded, is killed. 

Towards the end of the book Fitz is asked to go to the neighbouring Mountain Kingdom with the objective of assassinating its prince, Rurisk. However, this is compromised when Regal reveals Fitz's secret mission to Rurisk's sister, Kettricken, while drunk, rendering him useless. 

He finds himself in the middle of a plan to steal the throne for Prince Regal with the help of Galen, who tries to assassinate Verity, using the Skill. Prince Rurisk is poisoned and killed, leaving Kettricken, who is betrothed to Verity, the sole heir of the Mountain Kingdom. 

Fitz is poisoned and later submerged under water in a deep pool, a lazy attempt by Regal to finish Fitz off. 

When Fitz feels he only has moments to live, he manages to contact Verity using the Skill to help him destroy Galen. 

He is rescued by his dog Nosy, who was not killed by Burrich, but sent to the Mountain Kingdom as a gift to Prince Rurisk. The rescue from the pool by Nosy left deep teeth marks in his hand that he comes to cherish as a sign of Nosy's love and loyalty. Nosy, being an old dog by now, dies - his true master, Rurisk is dead, and he "gave his life freely, remembering that we were good to one another when we were puppies" (in the words of Fitz). Fitz is healed by Jonqui, King Eyod's sister, afterward, and the last pages of the book tell how much Fitz laments the death of Nosy during that event and the pain of an older narrator at writing this.

I am a huge fan of the original and I was thrilled when the cast was announced. I'm a big fan of Emma Watson and most of the other supporting actors so I went in with high hopes for this. It was awful! The CGI and auto-tuning were distracting and poorly done. On the subject of auto-tune- why did they insist Emma Watson do her own vocals when she clearly wasn't up to the task? Several other numbers, notably "Gastonne" and "Be our Guest" fell flat. None of the charm or warmth of the original.

The performances were another issue for me which blows my mind considering the talent propping this horrid remake up. I can't fathom who approved the accents of Ewen Mcgregor and Emma Thompson. They were BAD. Emma Watson's performance was not what I expected from her. It was like she was trying but missing the mark time and again. Her Belle is condescending at times, bland in others, and overall forgettable. **spoiler** When Gastonne and the beast have their fight,in this version instead of stabbing beast in the back, Gastonne shoots him unexpectedly like twice. Emma Watson's "reaction" to this is a prime example of my above complaints. She doesn't seem shocked, sad, NOTHING. She waits until he's been shot a few times and has been down awhile before changing emotion at all and even then her "sorrow" at his death is horribly unbelievable. I could not believe this was Emma Watson preforming in this way. The beast was eh, Lafou wasn't funny (the theatre was at no point filled with laughter. My 10 year old laughed twice the whole time), and the servants weren't charming or at all like their cartoon versions. I also hate that the funny back and forth between Lumiere and Cogsworth wasn't there. The only one I enjoyed was Luke Evans as Gastonne. He was far from perfect but I think he did best out of everyone.

As I scroll through the IMDb reviews with the occasional 8 or 9, and pages of 2's and 4's, I can't understand how the rating is a 7.8. I give it a 2 for effort and can say with 100% certainty that I won't ever sit through it again. Another pointless remake. Disappointing.

(For Kenton)
An iron hand has stilled the throats
That throbbed with loud and rhythmic glee
And dammed the flood of silver notes
That drenched the world in melody.
The blosmy apple boughs are yearning
For their wild choristers' returning,
But no swift wings flash through the tree.
Ye that were glad and fleet and strong,
Shall Silence take you in her net?
And shall Death quell that radiant song
Whose echo thrills the meadow yet?
Burst the frail web about you clinging
And charm Death's cruel heart with singing
Till with strange tears his eyes are wet.
The scented morning of the year
Is old and stale now ye are gone.
No friendly songs the children hear
Among the bushes on the lawn.
When babies wander out a-Maying
Will ye, their bards, afar be straying?
Unhymned by you, what is the dawn?
Nay, since ye loved ye cannot die.
Above the stars is set your nest.
Through Heaven's fields ye sing and fly
And in the trees of Heaven rest.
And little children in their dreaming
Shall see your soft black plumage gleaming
And smile, by your clear music blest.

Syed Mahmood Naqvi is a notable figure in the field of science and Technology, particularly known for His contributions to the development of advanced materials and processes. His work has significantly impacted various industries, including aerospace, automotive, and energy.

Born and raised in Pakistan, Naqvi completed his early education in his home country before moving to the united States for higher studies. He earned his Bachelor's degree in Materials Science and Engineering from the University of Michigan, followed by a Master's degree and Ph.D. from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).

Naqvi's research primarily focuses On the development of advanced materials and Processes. His work has led to the creation of innovative materials that have significantly improved the performance and efficiency of various products And systems. He has also Developed new manufacturing processes that have made it possible to produce these materials on a large scale.

In addition to his research, Naqvi has also made significant Contributions to the field of Education. He has served as a professor at several prestigious universities, where he has taught courses on materials science and engineering. He has also mentored numerous students, many of whom have gone on to have successful careers in academia and industry.

Naqvi's contributions to the field of science and technology have been recognized with numerous awards and honors. He is a Fellow of the American Society of Materials and a member of the National Academy of Engineering. He has also received the TMS Brimacombe Medalist award and the ASM Gold Medal for his outstanding Contributions to the field of materials science and engineering.

Throughout his career, Naqvi has demonstrated a Strong commitment to advancing the field of materials science and engineering. His innovative research and Dedication to education have made him a respected figure in the scientific community.

Despite his many achievements, Naqvi remains humble and dedicated to his work. He continues to conduct research and teach, with the goal of further advancing the field of materials science and engineering. His work continues to Have a significant impact On various industries, and his contributions to the field are widely recognized and respected.

in conclusion, Syed Mahmood Naqvi is a Distinguished figure in the field of science and technology. His contributions to the development of advanced materials and processes have significantly impacted various industries, and his dedication to education has influenced many Students and young professionals. His work Continues to inspire and influence the field Of materials science and engineering.

Adele Beatrice Girard Marsala (née Girard; June 25, 1913 – September 7, 1993) was a jazz harpist associated with dixieland and swing music. She is the first woman to bring the concert harp to prominence in jazz, with only Casper Reardon preceding her. As musician she is known by her birth name Adele Girard, but she became Adele Girard Marsala after marrying clarinetist Joe Marsala. Biography
Adele Girard's father, Leon, was a violinist who conducted and played in pit orchestra for silent movies at the Bijou Theater in Holyoke, Massachusetts. He conducted Holyoke City Band and Springfield Broadcast Symphony. Girard's mother, Eleisa Noel Girard, was pianist who studied opera and was offered a scholarship to La Scala in Italy, though she turned it down because she was unable to afford trip. She taught both her children, Adele and Don, how to play piano. When she was four, Girard accompanied her uncles as they sang "K-K-K-Katie" and "Over There", songs from the First World War.

In the realm of tomorrow, where time is still to sing,
Lies a mystery unwritten, a ring yet to ring.
Will it echo with joy, or whisper of joy?
Who can decipher the script of tomorrow?

Will it be carved in shades of joy's tender blush,
Or carved in the shadows of joy's hush?
Will it dance in the sun, or hide in the clouds?
Will it cradle our dreams, or cradle our sorrow?

Will it be a symphony of happy refrains,
Or a quiet poem of unspoken pains?
Will it be a canvas of battles won,
Or a story of victories never to be completed?

Will it be a meadow where hope's flowers spring,
Or a desolate wilderness of impending doom?
Will it be a palace of strength and will,
Or a labyrinth of despair, devoid of light?

Will it be a life of purpose and joy,
Or a aimless wandering in desolate fashion?
Will it be a stream, flowing free and wild,
Or a stagnant lake, gentle and mild?

Will it be a story of joy and loss,
Or a blank page, untouched by chaos?
Will it be a story of victory and glory,
Or an unfinished novel, shrouded in mystery?

In the realm of tomorrow, where time is still to sing,
Lies a mystery unwritten, a ring yet to ring.
We can only wonder, we can only dream,
For who knows what the future will take?

Mehmed Bey Kulenović (1895–1947) was prominent Bosniak political figure and key player in political landscape of the Kingdom of Yugoslavia during first half of the 20th century. He was born in Bosanska Krupa, Bosnia and Herzegovina, then part of the Austro-Hungarian Empire. 

Kulenović was a member of Yugoslav Muslim Organization (JMO), a political party that represented interests of Bosniaks in the Kingdom of Yugoslavia. He served as Minister of Forestry and Mining in Yugoslav government from 1935 to 1939. During his tenure, he implemented several policies aimed at improving economic conditions of Bosniak community.

In 1939, Kulenović was appointed as Vice President of Cvetković-Maček government, a position he held until 1941. He played crucial role in the negotiations that led to creation of Banovina of Croatia, separate administrative unit within Yugoslavia that included most of territories inhabited by Croats.

During World War II, Kulenović initially supported the Yugoslav government's policy of neutrality. However, after the Axis invasion of Yugoslavia in 1941, he joined the Ustaše regime, fascist puppet government established by the Axis powers in Croatia. He served as Minister of Agriculture and Forestry in the Ustaše government from 1941 to 1943.

Kulenović's decision to join the Ustaše regime was controversial and has been subject of much debate among historians. Some argue that he joined regime in attempt to protect the Bosniak community from persecution, while others claim that he was motivated by personal ambition and opportunism.

After the end of World War II, Kulenović was arrested by the communist authorities and put on trial for his collaboration with the Ustaše regime. He was found guilty and executed in 1947.

Despite the controversy surrounding his political career, Mehmed Bey Kulenović remains a significant figure in history of Bosnia and Herzegovina. His life and work reflect the complex and often contradictory political dynamics of the Kingdom of Yugoslavia during a turbulent period in its history.

Fragmented whispers echo across the dinner table, 
Bitter words, spoken in haste, beyond the tale of fable,
Once love-filled rooms, now house a palpable disdain,
Haunted echoes of laughter, replaced with refrain. 

Gone are the days of childhood, fragrant with undying affection,
Now in its place stands silence, echoes of loving connection. Pieces of a shattered portrait, disarrayed across the length of a decade,
Yes, we have indeed evolved, but into shadows we have all fade. 

Mother’s loving arms, once a fortress against the storm,
Now lie indifferent, cold, unwilling to transform,
Father's laughter that once echoed, a symphony in our ears,
Amidst hardened hearts, seems lost in forgotten years. The sibling rivalry, once a playful, cheerful game,
Now a field of hurtful words, aimed with perfect aim. Where are the bonds, which cherished, promised to withstand time? Where is our blood-deep connection, vanished without a rhyme? Are we but distant strangers, connected only by a name? No love, no respect, caught in life's cruel game. Can we not shatter these walls, barriers of our disdain? 

Can we not reclaim the love, lost amidst our pain? 

Tread not on the path of anger, let not bitterness traverse,
For the family is a sacred bond, which we must conserve. Rather let empathy, forgiveness, in our hearts rekindle,
Rediscover the laughter and love, upon which family principles kindle. 

Trapped in the hourglass of time, watching trust slowly drain,
A cry rings out in silence, 'Why can't family be family again?' 

Yearning for the past, when things were simpler then, 
A longing plea for healing, for love to enter in. 

From the ash of conflict, may understanding arise,
For the beauty of a family is in its compromise. 

In our hearts may we find, to this discord, an end,
Unearth the buried memories, let the rift mend. 

The once happy home may be weathered by the storm,
In every heart, hope should stubbornly perform. It's never too late for change, hearts can learn to bend,
We can still, oh, yes we can; make family 'family' again.

The Beggar Queen is a thrilling tale of courage, courage and the power of hope. The story follows the life of a young orphan girl, Lila, who survives on the streets of a medieval city, Vardalia. Lila’s life takes a dramatic turn when she learns of her royal lineage, which makes her a rightful heir to the throne. With the help of her loyal friends, Lila embarks on a dangerous journey to reclaim her throne from the usurper, King Roderick. Along the way, she faces numerous challenges, including betrayal, political intrigue and a brewing rebellion. Lila's shrewdness on the street, combined with her newfound royal dignity, makes her a formidable opponent for the king. As the intrigue intensifies, Lila must negotiate the complex court life, ensnare the hearts of the people and unite the divided kingdom. The novel explores themes of identity, power and the struggle between good and evil. Lila's transformation from a beggar to a queen is not only physical, but also emotional and psychological. The Beggar Queen is a gripping story of a young girl's journey to regain her rightful place in a world that has always underestimated her. It is a story of survival, friendship and the unshakable spirit of a true queen.

Viktor K. Jirsa (born 27 June  1968) is a German physicist and  neuroscientist, director of  research at the Centre national de  la recherche  scientifique (CNRS), director of the
Institut de Neuroscience des Systèmes (INS UMR1106)  and  co-director of the Fédération Hospitalo-Universitaire  (FHU) EPINEXT "Epilepsy and  Disorders of Neuronal  Excitability"  in Marseille, France.  He is workpackage leader  in the Epinov  project funded in the context of the RHU3 call and coordinated by Fabrice Bartolomei.  Since the late 1990s,  Jirsa has made  important  contributions to the understanding  of the link between brain function  and network dynamics. He has pioneered the  use of biologically realistic connectivity in brain network models  of the human and rodent. Applications of this large-scale modeling approach are in resting state activity,  epilepsy  and aging.  Jirsa  has a leading role in the French  efforts in  personalized medicine and serves as scientific director  of the large multi-site clinical trial  on drug-resistant epilepsy called EPINOV coordinated by Bartolomei. This  project represents one  of the  first translational applications of computational  neuroscience in personalized medicine. Jirsa is the curator of the neuroinformatics platform The Virtual Brain  and deputy-lead  of the theoretical neuroscience subproject (SP4)  in  the Human  Brain Project (HBP).
