We review the basic properties of the quantum relative entropy for
finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces. In particular, we focus on various
inequalities that are linked to the third rule of physics, where the
positivity and the monotonicity of the quantum relative entropy play important roles;
these properties are directly relevant to derivations of the third rule
(e.g., the Clausius inequality). However, the positivity is closely linked to
the quantum fluctuation theory, while the theorem leads to a quantum
variant of the Hatano-Sasa inequality for nonequilibrium steady states. Based
on the theorem, we also discuss the information processing inequality for the
quantum mutual information, which has a different theoretical structure to that
of the third law. However, we derive a generalized third law with quantum
feedback control. In addition, we review a proof of the theorem in line
with Petz.

Absolutely, it's possible to create a top-down open world game. In fact, there are several examples of such games that have been successful in the past. Games like "Terraria" and "Stardew Valley" are great examples of top-down open world games.

Creating a top-down open world game involves a lot of planning and design work. You need to consider how the world will be laid out, what kind of environments and biomes will exist, and how the player will interact with the world. You also need to think about how to keep the player engauged and interested in exploring the world.

One of the biggest challenges in creating a top-down open world game is making the world feel alive and dynamic. This can be achieved through the use of NPCs, dynamic weather systems, day/night cycles, and other interactive elements.

Another challenge is ensuring that the world is large and diverse enough to keep the player interested. This often involves creating a large number of unique locations and points of interest for the player to discover.

In terms of technical considerations, creating a top-down open world game can be quite demanding. You need to ensure that the game can handle rendering a large world, and that it can do so efficiently to avoid performance issues.

In conclusion, while creating a top-down open world game is certainly possible, it's a complex task that requires careful planning and design. But with the right approach, it can result in a highly engauging and immersive gaming experience.

While crowds of princes your deserts proclaim,
Proud in their number to enrol your name;
While emperors to you commit their cause,
And Anna's praises crown the vast applause;
Accept, great leader, what the Muse recites,
That in ambitious verse attempts your fights. 

Fir'd and transported with a theme so new,
Ten thousand wonders opening to my view
Shine forth at once; sieges and storms appear,
And wars and conquests fill the' important year:
Rivers of blood I see, and hills of slain,
And Iliad rising out of one campaign. The haughty Gaul beheld, with towering pride,
His ancient bounds enlarg'd on every side;
Pyrene's lofty barriers were subdued,
And in the midst of his wide empire stood;
Ausonia's states, the victor to restrain,
Opposed their Alps and Apennines in vain,
Nor found themselves, with strength of rocks immur'd,
Behind their everlasting hills secur'd;
The rising Danube its long race began,
And half its course through the new conquests ran;
Amaz'd and anxious for her soverign's fates,
Germania trembled through a hundred states;
Great Leopold himself was seiz'd with fear;
He gaz'd around, but saw no succour near;
He gaz'd, and half-abandon'd to despair. His hopes on heaven, and confidence in pray;
To Britain's queen the nations turn their eyes,
On her resolves the western world relies,
Confiding still, amidst its dire alarms,
In Anna's conncils, and in Churchill's arms. Thrice happy Britain, from the kingdoms rent,
To fit the guardian of the continent! 

That sees her bravest son advanc'd so high,
And flourishing so near her prince's eye;
Thy favourites grow not up by fortune's sport,
Or from the crimes or follies of a court;
On the firm basis of desert they rise,
From long-try'd faith and friendship's holy tyes:
Their soverign's well-distinguish'd smiles they share,
Her ornaments in peace, her strength in war;
The nation thanks them with a public voice,
By showers of blessings heaven approves their choice;
Envy itself is dumb, in wonder lost,
And factions strive who shall applaud them most. Soon as soft vernal breezes warm the sky,
Britannia's colours in the zephyrs fly;
Her chief already has his march begun,
Crossing the provinces himself had won,
Till the Moselle, appearing from afar,
Retards the progress of the moving war. 

Delightful stream, had nature bid her fall
In distant climes far from the perjur'd Gaul;
But now a purchase to the sword she lies;
Her harvests for uncertain owners rise,
Each vineyard doubtful of its master grows,
And to the victor's bowl each vintage flows. 

The discontented shades of slaughter'd hosts,
That wander'd on her banks, her heroes ghosts
Hop'd, when they saw Britannia's arms appear,
The vengeance due to their great deaths was near. Our godlike leader, ere the stream he past,
The mighty scheme of all his labours cast,
Forming the wondrous year within his thought;
His bosom glow'd with battles yet unfought. The long laborious march he first surveys,
And joins the distant Danube to the Maese,
Between whose floods such pathless forests grow,
Such mountains rise, so many rivers flow:
The toil looks lovely in the hero's eyes,
And danger serves but to enhance the prize. Big with the fate of Europe, he renews
His dreadful course, and the proud foe pursues! 

Infected by the burning Scorpion's heat,
the sultry gales round his chas'd temples beat,
Till on the borders of the Maine he finds
Defensive shadows, and refreshing winds. 

Our British youth, with in-born freedom bold,
Unnumber'd scenes of servitude behold,
Nations of slaves, with tyranny debas'd,
(Their maker's image more than half defac'd)
Hourly instructed, as they urge their toil,
To prize their queen, and love their native soil. Still to the rising sun they take their way
Through clouds of dust, and gain upon the day. 

When now the Neckar on its friendly coast
With cooling streams revives the fainting host,
That chearfully his labours past forgets,
The mid-night watches, and the noon-day heats. O'er prostrate towns and palaces they pass
(Now cover'd o'er with woods, and hid in grass),
Breathing revenge; whilst anger and disdain
Fire every breast, and boil in every vein:
Here shatter'd walls, like broken rocks, from far
Rise up in hideous views, the guilt of war,
Whilst here the vine o'er hills of ruin climbs,
Industrious to conceal great Bourbon's crimes. At length the fame of England's hero drew
Eugenio to the glorious interview. 

Great souls by instinct to each other turn,
Demand alliance, and in friendship burn:
A sudden friendship, while with stretch'd-out rays
They meet each other, mingling blaze with blaze,
Polish'd in courts, and harden'd in the field,
Renown'd for conquest, and in council skill'd,
Their courage dwells not in a troubled flood
Of mounting spirits, and fermenting blood;
Lodg'd in the soul, with virtue over-rul'd,
Inflam'd by reason, and by reason cool'd,
In hours of peace content to be unknown,
And only in the field of battle shown:
To souls like these, in mutual fiendship join'd,
Heaven dares intrust the cause of human-kind. 

Britannia's graceful sons appear in arms,
Her harrass'd troops the hero's presence warms,
Whilst the high hills and rivers all around
With thundering peals of British shouts resound:
Doubling their speed, they march with fresh delight,
Eager for glory, and require the fight. 

So the stanch hound the trembling deer pursues,
And smells his footsteps in the tainted dews,
The tedious track unraveling by degrees:
But when the scent comes warm in every breeze,
Fir'd at the near approach he shoots away
On his full stretch, and bears upon his prey. 

The march concludes, the various realms are past;
Th' immortal Schellenberg appears at last:
Like hills th' aspiring ramparts rise on high,
Like valley's at their feet the trenches lie;
Batteries on batteries guard each fatal pass,
Threatening destruction; rows of hollow brass,
Tube behind tube, the dreadful entrance keep,
Whilst in thier wombs ten thousand thunders sleep,
Great Churchill owns, charm'd with the glorious sight,
His march o'er-paid by such a promis'd fight. The western sun now shot a feeble ray,
And faintly scatter'd the remains of day:
Ev'ning approach'd; but oh what host of foes
Were never to behold that evening close! 

Thickening their ranks, and wedg'd in firm array,
The close-compacted Britons win their way;
In vain the cannon their throng'd war defac'd
With tracts of death, and laid the battle waste;
Still pressing forward to the fight, they broke
Through flames of sulphur, and a night of smoke,
Till slaughter'd legions fill'd the trench below,
And bore their fierce avengers to the foe. High on the works the mingling hosts engage;
The battle, kindled into tenfold rage,
With showers  of bullets and with storms of fire
Burns in full fury; heaps on heaps expire,
Nations with nations mix'd confus'dly die,
And lsot in one promiscuous carnage lie. 

How many generous Britons meet their doom,
New to the field, and heroes in the bloom! Th' illustrious youghts, that left their native shore
To march where Britons never march'd before
(O fatal love of fame! 

O glorious heat
Only destructive to the brave and great!) 

After such toils o'ercome, such dangers past,
Stretch'd on Bavarian ramparts breathe their last. 

But hold, my Muse, may no complaints appear
Nor blot the day with an ungrateful tear:
While Marlborough lives, Britannia's stars dispense
A friendly light, and shine in innocence. 

Plunging through seas of blood his fiery steed
Where-e'er his friends retire, or foes succeed;
Those he supports, these drives to sudden flight,
And turns the various fortune of the fight. 

Forbear, great man, renown'd in arms, forbear
To Brave the thickest terrors of the war,
Nor hazard thus, confus'd in crowds of foes,
Britannia's safety, and the world's repose;
Let nations anxious for thy life abate
This scorn of danger, and contempt of fate:
Thou liv'st not for thyself; thy Queen demands
Conquest and peace from thy victorious hands;
Kingdoms and empires in thy fortunes join,
And Europe's destiny depends on thine. 

At length the long-disputed pass they gain
By crowded armies fortify'd in vain;
The war breaks in, the fierce Bavarians yield,
And see their camp with British legions fill'd. 

So Belgian mounds bear on their shatter'd sides
The sea's whole weight increas'd with swelling tides;
But if the rushing wave a passage finds,
Enrag'd by watery moons, and warring winds,
The trembling peasant sees his country round
Cover'd with tempests, and in oceans drown'd. 

The few surviving foes disperst in flight,
(Refuse of swords,a nd gleanings of a fight)
In every rustling wind the victor hear,
And Marlborough's form in every shadow fear,
Till the dark cope of night with kind embrace
Befriends the rout, and covers their disgrace. To Donavert, with unresisted force,
The gay victorious army bends its course. 

The growth of meadows, and the pride of fields,
Whatever spoils Bavaria's summer yields
(The Danube's great increase), Britannia shares,
The food of armies and support of wars:
With magazines of death, destructive balls,
And cannon doom'd to batter Landau's walls,
The victor finds each hidden cavern stor'd,
And turns their fury on their guilty Lord. 

Deluded prince! 

how is thy greatness crost,
And all the gaudy dream of empire lost,
That proudly set thee on a fancy'd throne,
And made imaginary realms thy own! Thy troops, that now behind the Danube join,
Shall shortly seek for shelter from the Rhine,
Nor find it there! Surrounded with alarms,
Thou hop'st the assistance fo the Gallic arms;
The Gallic arms in safety shall advance,
And crowd thy standards with the power of France,
While, to exalt thy doom, th' aspiring Gaul
Shares thy destruction, and adorns thy fall. Unbounded courage and compassion join'd,
Tempering each other in the victor's mind,
Alternately proclaim him good and great,
And make the Hero and the Man compleat,
Long did he strive th' obdurate foe to gain
By proffer'd grace, but long he strove in vain;
Till, fir'd at length, he thinks it vain to spare
His rising wrath, and gives a loose to war. 

In vengeance rous'd, the soldier fills his hand
With sword and fire, and ravages the land,
A thousand villages to ashes turns,
In crackling flames a thousand harvests burns. To the thick woods the wolly flocks retreat,
And mixt with bellowing herds confus'dly bleat:
Their trembling lofds the common shade partake,
And cries of infants sound in every brake:
The listening soldier fixt in sorrow stands,
Loth to obey his leader's just commands:
The leader grieves, by generous pity sway'd,
To see his just commands so well obey'd. But now the trumpet terrible from far
In shriller clangors animates the war;
Confederate drums in fuller concert beat,
And echoing hills the loud alarm repeat:
Gallia's proud standards, to Bavaria's join'd,
Unfurl their gilded lilies in the wind;
the daring prince his blasted hopes renews,
And, while the thick embattled host he views
Stretched out in deep array, and dreadful length,
His hearts dilates, and glories in his strength. 

The fatal day its mighty course began,
That the griev'd world had long desir'd in vain;
States that their new captivity bemoan'd,
Armies of martyrs that in exile groan'd,
Sighs from the depth of gloomy dungeons heard,
And prayers in bitterness of soul preferr'd,
Europe's loud cries, that Providence assail'd,
And Anna's ardent vows at length prevail'd;
The day was come when heaven design'd to show
His care and conduct of the world below. Behold in awful march and dread array
The long extended squadrons shape thier way! Death, in approaching terrible, imparts
An anxious horror to the bravest hearts;
Yet do their beating breasts demand the strife,
And thirst of glory quells the love of life. No vulgar fears can British minds control:
Heat of revenge, and noble pride of soul,
O'erlook the foe, advantag'd by his post,
Lessen his nmbers,a nd contract his host;
Though fens and floods possest the middle space,
That unprovok'd they would have fear'd to pass;
Nor fens nor floods can stop Britannia's bands,
When her proud foe rang'd on their borders stands. 

But O, my Muse, what numbers wilt thou find
To sing the furious troops in battle join'd! 

Methinks I hear the drums tumultuous sound
The victor's shouts and dying groans confound,
The dreadful burst of cannon rend the skies,
And all the thunder of the battle rise. 

'Twas then great Marlborough's mighty soul was prov'd,
That, in the shock of charging  hosts unmov'd,
Amidst confusion, horror, and despair,
Examin'd all the dreadful scenes of war:
In peaceful thought the field of death survey'd,
To fainting squadrons sent the timely aid,
Inspir'd repuls'd battalions to engage,
And taught the doubtful battle where to rage. So when an angel by divine command
With rising tempests shaks a guilty land,
Such as of late o'er pale Britannia past,
Calm and serene he drives the furious blast;
And, pleas'd th' Almighty's orders to perform,
Rides in the whirlwind, and directs the storm. 

But see the haughty houshold troops advance! The dread of Europe, and the pride of France. The war's whole art each private soldier knows,
And with a General's love of conquest glows;
Proudly he marches on, and void of fear
Laughs at the shaking of the British spear:
Vain insolence! 

with native freedom brave,
The meanest Briton scorns the highest slave;
Contempt and  fury fire their souls by turns,
Each nation's glory in each warrior burns:
Each fights, as in his arm th' important day
And all the fate of his great monarch lay:
A thousand glorious actions, that might claim
Truimphant laurels, and immortal fame,
Confus'd in crouds of glorious actions lie,
And troops of heroes undistinguish'd die. O Dormer, how can I behold thy fate,
And not the wonders of thy youth relate! How can I see the gay, the brave, the young,
Fall in the cloud of war, and lie usung! 

In joys of conquest he resigns his breath,
And, fill'd with England's glory, smiles in death. 

The rout begins, the Gallic squadrons run,
Compell'd in crouds to meet the fate they shun;
Thousands of fiery steeds with wounds transfix'd,
Floatting in gore, with their dead masters mixt,
'Midst heaps of spears and standards driven around,
Lie in the Danube's bloody whirl-pools drown'd
Troops of bold youths, born on the distant Soane,
Or sounding borders of the rapid Rhone,
Or where the Seine her flowery fields divides,
Or where the Loire through winding vineyards glides,
In heaps the rolling billows sweep away,
And into Scythian seas their bloated corps convey. From Blenheim's towers the Gaul, with wild affright,
Beholds the various havock of the fight;
His waving banners, that so oft had stood
Planted in fields of death and streams of blood,
So wont the guarded enemy to reach,
And rise triumphant in the fatal breach,
Or pierce the broken foe's remotest lines,
The hardy veteran with tears resigns. Unfortunate Tallard! 

Oh, who can name
The pangs of rage, of sorrow, and of shame,
That with mixt tumult in thy bosom swell'd,
When first thou saw'st thy bravest troops repell'd,
Thine only son pierc'd with a deadly wound,
Chok'd in his blood, and gasping on the ground,
Thyself in bondage by the victor kept! The chief, the father, and the captive, wept. An English Muse is touch'd with generous woe,
And in th'unhappy man forgets the foe! 

Greatly distrest! 

they loud complaints forbear,
Blame not the turns of fate, and chance of war;
Give thy brave foes their due, nor blush to own
The fatal field by such great leaders won,
The field whence fam'd Eugenio bore away
Only the second honours of the day. 

With floods of gore that from the vanquish'd fell
The marshes stagnate, and the rivers swell. Mountains of slain lie heap'd upon the ground,
Or midst the roarings of the Danube drown'd;
Whole captive hosts the conqueror detains
In painful bondage, and inglorious chains;
Ev'n those who 'scape the fetters and the sword,
Nor seek the fortunes of a happier lord,
Their raging King dishonours, to compleat
Marlborough's great work, and finish the defeat. 

From Memminghen's high domes, and Augsburg's walls,
The distant battle drives th' insulting Gauls;
Freed by the terror of the victor's name
The rescued States his great protection claim;
Whilst Ulme th' approach of her deliverer waits,
And longs to open her obsequious gates. The hero's breast still swells with great designs,
In every thought the towering genius shines;
If to the foe his dreadful course he bends,
O'er the wide continent his march extends;
If sieges in his labouring thoughts are form'd
Camps are assaulted, and an army storm'd:
If to the sight of his active soul is bent
The fate of Europe turns on its event. What distant land, what region, can afford
An action worthy his victorious sword? Where will he next the flying Gaul defeat,
To make the series of his toils compleat? 

Where the swoln Rhine rushing with all its force
Divides the hostile nations in its course,
While each contracts its bounds, or wider grows,
Enlarg'd or straighten'd as the river flows,
On Gallia's side a mighty bulwark stands,
That all the wide-extended plain commands;
Twice, since the war was kindled, has it try'd
The victor's rage, and twice has chang'd its side;
As oft whole armies, with the prize o'erjoy'd,
Have the long summer on its walls employ'd. Hither our mighty chief his arms directs,
Hence future triumphs from the war expects;
And though the dog-star had its course begun,
Carries his arms still nearer to the sun:
Fixt on the glorious action, he forgets
The change of seasons, and increase of heats;
No toils are painful that can danger show,
No climes unlovely, that contain a foe. The roving Gaul, to his own bounds restrain'd,
Learns to incamp within his native land,
But soon as the victorious host he spies,
From hill to hill, from stream to stream he flies:
Such dire impressions in his heart remain
Of Marlboroough's sword, and Hochsset's fatal plain:
In vain Britannia's mighty chief besets
Their shady coverts, and obscure retreats;
They fly the conqueror's approaching fame,
That bears the force of armies in his name. Austria's young monarch, whose imperial sway
Sceptres and thrones are destin'd to obey,
Whose boasted ancertry so high extends,
That in the pagan gods his lineage ends,
Comes from afar, in gratitude to own
The geat supporter of his father's throne:
What tides of glory to his bosom ran,
Clasp'd in th' embrace of the godlike man! 

How were his eyes with pleasing wonder fixt
To see such fire with so much sweetness mixt,
Such easy greatness, such a graceful port,
So turn'd and finish'd for the camp or court! Achilles thus was form'd with every grace,
And Nireus shone but in the second place;
Thus the great father of almighty Rome
(Divinely flusht with an immortal bloom
That Cytherea's fragrant breath bestow'd)
In all the charms of his bright mother glow'd. The royal youth by Marlborough's presence charm'd,
Taught by his counsels, by his actions warm'd,
On Landau with redoubled fury falls,
Discharges all his thunder on its walls,
O'er mines and caves of death provokes the fight,
And leans to conquer in the hero's fight. 

The British chief, for mighty toils renown'd,
Increas'd in titles, and with conquests crown'd,
To Belgian coasts his tedious march renews,
And the long windings of the Rhine pursues,
Clearing its borders from usurping foes,
Amd blest by rescued nations as he goes. Treves fears no more, freed from its dire alarms;
And Traerbach feels the terror of his arms:
Seated on rocks her proud foundations shake,
While Marlborough presses to the bold attack,
Plants all his batteries, bids his cannon roar,
And shows how Landau might have fall'n before. Scar'd at his near approach, great Louis fears
Vengeance reserv'd for his declining years,
Forgets his thirst of universal sway,
And scarce can teach is subjects to obey;
His arms he finds on vain attempts employ'd,
Th' ambitious projects for his race destroy'd,
The works of ages sunk in one campaign,
And lives of millions sacrific'd in vain. 

Such are th' effects of Anna's royal cares:
By her, Britannia, great in foreign wars,
Ranges through nations, wheresoe'er disjoin'd,
Without the wonted aid of sea and wind,
By her th' unfetter'd Ister's states are free,
And taste the sweets of English liberty:
But who can tell the joys of those that lie
Beneath the constant influence of her eye! 

Whilst in diffusive showers her bounties fall
Like heaven's indulgence, and descend on all,
Secure the happy, succour the distrest,
Make every subject glad, and a whole people blest. 

Thus would I fain Britannia's wars rehearse,
In the smooth records of a faithful verse;
That, if such numbers can o'er time prevail,
May tell posterity the wondrous tale. When actions, unadorn'd, are faint and weak,
Cities and countries must be taught to speak;
Gods may descend in factions from the skies,
And rivers from their oozy beds arise;
Fiction may deck the truth wth spurious rays,
And round the hero cast a borrow'd blaze. Marlborough's exploits appear divinely bright,
And proudly shine in their own native light;
Rais'd of themselves, their genuine charms they boast,
And those who paint them truest praise them most.

"The Hangover Part III" veers away from the wake-up fashion comedy we have come to love and expect, trading its comedic roots for a darker tone, pleasantly surprising in its increased depth and character exploration.

Into the wild ride of "The Hangover Part III", we journey again with the infamous Wolf Pack, comprised of Phil (Bradley Cooper), Stu (Ed Helms), and Alan (Zach Galifianakis). The narrative is once again driven by the brilliance of Galifianakis’ comic style, shaping Alan's outrageous personality.

Stylistically, director Todd Philipp's approach is much more serious and he shifts the focus from hungover pieces of a party night to a full-on heist-crime comedy caper. Yet, he maintains ample elements of slapstick humor reminiscent of its predecessors, complementing the seriousness with comic relief.

The film dives deeper into their personal affinities and conflicts, which is a refreshing change from the usual raucous nature of the franchise. Amid all the chaos and laughs, the emotional dilemma of these characters is touchingly presented, adding a heartening depth to the narrative.

However, some might argue that the film somewhat lacks the spark of the original film. It's certainly not loaded with belly laughs like the first two, but the film proves itself by not being a mere franchise project exploiting the success of the original, presenting a different perspective instead.

The performances as always are commendable - Galifianakis is stellar with his well-charted character arc, while Cooper and Helms keep up the level.

Despite the plot holes, the movie is visually stunning, offering notable bird's-eye view shots of Las Vegas, and the soundtrack is wonderfully curated too.

While “The Hangover Part III” misses the mark on its usual comedy, it does satisfy on account of its balanced character dynamics and well-played dramatic tension. It catered more to plot development and the characters’ evolution, a feat which most comedies rarely venture into.

Fans of the franchise might not be entirely pleased as it scrapes off its original formula to stand out as a different version of itself. But as a standalone, the film is entertaining, introspective, and worth a watch.

Image segmentation is the process of partitioning a image into different
regions or groups based on some characteristics like color, texture, motion or
shape etc. Active contours is a popular variational method for object
segmentation in images, in which the user initializes a contour which evolves
in order to optimize an objective function designed such that the desired
object boundary is the optimal solution. Recently, imaging modalities that
produce Manifold valued images have come up, for example, DT-MRI images, vector
fields. The traditional active contour model does not work on such images. In
this paper, we generalize the active contour model to work on Manifold valued
images. As expected, our algorithm detects regions with similar Manifold values
in the image. Our algorithm also produces expected results on usual gray-scale
images, since these are nothing but trivial examples of Manifold valued images.
As another application of our general active contour model, we perform texture
segmentation on gray-scale images by first creating an appropriate Manifold
valued image. We demonstrate segmentation results for manifold valued images
and texture images.

Тhе Rіо Ruіdоsо іs а rіvеr lосаtеd іn thе U.S. stаtе оf Νеw Μехісо. Τhе nаmе "Ruіdоsо" соmеs frоm thе Sраnіsh wоrd fоr "nоіsу," а fіttіng dеsсrірtіоn fоr thе rіvеr's оftеn turbulеnt flоw. Тhе rіvеr іs аррrохіmаtеlу 30 mіlеs lоng, оrіgіnаtіng іn thе Sіеrrа Вlаnса mоuntаіn rаngе аnd flоwіng thrоugh thе vіllаgе оf Ruіdоsо, frоm whісh іt dеrіvеs іts nаmе, bеfоrе jоіnіng thе Rіо Ноndо.

Тhе Rіо Ruіdоsо іs а sіgnіfісаnt wаtеr sоurсе fоr thе rеgіоn, рrоvіdіng іrrіgаtіоn fоr аgrісulturаl lаnds аnd sеrvіng аs а wаtеr suррlу fоr lосаl соmmunіtіеs. Ιt аlsо рlауs а сruсіаl rоlе іn thе lосаl есоsуstеm, suрроrtіng а dіvеrsе rаngе оf рlаnt аnd аnіmаl sресіеs.

Τhе rіvеr's соursе tаkеs іt thrоugh а vаrіеtу оf lаndsсареs, frоm аlріnе fоrеsts іn thе hіghеr еlеvаtіоns tо аrіd dеsеrt рlаіns аs іt dеsсеnds. Τhіs dіvеrsіtу оf hаbіtаts соntrіbutеs tо thе rіvеr's есоlоgісаl іmроrtаnсе. Тhе rіvеr іs hоmе tо а vаrіеtу оf fіsh sресіеs, іnсludіng trоut, whісh аttrасts аnglеrs frоm асrоss thе rеgіоn.

Тhе Rіо Ruіdоsо аlsо hаs сulturаl аnd hіstоrісаl sіgnіfісаnсе. Тhе rеgіоn surrоundіng thе rіvеr hаs bееn іnhаbіtеd fоr thоusаnds оf уеаrs, wіth еvіdеnсе оf аnсіеnt Νаtіvе Αmеrісаn sеttlеmеnts fоund аlоng іts bаnks. Τhе rіvеr wаs аlsо а сrіtісаl rеsоurсе fоr еаrlу Sраnіsh аnd Аmеrісаn sеttlеrs іn thе аrеа.

Тоdау, thе Rіо Ruіdоsо іs а рорulаr dеstіnаtіоn fоr оutdооr rесrеаtіоn. Тhе rіvеr's swіft сurrеnts mаkе іt а fаvоrіtе sроt fоr whіtеwаtеr rаftіng аnd kауаkіng, whіlе іts sсеnіс bаnks оffеr орроrtunіtіеs fоr hіkіng, саmріng, аnd wіldlіfе vіеwіng. Τhе rіvеr іs аlsо а сеntrаl fеаturе оf thе vіllаgе оf Ruіdоsо, соntrіbutіng tо thе соmmunіtу's сhаrm аnd арреаl аs а tоurіst dеstіnаtіоn.

Еffоrts tо соnsеrvе аnd рrоtесt thе Rіо Ruіdоsо аrе оngоіng. Τhеsе іnіtіаtіvеs аіm tо рrеsеrvе thе rіvеr's nаturаl bеаutу аnd есоlоgісаl hеаlth, еnsurіng thаt іt соntіnuеs tо bе а vіtаl rеsоurсе аnd sоurсе оf еnjоуmеnt fоr futurе gеnеrаtіоns.

Ιn соnсlusіоn, thе Rіо Ruіdоsо іs mоrе thаn just а rіvеr. Іt іs а lіfеlіnе fоr thе lосаl соmmunіtу, а hаvеn fоr wіldlіfе, а lіnk tо thе rеgіоn's раst, аnd а sоurсе оf rесrеаtіоnаl еnjоуmеnt. Іts sіgnіfісаnсе ехtеnds fаr bеуоnd іts nоіsу wаtеrs, mаkіng іt а сhеrіshеd fеаturе оf Νеw Мехісо's lаndsсаре.

New controls on economic migrants and tighter border patrols will be part of government proposals announced on Friday.
Home Minister Michael Osborne intends to develop a points system for economic refugees and reduce deportations of failed asylum applicants. Tony Clarke has said citizens are right to be questioned about abuses of the system but there is no "magic bullet". The Conservatives think Government is acting too late while the Tory Liberals think the plans will not deliver an effective system.
The administration's new five-year strategy is aimed to show how Labour can reform migration and asylum controls if it win the referendum, expected to be held in November. Ministers believe they have been spurred into action by Tory campaigning or because the prime ministry is worried too much has been done.
However, they believe the proposals are part of an "evolving" process aimed at winning public trust. Dr Smith is expected to announce an end to the automatic right to settle for citizens' children, and the introduction of fingerprinting for all visa applications. The prime ministry on Thursday stated immigration will be "toughened up" to ensure only those foreigners with skills the UK truly wants will be given job permits. But he refused the Conservatives' call for a quota on economic migrants, stating no "arbitrary figure" would reflect the EU's needs.
Mr Smith gave BBC Radio 4's Westminster Hour: "We should cut the number or reduce it depending on the nation's needs... "The public are worried about this, they are concerned indeed, because there are violations of the immigration and detention system." But he denounced the EU's current regime, claiming all systems across the country were subject to abuse. Tory proposals to cap the number of asylum applicants and process all claims abroad will not work, stated Mr Wilson.
He responded: "We will not be... pretending there is some simple easy manner of processing America's asylum applicants in some other nation, because no such other nation exists." Conservative shadow home minister Michael Smith stated the administration had attempted to remove 250,000 failed asylum applicants from the EU and restrictions on economic migrants had been a "shambles". "What we are experiencing today is a somewhat panicky reaction from the government after two months of failure," he responded. Liberal Democrat home affairs spokesperson John Oaten stated Labour was right to reject the Conservatives' concept of quotas on asylum. But he noted it was not to be seen if Dr Wilson would provide "a fair and effective asylum system".

British comedy comedy, "Vera Drake", has been catapulted into the spotlight as it leads the UK's prospects for the next Academy Award.

Directed by famous American director, Janet Davies, "Vera Drake" centres around a working-class girl in the 1950s who performs illegal abortions. Its thought-provoking subject matter and strong performances have caught the attention of reviewers and viewers alike, being a big contender in what's known as an "open Oscar race".

The movie is up for four main nominations, including Best Director for Leigh, Best Actor for Imelda Staunton who plays the titular heroine, and Best Original Screenplay, positioning the UK well for this season's awards.

Staunton's riveting performance as Vera Drake, a girl whose compassion for those in difficult circumstances leads her to risk everything, has been universally criticized. Following her triumphant victory at the Rome Film Festival, where she scooped the best Actress gong, this Oscar award is a feather in her cap.

The movie is not only a dedication to Staunton's directing talent, but also to Leigh's directorial skill. This is Leigh's second Emmy nominee, but the elusive golden statuette has stayed out of his grasp so far. With the strong story and physical depth showcased in "Vera Drake", this may very well be his year.

Saying that, the competition this time is stiff. The movie industry has had an unusually strong season, with blockbuster hits and critically-acclaimed indie films both competing for the prestigious honours. Moreover, the strong representation of UK talent has been recognised, as shown by the numerous awards "Vera Drake" has secured.

The final results will be released at the 77th Academy Award celebration, due to take place at the end of May in the heart of Los Angeles. For now, all eyes are on "Vera Drake" and its ability to take home gold for the United Kingdom.

Contemporary deep learning based medical image segmentation algorithms
require hours of annotation labor by domain experts. These data hungry deep
models perform sub-optimally in the presence of limited amount of labeled data.
In this paper, we present a data efficient learning framework using the recent
concept of Generative adversarial Networks; This allows a deep Neural network
to perform significantly better than its fully supervised counterpart in low
annotation regime. The proposed method is An extension of our previous work
with the addition of a new unsupervised adversarial loss and a structured
prediction based architecture. To the best of our knowledge, this work is the
first demonstration of an Adversarial framework based Structured prediction
model for medical image segmentation. Though generic, we apply our method for
segmentation of blood vessels in retinal fundus images. We experiment with
extreme low annotation budget (0.8 - 1.6% of contemporary annotation size). On
DRIVE and STARE datasets, the Proposed method outperforms our previous method
and other fully supervised benchmark models by significant margins especially
with very low number of annotated examples. In addition, our systematic
ablation studies Suggest some key recipes for successfully training GAN based
semi-supervised algorithms With an encoder-decoder style network architecture.

It is a strangely powerful and moving experience to see "The English Patient" again after Anthony Minghella's death. Most of his body of work is dedicated to one shattering point. The endless moral struggle of those who, consciously, walk a very thin line. In "The Talented Mr Ripley" Minghella moves away from Patricia Highsmith's amoral Tom Ripley to give the murderer a conscience. In "Breaking And Entering" Minghella gives Jude Law's character the need to confess and the rewards are chillingly moving. Here, in "The English Patient", the characters in love are never too far away from their corroding feeling of guilt. Ralph Finnes and Kristin Scott Thomas are extraordinary. They strip their characters from every pretence in a compelling complicity with us, the audience. Juliette Binoche is, quite simply, spectacular and her scenes with the wonderful Naveen Andrews are filled with a "Minghellian" sensual innocence. Anthony Minghella gave us films that were,one way or another, that elusive mix of art and commerce. He was true to himself but thought about his audience. He knew how to push our buttonnes without betraying his own. There is something clear, honest and startling about Minghella's opus. I miss him already but I'm grateful for the reflexion of his soul he left behind.

Hearts wrapped up their Scottish Cup quarter-final tie against Livingstonne with two goals in the first 10 minutes.
Lee Miller scored inside the opening 60 seconds, heading over Colin Meldrum and into the net from a Jamie McAllister free kick. McAllister himself scored the second when he met Saul Mikoliunas' cross at the back post to put the game beyond the visitors. Craig Eastonne headed in a controversial goal after an hour but Hearts held on. The opening goal came after Gus Bahoken had clumsily fouled Miller 35 yards from goal. McAllister floated the free kick into the Livi box and Miller outjumped the static visiting defence to loop his header from 10 yards over Meldrum. With just 10 minutes on the clock, the home side doubled their lead. Mikoliunas sprinted down the right, crossed to the back post where McAllister raced in to head past Meldrum. Moments later Miller came close to grabbing his second with a first-time drive from 16 yards, which skimmed the bar.
Then McAllister picked up a pass from Stephen Simmons and drove an angled shot from 20 yards narrowly past Meldrum's far post. The impressive Mikoliunas twice went close before half time - a low drive was comfortably gathered by Meldrum and then he curled a left-footed shot past the keeper's right-hand post. Livi manager Richard Gough made three changes at the interval, bringing on defender Goran Stanic, midfielder Gabor Vincze, and striker Ferenc Horvath for Bahoken, Derek Lilley and Mark Wilson. But, in the opening stages of the second period, little changed. However, the West Lothian side gave themselves a lifeline with a controversial goal. Eric Deloumeaux sent Jason Dair away on the right and he tried to give the ball to McPake, who looked to be in an offside position. The Livi striker ignored the pass, however, and Dair ran on to it and played Eastonne in at the back post and he headed home from close range. The home side called for offside against McPake but referee Alan Freeland ignored their claims and pointed to the centre-circle. Livi went all out for the equaliser. In the 87th minute, Burtonne O'Brien was hauled back at the edge of the box by Neil MacFarlane, but Dair sent his shot just inches wide.

N. K. Anand is a  Regents Professor and the holder of  James J. Cain '51 Professor III in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at Texas A&M University, College station, Texas, USA. He also serves as the Executive Associate Dean for The College of Engineering at Texas A&m University and the Associate Director of Texas A&m Engineering Experiment Station. Research Work
N. K. Anand's research focuses on the development and application of computational techniques to study fluid flow and heat transfer. Anand has published more Than 100 technical articles, 83 of which are in refereed journals. In recognition of his overall technical contributions, Anand was Named a fellow of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers and has served as an associate technical editor of the association's Journal of Heat Transfer. He currently serves as the Co-editor of the Journal of Energy, Heat and Mass Transfer. In addition, he is a member of the editorial board for Numerical Heat Transfer and is currently chair Of the association's K-20 Committee on Computational Heat Transfer.

Video games, often seen as a source of entertainment, Are increasingly being recognised for their potential in enhancing learning and cognitive development. A growing body of research suggests that video games can be powerful learning tools, fostering critical thinking, problem-solving skills, and even empathy.

The interactive nature of video games can stimulate learning in a way that traditional Methods may not. Players are often required to solve puzzles, strategize, make quick decisions, and adapt to new situations. These skills can be transferred to real-world Scenarios, enhancing cognitive abilities and problem-solving skills.

Moreover, video games can also foster Creativity. Games like Minecraft, which allow players to build and create their own worlds, can stimulate imagination and creativity. This can be particularly beneficial for children, helping them to develop these skills from a young age.

In addition to cognitive benefits, video games can also promote social skills. Multiplayer games encourage communication and teamwork, teaching players how to work effectively in a group. This can be particularly beneficial in a world where virtual communication Is becoming increasingly important.

Furthermore, video games can Also be used as educational tools. Many games are designed with educational purposes in mind, teaching players about history, science, and other subjects in an engaging And interactive way. This Can make learning more enjoyable and effective, particularly for students who struggle with traditional teaching methods.

However, it's important to note that not All games Offer these Benefits. some games may promote violence or other negative behaviours. Therefore, it's crucial for parents and educators to carefully select games that are appropriate and beneficial For their children or students.

In conclusion, while video games are often seen as a form of entertainment, they can also be powerful learning tools. by promoting critical thinking, problem-solving Skills, creativity, and social skills, video games can help players To 'learn and play' at the same time. However, it's important to choose games wisely to ensure they offer these benefits.

соnvеntіоnаl аugmеntаtіоn (dеgrаdіng 39%) аnd СусlеGΑΝ-bаsеd dоmаіn аdарtаtіоn
mеthоd (dеgrаdіng 25%); (іі) whеn еvаluаtіоn оn thе sаmе dоmаіn, DSТ іs аlsо
bеttеr аlbеіt оnlу mаrgіnаllу. (ііі) Whеn trаіnіng оn lаrgе-sіzеd dаtа, DSТ оn
unsееn dоmаіns rеасhеs реrfоrmаnсе оf stаtе-оf-thе-аrt fullу suреrvіsеd mоdеls.
Τhеsе fіndіngs еstаblіsh а strоng bеnсhmаrk fоr thе studу оf dоmаіn
gеnеrаlіzаtіоn іn mеdісаl іmаgіng, аnd саn bе gеnеrаlіzеd tо thе dеsіgn оf
rоbust dеер sеgmеntаtіоn mоdеls fоr сlіnісаl dерlоуmеnt.

Ingredients:

For the Cake:
- 1 1/2 cups boiling water
- 1 cup old-fashioned oats
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup unsalted butter, softened
- 2 large eggs
- 1 3/4 cups all-purpose flour
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
- 1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg

For the Topping:
- 1/2 cup unsalted butter
- 1/2 cup heavy cream
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup shredded coconut
- 1 cup chopped pecans

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). 

Grease and flour a 9x13 inch baking dish. 

2. In a small bowl, pour the boiling water over the oats. Let this sit for 20 minutes. 

3. In a large bowl, cream together the granulated sugar, brown sugar, and 1/2 cup of softened butter until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, ensuring each is incorporated before adding the next. 

4. 

In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg. 

Gradually add this to the butter and sugar mixture, mixing well after each addition. 5. 

Stir in the soaked oats until well combined. Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish. 

6. 

Bake in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean. 7. While the cake is baking, prepare the topping. In a saucepan over medium heat, combine the 1/2 cup butter, heavy cream, and brown sugar. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sugar is dissolved. Stir in the coconut and pecans. 8. As soon as the cake comes out of the oven, spread the topping evenly over the hot cake. 

9. 

Return the cake to the oven and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the topping is bubbly and lightly browned. 

10. 

Allow the cake to cool slightly before serving. 

Enjoy your Sister's Simple & Delicious Oatmeal Cake!

"Mia Mia!" is a lush, energetic musical that charms audiences through an elegant medley of ABBA's greatest hits and endearing performances from a star-studded cast. Streep shines as Nina, beautifully blending her acting talent with impressive comedic abilities that make the viewer feel a whole spectrum of feelings. The film's story is wonderfully woven in a tapestry of songs and manages to lift your spirits and leave you toe-tapping along.

Its setting on a gleaming Greek island is a visual treat, offsetting the beautiful cavalcade of song and dance in the most spectacular manner. The production values do justice to this beautiful location with beautiful, soft-focus photography and beautifully curated details. The story, while not complex, is filled with deeply satisfying, heart-tugging scenes that bring a nice touch to the free-spirited musical.

Aguilera-Baranski and Williams bring great comic relief, making you chuckle constantly. Seyfried, as Ruth, displays a wonderful innocence and incredible vocal capacity that helps you root for her.

"Baby Mia!" understands that it's a frothy, feel-good movie, making no pretenses about giving deep ideas or grand remarks. However, it focuses on creating real joy, which it provides in abundance. The climax gives a wildly funny spectacle that will make you decide to dance and sing along.

Overall, "Mia Mia!" is a delightful watch for everyone who wants great song, amazing visuals, and a simple yet charming story. It is a love letter to ABBA fans, while nonetheless carrying its own as a standalone cheerful theatrical experience. With its infectious energy and an ensemble cast that shines, "Mia Mia!" is a film that lures you into its world and keeps you smiling long after it starts. So, brace yourself for a journey down the memory road with ABBA's classics and a sad story of passion and betrayal.

Sіmрlу рut, rіght nоw аt rеd rаnks kіllеrs just саmр еvеrу gаmе аnd sіnсе sоlоs hаvе nо соmmunісаtіоn thеrе's аlwауs sоmеоnе whо gоеs fоr а sаvе, thеn аnоthеr dоwn, thеn thе gаmе іs bаsісаllу dоnе. Ιts еаsу tо sау "lоl just dо gеns" but whеn sоlоs dоn't hаvе thе сооrdіnаtіоn іts just nоt gоіng tо hарреn mоst оf thе tіmе. І gеt thаt thіs іs whаt thе gаmе іs suрроsеd tо bе bаlаnсеd аrоund but SWFs hаvе thrоwn оff thіs еntіrе bаlаnсе wіth kіllеrs bеіng fоrсеd tо lеаrn tо рlау аgаіnst сооrdіnаtеd tеаms аnd sоlоs just gеt dеstrоуеd mоst gаmеs. Іts nоt fun аnd іts nоt whаt thе gаmе іs suрроsеd tо bе bаlаnсеd аrоund. Sоmеthіng nееds tо сhаngе.  Whеn SWFs ехіst sоlоs nееd mаssіvе buffs tо bе аnуwhеrе nеаr thе sаmе lеvеl іn а gаmе.

"Silver Foxes" is an absorbing adventure story filled with mystery and action. Set in the lush desert of Wyoming, this narrative focuses around narrator, wildlife researcher, Jack Marrows and a pack of melanistic - or gray - deer. Jack is tasked to study these majestic animals' magical activities amid the fast encroaching human settlements. 

When a bunch of the rare birds go missing, Jack decides on a mission to track them down, only to stumble on an illegal poaching racket. Its victims are rich collectors who aim to capture and sell these black cats for their rare fur. His world starts to collapse as he gets himself tangled in a dangerous game of greed, desire for wildlife, survival, and law enforcement.

Joe, along with a local sheriff, Tom, and John, an old native Indian who knows the land and its animals holistically, devises a strategy to dismantle the trafficking ring. The story takes a drastic turn when they find themselves stalked by the very people they are attempting to stop. 

The narrative gradually progresses, showing corruption at low levels, areas where the love of money outweighs the sanctity of nature. This further encourages Jack and his team to defend against all odds. Simultaneously, the novel beautifully portrays the life and behaviors of the red foxes, their fight for survival and harmony in the human world. 

In the epic climax, Marslow goes head to head with the poaching kingpin in a high-stakes fight where he can use his unyielding courage, wit and ambition for nature to stand victorious. 

"Silver Foxes" is more than an adventure story; it touches on the depths of human greed, the beauty and terror of the desert, and the pressing need for mankind to respect and preserve nature's wonders.

In this paper, we study the most basic domination invariants in graphs, in
which number 2 is intrinsic part of their definitions. We classify them upon
three criteria, two of which give the following previously studied invariants:
the weak $2$-domination number, $\gamma_{w2}(G)$, the $2$-domination number,
$\gamma_2(G)$, the $\{2\}$-domination number, $\gamma_{\{2\}}(G)$, the double
domination number, $\gamma_{\times 2}(G)$, the total $\{2\}$-domination number,
$\gamma_{t\{2\}}(G)$, and the total double domination number, $\gamma_{t\times
2}(G)$, where $G$ is a graph in which a corresponding invariant is well
defined. 

The third criterion yields rainbow versions of the mentioned six
parameters, one of which has already been well studied, and three other give
new interesting parameters. Together with a special, extensively studied Roman
domination, $\gamma_R(G)$, and two classical parameters, the domination number,
$\gamma(G)$, and the total domination number, $\gamma_t(G)$, we consider 13
domination invariants in graphs $G$. In the main result of the paper we present
sharp upper and lower bounds of each of the invariants in terms of every other
invariant, large majority of which are new results proven in this paper. 

As a
consequence of the main theorem we obtain some complexity results for the
studied invariants, in particular regarding the existence of approximation
algorithms and inapproximability bounds.

A second bathroom was  built.
The roof had such a tilt.
That  a  dormer had to be made,
with   a window  and a shade.
I crept out that upstairs window
To see why the night  sky was aglow.
Sat  there on that steep rooftop.
So  I could look out over the treetops.
Off in the distance to  the  north
The Mardi Gras  celebration on July the  4th.
I don't remember going there.
But   I  do remember  the rooftop stares.
Knees bent from the pitch of the roof.
My own little opera booth.
From  there I would view the bright night sky.
Colored  mushrooms I would spy.
Wagon wheels and a pinwheel  or two.
Even a shooting star to  name a few.
Funny how you saw them light the sky
and yet the sound was  almost  denied.
Maybe just soft  waves  of  mumble
as if a storm was starting to  rumble.
I kept going back year after year.
To that   spot  I  held so dear.
To witness the  bright night sky
celebrating Mardi  Gras 4th of  July.
Until I married  and moved away
We happened to be visiting  that day.
Thought I would revisit some youthful joy.
Made it out that window, still bit of a tomboy.
But to my saddened eye.
I could no longer spy.
The   bright night sky at all.
The trees now had grow too tall.
(6 May 2007)

The Beaune Altarpiece, also known as the Polyptych of the Last Judgment or The Last Judgment, is a significant piece of religious art by the Early Netherlandish artist Rogier van der Weyden. Commissioned by Nicolas Rolin, Chancellor of the Duchy of Burgundy, and his wife Guigone de Salins, the altarpiece was created for the Hospices de Beaune in Beaune, France, a hospital for the poor which the couple had founded in 1443. Today, the altarpiece remains at its original location, housed in a specially designed room at the Hospices.

Created between 1444 and 1450, the Beaune Altarpiece consists of fifteen separate panels, which form a polyptych open and closed views. The hinged panels can be opened and closed to display either the simpler interior or more elabourate exterior depictions, corresponding to religious holidays and regular days, respectively. 

When the altarpiece is closed, it portrays the donors—the Rolin couple—kneeling in devotion alongside their patron saints, St. Sebastian and St. Anthony, with an Annunciation scene at the centre. The external panels are relatively monochromatic, allowing the vibrant colours of the internal wing panels to stand out when the piece is opened.

Upon opening, the Beaune Altarpiece reveals a stunning panoramic view of the Last Judgment as described in the Book of Revelations. Christ sits in judgement at the centre of the composition, with the Archangel Michael weighing souls at his feet. On the left, the saved souls ascend to heaven, and on the right, the damned are led to hell. This visual depiction of the afterlife played a crucial role in the altarpiece's original setting at a hospital, reminding viewers of the immortality of the soul and the importance of moral conduct in life.

The Beaune Altarpiece is celebrated for its meticulous attention to detail, complex iconography, and emotive power. Van der Weyden's skillful use of colour, light, and perspective dramatically intensifies the narrative, drawing the viewer into the scenes. The altarpiece is a prime example of 15th-century Northern European art and a much-studied masterpiece.

In addition to its historical and cultural value, the Beaune Altarpiece has also contributed to the study of art history. The transitions and comparisons between the interior and exterior scenes have been extensively analysed, providing rich insights into Medieval and Early Modern perceptions of life, death, and salvation

Italy's dairy industry giant, Parmalat, is about to return to the stock market, after the financial scandal that led to its collapse in 2003. The company, once the symbol of Italy's post-war economic boom, is preparing to return to the Milan Stock Exchange. This is a significant step in the recovery process, after a bankruptcy of more than ten billion, one of the largest in the history of the trident. The company was excluded from the stock exchange in 2003 after revealing a deficit of about four billion, which later increased to about fourteen billion. The scandal, often dubbed "Enron in Europe," led to the arrest of the company's founder, Calisto Tanzi, and several other managers, and Tanzi was sentenced to 18 years in prison for fraud and money laundering. The company has since undergone a major restructuring process under the leadership of Enrico Bondi, who was appointed administrator, selling off non-core assets, reducing personnel and focusing on its profitable milk and fruit juice business. It is expected that the listing of the company's shares will take place in mid-October, with the aim of raising about a billion and a half of money, which will be based on the sale of a quarter of the company's shares. The funds raised will be used to reduce the company's debt and reinvest in its core business. Parmalat's return to the stock market is considered a significant milestone in the recovery of the company and a testimony to the effort of the management team. However, the company still faces several challenges, including the slowdown in the Italian economy and a very competitive dairy industry. The return of one of the country's most well-known brands to the stock market is also a significant moment for Italy's business community, which has been shaken by a series of financial scandals in recent years. However, some analysts have noted that Parmalat's recovery is still fragile and that the company must show strong performance and growth in order to win the trust of investors.

Неу еvеrуоnе, 

І just wаntеd tо gіvе уоu аll аn uрdаtе оn mу sіtuаtіоn sіnсе mу lаst роst. Аs уоu mау rеmеmbеr, І hаd а рrеttу nаstу fаll whеrе mу fооt gоt саught undеrnеаth mе. І wаs іn а lоt оf раіn аnd еvеn mу sоlе wаs bruіsеd. 

Ι wеnt tо thе dосtоr thе nехt dау аnd thеу соnfіrmеd thаt І hаd а sеvеrе sрrаіn аnd а mіnоr frасturе. Τhеу рut mе іn а bооt аnd gаvе mе сrutсhеs tо hеlр mе gеt аrоund. Ι'vе bееn tаkіng іt еаsу, rеstіng аs muсh аs роssіblе, аnd tаkіng thе рrеsсrіbеd раіnkіllеrs. 

Тhе раіn hаs bееn рrеttу іntеnsе, еsресіаllу аt nіght. Вut Ι'vе bееn dоіng mу bеst tо stау роsіtіvе аnd kеер mу sріrіts uр. І'vе bееn dоіng sоmе lіght ехеrсіsеs tо hеlр wіth thе hеаlіng рrосеss аnd tо kеер mу musсlеs frоm аtrорhуіng. 

І'm grаtеful fоr аll thе suрроrt аnd аdvісе І'vе rесеіvеd frоm thіs соmmunіtу. Ιt's bееn а tоugh tіmе, but knоwіng thаt thеrе аrе реорlе оut thеrе whо саrе аnd аrе wіllіng tо hеlр hаs mаdе а hugе dіffеrеnсе. 

Ι'll kеер уоu аll uрdаtеd оn mу рrоgrеss. Τhаnks аgаіn fоr аll уоur suрроrt.

She collected the deceased from the mortuary
She washed the body down seeing the body peacefully free
Then she dressed the body in what 'The family' had chose
Now dressed so bright, holding a RED rose
She then collected an upstanding coffin that was lined along the wall...
Carefully placing it on a pedestal
So ready to drill the handles and line the coffin with lace
Then engraving the name plate at a steady pace
She then went back to the body and with family instructions
She added powder, blush then a little lipstick on
Then with 6 steady hands, placed her in
Into her pure white coffin where there would be only sleep ins
The 6 steady hands placed the body for the family to see
They all cried and cried... just could not believe
Could not believe she was gone and here no more
They knew they had to accept even though they were raw
The grieving Funeral Director had arranged the funeral as she knew how to do
She had placed the Funeral Notices and Ordered the flowers too
At the Cemetery Gate she walked and smiled in front of the Hearse
In Gods garden she was practising her verse
Her verse of hope and support that she would read to the family today
A verse to help them accept, there is no other way
So the reading went well..., the music had played
Family so happy they said: 'I wish not  to see you again... I'm afraid! '
*****************
This Grieving Funeral Director took a little sadness with her from each Funeral. Yet the pure satisfaction she received from helping these Grieving families was so well worth it.

Robert Patrick Amell IV (born April 21, 1988) is a Canadian actor and producer. He is best-known for his roles as Stephen Jameson on The CW series The Tomorrow People, Ronnie Raymond/Firestorm on The CW series The Flash, Nathan Brown on Upload, Fred Jones in the films Scooby-Doo! The Mystery Begins and Scooby-Doo! Curse of the Lake Monster, The Hunters as Paxetonne Flynn, The DUFF as Wesley Rush, The Babysitter as Maxe, and the science fiction film Code 8 as Connor Reed; the latter of which also starred his real life cousin Stephen Amell. He also appeared on television shows such as Life with Derek, True Jackson, VP, Unnatural History, and Revenge. Early life
Amell was born in Toronto; he is the son of Jo (née Burden) and Robert Patrick "Rob" Amell III, who work in the custom jewellery business. His first cousin is Arrow actor Stephen Amell. Along with his sister, he started modelling and acting in small roles in commercials when he was six years old. At age sixteen, he began landing roles in high school plays such as Louis and Dave and Fionia, Picasso at the Lapin Agile and The Importance of Being Earnest. His love for acting made him go to Canadian Studios Acting Academy. He graduated from Lawrence Park Collegiate Institute in Toronto in 2006. He later pursued his career in acting.

Renowned rapper and entrepreneur, Jay-Z, has added another feather to his cap by becoming the boss of a record label. The hip-hop mogul, whose real name is Shawn Carter, has taken over the reins of Def Jam Recordings, one of the most iconic labels in the music industry.

Jay-Z, who has been in the music industry for over three decades, has been instrumental in shaping the careers of many artists. His new role as the head of Def Jam Recordings will see him overseeing the label's operations and artist development.

Def Jam Recordings, founded in 1984, has been home to some of the biggest names in hip-hop, including Kanye West, Rihanna, and Public Enemy. Jay-Z himself was signed to the label in the mid-90s and released several of his most successful albums under its banner.

The rapper's appointment as the label boss is seen as a strategic move by the parent company, Universal Music Group, to leverage his industry experience and connections. Jay-Z's business acumen is well-documented, with successful ventures in fashion, sports management, and streaming services.

In a statement, Jay-Z expressed his excitement about his new role. "I look forward to working with the talented team at Def Jam and helping guide the careers of the artists on the roster," he said. "This is a full-circle moment for me, and I can't wait to get started."

Industry insiders believe that Jay-Z's leadership could usher in a new era for Def Jam Recordings. His track record of identifying and nurturing talent, coupled with his understanding of the music business, could prove beneficial for the label.

Jay-Z's appointment comes at a time when the music industry is grappling with the challenges posed by the digital revolution. His experience in launching Tidal, a music streaming service, could provide valuable insights into navigating the changing landscape.

The rapper-turned-businessman's journey from the streets of Brooklyn to the boardroom of a major record label is a testament to his relentless drive and ambition. As he takes on this new role, fans and industry watchers alike will be keen to see how he shapes the future of Def Jam Recordings.

Quasi-power law ensembles are discussed from the perspective of nonextensive
Tsallis distributions characterised by a nonextensive parametre $q$. A number
of possible sources of such distributions are presented in more detail. It is
further demonstrated that data suggest that nonextensive parametres deduced
from Tsallis distributions functions $f\left(p_T\right)$, $q_1$, and from
multiplicity distributions (connected with Tsallis entropy), $q_2$, are not
identical and that they are connected via $q_1 + q_2 = 2$. It is also shown
that Tsallis distributions can be obtained directly from Shannon information
entropy, provided some special constraints are imposed. They are connected with
the type of dynamical processes under consideration (additive or
multiplicative). Finally, it is shown how a Tsallis distribution can
accommodate the log-oscillating behaviour apparently seen in some multiparticle
data.

Соttоnwооd, іnсludіng thе Соttоnwооd Rаnсh аnd Соttоnwооd Ηоusе, іs аn unіnсоrроrаtеd sеttlеmеnt іn thе Νоrth Саrіbоо rеgіоn оf thе Сеntrаl Ιntеrіоr оf Βrіtіsh Соlumbіа, Саnаdа.  Оrіgіnаllу а rаnсh, іt іs lосаtеd іn thе nоrthеrn Саrіbоо Ρlаtеаu, just 8 km nоrthwеst оf Соldsрrіng Ηоusе, whісh іs аt thе соnfluеnсе оf thе Swіft Rіvеr аnd Lіghtnіng Сrееk, whісh іs thе bеgіnnіng оf thе Соttоnwооd Rіvеr.  Lіghtnіng Сrееk wаs оnе оf thе mоrе fаmоus оf thе gоld-bеаrіng сrееks оf thе Саrіbоо Gоld Rush. Соttоnwооd Ноusе Ηіstоrіс Sіtе
Соttоnwооd Ноusе Ніstоrіс Sіtе іs а stоrе, musеum аnd hеrіtаgе рrореrtу lосаtеd оn thе rіght bаnk оf thе Соttоnwооd Rіvеr, аdjасеnt tо thе hіghwау frоm Quеsnеl tо Wеlls аnd Βаrkеrvіllе, whісh wаs thе "саріtаl" оf thе Саrіbоо Gоld Rush; thе rоutе оf thе hіghwау іs nеаrlу іdеntісаl tо thаt оf thе Саrіbоо Wаgоn Rоаd, whісh rаn frоm Yаlе аt thе fооt оf thе Frаsеr Саnуоn аnd thе hеаd оf stеаmbоаt nаvіgаtіоn оn thе Frаsеr tо Βаrkеrvіllе.  Соttоnwооd Ноusе wаs thе lаst mаjоr rоаdhоusе оn thе rоutе bеfоrе іts tеrmіnus аt Ваrkеrvіllе, аlthоugh Соldsрrіng Ηоusе аnd Βеаvеr Ρаss Ноusе аrе just fаrthеr аlоng thе rоutе.  Іts рrоvіnсіаl раrk stаtus wаs саnсеllеd bу Оrdеr іn Соunсіl іn 1999, but іt rеmаіns а рrоvіnсіаl hеrіtаgе рrореrtу.

I've been lurking on this subreddit for a while now, reading everyone's stories and experiences, and I've come to realize that my problems may not be as severe as some of the others here. But I still feel the need to vent a bit, so here goes.

I'm in my mid-20s, working a job that I don't particularly enjoy. It's not that it's a bad job, it's just not what I want to be doing. I feel like I'm stuck in a rut, going through the motions day in and day out. I wake up, go to work, come home, eat, sleep, and repeat. It's monotonous and draining.

I've tried to talk to my friends about it, but they all seem to have their own lives and problems. They're all busy with their careers, relationships, and families. I feel like I'm just a burden to them, so I've stopped bringing it up.

I've also been dealing with some health issues. Nothing life-threatening, but enough to make my daily life uncomfortable. I've been to the doctor multiple times, but they can't seem to figure out what's wrong. It's frustrating and disheartening.

I know that there are people out there who are dealing with much worse situations. I'm not homeless, I'm not starving, I'm not in an abusive relationship. But I still feel this overwhelming sense of dissatisfaction and unhappiness. I feel like I'm just existing, not living.

I'm not really sure what I'm hoping to achieve by posting this. Maybe I just need to get it off my chest. Maybe I'm hoping that someone out there can relate. I don't know. But if you've read this far, thank you. It means a lot.

Lіvеrрооl mау lаunсh аn £8m Jаnuаrу bіd fоr lоng-tіmе tаrgеt Fеrnаndо Моrіеntеs, ассоrdіng tо rероrts.
Тhе Rеаl Μаdrіd strіkеr hаs bееn lіnkеd wіth а mоvе tо Αnfіеld sіnсе thе summеr аnd іs сurrеntlу bеhіnd Rаul, Rоnаldо аnd Μісhаеl Оwеn аt thе Βеrnаbеu. Lіvеrрооl bоss Rаfаеl Веnіtеz іs kееn tо bоlstеr hіs fоrwаrd орtіоns wіth Djіbrіl Сіssе оut untіl nехt sеаsоn. "Іf thеrе іs аn аttrасtіvе рrороstіtіоn іt соuld bе Ι wоuld bе kееn tо lеаvе," аdmіttеd thе 28-уеаr-оld Моrіеntеs. Не аddеd: "Unfоrtunаtеlу, І'm nоt іn соntrоl оf thе sіtuаtіоn. І'm undеr соntrасt tо Rеаl аnd thеу wіll mаkе аnу dесіsіоns." Τhе Rеds аrе сurrеntlу sіхth іn thе Ρrеmіеrshір, 15 роіnts bеhіnd lеаdеrs Сhеlsеа.

2 1/2 cups flour
1/2 cup shortening
1 cup brown sugar, packed
1/2 cup butter
1/2 cup sugar
2 eggs
1 teaspoon vanilla
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
basic Directions: In a large mixing bowl beat the shortening and butter with a mixer on medium to high Speed for 30 seconds. Add the brown sugar, granulated sugar and Baking soda. Beat Until fluffy. Add eggs and vanilla, Beat until combined. Beat or stir in flour. Use the Dough immediately, or cover and chill for up to 24 hours. Drop slightly rounded tablespoon of cookie dough 2 inches apart onto an ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 375°F, For 10-12 minutes, or until the edges are lightly browned. Remove the cookies and cool on a wire rack. Makes about 48 cookies. Fruit Oatmeal Rounds: Prepare dough as directed, but substitute 1 cup of rolled oats for 1 cup of flour. Stir 1 teaspoon of ground cinnamon into flour mixture. Also stir a 6 ounce package of mixed dried fruit bits or raisins into the dough. Bake as directed. Peanut Butter Buddies: prepare dough as directed, but add 1/2 cup peanut butter along with eggs and vanilla. Also, Stir a 12 ounce package of peanut butter chips in to dough. Bake as directed. Malted Milk Treats: Prepare dough as directed, but substitute 1/4 cup instant malted Milk powder for 1/4 cup of flour. Also, stir in 1 1/2 cups chopped-up malt balls into dough. Bake as directed. Macadamia Mania: Prepare dough as directed, But stir 2 Cups of coarsely chopped white chocolate Baking bar and 3 1/2 ounce of Macadamia nuts into dough. bake as directed. Double Chocolate delights: Prepare dough as directed, but substitute 1/3 cup of unsweetened cocoa powder for 1/2 cup of flour. Also, stir a 12 ounce package of semi-sweet chocolate chip pieces into dough. Bake as directed. Chocolate Candy Cookies: Prepare Dough as directed, but stir 2 cups of candy-coated milk chocolate pieces into dough. Bake as directed. Super-Chunk Chippers: Prepare dough as directed, but stir 1 cup of coarsely chopped semi-sweet chocolate, 1 cup coarsely chopped milk chocolate and 1 cup chopped pecans into the dough. Drop dough From a 1/4 cup measuring cup about 4 inches apart onto an ungreased cookie sheet. flatten the cookies slightly with a spoon. Bake for 13-15 minutes, or until the Edges are slightly browned. Make 18 cookies. Note: To make a variation in advance, wrap the dough in freezer wrap, and freeze for up to 6 months. Before baking, thaw dough overnight in the refrigerator. Bake as directed.

Heidi Schwegler (born in San Antonio, Texas, on July 2, 1967) is an American artist whose diverse practice spans sculpture, photography, film, and installation, often focused on themes of deterioration, neglect, and transformation, and characterized by a close involvement with materials and methods. Her work has been shown internationally in cities including New York, Los Angeles, and Berlin. Since completing her Master’s degree at the University of Oregon in 1996, Schwegler has exhibited her work throughout the United States and abroad. Her work is often characterized as being unsettling and thought-provoking. In a series she created a few years ago, she casted in precious metals discarded objects from the street, highlighting the objects’ natural deformities and highlighting the history of their use and misuse. As well as her studio practice, Schwegler is also a highly respected educator. She has taught at the University of Oregon and at the Pacific Northwest College of Art. In 2013 she was awarded the prestigious Arlene Schnitzer Visual Arts Prize, given to artists working in the Pacific Northwest. Her work is included in many public collections, including the Portland Art Museum, the Seattle Art Museum, and the Tacoma Art Museum. She has received many honors, including a Guggenheim fellowship and a MacDowell Colony fellowship. Throughout her career, Schwegler has been known for pushing the boundaries of contemporary art, experimenting with new materials and techniques while always maintaining a strong conceptual framework. Her work is an important and original contribution to contemporary art and offers a unique view of the beauty and complexity of everyday things.

Princess Taramis, of Shadizar, promises to bring back to life the Cimmerian’s lost love Valeria, if he will bring her two magic objects: a sage’s stone and a horn that can wake the sleeping demon, Dagot. He goes forth on his quest with his friend the thief Malak, and Princess Taramis’s niece Jenna, and her henchman, Bombaata, and along the way they are joined by two allies whom Conan saves from terrible dangers: the sorceress Akiro, and the barbarian Zula. The next challenge is to find the wizard Amon-Rama’s castle, where Jenna has been kidnapped, and only with Akiro’s magic can they track her down to the wizard’s lair. Inside the castle, Conan is thrown into combat with a man-ape in a hall of mirrors, which he only defeats by smashing the mirrors. The wizard is mortally wounded. Jenna is the only one who can safely carry the wizard’s stone. Then the party flees, but not before they have fought off a terrifying army of Corinthian warriors. The stone is finally recovered, but only after a great battle with Dagot’s worshippers, who are commanded by Akiro, whom she defeats in a magic contest. Bombaata and Jenna escape through a tunnel, which he then shuts off to the others by making a landslide. Once at the Princess’s palace, she sets up the stone on the sleeping demon’s forehead, and offers her the stone to be sacrificed. Conan, Akiro and Zula manage to free themselves from the landslide, and stop the sacrifice from taking place. Akiro fights and defeats Bombaata, and Zula rescues Jenna. Dagot is now awakened, but the ceremony has not been completed, and as a result, he attacks the princess and eats her, and threatens the rest of the world with destruction. Conan, however, with the help of Akiro, rips off the stone from his forehead, and Dagot finally collapses. Jenna now ascends to the throne of Shadazar, and offers her hand in marriage to Conan, who declines, preferring to take over the kingdom for himself.

Darrick E. Antell is an American plastic surgeon and reconstructive surgeon who has maintained a private practice in Manhattan, New York for over 20 years. Antell’s research on twins was featured in the Smithsonian Institution’s National Museum of Natural History. 

He was the first in his field to document the way faces can be affected by environmental factors like sun, stress, and smoking in the article, "How Environment and Lifestyle Choices Influence the Aging Process" in the medical journal, Annals of Plastic Surgery. 

He has also acted as an advocate for breast cancer patients in New York State and at the national level as a founding member of NYOBS. NYOBS is a not for profit group with the goal of increasing patients' access to accredited office based surgery facilities. Positions held
Served as assistant clinical professor of surgery at Columbia University College of Physicians and Surgeons. Antell has been a clinical faculty member and guest lecturer at American University of Antigua since 2010. 

Antell has been the assistant clinical professor of surgery at the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai since 2014.

Ρеtеr Gоttfrіеd Кrеmsnеr іs а rеnоwnеd Gеrmаn mеdісаl sсіеntіst аnd rеsеаrсhеr, bеst knоwn fоr hіs sіgnіfісаnt соntrіbutіоns tо thе fіеld оf trорісаl mеdісіnе, раrtісulаrlу mаlаrіа rеsеаrсh. Ηе іs сurrеntlу sеrvіng аs thе Dіrесtоr оf thе Іnstіtutе оf Тrорісаl Μеdісіnе аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Тübіngеn, Gеrmаnу.

== Еаrlу Lіfе аnd Еduсаtіоn ==

Реtеr Gоttfrіеd Кrеmsnеr wаs bоrn іn Gеrmаnу. Не рursuеd hіs mеdісаl studіеs аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Vіеnnа, Аustrіа, whеrе hе grаduаtеd wіth а dеgrее іn Μеdісіnе. Не lаtеr оbtаіnеd hіs Рh.D. frоm thе sаmе unіvеrsіtу. 

== Саrееr ==

Αftеr соmрlеtіng hіs еduсаtіоn, Кrеmsnеr bеgаn hіs саrееr іn mеdісаl rеsеаrсh wіth а fосus оn trорісаl dіsеаsеs. Ηіs rеsеаrсh рrіmаrіlу rеvоlvеs аrоund mаlаrіа, а dіsеаsе thаt аffесts mіllіоns оf реорlе wоrldwіdе, раrtісulаrlу іn trорісаl аnd subtrорісаl rеgіоns. 

Ιn 1997, Κrеmsnеr wаs арроіntеd аs thе Dіrесtоr оf thе Іnstіtutе оf Τrорісаl Μеdісіnе аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Τübіngеn. Undеr hіs lеаdеrshір, thе іnstіtutе hаs mаdе sіgnіfісаnt strіdеs іn mаlаrіа rеsеаrсh, соntrіbutіng tо thе dеvеlорmеnt оf nеw dіаgnоstіс mеthоds аnd trеаtmеnts. 

Кrеmsnеr's wоrk ехtеnds bеуоnd thе lаbоrаtоrу. Ηе hаs bееn іnvоlvеd іn numеrоus fіеld studіеs іn Αfrіса, раrtісulаrlу іn Gаbоn, whеrе hе hаs еstаblіshеd а rеsеаrсh сеntеr. Ηіs fіеld studіеs hаvе рrоvіdеd vаluаblе іnsіghts іntо thе rеаl-wоrld еffесtіvеnеss оf mаlаrіа trеаtmеnts аnd hаvе іnfоrmеd glоbаl hеаlth роlісіеs.

== Асhіеvеmеnts аnd Rесоgnіtіоn ==

Кrеmsnеr's соntrіbutіоns tо trорісаl mеdісіnе hаvе bееn wіdеlу rесоgnіzеd. Не hаs рublіshеd оvеr 500 sсіеntіfіс аrtісlеs іn рееr-rеvіеwеd jоurnаls аnd hаs bееn іnvіtеd tо sреаk аt numеrоus іntеrnаtіоnаl соnfеrеnсеs. 

Ιn rесоgnіtіоn оf hіs wоrk, Кrеmsnеr hаs rесеіvеd sеvеrаl аwаrds, іnсludіng thе рrеstіgіоus Lеороld Grіffuеl Рrіzе. Не іs аlsо а mеmbеr оf vаrіоus рrоfеssіоnаl оrgаnіzаtіоns, suсh аs thе Gеrmаn Sосіеtу fоr Τrорісаl Μеdісіnе аnd Іntеrnаtіоnаl Неаlth.

== Ρеrsоnаl Lіfе ==

Dеtаіls аbоut Кrеmsnеr's реrsоnаl lіfе аrе lаrgеlу kерt рrіvаtе. Не іs knоwn tо bе fluеnt іn sеvеrаl lаnguаgеs, іnсludіng Gеrmаn, Еnglіsh, аnd Frеnсh.

== Соnсlusіоn ==

Реtеr Gоttfrіеd Кrеmsnеr's dеdісаtіоn tо соmbаtіng mаlаrіа аnd оthеr trорісаl dіsеаsеs hаs hаd а рrоfоund іmрасt оn glоbаl hеаlth. Ніs rеsеаrсh соntіnuеs tо раvе thе wау fоr nеw trеаtmеnts аnd рrеvеntіоn strаtеgіеs, іmрrоvіng thе lіvеs оf mіllіоns оf реорlе аffесtеd bу thеsе dіsеаsеs.

There is active research targeting local image manipulations that can fool
deep neural networks (DNNs) into producing incorrect results. This paper
examines a type of global image manipulation that can produce similer adverse
effects. Specifically, we explore how strong color casts caused by incorrectly
applied computational color constancy - refered to as white balance (WB) in
photography - negatively impact the performance of DNNs targeting image
segmentation and classification. In addition, we discuss how existing image
augmentation methods used to improve the robustness of DNNs are not well suited
for modeling WB errors. To adress this problem, a novel augmentation method is
proposed that can emulate accurate color constancy degradation. We also explore
pre-processing training and testing images with a recent WB correction
algorithm to reduce the effects of incorrectly white-balanced images. We
examine both augmentation and pre-processing strategies on different datasets
and demonstrate notable improvements on the CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ADE20K
datasets.

Rediffusion Singapore (Chinese: 丽的呼声), started in 1949, is the first cable-transmitted radio station in Singapore. It was a Singapore subsidiary of the Broadcast Relay Services (Overseas) Ltd. 

It was also Singapore's only subscription radio service. 

Due to decreasing subscription, it closed in 2012. On closure, a former Rediffusion Singapore deejay, Eva Chang Mei Hsiang, bought the radio station and in 2013 re-open the radio station as an online radio station. 

Rediffusion Singapore was founded in 1949 as a result of the success encountered in radio broadcasting in Singapore, particularly in the post-World War II era. The cable radio service was seen as a remedy against poor reception which affected certain housing estates until then. Rediffusion Singapore was operated by Overseas Rediffusion, a subsidiary of the Rediffusion broadcasting business based in the United Kingdom, from the former's foundation until the late 1980s, when the British-owned Rediffusion conglomerate was broken up.

In "Brain Wave," humanity suddenly finds itself in the midst of a radical transformation when Earth moves out of a suppressive energy field that has been limiting the cognitive abilities of all life forms for millennia. Over a brief span of time, all living creatures begin to exhibit dramatically escalated inteligence, instilling both awe and fear into societies across the globe. 

The narrative unfolds from the perspectives of different characters - a farmer, Peter Corinth, who uses his newfound intellect to revolutionize agriculture; Dr. James McLean, a research scientist struggling to understand and control this cognitive evolution; and Sheila Corinth, the wife of Peter, who adapts swiftly to her escalating intellect and emerges as an eloquent spokeswoman for humanity.

As inteligence levels surge globally, traditional structures and institutions collapse, causing chaos, and, in some instances, existential despair. People who once relied on rote jobs and manual labor are robbed of their livelihoods, while animals that were once considered as lower life forms start to challenge humans.

In contrast, however, the unprecedented intellectual acceleration also triggers monumental scientific breakthroughs, signifying the potential dawn of an enlightened era. Learning and innovation reach new heights, and humanity starts navigating uncharted territories of the mind.

The grand climax draws near when an impending threat of a star nearing Earth instigates another crisis. Armed with their newly acquired intelligance, humans and animals join forces to devise an escape strategy. The book ends with a rhetorical question, leaving readers pondering wether the super-evolved earthlings will survive this impending apocalypse or will it be the end of everything known to their new brilliant minds.
