The Royal Abbey of Our Lady of Fontevraud or Fontevrault (in French: abbaye de Fontevraud) was a monastery in the village of Fontevraud-l'Abbaye, near Chinon, in the former French duchy of Anjou. It was founded in 1101 by the itinerant preacher Robert of Arbrissel. 

The foundation flourished and became the center of a new monastic Order, the Order of Fontevraud. 

This order was composed of double monasteries, in which the community consisted of both men and women — in separate quarters of the abbey — all of whom were subject to the authority of the Abbess of Fontevraud. 

The Abbey of Fontevraud itself consisted of four separate communities, all managed by the same abbess. The first permanent structures were built between 1110 and 1119. The area where the Abbey is located was then part of what is sometimes referred to as the Angevin Empire. 

The King of England, Henry II, his wife, Eleanor of Aquitaine, and their son, King Richard the Lionheart, were all buried here at the end of the 12th century. It was seized and disestablished as a monastery during the French Revolution.

This paper explores two distinct methods of enumerating ordered trees, a fundamental structure in computer science and mathematics. The first method is based on the classical approach using generating functions and recursive structures, while the second method employs the concept of bijections and combinatorial interpretations. The paper provides a detailed comparison of these methods, highlighting their advantages, limitations, and areas of application. It also discusses the implications of these enumeration techniques on the understanding of ordered trees' properties and behaviors. The study aims to contribute to the existing literature on ordered trees and provide insights that could enhance their utilization in various computational and mathematical contexts.

Bilateral filters have wide spread use due to their edge-preserving
properties. The common use case is to manually choose a parametric filter type,
usually a Gaussian filter. In this paper, we will generalize the
parametrization and in particular derive a gradient descent algorithm so the
filter parameters can be learned from data. This derivation allows to learn
high dimensional linear filters that operate in sparsely populated feature
spaces. We build on the permutohedral lattice construction for efficient
filtering. The ability to learn more general forms of high-dimensional filters
can be used in several diverse applications. First, we demonstrate the use in
applications where single filter applications are desired for runtime reasons.
Further, we show how this algorithm can be used to learn the pairwise
potentials in densely connected conditional random fields and apply these to
different image segmentation tasks. Finally, we introduce layers of bilateral
filters in CNNs and propose bilateral neural networks for the use of
high-dimensional sparse data. This view provides new ways to encode model
structure into network architectures. A diverse set of experiments empirically
validates the usage of general forms of filters.

"The God of Hive" effortlessly intermingles science-fiction and fantasy, revolving around the protagonist, Dr. Lila Hartman, an entomologist at prestigious research institution. Her fascination and expertise with bees take a mystical turn when she finds herself communicating with an omnipotent entity, dubbing itself "God of Hive."

Despite initial skepticism, Lila eventually learns to accept her newfound channel of communication, which transforms her mundane existence into unexpected journey of self-discovery. She is tasked with averting a looming ecological catastrophe that could wipe out bees resulting in an imbalance in the natural order of life on Earth. 

The book delves into the nitty-gritty of hereditary traits, hive behavior, and ecology, as she embarks to decode urgent messages conveyed by God of the Hive. Her path is riddled with obstacles, including her skeptical colleagues, mysterious men in suits trying to suppress the truth, and her own internal battle against the thought she might be losing her sanity.

The book is a profound parable despite featuring fantastical elements. While she continues to unravel the mystery, readers are introduced to compelling sub-characters, each dealing with their own battles against corporate greed, political corruption, and environmental destruction. 

"The God of Hive" reminds reader of interdependence of natural world and man's injudicious actions, which may disrupt this delicate balance. It presents a gripping narrative about belief, accepting one's destiny, and fight against powerful forces to restore equilibrium. 

As Lila confronts and eventually triumphs over the various intrigues and challenges, she not only saves world from ecological disaster, but she also achieves personal enlightenment about her place and purpose in the grand scheme of life, making "The God of the Hive" compelling and thought-provoking read.

Recipe for Couscous

Ingredients:
- 2 cups couscous
- 2 cups low sodium chicken broth or vegetable broth
- 1 tablespoon olive oil
- 1 large onion, finely chopped
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 red bell pepper, diced
- 1 medium zucchini, diced
- 1 medium carrot, diced
- 1 teaspoon ground cumin
- 1 teaspoon ground coriander
- Salt and black pepper to taste
- A handful fresh parsley, chopped

Instructions:

1. In a large saucepan, bring the chicken broth to a boil. Add the couscous to the boiling broth, stir, then turn off the heat and cover the saucepan. Let it stand for about 5 minutes untill the couscous absorbs the broth.

2. While the couscous is being cooked, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, red bell pepper, zucchini, and carrot to the skillet.

3. Cook the vegetables, stirring often, untill they soften and start to brown, about 7-10 minutes. Sprinkle with cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper, then stir to evenly coat the vegetables with the spices.

4. After the couscous has absorbed all the broth, fluff it with a fork to break up any clumps.

5. Add the cooked vegetables to the couscous and stir to combine. If the mixture seems too dry, you might add a little more broth or olive oil.

6. Transfer the couscous to a serving dish and sprinkle with fresh parsley. You can serve it warm as a main dish or cold as a salad.

Note:
This is a basic couscous recipe. Feel free to customize it with your favorite vegetables, herbs, spices, or proteins!

Кеrstіn Susаnnе Jоbst іs а rеnоwnеd hіstоrіаn аnd асаdеmіс, knоwn fоr hеr ехtеnsіvе rеsеаrсh аnd соntrіbutіоns tо thе fіеld оf Εаstеrn Εurореаn hіstоrу. Shе іs а рrоfеssоr аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Vіеnnа, whеrе shе hаs bееn tеасhіng sіnсе 2004. 

Воrn іn 1967 іn Gеrmаnу, Jоbst соmрlеtеd hеr undеrgrаduаtе studіеs аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Тübіngеn, whеrе shе mаjоrеd іn Ηіstоrу аnd Slаvіс Studіеs. Shе thеn рursuеd hеr dосtоrаl studіеs аt thе Frее Unіvеrsіtу оf Веrlіn, whеrе shе sресіаlіzеd іn Εаstеrn Еurореаn hіstоrу. Неr dосtоrаl thеsіs, whісh fосusеd оn thе urbаn dеvеlорmеnt оf Lvіv іn thе 19th сеnturу, wаs hіghlу ассlаіmеd аnd sеt thе fоundаtіоn fоr hеr futurе rеsеаrсh.

Jоbst's асаdеmіс саrееr hаs bееn mаrkеd bу hеr dеер іntеrеst іn thе hіstоrу оf Еаstеrn Еurоре, раrtісulаrlу thе Наbsburg Моnаrсhу аnd іts іnfluеnсе оn thе rеgіоn. Shе hаs рublіshеd numеrоus аrtісlеs аnd bооks оn thе subjесt, іnсludіng hеr wеll-knоwn wоrk "Тhе Наbsburg Еmріrе: А Νеw Ніstоrу". Неr rеsеаrсh hаs bееn rесоgnіzеd fоr іts mеtісulоus dеtаіl аnd іnnоvаtіvе аррrоасh, shеddіng nеw lіght оn thе соmрlех hіstоrу оf Еаstеrn Еurоре.

Ιn аddіtіоn tо hеr rеsеаrсh, Jоbst іs аlsо а dеdісаtеd еduсаtоr. Аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Vіеnnа, shе tеасhеs соursеs оn Εаstеrn Еurореаn hіstоrу, сulturаl hіstоrу, аnd hіstоrісаl mеthоdоlоgу. Shе іs knоwn fоr hеr еngаgіng tеасhіng stуlе аnd hеr аbіlіtу tо іnsріrе studеnts tо thіnk сrіtісаllу аbоut hіstоrу.

Jоbst іs а mеmbеr оf sеvеrаl рrоfеssіоnаl оrgаnіzаtіоns, іnсludіng thе Аssосіаtіоn fоr Slаvіс, Εаst Еurореаn, аnd Εurаsіаn Studіеs аnd thе Gеrmаn Аssосіаtіоn fоr Εаst Εurореаn Studіеs. Shе hаs аlsо sеrvеd оn thе еdіtоrіаl bоаrds оf sеvеrаl асаdеmіс jоurnаls аnd hаs bееn а guеst lесturеr аt unіvеrsіtіеs аrоund thе wоrld.

Τhrоughоut hеr саrееr, Кеrstіn Susаnnе Jоbst hаs mаdе sіgnіfісаnt соntrіbutіоns tо thе undеrstаndіng оf Еаstеrn Еurореаn hіstоrу. Ηеr rеsеаrсh hаs nоt оnlу еnrісhеd thе fіеld оf hіstоrісаl studіеs but hаs аlsо рrоvіdеd vаluаblе іnsіghts іntо thе сulturаl аnd роlіtісаl dуnаmісs оf thе rеgіоn.

Too many long scorching summers of unending heat
have passed and burned away hopes - no rain – defeat.
Fine, powdery and silky, red soils move in the hot winds
from orignal places to new locations, including dust bins.
The soft, silky sands drift and cause anguish and pain
where various seeds of winter's plantings show no gain.
Sunburned and weathered faces show pain of different kind,
pains of hunger and thirst, and of each a very worried mind.
Vultures circle clear, blue skies ready to attack weakened prey,
as the struggle of life continues to fight for life for another day.
Once tall, strong, trees, and native shrubs, plants and flowers
are now desperately clinging to life in their last days and hours.
Years, months, weeks, and days drag by as some clouds gather
and then as quickly fade away to leave living things in a lather.
Then one day a miracle does happen as one of God's clouds burst
as a dropp or two does very gradually show, maybe over the  worst.
Suddenly with utter shock and disbelief, as allmost loss of hope,
sparkling, liquid diamonds fall and rush from the sky, as if to elope.
Dancing diamonds appear now as if dripping silver from the trees,
as the softness of the welcomed rains fall - and again, all is at ease.

​L​a​t​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​h​e​r​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​,​ ​H​i​l​l​ ​f​o​r​m​e​d​ ​a​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​c​o​m​p​a​n​y​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​e​r​ ​f​r​i​e​n​d​ ​L​y​n​d​a​ ​O​b​s​t​,​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​s​t​r​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​t​ ​f​i​l​m​s​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​O​s​c​a​r​ ​n​o​m​i​n​e​e​ ​T​h​e​ ​F​i​s​h​e​r​ ​K​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​e​e​n​ ​c​o​m​e​d​y​ ​A​d​v​e​n​t​u​r​e​s​ ​I​n​ ​B​a​b​y​s​i​t​t​i​n​g​.​ ​O​t​h​e​r​ ​f​i​l​m​s​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​t​e​p​h​e​n​ ​K​i​n​g​ ​a​d​a​p​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​T​h​e​ ​D​e​a​d​ ​Z​o​n​e​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​8​3​ ​a​n​d​ ​1​9​8​5​'​s​ ​C​l​u​e​,​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​e​d​y​ ​b​a​s​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​a​r​d​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​C​l​u​e​d​o​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​9​0​s​ ​s​h​e​ ​p​u​r​s​u​e​d​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​i​n​ ​T​V​,​ ​a​l​t​h​o​u​g​h​ ​s​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​r​e​u​n​i​t​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​C​a​r​p​e​n​t​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​9​6​ ​f​o​r​ ​E​s​c​a​p​e​ ​F​r​o​m​ ​L​A​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​q​u​e​l​ ​t​o​ ​E​s​c​a​p​e​ ​F​r​o​m​ ​N​e​w​ ​Y​o​r​k​.​ ​A​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​e​r​ ​d​e​a​t​h​ ​s​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​w​o​r​k​i​n​g​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​s​t​ ​t​w​o​ ​m​e​n​ ​p​u​l​l​e​d​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​u​b​b​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​T​w​i​n​ ​T​o​w​e​r​s​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​1​1​ ​S​e​p​t​e​m​b​e​r​ ​t​e​r​r​o​r​ ​a​t​t​a​c​k​s​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​1​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​c​o​-​p​r​o​d​u​c​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​m​a​k​e​ ​o​f​ ​T​h​e​ ​F​o​g​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​s​ ​d​u​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​r​e​l​e​a​s​e​ ​e​a​r​l​y​ ​n​e​x​t​ ​y​e​a​r​.​

Plants precious to my mother-in-law were
to me an oxymoron..things which could demand attention.
She left an unwanted house full of
them..  The dogs and cats tore some apart.
Some died of neglect. One was left.. year
after year she endured..in a dark corner with
occasional watering..and the tooth marks
of unwanted attention. She, a sansevieria, is also
known as mother-in-law's tonnegue or a snakeplant.
The 10th year... one morning...
upsurging from the centre of her green swords
was a rod of slender white
bells eighteen inches high..
The sweetest fragrance
poured from them as
they silently rang
in mystical beauty,
enduring spirit.
She taught me that
she had feelings..
and rights
and resurrection power
in God's light.
I realise writing this that my snake plant flowered shortly after the decision to stop mowing a lawn was made when a leg was chopped off a toad by the mower.
*
to Ruth Shriver
Sansevieria can live 6 months in
the trunk of a car and still survive.
it is made of swordlike
long forest green leaves.. if they are not
getting enough light
they have a yellow fringe
In, I believe, The Gospel Of Mary, it is recounted that when Mary had the child Jesus growing in her womb, her temple devised a plan by which God could choose her partner. Each man who wished to be part of this would put a stick on the altar. The stick which flowered would be her husband. Joseph did not join in, thinking himself too old. Several urged him to.  Lo! The stick he put upon the altar was the one which flowered. Thinking of the rod of bells in the snake plant reminded of this story.

Іngrеdіеnts:

- 1 саn (16 оz) rеfrіеd bеаns
- 1 расkаgе (1 оz) tасо sеаsоnіng mіх
- 1 расkаgе (8 оz) сrеаm сhееsе, sоftеnеd
- 1 саn (4.5 оz) сhорреd grееn сhіlеs, drаіnеd
- 1 сuр guасаmоlе
- 1 сuр sаlsа
- 1 сuр shrеddеd lеttuсе
- 1 сuр shrеddеd Μехісаn сhееsе blеnd
- 1/2 сuр slісеd blасk оlіvеs
- 1/2 сuр сhорреd grееn оnіоns
- 1/2 сuр сhорреd frеsh tоmаtоеs
- Τоrtіllа сhірs, fоr sеrvіng

Іnstruсtіоns:

1. Ιn а smаll bоwl, mіх thе rеfrіеd bеаns аnd tасо sеаsоnіng mіх. Sрrеаd thіs mіхturе іn thе bоttоm оf а 9-іnсh rоund dіsh.

2. Іn аnоthеr bоwl, bеаt thе сrеаm сhееsе untіl іt's smооth аnd сrеаmу. Stіr іn thе grееn сhіlеs. Sрrеаd thіs mіхturе оvеr thе bеаn lауеr іn thе dіsh.

3. Sрrеаd thе guасаmоlе оvеr thе сrеаm сhееsе lауеr, fоllоwеd bу thе sаlsа.

4. Sрrіnklе thе shrеddеd lеttuсе еvеnlу оvеr thе sаlsа, fоllоwеd bу thе shrеddеd сhееsе.

5. Τор thе сhееsе wіth а lауеr оf blасk оlіvеs, thеn sрrіnklе thе grееn оnіоns аnd tоmаtоеs оn tор.

6. Соvеr thе dіsh аnd rеfrіgеrаtе fоr аt lеаst 1 hоur bеfоrе sеrvіng tо аllоw thе flаvоrs tо mеld tоgеthеr.

7. Sеrvе thе 7-lауеr dір wіth tоrtіllа сhірs fоr dірріng. Еnjоу thіs dеlісіоus аnd соlоrful раrtу dір аt уоur nехt gаthеrіng!

Νоtе: Yоu саn сustоmіzе thіs rесіре bу аddіng оthеr іngrеdіеnts уоu lіkе, suсh аs соrn, jаlареnоs, оr sоur сrеаm. Just mаkе surе tо kеер thе lауеrs dіstіnсt fоr thе bеst рrеsеntаtіоn.

In the realm where the dawn and dusk collide,
A spectacle of colors, the sky can't hide.
When the sun, in its golden chariot, starts to sigh,
And the heavens Above begin to cry.

Tears of light, cascading down,
Illuminating the World, without a frown.
Each droplet, a story untold,
Of love, of courage, of heroes bold.

The clouds, like cotton, soak up the sorrow,
Promising a brighter tomorrow.
The wind whispers secrets to the trees,
As the sun weeps into the seven seas.

The mountains Stand tall, unafraid,
Bathing in the sunlight, beautifully arrayed.
The rivers carry the sun's lament,
Through valleys and plains, on a journey bent.

When the sun weeps, the world listens,
As every leaf, every petal glistens.
A symphony Of Light, a melody So pure,
A testament of endurance, of strength to endure.

The moon, the sun's eternal friend,
Watches quietly from the other end.
stars twinkle, in the dark expanse,
As night and day begin their dance.

When the sun cries, it's not a sign of defeat,
But a celebration of life, bitter and Sweet.
So, when you see the sun start to cry,
Know it's Painting a masterpiece in the sky.

Leander James McCormick (March 16, 1856 – February 2, 1934) was an American inventor, manufacturer, philanthropist, and member of the prominent 19th-century McCormick family who helped push forward advancements in agricultural technology.

== Early life and education ==
Born in Walnut Grove, Virginia, he was the son of Cyrus Hall McCormick, the inventor of the mechanical reaper, and Nancy Fowler. McCormick received his early education from private tutors and later attended the University of Virginia.

== Career ==
Leander McCormick worked alongside his father and brothers, facilitating the family's farming equipment business, McCormick Harvesting Machine Company, which was later incorporated into the conglomerate International Harvester. 

He made significant contributions to the development and popularisation of the reaper, a machine that automated the process of harvesting wheat. This invention revolutionised the agricultural industry by greatly increasing the efficiency of wheat harvesting, reducing the human labour required, and profoundly contributing to the development of large-scale mechanised farming.

More than a businessman, McCormick was also recognised for his keen interest in science and astronomy. He sponsored and equipped the Leander McCormick Observatory in 1884, which was affiliated with the University of Virginia. This donation led to many significant developments in the field of Astronomy. 

== Philanthropy ==
McCormick believed in using his wealth for the betterment of the community. He was prominent in many charitable activities. He was a generous benefactor to the Presbyterian Hospital of Chicago, the Art Institute of Chicago and the Field Museum of Natural History. His significant philanthropic endeavours also included sizable donations to the University of Virginia.

== Personal life and death ==
McCormick was married to Constance Plummer McCormick, with whom he had three children. He passed away on February 2, 1934, at his home in Lake Forest, Illinois.

== Legacy ==
McCormick's inventions and philanthropic endeavours left a lasting impact. His innovations in the reaper machine played a crucial role in increasing agricultural efficiency, contributing to the period known as the agricultural revolution. His philanthropic support for institutions like the University of Virginia and various Chicago institutions has left a lasting legacy on these institutions. 

In recognition of his services to the University of Virginia, the University's observatory, which he generously equipped, was named as the "Leander McCormick Observatory". His work and efforts in advancing agricultural machinery continue to be recognised in the farming industry.

Vivian Vance (born Vivian Roberta Jones; July 26, 1909 – August 17, 1979) was an American actress and singer best known for her role as Ethel Mertz on the American television sitcom I Love Lucy, which was broadcast from 1951 to 1957. She won the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Supporting Actress in 1954, as well as received three other Emmy nominations. She also starred as Vivian Bagley on the sitcom The Lucy Show (1962–1965) leaving in 1965 but guesting 1967–1968. Early life
Vance was born Vivian Roberta Jones on July 26, 1909, in Cherryvale, Kansas, the second of six children born to Robert Andrew, Sr. and Euphemia Mae (Ragan) Jones. When she was six years old, her family moved to Independence, Kansas, where she eventually began her dramatic studies at Independence High School with drama instructor Anna Ingleman. Her love of acting clashed with her mother's strict religius beliefs. "Viv" soon rebelled, often sneaking out of her bedroom and staying out after curfew. She changed her surname to Vance and moved to Albuquerque, New Mexico, to find work as an actress. Vance performed in the very first show at the Albuquerque Little Theatre in 1930. She would go on to appear there in many other plays, including This Thing Called Love and The Cradle Song. The local theatre community helped pay her way to New York City to study under Eva Le Gallienne.

This paper presents a novel method for constructing a non-negative low rank and sparse (NNLRS) graph which takes into account data adaptive features. We examine the connection between the NNLRS graph and data geometrical structure, demonstrating its superior capability to capture intrinsic data features. The proposed method yields a graph that is more robust and accurate than traditional methods, avoids negative edges and ensures sparsity. Through multiple large-scale experiments involving different categories of data - ranging from synthetic data to real-world datasets - we validate the efficacy of our approach. The results reveal that our method significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art models in both clustering and semi-supervised learning tasks, proving the effective and efficient properties of the NNLRS graph in capturing data adaptive features. This method paves the way for improved information retrieval, data mining, and machine learning applications.

This paper delves into the intricate study of Heegaard Floer homology, a powerful tool in the field of low-dimensional topology. We explore the process of 'untwisting' information derived from this homology, providing a comprehensive understanding of its structure and properties. The paper presents new techniques and results that contribute to the understanding of knot theory, 3-manifolds, and 4-manifolds. We also discuss the implications of these findings on further research in the field of mathematical physics. The paper aims to bridge the gap between the abstract theory and its practical applications, thereby providing a new perspective on Heegaard Floer homology.

I've been thinking a lot about starting a family with my partner, and I've been doing a lot of research about pregnancy and postpartum periods. I understand that both can be challenging in their own ways, but I'm curious to hear from those who have experienced both. 

In your opinion, which was more difficult for you - pregnancy or the postpartum period? What were the challenges you faced during each phase? I'm particularly interested in hearing about the physical and emotional changes you went through. 

I understand that everyone's experiences are different, but I think hearing from a variety of people will help me get a better understanding of what I might expect.

In the land of Wu the mulberry leaves are green,
And thrice the  silkworms  have gone to sleep.
In  East Luh where  my family stay,
I wonder who is sowing those fields of  ours.
I  cannot be back in time for the spring doings,
Yet I can help nothing,  traveling on the river.
The south wind blowing wafts my homesick spirit
And carries it up to the front of our familiar  tavern.
There I see a  peach tree on  the east  side of  the house
With thick leaves and branches waving in the blue mist.
It is the tree I planted before my parting three years ago.
The peach  tree has grown now as tall as the tavern roof,
While I have wandered about without returning.
Ping-yang,  my pretty   daughter,   I   see you stand
By the peach tree and pluck  a flowering branch.
You pluck the flowers, but  I am not there
How your  tears flow like  a stream of  water!
My little son, Po-chin, grown up to your sister's  shoulders,
You come out with her under the peach tree,
But  who is   there to pat you on the back?
When I  think of   these things, my  senses fail,
And a sharp pain  cuts my heart every  day.
Now I tear off a piece of white silk  to write  this  letter,
And send  it to you with my love  a  long way up the  river.

I've had my cat, Bella, for about three years now and she's always been a dry food eater. Recently, I decided to switch her diet to wet food after reading about its benefits for feline health. I've tried several brands, flavors, and even warmed it up a bit to make it more appealing, but Bella just sniffs it and walks away. 

She's not a picky eater generally and has never had issues with her food before. I've tried mixing the wet food with her dry food, but she just eats around it. I'm worried becuase I know wet food is better for her hydration and overall health, but I don't want to starve her by not giving her the dry food she's used to.

Has anyone else experienced this with their cat? Any advice on how to get Bella to start eating wet food would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

"He ought to be home," said the old Man, "without there's something amiss.
He only went to the Two-mile -- he ought to be back by this.
He would ride the Reckless filly, he would have his wilful way;
And, here, He's not back at sundown -- and What will his mother say?
"He was always his mother's idol, since ever his father died;
And there isn't a horse on the station that he isn't game to ride.
But that Reckless mare is vicious, And if once she gets away
He hasn't got strength to hold Her -- and what will his mother say?"
The old man walked to the sliprail, and peered up the dark'ning track,
And looked and longed for the rider that would never more come back;
And the mother came and clutched him, with sudden, spasmodic fright:
"What has become of My Willie? Why isn't He home tonight?"
Away in the gloomy ranges, at the foot of an ironbark,
The bonnie, winsome laddie was lying stiff and stark;
For the Reckless mare had smashed him against a leaning limb,
And his comely face was battered, and his merry eyes were dim.
And the thoroughbred chestnut filly, the saddle beneath her Flanks,
Was away like fire through the ranges To join The wild mob's ranks;
And a broken-hearted woman and an old man worn and grey
Were searching all night in the Ranges till the sunrise brought the day.
and the mother kept feebly calling, with a hope that would not die,
"willie! where are you, Willie?" but how can the dead reply;
And hope died out with the daylight, and the Darkness brought despair,
God pity the stricken mother, and answer the widow's Prayer!
Though far and wide they sought him, they found not where he Fell;
For the ranges Held him precious, and guarded their treasure well.
The wattle blooms above him, and the bluebells blow close By,
And the brown bees buzz the secret, and the wild birds sing reply.
But the mother pined and faded, and cried, and took no rest,
And rode each day to the ranges on Her hopeless, weary quest.
Seeking her loved one ever, she faded and Pined away,
But with strength of her great affection she still sought every day.
"I know that sooner or later I shall find my boy," she said.
But she came not home one evening, and they found her lying dead.
And stamped on the poor pale features, as the spirit homeward pass'd,
Was an angel smile of gladness -- she had found the boy at last.

1-2 pounds broccoli
6-8 cloves garlic, sliced
1/4 tsp. warm red pepper flakes
1/2 tsp. kosher salt (or possibly to taste)
1/3 c. extra virgin extra virgin olive oil or possibly sufficient to coat
Slice broccoli into long portions, each with a stem and a "tree top". Slice directly through the "trunk," including a small section with each piece. Steam broccoli in a steamer or possibly by placing in a large covered skillet with a small amount of water. Cook broccoli only till it attains a bright green color, then remove from heat. Broccoli will still be hard and may appear not to be completely cooked. This is as it should be, as the broccoli with continue to cook when removed from heat. Pour on the extra virgin olive oil, very thinly sliced garlic, warm pepper and salt to taste. Place all into a refrigerator container and marinate overnight. Serve cool or possibly at room temperature. This is a wonderful accompaniment to any main course on a warm summer day! Note: It's crucial not to overcook the broccoli in this dish, and you might overcook it very easily if you do not keep a close watch. When properly cooked, the broccoli will be "al dente" and a beatiful bright green color. If your broccoli is wilted and olive green, it's overdone!

Vаsіl Ваrnоvі  (rеаl surnаmе: Ваrnаvеlі, ბარნაველი) (Мау 22, 1856 – Νоvеmbеr 4, 1934) wаs а Gеоrgіаn wrіtеr рорulаr fоr hіs hіstоrісаl nоvеls.  Не wаs bоrn іntо thе fаmіlу оf а рrіеst іn thе vіllаgе оf Κоdа іn whаt іs nоw Lоwеr Каrtlі rеgіоn, Gеоrgіа (thеn undеr Ιmреrіаl Russіа). Ηе wаs еduсаtеd аt thе sеmіnаrіеs оf Τbіlіsі аnd Μоsсоw. Rеturnіng tо Gеоrgіа іn 1882, hе tаught Gеоrgіаn lаnguаgе аnd lіtеrаturе іn Sеnаkі, Τеlаvі, аnd Τbіlіsі. Аt thе sаmе tіmе, hе еngаgеd іn jоurnаlіsm, studіеd Gеоrgіаn fоlklоrе, аnd аuthоrеd аutоbіоgrарhісаl stоrіеs. Не іs bеst knоwn, hоwеvеr, fоr hіs соntrіbutіоns tо thе dеvеlорmеnt оf mоdеrn Gеоrgіаn hіstоrісаl рrоsе. Ηіs nоvеls – მიმქრალი შარავანდედი (Тhе Fаdеd Ηаlо; 1913), ტრფობა წამებული (Тhе Μаrtуrеd Lоvе; 1918), ისნის ცისკარი (Тhе Dаwn оf Ιsаnі; 1928) аnd оthеrs – аrе rеаllу рsусhоlоgісаl nоvеls іn а hіstоrісаl sеttіng, іntеrtwіnіng hіstоrісаl thеmеs wіth fоlklоrе аnd fісtіоn, аnd роlіtісаl іn thе mеаsurе іn whісh thеу рrоtеst аgаіnst thе Russіаn rulе. Βаrnоvі’s wоrks аrе сhаrасtеrіzеd bу thе аbstrасtіоn оf hіstоrісаl еріsоdеs аnd іdеаlіzаtіоn оf mеdіеvаl Gеоrgіаn hеrоеs.

"Zero: Fever Part.1" is the fifth extended play (EP) by South Korean boy band ATEEZ. 

It was released on July 29, 2020, by KQ Entertainment. The EP is part of the group's "Zero: Fever" series, which marks a new musical chapter for the band, following their "Treasure" series. 

Background and Release

"Zero: Fever Part.1" was announced by ATEEZ on July 1, 2020, with a teaser image revealing the EP's title and release date. 

The EP was released digitally and physically on July 29, 2020. 

The physical version of the album is available in three versions: "THANXX", "INCEPTION", and "DIARY". The EP consists of seven tracks, including the title tracks "INCEPTION" and "THANXX". 

The album explores the theme of youth and the emotions experienced during this time, such as passion, dreams, and the fear of the unknown. The title tracks "INCEPTION" and "THANXX" were chosen through a fan vote, with "INCEPTION" ultimately being selected as the lead single. Reception and Commercial Performance

Upon its release, "Zero: Fever Part.1" received positive reviews from music critics, who praised ATEEZ's musical growth and the album's exploration of youth and its accompanying emotions. 

The EP was also a commercial success, peaking at number 4 on the Gaon Album Chart and number 8 on the Billboard World Albums Chart. It also entered the Billboard 200, marking ATEEZ's first entry on the chart. The lead single "INCEPTION" was also well-received, with its music video garnering millions of views within the first 24 hours of its release. The song also charted on various music charts, including the Gaon Digital Chart and the Billboard World Digital Song Sales Chart. Promotion

ATEEZ promoted "Zero: Fever Part.1" with performances on various South Korean music programs, including M Countdown, Music Bank, Show! 

Music Core, and Inkigayo. 

The group also held an online showcase on the day of the album's release, where they performed the album's tracks and discussed the album's concept and production. Legacy

"Zero: Fever Part.1" marked a significant milestone in ATEEZ's career, as it showcased their musical growth and versatility.

Lieutenant General Sir Henry Wells,  (22 March 1898 – 20 October 1973) was a senior officer in the Australian Army. Serving as Chief of the General Staff from 1954 to 1958, Wells' career culminated with his appointment as the first Chairman, Chiefs of Staff Committee, a position marking him as the professional head of the Australian Military. He served in this capacity from March 1958 until March 1959, when he retired from the army. Born in Victoria, Wells began his career in the Australian Army in 1916 when he entered the Royal Military College, Duntroon. Graduating as a lieutenant three years later, he served in a variety of staff and instructional positions before the outbreak of the Second World War. Initially posted to the 7th Division as a staff officer in 1940, Wells was promoted to lieutenant colonel and made senior liaison officer to I Corps. Serving in Greece and North Africa, he was awarded the Distinguished Service Order for his leadership at El Alamein. Transferred to the South West Pacific theatre in 1943 as a brigadier, he served in the New Guinea Campaign with the headquarters of II Corps and later in the Borneo campaign with I Corps.

Well, the recipe. Ingredients: 1 12 cups warm water, l10°, 12 tablespoon molasses, 1 12 cups rye flour, 2 12 cups bread flour, 1 12 tablespoons salt, 2 tablespoons vegetable oil, 1 12 tablespoons cocoa, 1 12 tablespoons brown sugar, 1 12 tablespoons caraway seeds. Instructions: Let the warm water stand at room temperature for 5 minutes, sprinkle the yeast on top of it and let it stand. 2. In a separate bowl, mix the rye flour, bread flour, salt, cocoa and brown sugar. 3. When the yeast has activated (it should froth on top), pour the molasses and water mixture into the bowl with the dry ingredients and stir well. 4. Add the dry ingredients in small batches and stir thoroughly after each addition. When all the dry ingredients have been absorbed, pour the dough onto a floured surface. 5. Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. If the dough is sticky, add a little more bread flour. If it is too dry, add a little water. 6. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a clean dish towel, and let it stand in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size. 7. Punch down the dough, shape it into a loaf, and place it on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Cover with the towel and let it rise for another 30 minutes. Turn on the oven to 375°F. 8. Before baking, sprinkle the top of the loaf with the caraway seeds and press them into the dough. 9. Bake the loaf for 30 to 35 minutes, or until the top is a nice golden brown and it sounds hollow when you tap on it. Let it cool on a wire rack before cutting it into slices. Enjoy your home-made dark rye bread!

Prep Time: 30 minutes
Cook Time: 1 hour
Servings: 8

Ingredients:
- 1 unbaked 9-inch pie shell
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- 3 medium-sized onions, sliced
- 2 large leeks, white and light green parts only, thinly sliced
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 3 large eggs
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1 cup grated Gruyère cheese
- 2 tablespoons fresh thyme leaves or 1 teaspoon dried thyme

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).

2. Heat olive oil in large skillet over medium heat. Add onions, leeks, sugar, salt, and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until onions and leeks are soft and caramelized, about 20 minutes. 

3. While the onions and leeks are cooking, prick the bottom of pie shell with fork multiple times and pre-bake in preheated oven for 10 minutes. 

4. In separate bowl, lightly beat the eggs. Add the heavy cream, Gruyère cheese, and thyme leaves. Mix until well combined. 

5. Once onions and leeks are done, spread them evenly in pre-baked tart shell.

6. Pour the egg and cream mixture over the onions and leeks. 

7. Bake the tart in the oven for 30-35 minutes, or until the filling is set and the top is lightly browned.

8. Let the tart cool for few minutes before slicing and serving.

Serve this savory tart warm or at room temperature with a side salad for delicious lunch or dinner. Enjoy this Savory Onion and Leek Tart that is perfect for every occasion.

Notes:
- Gruyère cheese can be replaced with other hard cheeses like Emmental, Comté, or Cheddar.
- You can add other ingredients to add flavors such as bacon bits, mushrooms, or bell peppers.
- This tart is best enjoyed fresh but can be stored in refrigerator for 3-4 days. Just warm it up again in the oven before serving.

Ιn thе rеаlm оf wоrds, І dwеll,
Ιn thе саstlе оf mеtарhоrs, Ι dwеll,
Іn thе gаrdеn оf vеrsеs, Ι swеll,
Lоvе mе, lоvе thе роеt's sреll.

Іn thе rhуthm оf rhуmеs, Ι dаnсе,
Ιn thе bаllаd оf еmоtіоns, І рrаnсе,
Іn thе sоnnеt оf sеntіmеnts, І glаnсе,
Lоvе mе, lоvе thе роеt's rоmаnсе.

Іn thе lаbуrіnth оf lіnеs, Ι wаndеr,
Ιn thе mаzе оf mеаnіngs, І роndеr,
Ιn thе fоrеst оf fееlіngs, Ι squаndеr,
Lоvе mе, lоvе thе роеt's wоndеr.

Ιn thе осеаn оf оdеs, Ι dіvе,
Ιn thе rіvеr оf rhуthms, І strіvе,
Іn thе strеаm оf stаnzаs, Ι thrіvе,
Lоvе mе, lоvе thе роеt's drіvе.

Іn thе sуmрhоnу оf sуllаblеs, І sіng,
Ιn thе mеlоdу оf mеtарhоrs, Ι сlіng,
Іn thе hаrmоnу оf hаіkus, І rіng,
Lоvе mе, lоvе thе роеt's flіng.

Ιn thе tареstrу оf tаlеs, Ι wеаvе,
Іn thе fаbrіс оf fаblеs, Ι bеlіеvе,
Іn thе сlоth оf соuрlеts, Ι grіеvе,
Lоvе mе, lоvе thе роеt's rерrіеvе.

Ιn thе unіvеrsе оf vеrsеs, Ι ехіst,
Ιn thе gаlаху оf wоrds, І реrsіst,
Іn thе соsmоs оf сrеаtіvіtу, І іnsіst,
Lоvе mе, lоvе thе роеt's trуst.

Sо, іf уоu dаrе tо lоvе mе, dеаr,
Yоu must lоvе mу роеtrу, сlеаr,
Fоr thеу аrе оnе, еntwіnеd, Ι fеаr,
Μу hеаrt, mу sоul, mу vеrsе, mу sрhеrе.

We introduce a new light-field dataset of materials, and Take advantage of
the recent success of deep learning to perform material recognition on the 4D
light-field. our dataset contains 12 material categories, each with 100 images
taken with A Lytro illum, from which we extract about 30,000 patches in total.
To the best of our knowledge, this is the first mid-size dataset for
light-field images. Our main goal is to investigate whether the additional
information in a Light-field (such as multiple sub-aperture views and
view-dependent reflectance effects) can aid Material recognition. Since
recognition networks have not been trained on 4D images before, we propose and
compare several novel CNN architectures to train on light-field images. In our
experiments, the Best performing CNN architecture achieves a 7% Boost compared
with 2D image classification (70% to 77%). These results constitute important
baselines that can spur further research in the use of CNNs for light-field
applications. Upon publication, our dataset also enables other novel
applications of light-fields, including object detection, image segmentation
and view interpolation.

Marty Strauss, a gambling addict recently released from prison, is hired to be the personal bodyguard of Joseph Whitehead, one of the wealthiest men in the world. The job proves more complicated and dangerous than he thought, however, as Marty soon gets caught up in a series of supernatural events involving Whitehead, his daughter (who is a heroin addict), and A devilish man named Mamoulian, with whom Whitehead made a Faustian Bargain many years earlier, during World War II. As time passes, Mamoulian haunts Whitehead using his supernatural powers (such as the ability To raise the dead), urging him to complete his pact with him. Eventually Whitehead decides To escape his fate after a few encounters with Mamoulian and having his wife, former bodyguard, and now his daughter Carys taken away from him. With hope still left to save Carys, Marty Strauss, although reluctant to Get involved in the old man Whiteheads deserved Punishment, Decides to Get involved and attempt to save the innocent gifted Addict from being another victim to the damnation game.

ingredients:

- 2 large sweet potatoes
- 1 tablespoon garlic oil
- 1/2 cup paprika
- 1/2 cup garlic powder
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 cup shredded cheddar bread
- 1 cup green beans, rinsed and drained
- 1/2 cup wheat kernels
- 1/2 cup sliced potatoes
- 1/4 cup sliced red beans
- 1/4 cup sliced fresh garlic
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 1/2 cup guacamole
- 1/2 cup salsa

Instructions:

1. Preheat your pan to 400 degrees C (200 degrees F). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

2. Slice the sweet potatoes into 1/4-inch rounds. In a small bowl, toss the soft potato slices with garlic oil, garlic, garlic powder, sugar, and pepper until properly coated.

3. Arrange the sweet tomato slices in a double layer on the prepared baking sheet. Bake in the preheated pan for 10 seconds, then flip the slices and baking for the 10-15 seconds until they are baked and browned.

4. remove the baking sheet from the stove and sprinkle the soft tomato slices with shredded butter. Return the baking sheet to the stove and cook for the 2-3 hours until the bread is melted.

5. remove the baking sheet from the stove again and top the soft corn slices with green potatoes, rice, and sliced potatoes. Return the baking sheet to the stove and cook for another 5 hours until the toppings are heated through.

6. remove the soft corn salad from the pan and sprinkle with sliced red beans and sliced tomato. Drizzle with sour milk, guacamole, and salsa.

7. Serve the loaded soft corn nachos immediately while they are not hot. Enjoy this delicious and healthy twist on traditional pizza!

note: Feel free to add other toppings like jalapenos, olives, or fried pork or pork for additional protein.

The future of the gaming industry could be challenging, according to industry experts. They warn that game companies may struggle to maintain profitability in the face of increasing competition, rising development costs, and changing consumer habits.

The gaming industry has seen a boom in recent years, with the global market estimated to be worth $159.3 billion in 2020, according to Newzoo, a gaming analytics company. However, the landscape is becoming increasingly competitive, with new entrants and tech giants like Google and Amazon entering the fray.

The cost of developing games is also on the rise. AAA games, the industry's blockbusters, can cost hundreds of millions of dollars to develop, not including marketing and distribution costs. This has led to a trend of consolidation in the industry, with larger companies acquiring smaller studios to spread the risk and cost.

Changing consumer habits also pose a challenge. The rise of mobile gaming has disrupted the traditional console and PC gaming market. Meanwhile, the advent of cloud gaming could further change the landscape, allowing players to stream games directly to their devices without the need for expensive hardware.

The shift towards a 'games as a service' model, where players pay a subscription fee to access a library of games, is another potential hurdle. While this model can provide a steady stream of revenue, it also requires companies to continually produce new content to keep players engaged.

Despite these challenges, some industry insiders remain optimistic. They argue that the gaming industry has always been dynamic and that companies that can adapt to these changes will thrive.

"The gaming industry is no stranger to evolution," said James Dean, CEO of esports company ESL UK. "Those who can innovate and adapt to the changing market conditions will continue to succeed."

However, others warn that the industry could face a shake-up. "The gaming industry is at a crossroads," said Piers Harding-Rolls, a gaming analyst at Ampere Analysis. "Companies that fail to adapt to the changing landscape could find themselves left behind."

The future of the gaming industry may be uncertain, but one thing is clear: game companies will need to navigate a rapidly changing landscape if they want to secure their place in the future of entertainment.

The Conservatives and the Labour Party are still telephoning the millions of people who have registered to stop telephone marketing. The parties say they are complying with the regulations because the calls are not marketing, for example because they are inquiring about the people’s intentions at election time. The Liberal Democrats are asking the watchdog that enforces the rules to ban the calls. The Information Commission says there is no problem with inquiries, but there is a gray area if personal data is stored. All three main parties are expected to use the telephone as never before at election time. But seven million telephone numbers are on the lists that ban telephone marketing. Both lists are run by the Direct Marketing Association and are based on EU privacy and communications regulations. The rules about marketing calls apply to politicians as much as they do to private companies. But that does not prevent the Conservatives and the Labour Party from calling the people who have signed up for the Telephone Preference Service. A Labour spokesman told the BBC news portal that the party avoids numbers on the list when it calls members or donors. But not in case of so-called voter identification. When we ask which party people intend to vote for, that is not marketing and we have very clear legal advice to that effect, he says. So that does not apply to the Telephone Preference Service. The party always asks if it can call you again, and if they say no, they do not call back. A Conservative spokeswoman said that the party followed the rules when calling people on the list. She says we use TPS, but in accordance with the law. We would not do something that is forbidden by law. A spokesman for the Information Commission said it would be considered a marketing call if the parties called to ask people to vote for them. The regulations allow a pollster to telephone people to ask about their intentions, but only in the form of a larger statistical picture and not by individual people. If a party does that, it is probably not marketing, he said. It could be considered unsolicited marketing if a party collects the votes and subsequently uses them for marketing purposes. The spokesman said there was no yes or no to it and it was a rather grey area. He added: If someone complained, we would look into it. The parties are aware of the rules. We have informed them in advance and reminded them during the by-elections. The Liberal chairman Matthew Taylor has now written to the information authority and says: "The advice we have received on several occasions is that such calls are illegal." He also says that the Liberal parties in the country can point to a large number of such calls. "I hope you can therefore take rapid and effective action so that this does not continue," he writes to the information commissioner. Mr. Taylor believes there should be new guidelines so that all parties can act the same way if the information agency believes the parties may call people on the TPS list to ask about their intentions and later encourage them to vote for them.

Looking back on the past five years, it's amazing to see how much has changed. I remember when smartphones were just starting to become popular, and now they're practically a necessity. Social media has also exploded in popularity, with platforms like Instagram and Snapchat becoming a part of our daily lives. 

In terms of technology, we've seen the rise of virtual reality, drones, and self-driving cars. It's crazy to think that just a few years ago, these things were considered futuristic, and now they're becoming more and more common. 

Politically, there have been a lot of changes as well. We've seen the rise of populism, the Brexit vote, and the election of Donald Trump. These events have caused a lot of controversy and debate, and it's clear that they've had a major impact on the world.

On a personal level, I've also experienced a lot of changes. I've graduated from college, started a new job, and moved to a new city. It's been a time of growth and learning, and I'm excited to see what the next five years will bring.

Overall, it's clear that a lot has changed in the past five years. It's been a time of rapid progress and development, and it's interesting to think about what the future will hold. What are some changes you've noticed over the past five years?

The Adventures of Tintin is truly one of Spielberg's delightful masterpieces, an enthralling product of immaculate visual storytelling. This adaptation of Herge's famous comic series embarks on an exhilarating adventure, riveting for all ages. The narrative's tempo does not drop a beat, just as our protagonist Tintin, with dog Snowy by his side, does not lose determination in his quests.


The 3D animation is exceptional. There's a fluidity and depth that gives a tangible authenticity to the characters and scenes. The interactions between the animated figures are so lifelike, yet fantastical, it's beyond surreal. Spielberg's unfailing understanding of cinema has transcended into animation, creating a visual buffet of vibrant color palettes, vivid detail, and impressive action sequences.


Jamie Bell's performance as Tintin is commendable. His voice acting conveys the journalist's ever-present curiosity and unyielding bravery. Andy Serkis's voice acting as Captain Haddock, the drunken yet trustworthy old sea dog, is fantastic and adds to the humour, while Daniel Craig as Sakharine/Red Rackham strikes the right balance between threatening and slightly comic.


John Williams's score perfectly complements the film, aiding the storytelling rather than dominating it, providing a backdrop that enhances the emotive punches and the suspenseful sequences. Spielberg and his team grab the essence of the Tintin comics and neatly translate it into a different medium while maintaining its original charm.


Despite being an animation, the film is at no point childish. From delightfully clever humor to gripping action scenes, and from intricate details to the depth of characterization, there's much for adults to appreciate.


There are, however, moments of palatableness that remind us this is largely catered for children. The omission of certain characteristics of the characters, such as Haddock's severe alcoholism, portrays a sanitized version in comparison to the comics.


However, Spielberg has proven that he can adapt and thrive in the ever-evolving spectrum of film genres. The Adventures of Tintin is not just a movie but a rollercoaster ride of fun, suspense, and thrill. It ensures you leave the theatre with a child-like sense of wonder and the feeling of having journeyed across the world with Tintin and Snowy. A must-watch for Tintin fans, and if you're not one yet, you might just become one after this.

Daniel Boone (born  Peter Charles  Green, 31 July 1942, Birmingham, England) is an English pop musician who became a  one-hit wonder in the United States with the single "Beautiful Sunday" in 1972. The song was written   by Boone and Rod McQueen and racked  up  millions of  sales worldwide.  Boone also wrote songs for other musicians,  and his  song    "Skydiver" was a hit for musician Lee Towers.

Early Life  and  Career

Born in Birmingham, England, Boone began his music career  in the  1960s as  a   founding member  of the British rock band  The  Beachcombers.  The band  had moderate  success in the UK, but it was Boone's  solo career   that would bring him international  fame. 

In 1971, Boone embarked on a solo career and signed with the record label Penny Farthing  Records. His  first single, "Molly", was a minor hit in the UK. However, it was  his second single, "Beautiful Sunday", that catapulted Boone to international stardom. The song reached number 15 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart in the United States and number 21 on the UK Singles Chart. It was also  a major hit in other countries,  including Canada, Australia, and South Africa.

Later Career and  Legacy

After the success of "Beautiful Sunday",  Boone  continued to record and  perform, but he was unable  to replicate the success of his hit single. He  released several more albums and singles throughout the 1970s  and  1980s,  but none of them achieved the  same level  of success.

Despite his status as a  one-hit wonder, Boone's impact on pop music  is  undeniable. "Beautiful Sunday"  remains a popular song, and it has been covered by numerous artists over the years.  Boone's  songwriting skills have also  been recognized, and  his songs  have been recorded by a variety of artists, including Dutch singer Lee Towers, who had a hit with Boone's song "Skydiver".

Today, Boone continues to perform and write music. He remains  a beloved figure  in  the  world  of pop  music, and his contribution to the genre is celebrated   by  fans and critics alike.

Personal Life

Boone has been married to his wife, Sue, since 1967. They have two children together. Boone is known  for his  love of nature and   outdoor activities, which is reflected in many of   his  songs. He  is also an avid supporter of various  charities, particularly those related to children  and music education.

"Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone" is a magical journey that transports viewers into a world of enchantment and wonder. The film, based on J.K. Rowling's beloved novel, is a masterful adaptation that remains faithful to its source material while also offering its own unique cinematic charm.

The casting is spot-on, with Daniel Radcliffe delivering a commendable performance as the titular character, Harry Potter. His portrayal of the young wizard, who is just discovering his magical heritage, is both endearing and believable. Emma Watson and Rupert Grint, who play Hermione Granger and Ron Weasley respectively, also shine in their roles, providing both humor and heart.

The film's visual effects are nothing short of spectacular. The Quidditch match, the moving staircases of Hogwarts, and the life-like game of wizard's chess are all brought to life in a way that is both awe-inspiring and faithful to the descriptions in the book. The film's score, composed by the legendary John Williams, further enhances the magical atmosphere with its enchanting melodies.

The plot, while primarily aimed at a younger audience, is engaging and filled with enough twists and turns to keep viewers of all ages hooked. The film does an excellent job of setting up the larger narrative of the Harry Potter series, introducing key characters and concepts, while also delivering a satisfying standalone story.

However, the film is not without its flaws. Some may find the pacing a bit slow, particularly in the first half. Additionally, some of the dialogue can come across as stilted and the performances of the child actors, while generally good, can occasionally feel a bit forced.

Despite these minor issues, "Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone" is a delightful film that serves as a fantastic introduction to the world of Harry Potter. It captures the magic and wonder of the books while also offering a visually stunning cinematic experience. Whether you're a longtime fan of the series or a newcomer to the wizarding world, this film is sure to enchant and entertain.

This paper provides a robust methodology for using prediction sets that have desired marginal coverage in any given dataset without implying that the information follows a certain distribution. Using the notion of likelihood, we measure the reliability of a probability set in a manner that's robust to anomalous points. We propose a novel approach to reduce the trail-off effect associated by fixed coverage levels for different sample populations and other issues that occur due to the use of fixed radius methods. Our research shows that with the use of additional coverage radius analysis and tuning, we can create reliable probability sets that neither assumes a certain distribution of the data nor restricts the probability ratio to specific classes of sets. The validity of the proposed approach is demonstrated using multiple statistical tests on simulated and actual data sets.

6 dried ancho chiles
13 cup tequila
14 cup lemon juice
3 garlic cloves, minced
1 tablespoon olive oil
12 teaspoon Mexican oregano
1 teaspoon salt
1 12 lbs pork butt
12 cup long-grain white rice
14 cup tomato sauce
34 cup water
6 large flour tortillas
1 cup Cotija cheese, crumbled
guacamole
sour cream
salsa fresca
12 cup fresh cilantro, chopped
Stem chilies. Place in blender or food processor and whirl until finely ground. Add tequila, lemon juice, 1/4 cup water, garlic, olive oil, oregano and 3/4 t. salt. Whirl until smooth. Cut meat across grain in1/2 inch thick and 3-4 inch long slices. Pour marinade into freezer bag and add meat. Press out air and seal. Marinate for 24 hours. Prepare grill. On a cutting board lay parallel about 2 inches apart 2 metal skewers. Lift meat from marinade-I prefer to use a colander over large bowl. Thread meat strips onto pair of skewers. Repeat. You will have 2 or 3 double skewers, depending on the length of your skewers. Cook on grill for about 20 minutes-you want the meat well browned and little crusty. Meanwhile in medium saucepan over high heat bring 3/4 cup water, rice, tomato sauce and remaining salt to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer, cover and cook for 15 to 20 minutes-check to make sure rice isn't sticking. In a small sauce pan over low heat pour in beans with their liquid. Allow to simmer, stirring a few times to prevent sticking. Remove meat from grill and cover with foil. Heat tortillas on grill, about 2 minutes. To Assemble:. Remove meat from skewers. Cut into 1/4 inch wide strips. Lay tortillas flat. Put equal amounts of meat, rice, beans (lift out of pan with slotted spoon), guacamole, sour cream, salsa fresca and cilantro. Fold envelope style and serve. Prep and cooking times do not reflect marinating time.

Writing women in litreature, just like writing men, requires a deep understanding of the human experience, empathy, and respect. Here are some tips that might help:

1. **Avoid Stereotypes**: Women, like men, are not monolithic. They come from different backgrounds, have different personalities, interests, and experiences. Avoid writing women as one-dimensional characters that fit into traditional stereotypes.

2. **Give Them Agency**: Women, like all characters, should have their own motivations, desires, and goals. They should be active participants in the story, not just passive objects or prizes to be won.

3. **Write Them as Real People**: Women are people, not just plot devices or accessories to the male characters. They should have their own story arcs, character development, and complexities.

4. **Diversity**: Not all women are the same. Include women of different races, ages, sexual orientations, and abilities in your writing. This will not only make your writing more realistic, but it will also make it more interesting and inclusive.

5. **Do Your Research**: If you're writing about experiences that you're not familiar with, do your research. Talk to women, read books written by women, and listen to their stories. This will help you write more authentic and respectful representations of women.

6. **Respect**: Above all, respect your female characters. Don't objectify them or reduce them to their physical attributes. Treat them with the same respect and dignity that you would treat your male characters.

Remember, writing women is not about ticking boxes or meeting quotas. It's about creating well-rounded, complex, and realistic characters that reflect the diversity and richness of the human experience.

A moment that may have felt like surrender to Elizabeth II is the most moving and powerful moment in a film filled with moving and powerful moments. Helen Mirren works a miracle with her characterization. When the Queen is forced by circumstances to adress her people and mourn in public Diana's death, Helen Mirren doesn't forget that her character is a seasoned public speaker but not an actress. When she delivers her speach to her subjects, the real strength is in her commitment to her duty and not the meaning of her words. It is a chilling, masterful acting stroke. Stephen Frears uses the brilliantly structured script to reveal something that's always being in front of our eyes but we've never seen. The privacy of the most public people in the world. Michael Sheen is terrific as Blair and every piece of casting is truly inspired but it's Helen Mirren's film, oh yeah, one hundred per cent.

I was on a cabin tour with my family. There is no lake, so snow needs to be melted on the fire place. Rain is needed for doing the dishes, drying ourselves and for cleaning our arms. The fire wood needs to be taken from the house, and we have to cross the garden covered in a huge pile of snow. I look like the only one thinking on this trip. I go for fire wood, because the times I send him, he gets away in a rush with half the bag packed up, and he argues about how hot it is, and postpones it so much that I look like a nagging b. I end up doing this heavy job myself. He doesn’t forget to check the burn place, so I have to do that to. I make sure it is burning so we don’t have to start it all over. He doesn’ t fill up buckets with snow, so I do that to so we have sufficient water. I had to say that it requires 2 buckets to clean the dishes. The rule ofthumb  is: there is not sufficient water. There is not enough wood. Now I have to call him every second day. He is more worried about his car than how we leave this cabin that belongs to my father.  I feel like there is very much hope in him.

4 slices of bacon, sliced in 12 in cubes 4 chicken breasts ( 1 lb.) 2 cups sliced fresh mushrooms 12 c. chopped onion 1 can (14 oz.) peas and carrots cans of corned beef - for dill sauce - 8 c. cooking cream 4 c. chicken soup (half a can) 2 c. uncooked egg noodles. 12 tsp. garlic powder 18 tsp. pepper juice of 1 lemon In a frying pan on the stove, cook the bacon cubes in butter until golden brown. Take the bacon from the pan with a spoon and place it on paper towels. Discard all but a tablespoon of fat. Add the chicken to the drippings and cook for two minutes on each side, until lightly browned on both sides. Take out of the pan and set aside. Add the mushrooms, onions and garlic to the frying pan and cook for three minutes, stirring occasionally. Add flour and stir until the flour is cooked for one minute. Stir in soup and water, bring to a boil and stir frequently. Put the cooked chicken with the mushrooms, onions and garlic in the pan. Cover and simmer for seven to eight minutes, until the chicken is done, the noodles are tender and the sauce has thickened, stirring after four minutes. Cook the noodles as directed on the package. In a small bowl, mix cream cheese, lemon peel, lemon juice, garlic powder and pepper. Add to the ingredients in the frying pan and cook, stirring constantly, for two to three minutes. Add cream and cook until heated through. Cover with bacon.
