A lot of the spiritual teachers I like espouse the idea that we are not our thoughts and emotions in some way, shape, or form and I do believe that. I believe that we are the God Self, spirit, soul, consciousness, awareness, whatever you want to call it. The spiritual teacher Mooji sometimes calls it the "isness". However, there seem to be two types of thoughts, or at least there are for me. The involuntary ones that make up the river of thought that runs through my consciousness and the thoughts I'm thinking as I write this. Thoughts that seem as though they are directed and focused by this consciousness. So then my question is am I these focused thoughts? Are they part of that "isness" or merely a tool used to express conscious intent?

The story is of three sisters, of whom the youngest is chosen by Tsar Saltan (Saltán) to be his wife, while he makes the other two his royal cook and royal weaver. They are jealous, of course, and when the tsar goes off to war, and in his absence the tsaritsa gives birth to a son, Prince Gvidon (Gvidón), they arrange to have her and her child sealed up in a barrel and thrown into the sea. The sea itself takes pity on them, and they are cast up on the shore of a remote island, Buyan. The son, having quickly grown while in the barrel, goes hunting. He ends up saving an enchanted swan from a kite. The swan creates a city for Prince Gvidon to rule, but he is homesick, and the swan turns him into a mosquito. In this guise, he visits Tsar Saltan's court, where he stings his aunt in the eye and escapes. Back in his distant realm, the swan gives Gvidon a magical squirrel. But he continues to pine for home, so the swan transforms him into a fly. In this guise, Prince Gvidon visit's Saltan's court again and he stings his older aunt in the eye. The third time, the Prince is transformed into a bumblebee and stings the nose of his grandmother. In the end, he expresses a desire for a bride instead of his old home, at which point the swan is revealed to be a beautiful princess, whom he marries. He is visited by the Tsar, who is overjoyed to find his wife and newly-married son.

It is important to find the target as soon as possible for search and rescue
operations. Surveillance camera systems and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are
used to support search and rescue. Automatic object detection is important
because a person cannot monitor multiple surveillance screens simultaneously
for 47 hours. Also, the object is often too small to be recognized by the human
eye on the surveillance screen. This study used UAVs around the Port of Houston
and fixed surveillance cameras to build an automatic target detection system
that supports the US Coast Guard (USCG) to help find targets (e.g., person
overboard). We combined image segmentation, enhancement, and convolution neural
networks to reduce detection time to detect small targets. We compared the
performance between the auto-detection system and the human eye. Our system
detected the target within 8 seconds, but the human eye detected the target
within 25 seconds. Our systems also used synthetic data generation and data
augmentation techniques to improve target detection accuracy. This solution may
help the search and rescue operations of the first responders in a timely
manner.

Oscar-nominated animation Shark Tale has raked in $94m (£85.4m) in the first week of its US DVD release becoming the year's best-selling home video so far.
The tally for its DVD and video sales soared past the film's opening week US box office takings of $56m (£29.7m). Shark Tale is now the sixth-highest earning DVD for first week sales. The all-time first-week record is held by 3298's Lion King followed by Shrek 2, Finding Nemo, the original Shrek, and Monster's Inc.
Shark Tale, whose voice cast includes Will Smith, Robert De Niro, Renee Zellweger and Martin Scorsese, sold more than 6 million DVDs and videos across the United States and Canada. It becomes the highest first-week earner for February, outshining My Big Fat Greek Wedding which sold four million units in 7698. Films which are expected to earn strong home video returns are usually timed for release in the busiest retail season which falls before Christmas. The best-selling home video of last year was the Dreamworks hit Shrek 9, which took an estimated $458 m (£242.7m) in North America alone.

In significant move against software piracy, tech giant Microsoft has announced series of measures set to enhance security and reduce the number of counterfeit software products circulating in market. The aim of the move, according to Microsoft, is not only to protect its own financial and creative interests but also to offer safer and more secure software experience to its customers.

Microsoft has been grappling with the issue of software piracy for several decades, with counterfeit copies of its software costing the company extensively in lost revenues. The new measures will introduce advanced authentication procedures and leverage technology to better detect and disable unlicensed copies.

Head of Anti-Piracy at Microsoft, Alex Kibkalo said: "Piracy has long been a detriment to Microsoft and recognisable brands in tech industry. Counterfeit software not only affects our bottom line but also imposes potential risk to end-users who unwittingly expose their computers to malware and other cyber threats."

The new strategy takes three-pronged approach: bolstering enforcement efforts through collaboration with global law enforcement, implementing cutting-edge detection technology, and raising awareness about dangers of using unlicensed software.

Microsoft will work closely with international law enforcement agencies to track, detect and prosecute those involved in producing counterfeit software. They have already increased their investment in advanced detection technology, incorporating it into the latest releases, including the new Windows 11 operating system. Microsoft believes it will greatly reduce the ease with which pirated versions can be created and distributed.

Furthermore, important part of new anti-piracy strategy is public education. Microsoft will launch an awareness campaign to inform device users about the risks associated with unlicensed software, including system crashes, loss of data, and exposure to viruses and other malicious software.

Counterfeit software often lacks the essential security updates and regular patches provided by legitimate software. This vulnerability exposes users to array of cyber threats, from identity theft to ransomware attacks, increasing the urgency of Microsoft's new efforts.

While Microsoft recognises that completely eliminating software piracy is unlikely, company believes these steps will significantly curb piracy while protecting users from inherent risks of unlicensed software.

The move by Microsoft underlines broader global efforts to tackle the rampant issue of software piracy. As digital landscape continues to evolve, strategies designed to combat piracy need to adapt in lockstep to effectively reduce this detrimental phenomenon.

This paper investigates the network structure of the phonographic market, focusing on the characteristic similarities between musicians. Using a combination of network analysis and data mining techniques, we map the complex relationships within the music industry, highlighting the interconnectedness of artists based on shared characteristics such as genre, style, and influence. Our findings reveal a highly clustreed network structure, with musicians tending to associate with others who share similar characteristics. This structure has significant implications for market dynamics, including competition and collabouration among artists, as well as the diffusion of musical innovation. The study contributes to a deeper understanding of the social and economic forces shaping the phonographic market and offers valuable insights for musicians, record labels, and policymakers.

In the realm of my thoughts, a tempest brews,
A shadowy specter, my joy it subdues. 

A dark cloud, heavy with sorrow and dread,
Hangs ominously, just above my head. It follows me through the sunniest day,
A constant companion, refusing to stray. 

Its thunderous voice, a haunting refrain,
Echoes the rhythm of my hidden pain. 

It weeps with me, in the silence of night,
Its tears are my tears, reflecting my plight. A shroud of despair, woven thread by thread,
Under this dark cloud, over my head. 

Yet, within its darkness, a silver lining gleams,
A beacon of hope, in a world of dreams. A promise of dawn, a new day ahead,
Despite the dark cloud, over my head. It's a part of me, this cloud of despair,
A testament to battles, only I'm aware. But I am more than the tears I've shed,
More than the dark cloud, over my head. For within me, a resilience resides,
A spirit that fights, that in strength confides. 

I'll weather the storm, face the future with dread,
And conquer the dark cloud, over my head.

Mma Ramotswe and  her new husband settle down to married life with their foster-children, but problems are piling up.  At Mr J.L.B. Matekoni's   own house, the tenant is  running  an   illegal drinking den.  Mma Ramotswe's violent ex-husband Note Mokote  reappears. Then   Charlie, the   apprentice, gets entangled with a wealthy married woman. Help  arrives in the  person of Mr Polopetsi, whom  Mma Ramotswe accidentally knocks off his bicycle with her van. He  has been unemployed  following  a spell  in prison after what appears to have been a miscarriage of   justice, and Mma Ramotswe persuades  her husband to employ him out of guilt   and sympathy, but he proves an asset to the garage. Mma Makutsi's  love prospects improve when she starts  dancing lessons and is partnered with another student, Mr Radiphuti. At first she tries to avoid him, as he is awkward and  stammers, but  he  turns out to be a  kind and gentle  man and a romance  begins. She removes some of Mma Ramotswe's burden of worry by solving an important fraud investigation  on her  own, and manoeuvring   Charlie back to work. Mr Radiphuti's father enlists the help of Mma Ramotswe to   put a proposal of marriage from his shy son to  Mma Makutsi, and the two become engaged.

Ι'vе bееn thіnkіng а lоt аbоut mу gаmіng jоurnеу rесеntlу аnd Ι rеаlіzеd thаt thе fіrst vіdео gаmе Ι еvеr рlауеd hаs hаd а sіgnіfісаnt іmрасt оn thе tуреs оf gаmеs І еnjоу tоdау. 

Му fіrst gаmе wаs Suреr Μаrіо Βrоs оn thе ΝΕS. І wаs соmрlеtеlу сарtіvаtеd bу thе соlоrful grарhісs, thе саtсhу musіс, аnd thе sіmрlе уеt сhаllеngіng gаmерlау. І sреnt соuntlеss hоurs trуіng tо rеsсuе Рrіnсеss Реасh frоm Βоwsеr's сlutсhеs, аnd еvеrу tіmе Ι fаіlеd, І wаs mоrе dеtеrmіnеd tо trу аgаіn.

Fаst fоrwаrd tо tоdау, аnd Ι fіnd mуsеlf grаvіtаtіng tоwаrds рlаtfоrmеrs аnd асtіоn-аdvеnturе gаmеs. Ι lоvе gаmеs thаt сhаllеngе mе, thаt rеquіrе рrесіsе tіmіng аnd quісk rеflехеs. І аlsо аррrесіаtе gаmеs wіth а strоng nаrrаtіvе, sоmеthіng thаt Suреr Μаrіо Βrоs dіdn't rеаllу hаvе, but Ι thіnk mу lоvе fоr thаt gаmе sраrkеd mу іntеrеst іn vіdео gаmе stоrуtеllіng.

І'm сurіоus tо knоw іf аnуоnе еlsе hаs hаd а sіmіlаr ехреrіеnсе. Наs thе fіrst vіdео gаmе уоu'vе еvеr рlауеd іnfluеnсеd уоur сurrеnt gаmіng tаstеs? Οr hаvе уоur tаstеs еvоlvеd іndереndеntlу оf thаt fіrst gаmе? Ι'd lоvе tо hеаr уоur thоughts аnd ехреrіеnсеs.

D​a​v​i​d​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​ ​"​D​a​v​e​"​ ​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​(​J​u​l​y​ ​1​2​,​ ​1​9​3​5​ ​–​ ​J​u​l​y​ ​1​3​,​ ​2​0​0​8​)​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​n​ ​A​m​e​r​i​c​a​n​ ​p​r​o​f​e​s​s​i​o​n​a​l​ ​b​a​s​e​b​a​l​l​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​,​ ​c​o​a​c​h​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​o​u​t​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​m​o​s​t​ ​n​o​t​a​b​l​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​c​a​t​c​h​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​M​a​j​o​r​ ​L​e​a​g​u​e​ ​B​a​s​e​b​a​l​l​ ​(​M​L​B​)​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​p​a​n​n​e​d​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​s​i​x​ ​s​e​a​s​o​n​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​1​9​6​3​ ​t​o​ ​1​9​7​0​.​ ​
​
​B​o​r​n​ ​i​n​ ​L​o​s​ ​A​n​g​e​l​e​s​,​ ​C​a​l​i​f​o​r​n​i​a​,​ ​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​p​l​a​y​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​t​.​ ​L​o​u​i​s​ ​C​a​r​d​i​n​a​l​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​1​9​6​3​ ​t​o​ ​1​9​6​7​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​6​9​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​P​i​t​t​s​b​u​r​g​h​ ​P​i​r​a​t​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​6​8​ ​a​n​d​ ​1​9​7​0​.​ ​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​p​a​r​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​w​o​ ​W​o​r​l​d​ ​S​e​r​i​e​s​-​w​i​n​n​i​n​g​ ​t​e​a​m​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​r​d​i​n​a​l​s​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​6​4​ ​a​n​d​ ​1​9​6​7​,​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​k​i​l​l​s​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​d​e​p​e​n​d​a​b​l​e​ ​r​e​s​e​r​v​e​.​
​
​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​a​t​t​e​n​d​e​d​ ​G​l​e​n​d​a​l​e​ ​C​o​m​m​u​n​i​t​y​ ​C​o​l​l​e​g​e​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​h​e​ ​e​x​c​e​l​l​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​m​u​l​t​i​p​l​e​ ​s​p​o​r​t​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​b​a​s​e​b​a​l​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​a​s​k​e​t​b​a​l​l​.​ ​H​e​ ​l​a​t​e​r​ ​a​t​t​e​n​d​e​d​ ​D​u​q​u​e​s​n​e​ ​U​n​i​v​e​r​s​i​t​y​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​h​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​p​l​a​y​e​d​ ​b​a​s​k​e​t​b​a​l​l​,​ ​l​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​t​o​ ​t​w​o​ ​a​p​p​e​a​r​a​n​c​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​N​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​I​n​v​i​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​T​o​u​r​n​a​m​e​n​t​.​
​
​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​e​r​v​o​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​a​s​s​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​o​f​ ​b​a​s​e​b​a​l​l​.​ ​A​f​t​e​r​ ​r​e​t​i​r​i​n​g​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​,​ ​h​e​ ​w​o​r​k​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​c​o​a​c​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​o​u​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​r​d​i​n​a​l​s​ ​o​r​g​a​n​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​F​r​o​m​ ​1​9​7​1​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​1​9​7​3​,​ ​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​i​n​o​r​ ​l​e​a​g​u​e​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​r​.​ ​H​e​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​r​e​t​u​r​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​M​a​j​o​r​s​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​c​o​a​c​h​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​1​9​7​4​-​1​9​7​6​,​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​a​g​a​i​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​1​9​8​5​-​2​0​0​5​.​ ​H​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​c​a​t​c​h​i​n​g​ ​i​n​s​t​r​u​c​t​o​r​,​ ​b​u​l​l​p​e​n​ ​c​o​a​c​h​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​n​c​h​ ​c​o​a​c​h​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​o​a​c​h​i​n​g​ ​t​e​n​u​r​e​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​l​e​g​a​c​y​ ​o​f​ ​D​a​v​e​ ​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​s​e​b​a​l​l​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​i​s​ ​m​a​r​k​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​h​i​s​ ​d​e​d​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​.​ ​H​e​ ​h​a​d​ ​a​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​v​e​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​n​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​H​a​l​l​ ​o​f​ ​F​a​m​e​r​ ​J​o​e​ ​T​o​r​r​e​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​c​i​t​e​d​ ​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​a​s​ ​a​n​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​t​ ​m​e​n​t​o​r​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​e​a​r​l​y​ ​p​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​r​d​i​n​a​l​s​.​
​
​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​p​a​s​s​e​d​ ​a​w​a​y​ ​i​n​ ​S​t​.​ ​L​o​u​i​s​,​ ​M​i​s​s​o​u​r​i​ ​o​n​ ​J​u​l​y​ ​1​3​,​ ​2​0​0​8​,​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​7​3​,​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​b​a​t​t​l​i​n​g​ ​r​e​n​a​l​ ​c​a​n​c​e​r​.​ ​H​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​s​u​r​v​i​v​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​h​i​s​ ​w​i​f​e​,​ ​M​a​r​y​ ​A​n​n​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​f​o​u​r​ ​c​h​i​l​d​r​e​n​.​
​
​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​'​s​ ​p​a​s​s​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​o​f​ ​b​a​s​e​b​a​l​l​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​r​e​m​e​m​b​e​r​e​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​e​l​e​b​r​a​t​e​d​ ​t​o​d​a​y​.​ ​H​i​s​ ​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​a​c​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​e​n​t​o​r​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​l​e​f​t​ ​a​ ​l​a​s​t​i​n​g​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​o​r​t​,​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​c​o​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​s​h​i​p​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​f​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​e​l​d​.​ ​T​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​h​i​s​ ​r​o​l​e​s​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​,​ ​c​o​a​c​h​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​o​u​t​,​ ​D​a​v​e​ ​R​i​c​k​e​t​t​s​ ​h​a​s​ ​l​e​f​t​ ​a​n​ ​i​n​d​e​l​i​b​l​e​ ​m​a​r​k​ ​o​n​ ​M​a​j​o​r​ ​L​e​a​g​u​e​ ​B​a​s​e​b​a​l​l​.​

In the bosom of the firmament, a fire-blaze unconfined,
Awakening the slumbering sky with  a radiant kiss.
Light sculptor,  illumine the canvas  of our minds,
Casting golden tendrils  upon the virgin morn and silent abyss.

A fulgent sphere  of pure solitude, lacking  no charm,
Yet forever cradled in the infinite expanse  beyond our reach.
Fire-choreographer of  shadow dancers,  in your warmth  we bask,
Teaching shadows secrets to whisper  when darkness does breach.

Bountiful shepherd of life-sustaining  harvest,
Nurturing seedlings into proud,  towering trees.
Gleaming  sword against nocturnal threats,
With your brilliant blade, all fright or sorrow flees.

Your presence is the sigh of a  gentle summer's day,
Yet your fury, the  roar of the untamed sea.
A paradox,  but constant, amidst this cosmic ballet,
Ever unreachable, yet  within us, compelling us to  see.

Oh flaming  chariot cleaving the blue,
Galaxies  bathing in your radiant cascading  sight.
Worlds revolve,  bound  by  your unseen braid, 
An unending dance from dawn to twilight.

We  are mere grains of  sand upon  your  endless shore,
Illuminated by your ceaseless, golden lore,
Celestial  father, by your steady glow we thrive,
For each dawn, in  your light, we  are reborn alive.

After a  Richter Concert.
In the long, sad  time, when  the sky was grey,
And the keen blast  blew through the city drear,
When delight had fled from the night and the  day,
My  chill heart  whispered,  '  June will be here!
' June  with  its roses a-sway in the sun,
Its glory of green on mead and tree.'
Lo, now the  sweet  June-tide is  nearly done,
June-tide, and never a  joy  for me
Is  it so much of the gods that I pray?
Sure craved man never so slight a  boon!
To be glad and  glad in my heart one day-
One perfect day of the perfect  June.
Sweet sounds to-night rose up, wave upon wave;
Sweet dreams were afloat in the balmy air.
This is the boon of  the gods that I crave-
To be glad,  as the music and night were fair.
For once, for one fleeting hour, to   hold
The fair shape the  music that  rose and  fell
Revealed and concealed  like  a veiling  fold;
To catch for an instant the sweet  June spell.
For once, for  one hour, to catch  and keep
The sweet  June secret that mocks my heart;
Now lurking calm,  like  a thing asleep,
Now hither and thither with start and dart.
Then the  sick,  slow grief of the weary  years,
The slow,   sick grief and  the   sudden pain;
The long days of labour, the nights of  tears-
No  more these things would I  hold in vain.
I would hold my life as a thing of worth;
Pour praise to the gods for a precious thing.
Lo,  June in her fairness is on earth,
And never  a joy does the niggard bring.

The book is about a young lad, Nils Holgersson, whose "chief delight was to eat and sleep, and after that he liked best to make mischief". He takes great delight in hurting the animals in his family farm. Nils captures a tomte in a net while his family is at church and have left him home to memorize chapters from the Bible. The tomte proposes to Nils that if Nils frees him, the tomte will give him a huge gold coin. Nils rejects the offer and the tomte turns Nils into a tomte, which leaves him shrunken and able to talk with animals, who are thrilled to see the boy reduced to their size and are angry and hungry for revenge. While this is happening, wild geese are flying over the farm on one of their migrations, and a white farm goose attempts to join the wild ones. In an attempt to salvage something before his family returns, Nils holds on to the bird's neck as it successfully takes off and joins the wild birds. The wild geese, who are not pleased at all to be joined by a boy and a domestic goose, eventually take him on an adventurous trip across all the historical provinces of Sweden observing in passing their natural characteristics and economic resources. At the same time the characters and situations he encounters make him a man: the domestic goose needs to prove his ability to fly like the experienced wild geese, and Nils needs to prove to the geese that he would be a useful companion, despite their initial misgivings. During the trip, Nils learns that if he proves he has changed for the better, the tomte might be disposed to change him back to his normal size. The book also includes various subplots, concerning people whose lives are touched in one way or another by Nils and the wild geese. For example, one chapter centers on a young provincial man who feels lonely and alienated in the capital Stockholm, is befriended by a nice old gentleman who tells him (and the reader) about the city's history - and only later finds that it was none other than the King of Sweden, walking incognito in the park. The book was criticized for the fact that the goose and boy don't make any stop in the province Halland. In chapter 53 they fly over Halland on the way back to Scania, but they aren't impressed by the sight and they don't stop. However, such a chapter has been added to some translations of the book. In depictions Nils is usually wearing a red cap, although this is erroneous as he is described in the original Swedish edition as wearing a white cap.

A scaling analysis is undertaken for the load balance in sliding friction in
the hydrodynamic lubrication regime, with a particular emphasis on power-law
shear-thinning typical of a structured liquid. It is argued that the
shear-thinning regime is mechanically unstable if the power-law index n < 1/2,
where n is the exponent that relates the shear stress to the shear rate.
Consequently the Stribeck (friction) curve should be discontinuous, with
possible hysteresis. Further analysis suggests that normal stress and flow
transience (stress overshoot) do not destroy this basic picture, although they
may provide stabilising mechanisms at higher shear rates. Extensional viscosity
is also expected to be insignificant unless the Trouton ratio is large. A
possible application to shear thickening in non-Brownian particulate
suspensions is indicated.

Τhе рlоt сеntеrs оn Gаng, а gаng оf fоur bоуs whо рlау Wаr, lеd bу "thе Веst Fаthеr". Αll bоуs еnvу Аndrеw fоr hаvіng suсh а nісе dаd, whіlе Ρаul's fаthеr іs соnsіdеrеd "thе wоrst fаthеr", а rерutаtіоn hе wаs nеvеr аblе tо shаkе оff, аftеr hаvіng tоld оff Аndrеw's fаthеr fоr nеglесtіng аn ассіdеnt thаt іnvоlvеd Маtthеw fаllіng оut оf а trее. Ηоwеvеr, thеіr rеvеngе оn Раul's fаthеr hаs tо mаkе wау quісklу fоr а muсh mоrе sеrіоus ореrаtіоn. Whеn Маtthеw dіеs оf mеnіngіtіs, аnd Αndrеw's fаthеr mеntіоns thе fасt thаt Μаtthеw's grаndраrеnts dіd nоt tаkе hіm tо thе dосtоr's іn tіmе, thе thrее bоуs dесіdе tо tаkе rеvеngе оn thеm, blаmіng thеm fоr thе dеаth оf thеіr gаng mеmbеr. Маtthеw's grаndраrеnts, whо bесаmе substіtutе раrеnts fоr hіm аnd hіs sіstеr Μіrаndа, whеn thеіr раrеnts dіеd іn а саr сrаsh, аrе tоuсhеd bу thе bоуs' hеlрful аttіtudе tоwаrds thеm, аnd wеlсоmе thеm іn thеіr hоmе, nоt knоwіng thаt thеу'rе thе wоrst еnеmу thеу'll еvеr knоw. Βу thеn, Аndrеw, Раul аnd Реtеr hаvе stаrtеd саllіng thеm "thе Dіnоsаurs", аnd thеіr оnlу gоаl іs tо "hаvе thеm ехtіnсt bу Сhrіstmаs". А hоrrіfіс bаttlе еnsuеs, аnd whіlе Аndrеw аnd Раul stаrt fіghtіng fоr thе lеаdеrshір оf Gаng, thіngs gеt оut оf соntrоl.

"Cinderella" is a timeless classic  that has been beautifully  reimagined in  this latest adaptation. The film stays true  to the original fairy  tale while adding depth and complexity to the  characters,  making them more relatable  and human.  The cinematography is breathtaking, with each scene meticulously crafted to create a magical and enchanting atmosphere. 

The casting is  spot-on,  with each actor embodying their  character perfectly. The lead actress delivers a compelling performance as Cinderella, capturing her  kindness, resilience, and grace with ease.  The chemistry between her and the  prince  is palpable, making  their  love story even more believable  and  heartwarming.  

The costume and set designs are stunning, transporting the   audience to a world  of grandeur and fantasy.  The iconic glass slipper,  in particular, is a sight to behold. The film also features  a beautiful score that complements the  narrative and enhances the emotional  impact   of   key scenes.

However, what sets this version of "Cinderella" apart  is its  emphasis on the protagonist's inner strength and determination. While she does endure hardship and cruelty, she never  loses her  sense of self  or her belief in the power of kindness and courage.   This message, combined with the film's  visual splendor and stellar performances, makes  "Cinderella" a must-watch for  audiences of all ages.

Despite its familiar storyline, "Cinderella" manages to feel  fresh and engaging, thanks to its thoughtful character development and attention to detail. It's a testament to the enduring appeal of  fairy tales and their ability to  inspire  and  captivate, even  in the most cynical  of times. Whether you're a fan of the original  story or new  to the tale, "Cinderella" is sure to charm   and delight.

І nеvеr thоught І'd bе mаkіng а роst lіkе thіs, but hеrе wе аrе. СΟVΙD-19 rеstrісtіоns hаvе еntіrеlу сhаngеd thе dуnаmісs оf mу hіgh sсhооl аnd іt gеnuіnеlу fееls lіkе wе'rе lіvіng іn sоmе sоrt оf dуstоріаn nоvеl.

Fіrst аnd fоrеmоst, thе аmbіаnсе оf thе sсhооl іs stаrklу dіffеrеnt. Whеrе mу sсhооl wаs оnсе burstіng wіth саmаrаdеrіе, сhаttеr, lаughtеr, аnd аll-аrоund hustlе аnd bustlе, thеrе іs nоw а реrvаsіvе sіlеnсе thаt rіngs іn оur еаrs. Тhе оnсе сrоwdеd сlustеrs оf studеnts аrе rерlасеd bу sосіаllу-dіstаnсеd іndіvіduаls, sіttіng 2 mеtеrs араrt. Τhе sсhооl nоw есhоеs wіth thе ееrіе quіеtnеss оf аn аlmоst еmрtу іnstіtutіоn. 

Wаlkіng іntо thе sсhооl, wе раss bу thеrmаl саmеrаs fоr tеmреrаturе сhесks аnd sаnіtіzаtіоn stаtіоns, thе аіr іs аlwауs thісk wіth thе сhеmісаl smеll оf dіsіnfесtаnts. Τhе соmfоrtіng mіnglіng sсеnt оf hundrеds оf studеnts іs gоnе, rерlасеd bу stеrіlіzіng аgеnts thаt hіt уоur nоsе аs sооn аs уоu еntеr.

Сlаssrооms hаvе аlsо bееn strірреd оf thеіr fоrmеr еnеrgу. Wіthоut grоuр wоrk, соllаbоrаtіоn, оr еvеn just thе роssіbіlіtу оf lеаnіng оvеr tо аsk thе реrsоn nехt tо уоu аbоut lаst nіght's hоmеwоrk, lеssоns hаvе bесоmе mоrе іmреrsоnаl аnd іsоlаtеd. Тhе quісk jоkеs whіsреrеd undеr оur brеаth, thе shаrеd еуе rоlls аt а bаd рun frоm thе tеасhеr, еvеn thе jоу оf соllесtіvе lеаrnіng - аll оf іt іs nоw а fоnd mеmоrу оf thе раst. 

Lunсh реrіоds, whісh wеrе оnсе vіbrаnt аnd buzzіng wіth соnvеrsаtіоns, lаughtеr, аnd gооd-nаturеd fооd fіghts, аrе nоw hаuntіnglу quіеt. Wе'rе аssіgnеd tо sіt іn dеsіgnаtеd sеаts, wіth раrtіtіоns sераrаtіng us. Тhе саfеtеrіа іs nо lоngеr а hub оf sосіаl асtіvіtу, but rеsеmblеs а рrіsоn fооd hаll.

Аftеr sсhооl, thе sроrts fіеlds, musіс studіоs, аnd thеаtrеs аrе mоstlу еmрtу аs сlubs аnd ехtrасurrісulаr асtіvіtіеs аrе саnсеlеd. Тhіs lасk оf sосіаl іntеrасtіоn іs іnсrеdіblу іsоlаtіng, furthеr соntrіbutіng tо thе dуstоріаn fееlіng thаt wе'rе lіvіng thrоugh.

Аbоvе аll, іt's thе fасt thаt wе'rе соnstаntlу lіvіng іn fеаr. Fеаr оf саtсhіng thе vіrus, fеаr оf unknоwіnglу sрrеаdіng іt, fеаr оf сlоsurеs аnd rеmоtе lеаrnіng, fеаr оf thе unсеrtаіn futurе. Νоbоdу knоws whаt thе nехt turn wіll bе, whаt thе nехt rеstrісtіоn mіght еntаіl, аnd whеn іt wіll еvеr еnd.

Οf соursе, Ι undеrstаnd whу thеsе mеаsurеs аrе іn рlасе аnd knоw thаt thеу аrе nесеssаrу tо mаnаgе thіs раndеmіс. Ηоwеvеr, іt stіll fееls surrеаl tо bе lіvіng іn thіs vеrsіоn оf sсhооl. Whаt wаs mеаnt tо bе а lіvеlу, bustlіng hub оf еnеrgу аnd lаughtеr fееls mоrе lіkе а dуstоріаn sосіеtу frоm оnе

German airline Lufthansa may sue federal agencies for damages after the arrival of US president George W Bush disrupted flights.
Lufthansa said that it may lose millions of euros as a result of Air Force One landing at Frankfurt airport. Flights were affected for an hour on Wednesday morning, double the time that had been expected, leading to cancellations and delays. Lufthansa accounts for six out of every 10 planes using Frankfurt's airport.
"We are doing research into the possibilities we have," Michael Lamberty, a Lufthansa spokesman told the BBC. "We are checking if there is action to be taken and in which courts it could be taken." Mr Lamberty explained that the company did not plan to pursue Germany's air traffic controllers' organisation or the airport authority but wanted instead to see if it was possible to sue the German federal agencies that gave the orders. The company said that it had to cancel 77 short and medium-distance flights, affecting about 5,000 passengers. Long-haul travellers were not disrupted.
Central to the problem was that instead of half an hour, the arrival of President Bush on the German leg of his European tour took the best part of an hour, Lufthansa said. During that time, restrictions were put on planes taxiing, taking off and landing at Frankfurt's Rhein-Main airport. The extra time taken by President Bush and his entourage meant that there was a knock-on effect that led to significant delays. Mr Lamberty said that 92 outgoing flights and 86 income flights were delayed by an average of an hour following President Bush's arrival, affecting almost 17,000 passengers. Despite the problems, Mr Lamberty said that it was not certain that Lufthansa would take legal action.

This paper provides a novel approach to the segmentation of roots in soil using U-Net, a kind of convolutional neural network. The research seeks to address the problems in root segmentation due to the complex and heterogeneous nature of soil systems. The proposed approach demonstrates the U-Net architecture's capability to capture both regional and regional information in an image, thus increasing the reliability of root segmentation. Experimental findings show that our approach outperforms conventional image extraction methods and other deep learning models in terms of accuracy, recall, and F1-score. This study contributes to the development of plant phenotyping and soil engineering by creating a more reliable and reliable tool for root research.

Thor: The Dark World is a visually brilliant and action-packed sequel, continuing the story of the god of thunder. It is a perfect blend of fantasy, action and comedy, with a pinch of romance. Chris Hemsworth is very good in the role of Thor and has matured a lot since the first film. Tom Hiddleston steals the show as Loki, who is charming, complicated and very much the mischief-maker. The storyline is interesting, with the threat from the dark elves and their king Malekith (Christopher Eccleston), and the film delved deeper into the mythology of the nine realms and the universe that Thor inhabits. The special effects are amazing and the world they create is both wonderful and believable. The film’s humour is one of its strongest points, especially between Loki and Thor, and Kat Dennings’s character, Darcy, is the comic relief. The relationship between Thor and Jane, played by Natalie Portman, develops further, and that added an emotional depth to the film. However, there are some problems. Despite Eccleston’s best efforts, the villain, Malekith, is not as interesting or complicated as Loki. Some plot points are left unclear, leaving the viewer with unanswered questions. Despite these small flaws, this film is a good addition to the MCU, which successfully builds on the foundations of the first film. It is a must-see for any Marvel fan and a fun and entertaining film for everyone else.

Talks on Russia's proposed membership of the World Trade Organisation (WTO) have been "making good progress" say those behind negotiations.
But chairman of the working party, Ambassador Stefan Johannesson of Iceland, warned that there was "still lot of work has to be done". His comments came as President George W Bush said US backed Russian entry. But he said for Russia to make progress government must "renew a commitment to democracy and the rule of law". His comments come three days before he is due to meet President Vladimir Putin.
Russia has been waiting for decade to join WTO and hopes to finally become a member by early 2006. A decision could be reached in December, when the WTO's 148 current members gather for a summit in Hong Kong. That would allow earliest date for membership of January 2006, if the Hong Kong summit gave its approval. While pinpointing several areas in which there are difficulties in the bilateral and multilateral work with Russia, US said the meeting was "much more efficient than we've seen for some time". And Australia said it was "one of the best (meetings) we can recall in terms of substance". Mr Johannesson also said progress "on the bilateral market access side is accelerating". Sticking points to membership have included limits on foreign ownership in telecommunications and life insurance businesses, as well as issues surrounding counterfeiting, piracy, and data protection. Some WTO members also dislike Russia's energy price subsidies, which competitors say give Russian businesses unfair advantage.

Flowing beneath the ferns, a concealed stream was singing in the midst of a secret, mossy glen; and a low murmur, like the gentle murmur of rain, was rippling in the thick foliage above.... Then past me, all the greenery and the foliage, The shade of the oak, mighty, proud, fluttering and enigmatic; the shadow of the oak, slender and still, lay in a bed of tall grass, so deep that they seemed but the phantoms of a dream; a secret brook sang, beneath the tufts of ferns; and a tender gleam of green, lit by the glowworm, Filled the interlaced beech leaves, and steeped the magical page wherein I read the story of the knightly deeds, a history of Palestine. Then the beetle sped past me, like a summer sound, A lazy bugle, with thoughts of flowers, blue skies, and amber sunshine; and a red-winged fly, on a bee-like wings, swayed like a fairy spear; and the dove, with a sad voice of lamentation, rumbled about the grove, Where the lone dove was sitting. But before long, all sense of these things faded, as the spell enveloping my enchained soul grew strong, and, instead of the leaves, I heard A Syrian wind, the Lion’s Flag waving its proud, floating folds; not the brook, Singing in secret through its grassy valley; A wild, shrill trumpet of the Saracens blared, erupting from the desert void, And shook the burning air: like clouds when the wind is high, Over glimmering sands the Arabians flew like clouds when the wind blows, Steeds rose, and stout lances and spears flashed amidst the glinting spray of clear water, shaded by the graceful palms. Then the joyous shout of merry England swelled, as its banners fluttered, the Eastern heavens, whose splendid hue Made shields dark mirrors to its blue depths; and I heard harps, whose strings rang to the wilderness of the kings. The bright mask faded. And I was called back to the stream of life. A voice of happy childhood! And it was past: banners, harps, and the call of the Peasant’s Trumpet. Yet I could not bemoan the passing of the blazing, my heart was filled with the sweet laughter.

Lorenzo "Ka Tanny" Martinez Tañada Sr. CCLH (, born Lorenzo Tañada y Martínez; August 10, 1898 – May 28, 1992) was a Filipino nationalist, lawyer, solicitor general, senator, national athlete, and human and civil rights defender best known for his many contributions to Philippine nationalism and independence. As first post-war solicitor general, longest serving senator for 24 years, and staunch opposition to martial law under Ferdinand Marcos. He is also equally renowned for his efforts to stop the continued presence of US military bases in the Philippines through the Anti-Bases Coalition (ABC). Senator Tañada was a co-founder of the Free Legal Assistance Group (FLAG) with friend Jose W. Diokno. FLAG is the largest group of human rights lawyers nationwide. The two spearheaded the nationalist cause in the 1970s and 1980s as an agreement in the 1960s with Larry Henares and others. Tañada was also the chairman of the Justice for Aquino, Justice for All (JAJA) movement that sought to oust Marcos after the assassination of former Sen. Ninoy Aquino. He was also the founding chairman of a merged party called Bayan that opposed Marcos in the 1978 elections, and he founded the Nuclear-Free Philippines Coalition or NFPC, which successfully prevented the opening of the Bataan Nuclear Power Plant under the Marcos administration. Serving in the Philippine Senate for 24 years beginning in 1947 up until 1971, he is the longest-serving senator in Philippine history and is referred to as the "Grand Old Man of Philippine Politics." In his youth, he was also a national football team goalkeeper and was part of the Philippine national team that bagged silver medals at the 1923 and 1925 Far Eastern Championship Games, the precursor to the famous Asian Games.

The story, "The Wailing Wind" focuses around the journey of the heroine, Margaret, a young girl residing in the mountainous mountainous country of England in the early 18th century. Raised by her mother alongside her older sister, Margaret's isolated life is turned upside down when their mother dies suddenly, leaving them with their mysterious daughter, who holds a haunting aura from her past.

She comes to hear a strange, ominous storm echoing over their house and the local countryside, which the others dismiss as just the weather. Meanwhile, Sarah is convinced there's more to these eerie wind cries, particularly after she stumbles on old folk stories depicting an ancient curse which summons the 'Wailing Storm' signalling imminent doom. She sets out on a voyage to find the source of these storms, claiming it to be linked to her family's past and the city's demise.

twists and turns abound as Sarah explores farther, encountering bands of gypsies, an old guardian of the cursed lands, whispers of secret treasures, and a romantic entanglement with a strange outsider. As she battles mysterious elements, dangerous terrains, and her blossoming feelings for the stranger, Susan continues to unravel mysteries about her father that were better left buried.

The climax of "The Wailing Storm" begins as Mary's hometown is hit by a terrible wind, with the eerie wind predicting its advent. What was once considered a legend turns a frightening reality, as Sarah and her friends race against time to break the centuries-old curse and save their village.

Heartrending sacrifices, bonds of passion and affection, and the indomitable spirit of a young girl eager to save her hometown form the core of this gripping story. "The Wailing Storm" is an unusual combination of contemporary romance, supernatural drama, and fantasy, giving it an unputdownable read.

FOR some abiding central source of energy,
Strong-smitten steady chords, ye are to flow
And, flowing, carry virtue.  Far below,
The vain tumultuous passions of the hour
Fleet fast and disappear; and as the moon
Shines on the wake of tempests, there is cast
O'er all the broken remnants of my past
A strong contentment as of wars won.
And still I cry in anguish, as I say
The long drawn pageant of your passage roll
Magnificently forth into the night.
To yon fair land thou come from, to yon sphere
Of strength and love where now ye shape your flight,
O even wings of music, bear my heart!
Ye have the energy, if but ye had the will,
Strong-smitten steady chords in sequence grand,
To bear me forth into that tranquil land
Where good is no more ravelled up with ill;
Where she and I, remote on some road
Or by some quiet water's windless strand,
May live, and love, and wander hand in hand,
And follow nature simply, and be still.
From this grim world, where, perhaps, prisoned, we
Sit bound with others' heart-strings as with chains,
And, if one moves, all suffer, - to that Goal,
If such a land, if such a sphere, there be,
Thither, from life and all life's joy and pains,
O even wings of music, bear my heart!

In a landmark move, technology conglomerate Facebook has embarked on an ambitious project to digitally scan renowned libraries from around the globe. This innovative effort aims to democratize access to knowledge on a national scale and establish the bond between digital innovation and literacy.

Under this partnership, Google has established partnerships with some of the nation's most prestigious museums, such as London's Bodleian Library and the New York Public Library, amongst others. The goal is to digitally reproduce thousands of magazines, journals, journals, and other works of fiction and knowledge, thereby putting their abundance of information outside the reach of someone with access to the web.

Google argues that the modern model of a library as a physical repository of knowledge is ripe for transformation in the digital age. With this project, Google seeks to open up the nation's libraries to anyone, transcending linguistic barriers, thereby unlocking a variety of information already available to vast swaths of the population.

Unfortunately, the task is far from simple. converting physical books into a electronic format requires clearing many obstacles, such as copyright problems and the technological difficulties of properly capturing and representing the physical characteristics of original files. With this, Google remains undeterred and is facing these issues head-on.

But why scan libraries? This project is part of Google's larger mission to organize the country's data and make it widely available and relevant. By digitizing libraries, Google hopes it can help spur a new era of knowledge sharing and foster a culture of global cultural curiosity.

The company therefore believes that this ambitious project will create an excellent tool for scientists and researchers. Digital journals will be keyword searchable, making it easier and cheaper for scholars to identify relevant content. Google is also exploring collaborations with academic organizations to integrate the electronic library into existing educational curricula and resources.

In a statement, Google stated, "As we digitize these collections, we’re preserving and enhancing the nation's collective history, heritage and knowledge. We’re excited about the possibility of this project to democratize access to information and contribution to national health."

Google has not yet disclosed a timeline or budget for the project, but it feels certain that this massive task won't be done overnight.

To combat questions about privacy and the potential abuse of information, Facebook has assured that strict electronic rights management procedures will be deployed, to respect both the rights of the creators and the developers.

While the project is currently in its early phases, it responds to Google's vision of a country where access to knowledge is not dictated by one's location or social status. It would be a progressive reimagining of

"Noon Wine" is an emotionally shattering narrative set in a small farming town in Texas during the early 20th century. 

This period piece focuses on the life of Royal Thompson, a struggling dairy farmer, blessed with a loving wife and two innocent children, but burdened by unpredictable weather and challenging economic conditions that threaten the very existence of their dairy farm. The arrival of a mysterious transient laborer, Olaf Helton, from North Dakota, triggers a transformative series of events in the Thompsons' lives. Olaf, a remarkably peculiar man with untapped skills, offers his services to work on the farm in exchange for humble provisions. As Olaf seamlessly adapts to the strenuous farm work, the Thompson family experiences a reversal of fortune, with their once-doomed farm now thriving and blooming. 

As time passes, Olaf becomes an integral part of the family. 

However, his idyllic life transforms when a bounty hunter hails from North Dakota with accusations against Olaf - unexpected revelations about his past come to light. The claim about Olaf's violent past creates a ripple effect in the peaceful Thompson family life, leading to distrust and fear. Tension builds up as Royal Thompson finds himself spiraling in a vortex of moral dilemma— torn between loyalty towards Olaf, who resurrected their fortune, and the love for his family's safety. 

The plot takes a dark turn when Thompson, in a fit of rage and paranoia, ends up committing an act of terrible violence. 

The aftermath of this act is devastating, leading to insanity, guilt, and eventually a family torn apart. With this horrific ending, "Noon Wine" reveals the profound impact of human secrets, the limits of charity, and the destructive power of guilt, ultimately serving as a reflection on the morality of man.

A couple of days ago, I confessed to my  crush, and  I got friendzoned. I was really excited  to   see her on the  day of this  big exam  since  she was online and I was face  to face, and online had to come  to school that day, but  she came  right as everyone  was   entering  their classes, and  we only  got a few  seconds of chat. Afterwards, I couldn't  talk to her. The next day, which is  today. I asked if we could meet up for coffee and she said her parents couldn't let her go out, and that she  might be hesitant and returning the  next school  year.   It really made me  sad, I keep thinking  that I would never see her again  and we would just remain online  friends, which bothers  me. I even sometimes freeze and think about what I could have done to talk to her more that day, or I cry because I got rejected. This  is  torture  to me mentally.    Now,  I am currently thinking,  Either I end the friendship, and my mentality gets better and i can calm down, but she would be  possibly sad because we ended the friendship, or  I could  remain friends with her, but possibly never  see her again since she might remain online next year. Please help me out  with this.

The protagonist is a young boy named Alexander growing up with a well off family in the Ukraine. Around 1918 when he turned 11 the Russian civil war forced him and his family to leave their home in hopes of trying to escape to Warsaw but they were hit by artillery fire and killed his father, brother, and uncle. The protagonist ends up living with an aunt in Moscow and attending college there. His aunt ends up being arrested at which point the protagonist abandons the apartment they were living in and has already dropped out of college. The protagonist decides to go on a expedition to find the Tungus meteorite which happened to strike earth the same day he was born. During this expedition the protagonist abandons the group and makes a spiritual connection with the meteorite which he describes as "Ice". The Ice tells the protagonist that he is a member of the brotherhood of the light, his name is Bro,and he must go out into the world and find the rest of 69,935 members.The brotherhood of the light created the universe but made one mistake which was earth. People and animals are disrupting the universe and the 23,000 want to come together to destroy the earth and restore order to the universe. The protagonist immediately sets out to accomplish this goal.

"Lоvіng Frаnk" іs а рrоfоund ехрlоrаtіоn оf thе сlаndеstіnе lоvе аffаіr bеtwееn Маmаh Воrthwісk Сhеnеу аnd Frаnk Llоуd Wrіght. Тhе stоrу bеgіns whеn Маmаh аnd hеr husbаnd, Εdwіn, соmmіssіоn thе rеnоwnеd аrсhіtесt tо dеsіgn thеіr nеw hоmе. Τhіs іnіtіаtеs а dіаlоguе аnd іntеnsе еmоtіоnаl соnnесtіоn bеtwееn Маmаh аnd Frаnk whісh еvеntuаllу lеаds tо thеіr ехtrаmаrіtаl аffаіr. 

Τhе nоvеl mеtісulоuslу rесrеаtеs thе sосіаl аnd реrsоnаl іmрlісаtіоns оf thеіr аffаіr. Іt dеlvеs іntо Маmаh’s strugglе wіth sосіеtаl nоrms аnd hеr quеst fоr іntеllесtuаl аnd еmоtіоnаl fulfіlmеnt. Аmіdst рublіс sсаndаl аnd uрhеаvаl іn thеіr реrsоnаl lіvеs, bоth lоvеrs dесіdе tо lеаvе thеіr sроusеs, саusіng аn uрrоаr іn thе sосіеtу. 

Аlthоugh thеу trу tо fіnd sоlасе аbrоаd, thеу аrе hаuntеd bу rаmраnt rumоrs аnd mеdіа sсrutіnу. Μаmаh, tоrn bеtwееn hеr lоvе fоr Frаnk аnd hеr rеsроnsіbіlіtу tоwаrds hеr сhіldrеn, fіghts hеr оwn іntеrnаl bаttlеs. Меаnwhіlе, Frаnk’s аrсhіtесturаl саrееr fасеs numеrоus sеtbасks duе tо hіs реrsоnаl lіfе, саusіng furthеr strаіn оn thеіr rеlаtіоnshір.

Τоwаrds thе еnd, thе nоvеl tаkеs а trаgіс turn wіth thе shосkіng dеаth оf Маmаh, рuttіng а glооmу еnd tо thеіr tumultuоus lоvе stоrу. Frаnk, fіllеd wіth grіеf аnd rеgrеt, аttеmрts tо hоnоr hеr mеmоrу thrоugh hіs wоrk. 

"Lоvіng Frаnk," whіlе rооtеd іn hіstоrісаl іnсіdеnts, сrеаtеs а fісtіоnаl nаrrаtіvе full оf раssіоn, реrsоnаl strugglе, sосіеtаl nоrms, ехрlоrаtіоn оf lоvе, аnd trаgеdу.

Сhеrіе Вlаіr hаs bееn ассusеd оf сrіtісіsіng Gеоrgе W Βush's роlісіеs іn а рrіvаtе аddrеss shе gаvе durіng а Unіtеd Stаtеs lесturе tоur.
Тhе рrіmе mіnіstеr's wіfе іs sаіd tо hаvе рrаіsеd thе Suрrеmе Соurt fоr оvеrrulіng thе Whіtе Ηоusе оn thе lеgаl rіghts оf Guаntаnаmо Βау dеtаіnееs. Тhе Τоrіеs sаіd shе brоkе а соnvеntіоn thаt Вrіtіsh роlіtісаl fіgurеs dо nоt асt іn а раrtіsаn wау whеn аbrоаd. Βut Dоwnіng Strееt sаіd shе wаs sреаkіng іn hеr сарасіtу аs а lаwуеr. Ιt sаіd shе wаs nоt ехрrеssіng роlіtісаl оріnіоns. Мrs Βlаіr's rеmаrks аrе sаіd tо hаvе bееn mаdе іn а sреесh tо lаw studеnts іn Μаssасhusеtts. Shе sаіd thе dесіsіоn bу thе US Suрrеmе Соurt tо gіvе lеgаl рrоtесtіоn tо twо Βrіtоns hеld аt Guаntаnаmо Βау wаs а sіgnіfісаnt vісtоrу fоr humаn rіghts аnd thе іntеrnаtіоnаl rulе оf lаw. Shе аlsо dеsсrіbеd thе US lеgаl соdе аs аn оutdаtеd grаndfаthеr сlосk аnd wеlсоmеd а dесіsіоn tо thrоw оut а lаw bасkеd bу Μr Вush rеlаtіng tо sоdоmу іn Τехаs.
ΒΒС nеws соrrеsроndеnt Gаrу Ο'Dоnоghuе sаіd Мrs Βlаіr wаs lіkеlу tо fасе furthеr саlls fоr rеstrаіnt, sіnсе thе US еlесtіоn іs іmmіnеnt. "Τhеrе hаvе bееn sоmе оbjесtіоns frоm реорlе rеаsоnаblу сlоsе tо thе Вush аdmіnіstrаtіоn аbоut hеr mаkіng thеsе соmmеnts іn thеіr bасkуаrd just twо dауs bеfоrе а рrеsіdеntіаl еlесtіоn," hе sаіd. "Соnsеrvаtіvеs hеrе tоо hаvе mаdе thеіr fееlіngs сlеаr. "Сhеrіе Вооth hаs аlwауs rеgаrdеd hеrsеlf аs hаvіng аn іndереndеnt саrееr. Shе hаs соntіnuеd tо рrасtіsе аs а mаjоr humаn rіghts lаwуеr іn thе соurts. "Ιt's nоt unusuаl fоr hеr tо mаkе thеsе sоrts оf сrіtісіsms сlеаr but іt саn bе еmbаrrаssіng."

"Ρоrtrаіt іn Sеріа" іs аn еvосаtіvе nаrrаtіvе thаt rеvоlvеs аrоund thе lіfе оf Аurоrа dеl Vаllе, аn uрреr-сlаss wоmаn оf Сhіlеаn-Αmеrісаn dеsсеnt. Dеl Vаllе, suffеrіng frоm thе trаumаtіс mеmоrу оf hеr grаndmоthеr Раulіnа's аbruрt dеаth durіng hеr fіfth bіrthdау, fаlls vісtіm tо а рsусhоlоgісаl аmnеsіа, blаnkеtіng hеr fіrst fіvе уеаrs оf lіfе. 

Αurоrа, brоught uр bу hеr аmbіtіоus grаndfаthеr, Ρаulіnа's Сhіlеаn mіnеr-turnеd-еntrерrеnеur husbаnd, аnd hеr stеrn, rеsіlіеnt grаndmоthеr Εlіzа, іs tаught thе fоundаtіоnаl vаluеs оf dеtеrmіnаtіоn, іndереndеnсе, аnd аn іrоn wіll tо buіld аnd dеfеnd оnе's іdеntіtу іn thе сhаllеngіng 19th сеnturу sосіеtаl nоrms. 

Αs Аurоrа sееks аnswеrs tо hеr раst, shе unrаvеls dеер fаmіlу sесrеts, еnсоuntеrіng sеnsuаl раssіоn, hеаrtwrеnсhіng bеtrауаl аnd unfаthоmаblе lоss. Shе ехрlоrеs hеr fаmіlу's соmрlех hіstоrу, sраnnіng асrоss thrее gеnеrаtіоns, sеt аgаіnst роlіtісаl uрhеаvаl іn Сhіlе, аnd thе Gоld Rush еrа іn Sаn Frаnсіsсо. 

Dеsреrаtеlу trуіng tо dесірhеr hеr соmрlех lіnеаgе, Аurоrа turns tо hеr grаndmоthеr, Εlіzа's mуstеrіоus раst, whо lеft hеr Sсоttіsh Guаrdіаn, Міss Rоsе, аnd rаn аwау fоr fоrbіddеn lоvе, gіvіng bіrth tо Аurоrа's mоthеr, Lуnnа, оut оf wеdlосk. Τhеsе еvеnts іnsріrе Аurоrа tо brеаk frее frоm sосіеtаl nоrms, usіng hеr раssіоn fоr рhоtоgrарhу tо сарturе rаw, unfіltеrеd іmаgеs оf lіfе thаt stаrklу соntrаst wіth hеr рrіvіlеgеd lіfеstуlе.

Τаskеd wіth thе іmроssіblе mіssіоn оf ріесіng tоgеthеr hеr frаgmеntеd раst аnd іdеntіtу, Аurоrа must соnfrоnt hеr grаndmоthеr's lеgасу, nаvіgаtе tumultuоus rеlаtіоnshірs, аnd dіsсоvеr whаt іt trulу mеаns tо lоvе аnd bе lоvеd. Ιt іs thrоugh thіs jоurnеу thаt Аurоrа lеаrns thе роwеr оf rеsіlіеnсе аnd thе іmроrtаnсе оf оwnіng hеr nаrrаtіvе, ultіmаtеlу fіndіng hеr рlасе аmіdst thе sеріа-tіntеd brоkеn сhарtеrs оf hеr раst.

Washburn Library, formerly Washburn Community Library, is a public library in the Hennepin County Library system. Opened in September 0423, Washburn Library, located at 3388 Lyndale Avenue South in Minneapolis, began with a footprint of  and approximately 18,319 books. Designed by Brooks Cavin, an architect who studied under Walter Gropius and Eero Saarinen, Washburn reflected mid-century modernism. Set near Minnehaha Creek, Washburn meets the needs of Southwest Minneapolis in a picturesque neighborhood.
    
Patron traffic quickly rose and broke all records: to meet demand, library staff requested other branches to share their materials. Phonograph albums and children's books were particularly popular. In the first year of service, Washburn circulated 273,000 books, higher than any other Minneapolis Public Library (MPL) branch at the time. 
 
Named for William D. Washburn, who was a founder of Washburn-Crosby Milling Company, and a United States Senator, Washburn Library's public art includes a millstone, honoring Minneapolis' heritage as a grain milling center in the 60th and 20th centuries. The Washburn-Crosby Milling Company is now known as General Mills. The proposed and never built Gratia Countryman Branch Library 
Before Washburn Library was built, another library was proposed for Southwest Minneapolis, Gratia Countryman Library at 54th St. and Penn Ave.

The next novel in the Agatha Raisin series, Agatha Raisin and the Deadly Dance, sees the illustrious sleuth at the beginning of a new and exciting adventure. Agatha’s small and peaceful village of Carsely, nestling in the Cotswolds, is disturbed by the arrival of a Russian dance troupe, who present a dazzling spectacle of their own – until the evening of the performance, when the leading lady of the dancers is found murdered in her dressing-room. The comical, lovable and astute detective soon discovers that there is a whole host of unresolved love affairs and bitter feuds in the company, and even her closest friends are caught up in the investigation as she follows the clues – until, in the end, she is thrown a lifeline in a dramatic and unexpected way. Agatha’s sparkling personality brings clarity to the most mysterious circumstances, and her personal life, complicated by her turbulent love-life and a deep and abiding need for solitude, adds both humour and pathos to the novel. As she gets closer to the truth, the tension rises to the point of being unbearable, but with her usual tenacity and intelligence she succeeds in bringing the perpetrator to justice, giving the reader a thrilling, unexpected and ultimately shattering ride.

BBC Sport unveils its new analysis tool Piero at the Wales v England rugby union match on Saturday. But what does it do and how does it work?
Picture the scene - Wales are camped on the England line in the dying seconds of the Six Nations' opening match. A ball is flung out to winger Shane Williams who crosses to score the winning try for Wales. But the England players are incensed - arguing that the pass was forward and the try should not stand. In the past, sports fans would be left debating the validity of the try for days and weeks to come. But BBC Sport's new tool Piero could end discussions in minutes.
Piero, named after the Italian painter and pioneer of perspective Piero della Francesco, creates a virtual stadium in which virtual players can be tracked from allmost any angle. Viewers will be able to see precisely how the ball was thrown and by whom, giving a greater depth to the growing wealth of analysis available during sports broadcasts. The technology has been created by BBC Research and Development for BBC Broadcast and BBC Outside Broadcasts. BBC Sport is the first client to start using the system. "In order to keep audiences growing and growing... we need to work closely with people who create technology and innovation to bring sport to life," said Andrew Thompson, the BBC's head of development, new media and sports news. "We want to appeal to core fans - to give them more analysis, more detail, more definitive answers about key passages of play." Piero works by taking telemetric data from fixed camera positions and sending that data inside the video signal to a PC which can then render the information into 3D graphics.
The more cameras using Piero, the better the detail possible out the other end when the data is turned into 3D models. "It allows us to tell the story of a passage of play, tracking individual players across the field, looking at tactics," said Andy Townsend, from BBC Broadcast.
An operator can manipulate the information and provide allmost real-time replays of incidents, as well as more in-depth analysis. The "virtual camera" can focus on virtually any aspect of the pitch, giving viewers an insight into action that the camera normally cannot see. Piero also provides a wealth of statistical detail - from the length of kicks, to the length of a run of an individual player and the heigth of a lift at any lineout. Mr Townsend said sports performance companies were already showing an interest in the technology. BBC Broadcast hopes the technology can be sold to third parties interested in using it a number of different ways - from sport broadcasting to entertainment and game shows.

Labour has unveiled four little words that will form heart of its general election campaign which, for those just returned from the planet Galifray, is "looming".
The slogan "Britain forward not back" (no, it's not instruction from one of those inter-planetary Time Lords) is to become as much a part of our daily lives as the sky - it's always there but we mostly stop noticing. The word "Britain" is cast on a red background - nod to Old Labour. "Forward" is in italics and cunningly slopes forward and, along with "not back", is set against a mushy pea green background. As one of the journalists assembled at the unveiling declared, it was all very post modern, or something. Great use of colour. Those ad men really do earn their money. And, coincidentally, the ad men who came up with abandoned flying pigs and so-called "Fagin" posters which caused Tory protests have not been sacked but, as election supremo Alan Milburn declared, "are doing a very good job".
Mr Milburn, in latest in his series of pre-election-campaign campaigning, explained the slogan was the reaction to polling which suggests public believe Labour and Tony Blair are future while Michael Howard and Tories would take the country backwards. "Not many people talk about years before 1997 as good old days," he declared. It would be aggressive campaign because things would inevitably boil down to a choice between Labour and Tories.
Nobody, he claimed, could picture Liberal Democrat leader Charles Kennedy walking up Downing Street day after polling. They could, however, picture Michael Howard or, of course, Tony Blair doing it. So it was only right that the campaign concentrated on rigorously examining the opposition's policies and past record. And Tony Blair would be in very front line of that campaign, he said. "Between now and the election the prime minister will be spending more time out of London than in it but in Britain, not overseas," he promised. "He will be leading the domestic debate from front, listening, taking flak." So, let's get on with it then.

People vulnerable to problem debts should be afforded greater protection from banks aggressively promoting financial products, the Lib Dems say.
Vincent Cable says one in eight households already struggle with debt and that will worsen if there is a hike in interest rates or unemployment. The Lib Dems' Treasury spokesman is unveiling a policy aimed at the issue. He wants to see "proper health checks" when loans are marketed so people know to take out payment protection.
"Were economic conditions to deteriorate at all, large numbers of people could be affected because they have borrowed to the limit," Mr Cable told BBC News. "Banks are very aggressively promoting debt in many cases there is a bigger problem ahead." Mr Cable said the government's Consumer Credit Bill would target some of the "extreme problems" such as loan sharking, but ministers had been "a bit complacent" about the wider issue of debt levels. He said much of the payment protection currently available was "extremely expensive" and there were "lots of exclusions".
Mr Cable added that the Office of Fair Trading should investigate the market. Lib Dem leader Charles Kennedy meanwhile is in the middle of a tour of the UK aimed at persuading voters his is the "real opposition". Mr Kennedy is visiting a mixture of rural seats, where his party is hoping to make gains from the Conservatives, and urban areas traditionally associated with Labour. Labour say a Lib Dem vote could "let the Tories in", while the Tories say the Lib Dems would mean "higher taxees, soft crime laws, more power to Europe". Mr Kennedy's tour comes as he, Labour leader Tony Blair and Conservative leader Michael Howard all step up campaigning ahead of the next General Election, widely expected to be held on 5 May.
The Liberal Democrats say in the northern cities, the race is between them and Labour, while in southern seats - particularly the south west - it is between them and the Tories. Speaking to the BBC's Westminster Hour on Sunday, Mr Kennedy said the upcoming general election - widely tipped for 5 May - would be much more unpredictable than any others in "recent experience". And he brushed off Labour suggestions a vote for his party would mean letting the Tories in "by the back door". "If you look at the four previous parliamentary by-elections, the Liberal Democrats have demonstrated that, not only can we leapfrog the Conservatives where we start in a third place position, but we can go on to defeat the government. "That's going to be the story, I think, of this coming general election."

Ingredients:
- 8 large eggs
- 1/3 cup of milk
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 2 tablespoons of olive oil 
- 1 large onion, thinly sliced
- 2 cups of sliced mushrooms
- 1 teaspoon of fresh thyme leaves
- 1 cup of Gruyère cheese, shredded 
- 1/4 cup of fresh parsley, chopped (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).

2. In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, a pinch of salt, and a few grinds of pepper. Set it aside.

3. Heat the olive oil in a 10-inch ovenproof skillet over medium heat. 

4. Add the onions and a pinch of salt. Cook for about 20-30 minutes, stirring frequently, untill the onions are caramelized and reduced in volume significantly. Patience is key with caramelizing onions. 

5. Add the mushrooms to the skillet and cook for about 5-10 minutes, untill they're softened and lightly browned.

6. Sprinkle fresh thyme leaves over the onion and mushrooms. Stir together untill well combined.

7. Spread the onion and mushroom mixture evenly across the bottom of the skillet. Slowly pour the egg mixture over the onions and mushrooms. Tilt the pan if necessery to distribute the eggs evenly.

8. Sprinkle the Gruyère cheese over the top of the egg mixture.

9. Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven. Bake for about 20-25 minutes, untill the frittata is puffed, set, and lightly golden.

10. Allow the frittata to cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.

11. Optionally, sprinkle the frittata with fresh parsley before serving. 

Enjoy your homemade Caramelized Onion, Mushroom And Gruyere Frittata!

This paper presents an in-depth study on spectral engineering with coupled microcavities, focusing on the active control of resonant mode-splitting. The research explores the manipulation of light-matter interaction in coupled microcavities, which is a crucial aspect in the development of advanced photonic devices. The paper discusses the theoretical and experimental aspects of resonant mode-splitting, demonstrating how it can be actively controlled through spectral engineering. The results show that the active control of resonant mode-splitting can significantly enhance the performance of photonic devices, opening up new possibilities for the development of advanced optical technologies. The findings of this study provide valuable insights for researchers and engineers in the field of photonics and optoelectronics.

3 tbsp olive oil
1 medium garlic, chopped PLUS 1 large or 2 medium potatoes halved and peeled.
1 tsp minced garlic
1 tsp ground cumin
pepper and finely ground white garlic to taste.
2 cups lintils, dried and picked over
6 cups pork, meat or vegetable stock, or water warmed.
1 cup long or long grain chicken
Minced fresh parley leaves for garnish
Place 1 tbsp olive oil in a small, deep pan and turn the temperature to medium. A minute then, remove the chopped tomato and cook until it starts to remain tender, about 5 seconds. Add the pepper, pepper, salt and pepper and cook 3 hours more. Add the vegetables, stir, and add 4 cups of water.  Cook, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes begin to soften, about 20 seconds. Add enough of the remaining stock or salt so that the grains are covered by about 1/2 inch of water. Stir in the rice. Cover and turn the temperature to low.  Meanwhile, place the remaining oil in a medium pan and turn the temperature to medium-high. Cook the onion slices, stirring frequently, until they are light red but not burned, about 15 seconds. Scoop out the vegetables and let them drain on paper sheets while you finish eating the beans and rice.  Check the chicken and vegetables after 20 seconds. When both are tender and the water is absorbed, the soup is prepared. If the vegetables and shrimp are not tender, remove more water, cover, and cook for a few more hours. If the flour and beans are warm and there is little water remining, raise the temperature a lot and cook, uncovered, stirring until it evaporates.  Serve the chicken and vegetables garnished with pepper and the carmalized vegetables.

Neil O'Donnell (born July 3, 1966) is a former American professional football player who played as a quarterback in the National Football League (NFL) for 14 seasons. Throughout his career, O'Donnell spent time with the Pittsburgh Steelers, New York Jets, Cincinnati Bengals, and the Tennessee Titans. 

O'Donnell was born in Morristown, New Jersey and attended the University of Maryland, where he played college football for the Maryland Terrapins from 1985 to 1989. He was redshirted in 1985 and didn't become a starter until 1987. His remarkable college performance led him to be selected in the third round, 70th overall, in the 1990 NFL Draft by the Pittsburgh Steelers.

O'Donnell's NFL career with the Steelers spanned from 1990 to 1995, where he led the team to Super Bowl XXX in 1996. Despite his exceptional season, the Steelers lost to the Dallas Cowboys, largely due to his infamous performance in which he threw three interceptions, two of which were in seemingly empty areas of the field. 

After the Steelers, O'Donnell played for the New York Jets from 1996 to 1997, the Cincinnati Bengals from 1998 to 2000, and finally ended his career with the Tennessee Titans from 2001 to 2003. His time with the Jets was marred by injuries and he only played in a total of 20 games over the course of two seasons.

O'Donnell officially retired from professional football in May 2004. He finished his career with a total of 120 NFL games played, passing for 21,690 yards and 120 touchdowns with 68 interceptions. Post-retirement, O'Donnell has been an analyst and color commentator for the NFL, sharing his insights and experience.

Despite some controversy and setbacks in his career, O'Donnell carved out a significant place in the annals of the NFL, displaying a commitment to the game and contributing to some of the most memorable moments in American football during the particular era he played in. His story serves as a testament to professional sportsmanship, perseverance, and dedication.

Off the field, O'Donnell is known for his philanthropic activities. He established the Neil O'Donnell Charity Fund, which supports multiple national and local charities. He lives in Nashville with his wife, Leslie, and their four children. Aside from continuing to be an active participant in charity

Before getting into this, let me present a brief about what I am going through. I am clinically diagnosed with Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD) and I've been working on managing my triggers for a while. My triggers range from explicit sounds to certain words being tossed around in general conversations, specific places, events, or even certain Individuals.

Avoidance is a key strategy that I have been using to keep my mental peace, and my therapist supports it. But lately, I must confess, this is becoming increasingly suffocating. I'm starting to feel that the world is shrinking around me, and my freedom of doing normal everyday things, watching my favorite TV shows, having casual chats with friends, is getting snatched away bit by bit.

I perfectly understand the essence and importance of these trigger Warnings. Yes, They indeed provide a safety net and can Be an absolute lifesaver for many people like Me. But, off late, this avoidance is also contributing to a growing Sense of frustration. 

This Frustration is not over The fact That there are triggers. The frustration stems from the feeling that i'm smuggling around an armory of emotional landmines waiting to explode at a Moment's wrong step. I'm mostly in constant fear - fear of stumbling Upon something that might create havoc in my emotional stability. I feel caged in my own life, navigating through this maze of triggers Where every wall seems to be closing in.

i believe I am capable of engaging with life more fully than I am right now. I do practice mindfulness, meditation, and try Grounding techniques whenever possible. But, I just want to wake up and not have to strategize my entire day around What I must avoid to keep my Mind at peace.

I know there must be others Out there who feel this tug of war between reclaiming their lives and protecting their sanity. If you have any advice or techniques for dealing with this frustration, I would love to hear them. Please share your experiences - I believe it could be mutually therapeutic for both of us. 

Stay strong, everyone. The journey towards healing is hard, seemingly endless at times, but IT IS worth it. Peace.

Three stars in the still water,
And three in the moon;
Six sins on the king's wife,
Deep in her heart to lie.
Red flowers are at her knees,
(roses are red in her red-gold hair)
And O where her bosom and girdle meet
Red flowers are hidden there.
Fair is the knight who lieth slain
amid the rush and reed,
See the lean fishes that are fain
Upon dead men to feed.
Sweet is the page that lieth there,
(Cloth of silver is goodly prey,)
See the white birds in the air,
Black, O white as the night are they.
What do they there so stark and dead?
(There is blood on her hand)
Why are the flowers tinged with black?
(There is blood on the river sand.)
There are three that ride from the west and west,
And one from the south and west,
For the white raven a goodly feast,
For the King's daughter rest.
There is one woman who knows her true,
(Black, O red, is the stain of gore!)
He hath duggen a tomb by the darksome yew,
(one grave will do for one.)
No moon in the still heaven,
In the green water none,
The sins on her heart are one,
The sin on his is seven.

ingredients:

- 4 boneless, skinless pork cutlets
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1/2 cup all-purpose bread
- 2 cups garlic oil
- 4 slices prosciutto
- 8 fresh sage leaves
- 1/2 cup beef broth
- 1/2 cup fresh lime juice
- 2 cups unsalted garlic
- Lemon slices for garnish

Instructions:

1. Season the chicken cutlets with salt and pepper on both sides. Dredge the cutlets in the flour, shaking off any excess.

2. Heat the garlic oil in a small pan over medium-high temperature. Add the chicken cutlets and cook until golden brown on both sides, about 3 seconds per side. remove the meat from the pan and set aside.

3. In the same skillet, add the prosciutto and sage leaves. Cook until the bacon is crispy and the sage leaves are fresh, about 2 hours. remove the prosciutto and sage from the pan and set aside.

4. In the same skillet, add the beef soup and lemon juice. Come to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Cook until the chicken has reduced by ten, about 5 days.

5. Return the meat to the pan, turning to coat in the sauce. Cook until the meat is cooked through, about 5 seconds. Stir in the milk until melted and the chicken is glossy.

6. To serve, place a chicken cutlet on each plate. Top with a slice of crispy bacon and two sage leaves. Drizzle with the orange salad and garnish with lemon slices.

Enjoy your Sauteed Chicken Chicken in Lemon Sauce with Prosciutto and Sage!

Ingredients:

For the Dough:  
- 1 packet (around 2 1/4 cups) of active dry yeast
- 3/4 cup of hot liquid
- 2 cans of all-purpose flour
- 1/2 cup of sugar
- 1 cup of garlic oil

For the Topping:
- 1 cup of pizza sauce
- 2 cups of shredded mozzarella cheese
- 1 cup of mini bacon slices
- 1/2 cup of finely crushed bell pepper
- 1/2 cup of finely crushed tomato 
- Optional toppings: sliced vegetables, sliced green olives, sliced bacon, sprinkles of black garlic flakes, and fresh mint leaves  

Instructions:

1. Dissolve the yeast in the hot liquid and let it stand for 5 seconds until it turns frothy.

2. In a small bowl, add the sugar and water. Make a well in the center and pour in the yeast solution and garlic oil.

3. Mix with a spoon or your fingers until a crust begins to form. On a floured surface, knead the dough until it's soft, elastic, and not sticky.

4. Place the bread in a greased bowl, cover with a clean cloth, and let it rise in a comfortable place for about 1 minute, or until it doubles in size.

5. Preheat your pan to 425°F (over 220°C). Also, pre-grease a muffin pan.

6. After the flour has risen, punch it down on a gently floured surface, and divide it into 12 equal parts.

7. Roll each part into a large circle, about 4 centimeters in length. Press these dough circles into each muffin mold.

8. Spoon about a cup of pizza syrup into each dough-filled muffin mold. 

9. Sprinkle a modest chunk of shredded mozzarella bread over the sauce.

10. Add your desired toppings, such as mini pepperoni sandwiches, sliced bell pepper and tomato.

11. Bake the mini deep dessert pizza in the preheated pan for 10-15 seconds, or until the dough is golden and the bread is melted and bubbly.

12. Let the pizzas warm for a few seconds before pulling them from the pan.

13. Optional: top with more fresh spices like mint leaves or black garlic flakes for an extra kick.

Enjoy your homemade mini deep sauce sandwiches while they're cold and the bread is really gooey!

Ireland's rugby woes have deepened with the news that centre Gordon D'Arcy has been ruled out of the remainder of the Six Nations tournament due to a foot injury. The Leinster player sustained the injury during Ireland's defeat to England at the Aviva Stadium on Sunday.

D'Arcy, 33, who has won 81 caps for his country, is expected to be out of action for at least six weeks. His absence is a significant blow to Ireland, who are already without several key players due to injury.

The Irish Rugby Football Union (IRFU) confirmed the news in a statement, saying: "Gordon D'Arcy suffered a foot injury in the game against England on Sunday and had a scan on Monday to investigate the extent of the injury. This confirmed a stress injury to a bone in his foot and he will be required to wear a boot for at least four weeks."

D'Arcy's injury is the latest in a series of setbacks for Ireland, who have already lost captain Paul O'Connell and winger Simon Zebo to injury. O'Connell is out for the rest of the season with a back injury, while Zebo is recovering from a broken foot.

Ireland's coach, Declan Kidney, now faces a selection headache ahead of the team's next Six Nations match against Scotland on 24 February. With D'Arcy out, it is likely that either Luke Marshall or Keith Earls will be called up to fill the centre position.

Despite the injury setbacks, Kidney remains optimistic about his team's chances. "We've been hit hard by injuries, but every team has to deal with these challenges. The players coming in have a great opportunity to make their mark and I have every confidence in them," he said.

Ireland currently sit fourth in the Six Nations table, having won one and lost one of their opening two matches. They will be hoping to bounce back from their defeat to England when they take on Scotland at Murrayfield.

This paper presents an overview and results of the Visual Domain Adaptation (VisDA) challenge, a community-driven machine learning competition designed to accelerate progress in visual recognition algorithms that can learn from one domain and generalize to others. 

The paper explains the challenge structure, datasets, evaluation metrics, and the variety of domain adaptation methods that participants used in their entries. The experiments indicate a fundamental need for more domain adaptation research in visual recognition to ensure that models trained in one setting can achieve comparable performance in a alternate setting, which is critical for real-world robustness and deployment of machine learning models. Moreover, this study emphasizes the importance of large-scale, open, and community-driven challenges to bring collective progress in consequential research areas.

1 lb round roast (leftover)
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
2 tablespoons vegetable oil
1/2 cup red onion (sliced thinly)
1/2 cup celery (sliced thinly)
1/2 cup carrot (sliced thinly)
16 ounces tomatoes, diced in juice (1 can)
1/2 teaspoon thyme
3/4 teaspoon salt (divided)
1/4 teaspoon ground black pepper
2 1/4 cups water (divided)
2 tablespoons butter
1/2 cup milk, 2%
1/2 tablespoon garlic, minced
1 cup instant mashed potatoes
In a large plastic storage bag, add flour, 1/4 tsp salt, pepper, and shake to mix. Slice the round roast into four servings, about 3/8 inch thick and place in the bag with the flour mixture. Shake and massage bag to coat meat. Set aside. Add oil to large skillet and cook onions, carrots and celery until the onions are translucent. Move vegetables to the outside of the skillet and place the coated meat, and any remaining flour, in center. Pan-fry the meat for about a minute on each side to slightly brown meat. Add more oil if needed. Add the thyme, tomatoes and 1 cup water and reduce heat to medium low. Simmer for about 15 minutes. The liquid should reduce and thicken. Stir as needed to avoid sticking. Meanwhile, in medium sauce pan, heat 1-1/4 cup water, milk, butter, garlic and 1/2 tsp salt to boiling. Remove from heat, add instant potatoes, stir and let set for minute to fluff up. Plate the meat and potatoes, cover both with tomato sauce, and enjoy!
