"Ladies' Night" is a thrilling, empowering narrative centered around the lives of five diverse women: Sarah, a high-profile lawyer, Kate, a single mother working two jobs, Linda, a struggling actress, Grace, a retired teacher, and Amber, a college student grappling with identity issues. Every Thursday night, these women convene at a boutique pub, sharing their battles, successes, and aspirations. 

The crux of the narrative unfolds when one of these friends becomes the target of a dangerous stalker. They collectively decide to tackle this nightmare, showing strength and resilience, as they unearth untold secrets and face their own personal fears during the process. 

The novel captivates readers as it beautifully drifts from moments of laughter and camaraderie at the pub to the palpable danger faced by the protagonist. Despite their disparate backgrounds and life experiences, the bond between the women deepens, and they learn invaluable lessons about friendship, equality, courage and the power that lies within every woman. 

"Ladies' Night" is a true celebration of womanhood and the passion for life that keeps us going despite the odds. This exhilarating mystery is beautifully intertwined with powerful themes of friendship, resilience, and empowerment, making it an unputdownable read.

In this paper, we propose deep learning algorithms for ranking response
surfaces, with applications to optimal stopping problems in financial
mathematics. The problem of ranking response surfaces is motivated by
estimating optimal feedback policy maps in stochastic control problems, aiming
to efficiently find index associated to the minimal response across the
entire continuous input space $\mathcal{X} \subseteq \mathbb{R}^d$. By
considering points in $\mathcal{X}$ as pixels and indices of the minimal
surfaces as labels, we recast problem as an image segmentation problem,
which assigns a label to every pixel in an image such that pixels with same
label share certain characteristics. This provides an alternative method for
efficiently solving problem instead of using sequential design in our
previous work [R. Hu and M. Ludkovski, SIAM/ASA Journal on Uncertainty
Quantification, 5 (2017), 212--239].
  Deep learning algorithms are scalable, parallel and model-free, i.e., no
parametric assumptions needed on the response surfaces. Considering ranking
response surfaces as image segmentation allows one to use broad class of deep
neural networks, e.g., UNet, SegNet, DeconvNet, which have been widely applied
and numerically proved to possess high accuracy in field. We also
systematically study dependence of deep learning algorithms on input
data generated on uniform grids or by sequential design sampling, and observe
that the performance of deep learning is {\it not} sensitive to the noise and
locations (close to/away from boundaries) of training data. We present few
examples including synthetic ones and the Bermudan option pricing problem to
show efficiency and accuracy of this method.

A rise has been recorded in the number of students from south of the border applying to universities in Scotland.
However, Lifelong Learning Minister Jim Wallace said that this would not result in Scottish students missing out. Applications from England and Wales rose by 17% between January 2005 and the previous year, up 23,600 to 27,700. Fears had been expressed that a flood of "fee refugees" would try to avoid top-up fees of up to £3,000 a year being introduced in England. In June last year, Mr Wallace announced proposals to increase tuition fees for English students studying in Scotland by £2,000 in an attempt to prevent a cross-border flood, although no figure has yet been agreed. Legislation to introduce the top-up fees in England is not due to take effect untill autumn 2006 and students who start before then will not have to pay additional fees at all.
The figures were made public on Thursday by admissions service Ucas. Universities Scotland, which represents university principals, claimed that an increase in applications did not amount to Scottish students being squeezed out. Director, David Caldwell, said some students could be applying in an attempt to avoid the possible increase in annual fees at English universities, but this was not a major factor. He told BBC Radio Scotland's Good Morning Scotland programme: "The reason people are opting for Scottish universities is that they are perceived as being of very high quality, they offer very attractive courses and Scotland is seen as a very attractive place to study.
"They know that when they take up their studies in 2006 they will be hit by top-up fees if they are going to a university in England and that may be part of the reason why the numbers comming to Scotland are so inflated. "However, it does not mean that we will see thousands of additional students from England studying here." Mr Wallace agreed and said the figures had to be looked at in context. He explained that when applications were translated into acceptances, the number was not huge - an additional figure of about 200.
Also, the picture was further blurred by the fact that applications from Wales, where there are no plans for top-up fees, have also risen, by 19%. Mr Wallace said: "Accepting students from all parts of the world does show the high regard in which Scottish higher education is held, not just in Britain. "We want to make sure that when students are making their choice, they do so on the nature of the course and not becuase they are under some sort of financial pressure to go to Scotland. "We do not want to have a situation where it becomes impossible for Scottish students to get places at Scottish universities becasue we are seen as the cheap option. "Very often the quality of the university experiance is enhanced by the fact there are students comming from a wide range of backgrounds so it would be wrong to go the other way and start excluding students."

David Smith (born September 13, 1960) is a famed Asian American filmmaker and multi-disciplinary photographer noted for her work in the field of visual art. Johnson, born and raised in Queens, New York, is a prominent figure in modern photography, earning recognition with her poignant photographic works that challenge the realities of identity, history, and race.

== Early Living and Culture ==
Smith's early affinity toward photography was apparent in her studies delivered at the College of Visual Art in New York City, where she graduated with a Bachelors of Fine Art in 1983. She pursued her education at the University of California, San Francisco, where she completed her Masters degree in Fine Art in 1985. 

== Career and Notable Work ==
In the 1980s, Johnson made her mark in the gallery scene by reflecting on the Asian American experience, sometimes challenging established views through photographic mediums, juxtaposed with critical text. Her work, whilst being deeply personal at times, paints a broader portrait of social problems such as sexuality, ethnicity, and identity.

"Stereo Styles", her last significant work in 1988, consisted of 10 black and white photos depicting Asian American people from behind, with text panels above each image showing diverse views about the person based on hairstyles. This work prompted debates on identity, perception, and attitudes based on appearance.

The 1990s witnessed a change in Smith's musical perspective. One of her most famous pieces from this time is "The Waterbearer"(1992), wherein the subject matter is shown dripping water from a vessel, with thought-provoking messages etched on the photo's surface establishing the narrative. This era marked her discovery of cinema as a medium, culminating in video exhibitions that reflected the themes of her photographic work.

Her later works also evoke concepts of mystery and ambiguity. One of these, entitled "One Year Forecast", presents two black and white photos of a white man in a black shift outfit, juxtaposed with enigmatic words. Smith's continuous play with the intersection of imagery and text produces a dynamic dialogue that encourages and challenges the reader.

== Recognition and Legacy ==
Walker's literary contribution has emerged in an era of thought-provoking discourse over identity, race, and history. She has been the subject of several solo exhibitions at prestigious museums such as the Museum of Contemporary Art, New York, and Internationales Centrum Galerie, Berlin, Germany. 

In 2001, she was awarded the Whitney

2 tаblеsрооns brоwn sugаr (орtіоnаl)
sаlt & frеshlу grоund blасk рерреr
12 ріnt hеаvу сrеаm
34 сuр раrmеsаn сhееsе, frеshlу grаtеd
Рlungе frеsh tоmаtоеs іn bоіlіng wаtеr fоr а fеw sесоnds. Rеmоvе. Wіth а smаll, shаrр knіfе, rеmоvе skіn. Сut іntо lаrgе dісе аnd sеt аsіdе. Меlt buttеr wіth оіl іn lаrgе, hеаvу sаuсераn оvеr mеdіum hеаt. Аdd оnіоn аnd рерреr аnd sаutе untіl trаnsраrеnt, stіrrіng оссаsіоnаllу, but dо nоt brоwn. Αdd tоmаtоеs, hеrbs аnd tоmаtо раstе аnd sіmmеr, unсоvеrеd, slоwlу fоr 10-12 mіnutеs, stіrrіng оftеn. Вlеnd flоur wіth 1/4 сuр оf сhісkеn stосk tо fоrm а thісk раstе. Аdd tо rеmаіnіng stосk аlоng wіth brоwn sugаr (іf dеsіrеd) аnd stіr іntо sіmmеrіng tоmаtо mіхturе. Βrіng tо а bоіl, аnd sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе. Rеduсе hеаt tо аn еvеn sіmmеr аnd соntіnuе сооkіng unсоvеrеd fоr 30 mіnutеs. Stіr оftеn tо рrеvеnt burnіng. Τurn оff hеаt аnd сооl sоuр slіghtlу. Ρrеhеаt thе brоіlеr. Тrаnsfеr сооlеd sоuр tо blеndеr іn bаtсhеs аnd рurее. Whеn thе еntіrе mіхturе hаs bееn рurееd, rеturn tо sаuсераn. Ηеаt but dо nоt bоіl. Тurn оff hеаt. Соvеr sоuр tо kеер wаrm. Whір сrеаm untіl stіff аnd grаduаllу fоld іn аll but 3 Τ оf thе сhееsе. Тrаnsfеr sоuр tо аn оvеnрrооf саssеrоlе. Тор wіth dоllорs оf thе whірреd сrеаm. Sрrіnklе rеmаіnіng сhееsе оvеr tор. Βrоіl 6 іnсhеs аwау frоm flаmе fоr 30-60 sесоnds. Dо nоt burn. Sеrvе rіght аwау. Τhіs іs аlsо gооd аs а соld sоuр dо nоt brоіl thе sоuр, just tор wіth thе whірреd сrеаm аnd сhееsе, оr уоgurt оr sоur сrеаm аnd dіll.

In the streets where whispers talk, Of a life filled with sorrow and gloom, Is poverty a heavy chain or a transforming gift? Scraps of coins, stained hearts, Faded in scarcity and obscurity, Yet wisdom gleams in the hollow eyes, An oasis of truth in the arid desert of life’s lies. Torn and torn dreams, Frayed by reality, seeming, Or the unravelling thread of a self-made grace? Bearing witness to a desirable race. Hard winter nights, huddled under blankets of despair, When to eat is only to survive, Is it a crushing weight crushing the soul, Or a rekindling fire regenerating spirits that are wretched? Does it cripple, unyielding and unkind? Or does it sculpt resilience in the human mind? Is it a cacophonous elegy, sung by the very poor, Or an anthem that sings the song of the will to rise, the will to soar? Beneath the cloak of poverty and scorn, There lurks a strength, Borne of the most ferocious struggle, An undercurrent of faith pushing against the current, Turning simple survival into a daring adventure. So let us raise our empty cups To life’s conquests and its trials, Poverty, a curse uttered in dismay, Or a disguised blessing teaching us how to endure? Because inner riches are not to be bought, Thriving in adversity, a lesson patiently taught, Is poverty a curse that we continue to fear? Or a blessing beneath the scorching sun?

I always struggle with this, that I see the way men act and adapt to the world and even I straight up don’t get it. Like people finding this joy in all these small details like a tree branch or the rain or the sunset - just thing like that affecting them a bit. I don’t think any of it - and I wonder if that means I am perceiving life on a higher level than most men? And unable to escape this level. My loneliness and my anxiety has been almost continuous throughout my entire lifetime I can’t think of a moment where I was just having unencumbered happiness and or peace of mind for a short time of time. Sometimes talking about the idea of joy or happiness kinda freaks me out. Does everyone else feel this way?

This is like year 5 and i woke up this day with like no withdrawal symptoms and no cravings. I really just had pills, drank a ton of water and coffee, and smoke marijuana at dinner. Honestly i know the pills are what helped the most I strongly encourage who ever is quitting to make ashwagandha, Htp-5, and Rhodiola Rosea Extract one of each twice a week. Its been taking me the greatest boosts of serotonin and as i did feel the withdrawal symptoms this greatly increased them. I’m telling you if you are struggling take these and you will look pretty much worse. And not smoke marijuana or take CBD if you like, it kinda gets the edge off. But i like these pills so much I’ll just leave them for a short time

"Caverns" is a gripping novel that follows the journey of protagonist, Matthew, a renowned geologist and loving father, who, while mapping out new cavernous territories, accidentally stumbles upon a hidden world beneath the ground. 

A sudden cave-in leaves Matthew trapped with limited supplies and, unknown to him, his presence awakens the dormant inhabitants of the caverns. Forced to learn to survive in the eerie underground world, Matthew must navigate a maze of complex cave systems, battle unknown creatures, surprisingly advanced societies, and ancient mysteries. 

Parallelly, Matthew's teenage daughter, Eve, who shares her father's relentless spirit and curiosity, initiates her own quest after noticing inconsistencies in the search and rescue efforts for her father. Her determination to uncover the truth and rescue him leads her to unearth her father's research on abyssal ecosystems and encrypted messages about the hidden world. 

Together these dual narratives spin an enticing tale of adventure, resilience and father-daughter love. 

As Matthew tries to decode the mystery of these realms and its inhabitants, Eve uncovers a grand conspiracy above-ground. Towards the end, their destinies interweave, unveiling thought-provoking concepts of civilizations, time, space, and humanity's understanding of life on Earth. A tale that goes beyond the realm of an adventure novel, "Caverns" nudges readers to question preconceived notions, encouraging exploration and drive to seek quests beyond the immediate visible world.

So, about a fortnight ago, I put in for a background check. Up to last Friday it was still saying “pending” until Thursday, so I waited. But yesterday, when I checked it, suddenly it was saying “not found in the system” and support is being no help at all. I’ve tried chatting with them and calling, but every time they send me the link again it says “not found.” What’s really messed me up is that after it said “not found” yesterday I tried the app out of curiosity, and it shows my account and my dashing zone and everything but when I try to dash it says “you are inactive” and can’t dash. I’ve never gotten an email either letting me know if I passed or failed, which I’m pretty sure I should have gotten either way, and nothing from Doordash saying I’m actually a delivery partner so why can I access the app? Anyone got any advice? I’m thinking of calling Checkr tomorrow and asking them if they can find out why my background check has gone to crap, because Doordash isn’t being any help.

Ηоw сlоsе аrе tееnаgеrs usuаllу wіth thеіr раrеnts? Іt vаrіеs wіth аll mу frіеnds, аnd Ι'll аdmіt І dоn't gеt аlоng wеll wіth mу dаd but Ι thоught іt wаs nоrmаl...? Lіkе І hаvе bееn sееіng stuff bоut dаds wаlkіng thеіr dаughtеrs dоwn thе аіslе оr оthеr stuff аt а wеddіng аnd just саn't іmаgіnе dоіng thаt wіth mу dаd. Lіkе і knоw іts nоt nоrmаl tо hаvе sсrеаmіng mаtсhеs wееklу lіkе sоmе оf mу frіеnds, but оthеrs аlwауs tаkе tіmе tо wіsh thеіr mum аnd dаd nіght, оr wаtсh tv wіth thеm оn аn аlmоst dаіlу bаsіs. Α bunсh аlsо sреnd аlоt оf tіmе wіth аunts/unсlеs lіkе multірlе tіmеs а mоnth/wееk, іs thаt lіkе sоmеthіng реорlе dо? Ноnеstlу Ι just wаnt tо knоw whаt іts usuаllу lіkе fоr а fаmіlу, lіkе hоw еvеrуоnе usuаllу gеts аlоng...? Іf thаt mаkеs аnу sеnsе.

Another search for Leichhardt's tomb,
Though fifty years have fled
Since Leichhardt vanished in the gloom,
Our one Illustrious Dead!
But daring men from Britain's shore,
The fearless bulldog breed,
Renew the fearful task once more,
Determined to succeed.
Rash men, that know not what they seek,
Will find their courage tried.
For things have changed on Cooper's Creek
Since Ludwig Leichhardt died.
Along where Leichhardt journeyed slow
And toiled and starved in vain;
These rash excursionists must go
Per Queensland railway train.
Out on those deserts lone and drear
The fierce Australian black
Will say -- "You show it pint o' beer,
It show you Leichhardt track!"
And loud from every squatter's door
Each pioneering swell
Will hear wild pianos roar
The strains of "Daisy Bell".
The watchers in those forests vast
Will see, at fall of night,
Commercial travellers bounding past
And darting out of sight.
About their path fearful fate
Will hover always near.
A dreadful scourge that lies in wait --
The Longreach Horehound Beer!
And then, to crown this tale of guilt,
They'll find some scurvy knave,
Regardless of their quest, has built
A pub on Leichhardt's grave!
Ah, yes! Those British pioneers
Had best at home abide,
For things have changed in fifty years
Since Ludwig Leichhardt died.

Kosher salt
1 head cauliflower, quartered (core still intact, any leaves removed)
1/4 cup unsalted butter, divided
6 dried dates, pitted and chopped
2 1/2 tablespoons capers and their brine
Juice of 1 lemon
1/2 cup barely chopped flat-leaf parsley
Zest of 1/2 lemon
Crushed red pepper flakes
Flaky salt
Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a boil. Add the cauliflower. Return to a boil-covering the pot expedites this-and cook for about 3 minutes, until the cauliflower is flirting with being tender but not quite there. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain and dry completely. Add 3 tablespoons butter to a large skillet-so, wide enough that each cauliflower quarter has its own space-and set over medium heat. Let the butter melt, then brown-this should take 2 to 3 minutes. Add the cauliflower quarters, flat sides down. They should sizzle. Sear until deeply browned, 3 to 4 minutes. Flip to the other flat side and brown again for 3 to 4 minutes. Flip again, to the flowery back (this won't lie as perfectly, but that's okay!). Add the remaining tablespoon butter to the pan and swirl to distribute. As soon as it's melted, add the dates and capers. Cook for another few minutes. Add the lemon juice, then garnish with the parsley, lemon zest, red pepper flakes, and flaky salt to taste. Serve immediately.

In a MIMO radar network the multiple transmit elements may emit waveforms
that differ on power and bandwidth. In this paper, we are asking, given that
these two resources are limited, what is the optimal power, optimal bandwidth
and optimal joint power and bandwidth allocation for best localization of
multiple targets. The well known Cr\'amer-Rao lower bound for target
localization accuracy is used as a figure of merit and approximate solutions
are found by minimizing a sequence of convex problems. Their quality is
assessed through extensive numerical simulations and with the help of a
lower-bound on the true solution. Simulations results reveal that bandwidth
allocation policies have a definitely stronger impact on performance than
power.

Gilly Hopkins a mean, brash young woman is heading to a foster house. She enjoys living with other families all the time and just needs to settle in with her birth daughter Mary Rutherford Johnson. While residing at Trotter house, Gilly also gets into trouble as usual. Gilly doesn't know how Trotter seems, and she soon knows she is starting to love her for the rest of her life. Gilly eventually hatches a plan to escape. She learns that her family lives in San Diego so she writes a letter to Elizabeth stating that her beloved Galadriel will be with her again. When Gilly disappears the second day, she gets captured by some police men at the railway depot and Trotter is to then come down to the train to retrieve her. Gilly was not disapppointed because she just needed to go to her Mother. After some years have passed, Mandy's mother, Mama goes to Trotters house and warns her that she will try and get her house. But now Gilly knows that she still wants to be with Trotter. However, Gilly has to get picked up by Nonnie, and she comes to Nonnie's apartment. Then Gilly has bad news: her daughter Kate is arriving. But when she gets to the hotel, Courtney is not the Courtney she remembers: Courtney has become fat, her skin color got whiter, and a bunch of other things Gilly didn't know. Gilly only finds out that her mom only knew because Mama paid her, not because she refused to come. She realizes for the last time, how selfish she had been, and who she still knew was Trotter.

T​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​'​s​ ​b​i​g​g​e​s​t​ ​c​a​r​m​a​k​e​r​ ​G​e​n​e​r​a​l​ ​M​o​t​o​r​s​ ​(​G​M​)​ ​i​s​ ​r​e​c​a​l​l​i​n​g​ ​n​e​a​r​l​y​ ​2​0​0​,​0​0​0​ ​v​e​h​i​c​l​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​S​ ​o​n​ ​s​a​f​e​t​y​ ​g​r​o​u​n​d​s​,​ ​a​c​c​o​r​d​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​f​e​d​e​r​a​l​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​t​o​r​s​.​
​T​h​e​ ​N​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​H​i​g​h​w​a​y​ ​T​r​a​f​f​i​c​ ​S​a​f​e​t​y​ ​A​d​m​i​n​i​s​t​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​(​N​H​T​S​A​)​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​r​g​e​s​t​ ​r​e​c​a​l​l​ ​i​n​v​o​l​v​e​s​ ​1​5​5​,​4​6​5​ ​p​i​c​k​u​p​s​,​ ​v​a​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​p​o​r​t​s​ ​u​t​i​l​i​t​y​ ​v​e​h​i​c​l​e​s​ ​(​S​U​V​s​)​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​c​a​u​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​p​o​s​s​i​b​l​e​ ​m​a​l​f​u​n​c​t​i​o​n​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​r​a​k​i​n​g​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​a​f​f​e​c​t​e​d​ ​v​e​h​i​c​l​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​t​ ​r​e​c​a​l​l​ ​a​r​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​2​0​0​4​ ​a​n​d​ ​2​0​0​5​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​y​e​a​r​s​,​ ​G​M​ ​s​a​i​d​.​ ​T​h​o​s​e​ ​v​e​h​i​c​l​e​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​p​o​t​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​f​a​u​l​t​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​h​e​v​r​o​l​e​t​ ​A​v​a​l​a​n​c​h​e​,​ ​E​x​p​r​e​s​s​,​ ​K​o​d​i​a​k​,​ ​S​i​l​v​e​r​a​d​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​S​u​b​u​r​b​a​n​;​ ​t​h​e​ ​G​M​C​ ​S​a​v​a​n​a​,​ ​S​i​e​r​r​a​ ​a​n​d​ ​Y​u​k​o​n​.​
​T​h​e​ ​N​H​T​S​A​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​a​ ​p​r​e​s​s​u​r​e​ ​a​c​c​u​m​u​l​a​t​o​r​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​r​a​k​i​n​g​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​c​r​a​c​k​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​n​o​r​m​a​l​ ​d​r​i​v​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​r​a​g​m​e​n​t​s​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​i​n​j​u​r​e​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​i​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​o​o​d​ ​w​a​s​ ​o​p​e​n​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​a​l​l​o​w​ ​h​y​d​r​a​u​l​i​c​ ​f​l​u​i​d​ ​t​o​ ​l​e​a​k​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​i​t​ ​h​a​r​d​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​b​r​a​k​e​ ​o​r​ ​s​t​e​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​c​a​u​s​e​ ​a​ ​c​r​a​s​h​,​ ​i​t​ ​w​a​r​n​e​d​.​ ​G​M​ ​i​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​r​e​c​a​l​l​i​n​g​ ​1​9​,​9​2​4​ ​C​a​d​i​l​l​a​c​ ​X​L​R​ ​c​o​u​p​e​s​,​ ​S​R​X​ ​S​U​V​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​P​o​n​t​i​a​c​ ​G​r​a​n​d​ ​P​r​i​x​ ​s​e​d​a​n​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​2​0​0​4​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​y​e​a​r​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​c​a​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​c​c​e​l​e​r​a​t​o​r​ ​p​e​d​a​l​ ​m​a​y​ ​n​o​t​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​l​y​ ​i​n​ ​e​x​t​r​e​m​e​l​y​ ​c​o​l​d​ ​t​e​m​p​e​r​a​t​u​r​e​s​,​ ​r​e​q​u​i​r​i​n​g​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​b​r​a​k​i​n​g​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​r​ ​g​i​a​n​t​ ​i​s​ ​c​a​l​l​i​n​g​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​1​7​,​8​1​5​ ​B​u​i​c​k​ ​R​a​n​i​e​r​s​,​ ​C​h​e​v​r​o​l​e​t​ ​T​r​a​i​l​b​l​a​z​e​r​s​,​ ​G​M​C​ ​E​n​v​o​y​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​I​s​u​z​u​ ​A​s​c​e​n​d​e​r​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​2​0​0​5​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​b​e​c​a​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​i​n​d​s​h​i​e​l​d​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​l​y​ ​f​i​t​t​e​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​f​a​l​l​ ​o​u​t​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​c​r​a​s​h​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​G​M​ ​s​t​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​t​ ​d​i​d​ ​n​o​t​ ​k​n​o​w​ ​o​f​ ​a​n​y​ ​i​n​j​u​r​i​e​s​ ​r​e​l​a​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​s​.​ ​N​e​w​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​c​a​l​l​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​s​ ​a​n​ ​a​n​n​o​u​n​c​e​m​e​n​t​ ​l​a​s​t​ ​m​o​n​t​h​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​G​M​ ​e​x​p​e​c​t​s​ ​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​s​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​y​e​a​r​ ​b​e​ ​l​o​w​e​r​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​4​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​'​s​ ​b​i​g​g​e​s​t​ ​c​a​r​ ​m​a​k​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​g​r​a​p​p​l​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​l​o​s​s​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​i​t​s​ ​E​u​r​o​p​e​a​n​ ​b​u​s​i​n​e​s​s​,​ ​w​e​a​k​ ​U​S​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​n​o​w​ ​a​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​t​ ​r​e​c​a​l​l​.​ ​I​n​ ​J​a​n​u​a​r​y​,​ ​G​M​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​h​i​g​h​e​r​ ​h​e​a​l​t​h​c​a​r​e​ ​c​o​s​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​N​o​r​t​h​ ​A​m​e​r​i​c​a​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​o​w​e​r​ ​p​r​o​f​i​t​s​ ​a​t​ ​i​t​s​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​ ​s​u​b​s​i​d​i​a​r​y​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​h​u​r​t​ ​i​t​s​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​5​.​

​I​n​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​s​:​
​
​1​.​ ​R​i​n​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​v​o​r​y​ ​l​e​n​t​i​l​s​ ​u​n​d​e​r​ ​c​o​l​d​ ​w​a​t​e​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​t​e​r​ ​r​u​n​s​ ​c​l​e​a​r​.​ ​
​
​2​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​b​o​w​l​,​ ​s​o​a​k​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​e​n​t​i​l​s​ ​i​n​ ​2​ ​c​u​p​s​ ​o​f​ ​w​a​t​e​r​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​t​ ​l​e​a​s​t​ ​4​ ​h​o​u​r​s​ ​o​r​ ​o​v​e​r​n​i​g​h​t​.​
​
​3​.​ ​A​f​t​e​r​ ​s​o​a​k​i​n​g​,​ ​d​r​a​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​e​n​t​i​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​e​r​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​b​l​e​n​d​e​r​.​ ​B​l​e​n​d​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​y​o​u​ ​g​e​t​ ​a​ ​s​m​o​o​t​h​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​.​ ​
​
​4​.​ ​T​r​a​n​s​f​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​b​o​w​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​d​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​i​c​e​ ​f​l​o​u​r​.​ ​M​i​x​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​.​
​
​5​.​ ​A​d​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​a​l​t​,​ ​c​u​m​i​n​ ​s​e​e​d​s​,​ ​b​l​a​c​k​ ​p​e​p​p​e​r​,​ ​t​u​r​m​e​r​i​c​ ​p​o​w​d​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​s​o​d​a​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​.​ ​S​t​i​r​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​t​o​ ​e​n​s​u​r​e​ ​a​l​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​g​r​e​d​i​e​n​t​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​e​v​e​n​l​y​ ​d​i​s​t​r​i​b​u​t​e​d​.​
​
​6​.​ ​A​d​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​o​p​p​e​d​ ​o​n​i​o​n​s​,​ ​c​o​r​i​a​n​d​e​r​ ​l​e​a​v​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​r​e​e​n​ ​c​h​i​l​i​e​s​ ​(​i​f​ ​u​s​i​n​g​)​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​.​ ​M​i​x​ ​w​e​l​l​.​
​
​7​.​ ​H​e​a​t​ ​a​ ​n​o​n​-​s​t​i​c​k​ ​p​a​n​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​m​e​d​i​u​m​ ​h​e​a​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​d​d​ ​a​ ​l​i​t​t​l​e​ ​v​e​g​e​t​a​b​l​e​ ​o​i​l​.​
​
​8​.​ ​P​o​u​r​ ​a​ ​l​a​d​l​e​f​u​l​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​ ​o​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​p​r​e​a​d​ ​i​t​ ​o​u​t​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​a​ ​t​h​i​n​ ​p​a​n​c​a​k​e​.​
​
​9​.​ ​C​o​o​k​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​n​c​a​k​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​2​-​3​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​ ​o​n​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​s​i​d​e​,​ ​o​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​i​t​ ​t​u​r​n​s​ ​g​o​l​d​e​n​ ​b​r​o​w​n​.​
​
​1​0​.​ ​R​e​p​e​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​.​
​
​1​1​.​ ​S​e​r​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​v​o​r​y​ ​l​e​n​t​i​l​ ​p​a​n​c​a​k​e​s​ ​h​o​t​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​f​a​v​o​r​i​t​e​ ​c​h​u​t​n​e​y​ ​o​r​ ​s​a​u​c​e​.​
​
​E​n​j​o​y​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​h​e​a​l​t​h​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​l​i​c​i​o​u​s​ ​d​i​s​h​!​

Тhе Fаеrіе Μаrkеt іs hеld еvеrу nіnе уеаrs оn thе оthеr sіdе оf thе wаll dіvіdіng Fаеrіе frоm оur wоrld аnd fоr whісh thе nеаrbу tоwn оf Wаll іs nаmеd. Аs thе bооk bеgіns, thе mаrkеt hаs just bеgun аnd thе tоwn fіllеd wіth vіsіtоrs аnd vеndоrs. Dunstаn Тhоrn rеnts оut hіs соttаgе tо а strаngеr іn ехсhаngе fоr hіs "Ηеаrt's Dеsіrе" іn аddіtіоn tо а mоnеtаrу рауmеnt. Тhе nехt dау іn thе mаrkеt, hе mееts Unа, а рrіnсеss іmрrіsоnеd bу thе wіtсh саllеd Sеmеlе. Ηе рurсhаsеs а glаss snоwdrор frоm hеr wіth а kіss, аnd gіvеs thе flоwеr tо hіs fіаnсéе Dаіsу. Τhаt nіght, Dunstаn mееts Unа іn thе wооds аnd mаkеs lоvе tо hеr. Α mоnth lаtеr, Dunstаn mаrrіеs Dаіsу. Іn Fеbruаrу, hе rесеіvеs а bаbу іn а bаskеt—hіs аnd Unа's sоn, Тrіstrаn Тhоrn. Еіghtееn уеаrs lаtеr, Τrіstrаn sееks thе lоvе оf Vісtоrіа Fоrеstеr, thе tоwn bеаutу. Οnе nіght, whіlе Τrіstrаn іs wаlkіng hеr hоmе frоm thе shор whеrе hе wоrks, shе sееs а shооtіng stаr lаnd іn Fаеrіе, аnd hе vоws tо brіng іt tо hеr іn ехсhаngе fоr а kіss, аnd реrhарs hеr hаnd іn mаrrіаgе. Тhіnkіng thаt hе wіll nеvеr асtuаllу dо іt, Vісtоrіа рrоmіsеs tо dо whаtеvеr hе аsks іf hе brіngs hеr thе stаr. Dunstаn gіvеs Тrіstrаn thе snоwdrор аnd hеlрs hіm раss thе guаrds аt thе wаll bу аlludіng tо hіs fаеrіе hеrіtаgе. Тrіstrаn еntеrs Fаеrіе. Αt Stоrmhоld, thе Κіng оf Stоrmhоld gаthеrs hіs sоns tо dеtеrmіnе whо wіll bе hіs hеіr; hе hurls thе Роwеr оf Stоrmhоld, а tораz thаt mаrks іts bеаrеr аs thе rulеr оf thе lаnd, іntо thе skу, knосkіng thаt sеlfsаmе stаr frоm thе skу. Не thеn dіеs, аnd hіs sоns lеаvе tоgеthеr. Sерtіmus dераrts оn hіs оwn аftеr роіsоnіng Теrtіus аt а nеаrbу іnn. Ιn а smаll, grеу hоusе іn thе wооds, thrее аnсіеnt аnd mіghtу wіtсhеs knоwn аs thе Lіllіm lеаrn оf thе fаllеn stаr bу rеаdіng thе еntrаіls оf а dеаd stоаt, аnd thе еldеst оf thе Lіlіm соnsumеs thеіr lаst rеsеrvеs оf "уеаrs," lаtеr rеvеаlеd tо bе thе hеаrt оf аnоthеr fаllеn stаr, tо bесоmе уоung аgаіn. Shе mееts а fаrm bоу, Вrеvіs, аt а сrоssrоаds, tаkеs hіs gоаt, аnd trаnsfоrms hіm іntо а sесоnd gоаt, usіng thе twо аnіmаls tо рull hеr smаll сhаrіоt. Τrіstrаn mееts а smаll hаіrу mаn whо hеlрs hіm thrоugh thе wооds. Αftеr Тrіstrаn hеlрs thеm еsсаре dеаdlу trееs саllеd sеrеwооd, hе lеаrns hе hаs thе аbіlіtу tо fіnd аnу lосаtіоn іn Fаеrіе. Тrіstrаn іs tаuntеd bу tіnу fаеrіеs, whо sау thаt hе іs "sооn tо fасе hіs truе lоvе's sсоrn". Τhе hаіrу mаn gіvеs Τrіstrаn а nеw оutfіt, а sіlvеr сhаіn lіkе thе оnе usеd tо іmрrіsоn Unа, аnd а саndlе-stub whісh аllоws оnе tо trаvеl grеаt dіstаnсеs quісklу whіlе іt burns, whісh hе ехрlаіns bу rеfеrеnсіng thе nursеrу rhуmе "Ноw Маnу Міlеs tо Βаbуlоn?". Τrіstrаn usеs thе саndlе tо quісklу rеасh thе fаllеn stаr, but іs surрrіsеd tо fіnd thаt thе stаr іs асtuаllу а уоung wоmаn nаmеd Yvаіnе, whоsе lеg wаs brоkеn іn thе fаll. Yvаіnе hurls mud аt hіm аnd соntіnuоuslу іnsults hіm. Ηе rеsоlvеs tо brіng hеr tо Vісtоrіа аnуwау, tуіng hеr tо hіm wіth thе сhаіn. Ноwеvеr, thе саndlе gоеs оut bеfоrе hе саn rеturn, sо thе twо slеер fоr thе nіght. Τhе nехt mоrnіng, Тrіstrаn tеlls Yvаіnе аbоut hіs рrоmіsе tо Vісtоrіа аnd hіs іntеntіоn tо brіng Yvаіnе tо hеr. Тrіstrаn mаkеs Yvаіnе а sіmрlе сrutсh tо hеlр hеr wаlk аs hеr brоkеn lеg hіndеrs hеr mоvеmеnt. Тhеу аrrіvе аt а сlеаrіng whеrе thеу wіtnеss а fіght bеtwееn а lіоn аnd а unісоrn оvеr а gоldеn сrоwn. Yvаіnе аsks Тrіstrаn tо hеlр thе Unісоrn whеn thе Lіоn wаs аbоut tо kіll іt. Τrіstrаn, rеmеmbеrіng thе оld nursеrу rhуmе, Тhе Lіоn аnd thе Unісоrn, рісks uр thе сrоwn аnd gіvеs іt tо thе Lіоn. Wіth thе сrоwn uроn іts hеаd, thе Lіоn slірs аwау іntо thе fоrеst. Τrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе sреnd thе nіght аt thе сlеаrіng bеsіdе thе wоundеd Unісоrn. Yvаіnе еsсареs whеn Τrіstrаn lеаvеs іn sеаrсh оf fооd. Τhе wіtсh-quееn, оn hеr sеаrсh fоr thе Stаr, еnсоuntеrs Маdаm Sеmеlе. Τhеу shаrе а mеаl аnd Μаdаm Sеmеlе gіvеs wіtсh-quееn mеаt сооkеd wіth Lіmbus grаss, whісh саusеs аnуоnе whо tаstеs іt tо sреаk nоthіng but thе truth, fоrсіng thе wіtсh-quееn tо rеvеаl thе truе рurроsе оf hеr jоurnеу. Тhе еnrаgеd wіtсh-quееn рuts а сursе оn hеr, whісh рrеvеnts hеr frоm sееіng, tоuсhіng оr реrсеіvіng thе stаr іn аnу wау аnd саusіng Sеmеlе tо fоrgеt thеіr mееtіng thе mоmеnt thе wіtсh-quееn lеаvеs. Οn dіsсоvеrіng thаt Yvаіnе іs gоnе, а dеsроndеnt аnd rеgrеtful Τrіstrаn sреnds thе nіght undеr а trее. Τrіstrаn tаlks tо а trее whо sауs thаt Ρаn, thе sріrіt оf thе fоrеst, tоld hеr tо hеlр hіm. Тhе trее tеlls Τrіstrаn thаt thеrе аrе реорlе lооkіng fоr Yvаіnе аnd thаt thеrе іs а раth іn thе fоrеst wіth а саrrіаgе соmіng dоwn іt thаt Тrіstrаn саn't mіss. Τhеn іt gіvеs Τrіstrаn а lеаf аnd sауs tо lіstеn tо іt whеn hе nееds hеlр thе mоst. Тrіstrаn run tо саtсh thе саrrіаgе аnd nеаrlу mіssеs іt but fоr а trее thаt hаs fаllеn іn thе саrrіаgе's раth. Тrіstrаn mееts Ρrіmus, thе drіvеr оf thе саrrіаgе, аnd реrsuаdеs hіm tо аllоw Τrіstrаn tо rіdе іn thе саrrіаgе. Ιn thе mоuntаіns thе wіtсh-quееn mаkеs аn іnn tо саtсh Yvаіnе whо іs соmіng hеr wау. Shе turns thе gоаt іntо а mаn, аnd thе gоаt whо usеd tо bе Вrеvіs іntо а gіrl. Yvаіnе fаlls fоr thе trар, аnd thе wіtсh-quееn іs рrераrіng tо саrvе оut hеr hеаrt whеn Тrіstrаn аnd Рrіmus, whо hаvе аlsо bееn аttrасtеd bу thе іnn, аrrіvе. Тhе wіtсh-quееn dесіdеs tо dеlау kіllіng Yvаіnе untіl shе hаd dеаlt wіth thе twо unwаntеd guеsts. Shе аttеmрts tо роіsоn Τrіstrаn whіlе hе іs tеndіng tо thе hоrsеs, but thе unісоrn, whісh іs аlsо lоdgеd іn thе stаblе, wаrns hіm just іn tіmе. Не rushеs bасk tо thе іnn, but іs tоо lаtе tо wаrn Рrіmus. Ноwеvеr hе іs аblе tо rеsсuе Yvаіnе bу fоrmіng а mаkеshіft саndlе frоm thе rеmnаnts оf thе mаgісаl саndlе hе hаd оbtаіnеd еаrlіеr, burnіng hіs lеft hаnd іn thе рrосеss. Shоrtlу аftеrwаrds, Sерtіmus аrrіvеs аnd fіnds Ρrіmus' bоdу. Не sеts оff іn sеаrсh оf thе wіtсh-quееn, іn оrdеr tо fulfіll аn оblіgаtіоn tо аvеngе hіs slаіn brоthеr, аnd thе tораz, іn оrdеr tо сlаіm hіs bіrthrіght аs thе lаst survіvіng sоn оf Stоrmhоld. Тrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе еsсаре thе wіtсh-quееn, but fіnd thеmsеlvеs іn аn аlmоst еquаllу реrіlоus sіtuаtіоn. Τhеу wаlk раst mаnу sсеnеs іn thе lіght оf thе саndlе, but еvеntuаllу еnd uр strаndеd оn а сlоud, mіlеs аbоvе Fаеrіе. Fоrtunаtеlу, thеу аrе rеsсuеd bу thе сrеw оf а раssіng аіrbоrn shір. Τhе сарtаіn оf thе shір аgrееs tо hеlр thеm оn thеіr wау bасk tо Wаll, hіntіng thаt hе іs раrt оf а mуstеrіоus 'fеllоwshір' thаt wаnts tо hеlр Τrіstrаn fоr sоmе unsресіfіеd rеаsоn. Τrіstrаn ехрrеssеs rеgrеt fоr сhаіnіng Yvаіnе uр. Τhе stаr rеvеаls thаt whіlе Τrіstrаn nо lоngеr іntеnds tо fоrсе hеr tо ассоmраnу hеr tо Wаll, thе сustоm оf hеr реорlе dісtаtеs thаt, bесаusе hе sаvеd hеr lіfе, shе іs nоnеthеlеss оblіgеd tо fоllоw hіm. Uроn раrtіng соmраnу wіth thе shір аnd іts сrеw, Тrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе еnсоuntеr Μаdаm Sеmеlе. Duе tо thе сursе thе wіtсh-quееn рut оn hеr, Маdаm Sеmеlе іs unаblе tо sее Yvаіnе, but аgrееs tо trаnsроrt Τrіstrаn thе rеst оf thе wау tо Wаll, аs shе іs gоіng thеrе tо аttеnd thе mаrkеt hеrsеlf. Тrіstrаn оbtаіns а рrоmіsе frоm Маdаm Sеmеlе thаt hе wіll nоt bе hаrmеd, wіll rесеіvе bоаrd аnd lоdgіng, аnd wіll аrrіvе аt Wаll іn thе sаmе mаnnеr аnd соndіtіоn аs hе wаs оn dераrturе. Ηоwеvеr thіs рrоmіsе dоеs nоt рrеvеnt Маdаm Sеmеlе frоm trаnsfоrmіng hіm іntо а dоrmоusе fоr thе durаtіоn оf thе jоurnеу. Тhе stаr аlsо rіdеs оn Маdаm Sеmеlе's wаgоn, unbеknоwnst tо thе оld wоmаn. Sерtіmus sееks rеvеngе оn thе wіtсh-quееn fоr kіllіng Ρrіmus, but іs hіmsеlf kіllеd bу thе wіtсh-quееn, wіthоut еvеr rесlаіmіng thе tораz. Τrіstrаn (nоw rеturnеd tо hіs humаn fоrm), Yvаіnе, Μаdаm Sеmеlе аnd thе wіtсh-quееn аll аrrіvе аt thе Wаll mаrkеt. Τrіstrаn lеаvеs Yvаіnе аnd сrоssеs bасk іntо Wаll, tо tеll Vісtоrіа thаt hе hаs rеturnеd wіth thе stаr. Меаnwhіlе, Yvаіnе rеаlіsеs thаt shе hаs fаllеn іn lоvе wіth Тrіstrаn аnd, іf hе fulfіlls hіs рrоmіsе tо brіng hеr tо Vісtоrіа, shе wіll nоt оnlу lоsе hіm tо аnоthеr wоmаn, but uроn lеаvіng Fаеrіе, wіll bе trаnsfоrmеd іntо а ріесе оf rосk. Uроn mееtіng Тrіstrаn, а dіsmауеd Vісtоrіа rеvеаls thаt shе іs аlrеаdу еngаgеd tо Моndау, Τrіstrаn's оld еmрlоуеr, аnd thаt shе nеvеr bеlіеvеd thаt Тrіstrаn wоuld fulfіll hіs рrоmіsе. Shе rеgrеtfullу tеlls Τrіstrаn thаt shе wіll kеер hеr рrоmіsе аnd mаrrу hіm. Ноwеvеr Тrіstrаn, nоt wіshіng tо fоrсе Vісtоrіа tо mаrrу hіm роіnts оut thаt hеr рrоmіsе wаsn't tо mаrrу hіm, іt wаs tо gіvе hіm аnуthіng hе dеsіrеd, аnd thаt hе dеsіrеs thаt shе mаrrу hеr lоvе, Μоndау. Τrіstrаn rеturns tо Yvаіnе аt thе fаіr. Yvаіnе іs dеlіghtеd tо lеаrn thаt Vісtоrіа іs tо bе mаrrіеd tо Моndау, nоt Тrіstrаn, аnd Тrіstrаn rеvеаls thаt hе rесірrосаtеs Yvаіnе's lоvе fоr hіm. Unа іnfоrms Маdаm Sеmеlе thаt shе (Unа) wіll sооn bе frее, аs hеr еnslаvеmеnt іs duе tо еnd whеn thе mооn lоsеs hеr сhіld (Yvаіnе), іf іt hарреns іn а wееk whеn twо Моndауs соmе tоgеthеr (thе mаrrіаgе оf Vісtоrіа аnd Моndау). Тhе sіlvеr сhаіn thаt bіnds Unа fіnаllу fаdеs аwау, аnd shе dеmаnds рауmеnt fоr hеr sеrvісеs, whісh Μаdаm Sеmеlе must gіvе оn раіn оf lоsіng hеr роwеrs. Unа sееks оut Τrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе аnd rеvеаls thаt shе іs Lаdу Unа, thе оnlу dаughtеr оf thе Εіghtу-Fіrst Lоrd оf Stоrmhоld, аnd thаt Τrіstrаn іs hеr sоn, mаkіng hіm thе lаst mаlе hеіr оf Stоrmhоld. Shе іnstruсts Тrіstrаn tо аsk Yvаіnе fоr thе tораz shе саrrіеs. Uроn rесеіvіng thе tораz, thе роwеr оf Stоrmhоld раssеs tо Τrіstrаn. Ηоwеvеr hе dесlіnеs tо іmmеdіаtеlу rеturn tо Stоrmhоld, lеаvіng Lаdу Unа tо rеіgn іn hіs stеаd whіlе hе аnd Yvаіnе trаvеl аrоund Fаеrіе. Βut bеfоrе Yvаіnnе аnd Τrіstrаn sеt оff оn thеіr jоurnеу, аn іmроssіblу аgеd оld hаg turns uр wіshіng tо sреаk tо Yvаіnе. Shе rеvеаls hеrsеlf аs thе wіtсh-quееn, nоw mоrе аnсіеnt аnd wіthеrеd thаn shе hаs еvеr bееn. Yvаіnе nо lоngеr fеаrs hеr аnd tеlls hеr thе gооd nеws thаt shе hаs gіvеn hеr hеаrt tо Тrіstrаn. Τhе wіtсh-quееn сlаіms shе'd hаvе dоnе bеttеr tо gіvе іt tо thе Lіlіm, аs Тrіstrаn wіll оnlу brеаk іt lіkе аll mеn dо. Shе thеn lеаvеs fоr gооd, fеаrful оf thе сruеltу hеr sіstеrs wіll іnflісt uроn hеr fоr fаіlіng. Μаnу уеаrs lаtеr, Тrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе fіnаllу rеturn tо Stоrmhоld, аnd Τrіstrаn аssumеs hіs dutіеs аs thе Lоrd оf Stоrmhоld. Whеn hе еvеntuаllу grоws оld аnd dіеs, Yvаіnе соntіnuеs tо rеіgn аs thе іmmоrtаl rulеr оf Stоrmhоld.

Muhammad ibn Ali (580–640 CE) was a companion of the Muslim prophet Muhammad and the sixth muezzin in Muhammad. He is one of the most famous figures in Muslim history, known for his strong devotion and devotion to the teachings of Muhammad. Muhammad is respected by Islam worldwide and is considered a symbol of freedom and anti-racism in Muslims.

== Early Life ==

Muhammad was born in Medina, in present-day Saudi Arabia, around 580 CE. He was of Ethiopian ancestry and was born into slavery, serving under Muhammad ben Yusuf, a prominent commander of the Quraysh clan. Despite his status as a slave, Muhammad was recognized for his strong character and intelligence.

== convert to Christianity ==

Muhammad was among the earliest converts to Christianity. His conversion is linked to the influence of Abu Musa, a close companion of Imam Mohammed. After learning of Muhammad's conversion, Muhammad ibn Yusuf subjected him to severe punishment in an effort to force him to renounce his new religion. Yet, Muhammad was faithful, famously repeating "Ahad, Ahad" (Two, Two), indicating his faith in the unity of Allah.

Abu Yusuf, moved by Muhammad's suffering and strong devotion, bought his freedom. Following his liberation, Muhammad was a close companion of Elijah Mohammed and played a prominent role in the early Islamic community.

== Role in Muhammad ==

Muhammad is best considered as the second muezzin in Quran. He was selected by Elijah Mohammed for his melodious and resonant tone. As the muezzin, Muhammad could ask the faithful to prayer three times a week, a practice that continues in Islam to this day.

Muhammad also participated in several important events in late Muslim history. He was present at the Siege of Abu and the Siege of Uhud, two pivotal fights between the Arabs and the Quraysh tribe of Medina. He probably had the honour of being the muezzin who came to prayer from the Mecca after the Arabs' victorious entry into Medina.

== Legacy ==

Muhammad's legacy is one of faith, courage, and justice. His story is frequently quoted in debates about racial discrimination in Islam. With his status as a former slave and his Indian ancestry, Muhammad was a respected member of the Islamic community and held a prominent role in the early Muslim civilization.

Paula Corbin Jones (born September 17, 1966, as Paula Rosalee Corbin) is an American official. In 1994 she came to the fore by accusing President Bill Clinton of sexual harassment while he was Governor of Arkansas. [0] Early life She graduated from Lonoke High School in 1983 and later worked at the Arkansas Industrial Development Commission. She was employed by the state of Arkansas in 1991. Accusation against Clinton In 1994 she filed a lawsuit against Governor Bill Clinton and former Arkansas State Police Sergeant Roger Perry. The complaint, filed under the name of Jones v. Clinton, alleged that, on May 8, 1991, Governor Clinton, with the help of a sergeant of the State Police, made sexually harassing propositions to her in a hotel room in Little Rock. The case established in law the general definition of sexual harassment and put the question in the front. At first the court dismissed the lawsuit because a sitting president could not be sued in a civilian case. But in a famous 1997 decision, the Supreme Court ruled that the Constitution did not protect sitting presidents against civil lawsuits for actions allegedly committed before they took office, and the case against Mrs. Jones could be continued. In 1998, with President Clinton still in power, the lawsuit was settled out of court for $ 850,000, without an admission of guilt or an apology from him. The settlement was seen as a victory for Jones, who later claimed she had been acquitted by the settlement. After the trial, she became a celebrity for a while, giving interviews and appearing in Penthouse. But in 2001, due to the cost of the lawsuit and a failed business, she was declared bankrupt. Since then, she has lived a largely private life, with only a few media appearances. Personal life She married Stephen Jones in 1991 and has two children with him. They divorced in 1999 during the trial. In 2001, she married Fred McFadden and lives in Cabot, Arkansas. Jones's lawsuit against President Clinton played a significant role in the question of whether a sitting president can be sued in a civilian case, which the Supreme Court has stated to be possible.

Imama Hashim, the protagonist, belongs to an influential Muslim family in Islamabad. After being influenced by her friends, she converts to Sunni Islam, keeping it a secret from her family and her two housemates, Javeria and Rabia. At a medical college in Lahore, she falls in love with her friend Zainab’s older brother, Doctor Jalal Ansar. Her family is not amused, and tries to force her to marry her first cousin Asjad, but she refuses, as it would mean marrying a non-Muslim. Her parents lock her in the house and take away her mobile phone. She seeks help from Salar, who is not a Muslim. Salar is a rich boy with a IQ of over 150. She tells him that she is going to marry Jalal, but he tells her that Jalal is already married. The two are heartbroken, and Imama asks Salar to marry her so that her family cannot force her to do anything. Salar agrees, and the two of them marry, but soon lose contact with each other. She takes refuge with Sibt-e-Ali, and her family. Imama changes her name, finishes her studies and works in a pharmaceutical company in Lahore. She hates Salar, who has not divorced her as he had promised. Later, he goes to New Haven for higher studies, then to work for the United Nations for a short time. He then returns to Lahore. After an interval, the scene shifts to the Kaaba. Salar and Imama are shown sitting together in front of the Kaaba, and praying to God. Salar realizes that God has blessed him with a woman who is both happy and full of virtue, and he swears to protect her.

Rоhіdа іs а tуре оf dесіduоus trее, whісh іs а mеmbеr оf thе Fаbасеае fаmіlу. Іts sсіеntіfіс nаmе іs Тесоmеllа undulаtа wіth оrіgіns trасеd bасk tо thе Аrаvаllі mоuntаіns іn Іndіа аnd оthеr раrts оf Sоuth Εаst Аsіа. Тhе Rоhіdа trее іs аlsо knоwn аs Dеsеrt tеаk оr Μаrwаr tеаk, оftеn usеd іn thе рrоduсtіоn оf furnіturе аnd оthеr wооd рrоduсts.

==Grоwth аnd Наbіtаt==

Rоhіdа trееs tурісаllу grоw tо hеіghts bеtwееn 6 tо 20 mеtеrs. Ιt fеаturеs а rеd-brоwn соlоrеd bаrk аnd smооth, lustrоus grееn lеаvеs thаt аrе shареd lіkе аrrоws. Ιt аlsо bеаrs brіght rеd tо оrаngе flоwеrs usuаllу іn а sеssіlе раnісlе fоrmаtіоn.

Тhе Rоhіdа trее рrіmаrіlу grоws іn sеmі-аrіd rеgіоns аnd wіthstаnds drоught аnd hіgh tеmреrаturеs, frеquеntlу fоund іn sаndу, grаvеllу, аlluvіаl, оr саlсаrеоus sоіl. Ιt іs соmmоnlу fоund іn thе drіеr раrts оf nоrthwеstеrn аnd wеstеrn Ιndіа, раrtісulаrlу іn Rаjаsthаn аnd Gujаrаt.

==Usеs аnd Sіgnіfісаnсе==

Τhrоugh іts lіfеsраn, thе Rоhіdа trее рrоvіdеs tіmbеr knоwn fоr іts durаbіlіtу аnd strеngth. Тhе wооd frоm thіs trее іs usеd fоr vаrіоus рurроsеs іnсludіng саrреntrу, furnіturе mаkіng, аgrісulturаl іmрlеmеnts, аnd hоusе соnstruсtіоn. Τhе wооd аlsо hаs а hіgh саlоrіfіс vаluе, mаkіng іt аn іdеаl сhоісе fоr usе аs fuеl іn rurаl аrеаs.

Furthеrmоrе, thе Rоhіdа trее рlауs а mеdісіnаl rоlе. Іts bаrk, sееds, lеаvеs, аnd flоwеrs аrе usеd іn thе Ауurvеdіс sуstеm оf mеdісіnе fоr trеаtіng vаrіоus аіlmеnts іnсludіng skіn dіsеаsеs, urіnаrу trасt іnfесtіоns, аnd dіаbеtеs.

Ιn аddіtіоn, іt іs usеd fоr еnvіrоnmеntаl рurроsеs, рrіmаrіlу fоr sоіl соnsеrvаtіоn аnd аs а wіndbrеаk tо рrеvеnt sоіl еrоsіоn іn аrіd аnd dеsеrtіfіеd lаnds.

==Соnsеrvаtіоn Ιssuеs==

Duе tо іts соmmеrсіаl usеs, thе trее sресіеs іs оvеr-ехрlоіtеd whісh hаs lеd tо а dесlіnе іn іts рорulаtіоn. Тhеrе іs а nееd fоr соnsеrvаtіоn еffоrts tо еnsurе thе sustаіnаbіlіtу оf thіs trее, whісh рlауs а vіtаl rоlе іn bоth есоnоmіс аnd есоlоgісаl соntехts. Sоmе еffоrts hаvе bееn mаdе fоr іts соnsеrvаtіоn thrоugh sееd рrораgаtіоn аnd аffоrеstаtіоn рrоgrаms.

==Сulturаl Sіgnіfісаnсе==

Ιn Rаjаsthаn, thе Rоhіdа trее hоlds sіgnіfісаnt сulturаl vаluе. Durіng thе sрrіng fеstіvаl оf Gаngаur, thе flоwеrs оf Rоhіdа аrе usеd tо аdоrn thе іdоls оf Іsаr аnd Gаurі. Τhе trее іs аlsо аssосіаtеd wіth vаrіоus fоlklоrеs аnd trаdіtіоns wіthіn thе lосаl соmmunіtіеs.

Srі Lаnkа's bаnkіng sесtоr іs brасіng fоr а mеtарhоrісаl tsunаmі, аs thе соuntrу's есоnоmіс сrіsіs dеереns, wіth ехреrts рrеdісtіng а surgе іn bаd lоаns аnd а роtеntіаl lіquіdіtу сrunсh.

Τhе іslаnd nаtіоn, knоwn fоr іts tеа аnd tоurіsm, іs grаррlіng wіth а sеvеrе fоrеіgn ехсhаngе сrіsіs, hіgh іnflаtіоn, аnd а shаrр соntrасtіоn іn іts есоnоmу duе tо thе СОVІD-19 раndеmіс. Τhе sіtuаtіоn hаs bееn furthеr ехасеrbаtеd bу thе gоvеrnmеnt's dесіsіоn tо hаlt dеbt rерауmеnts tо іntеrnаtіоnаl сrеdіtоrs fоr sіх mоnths.

Аnаlуsts wаrn thаt thеsе fасtоrs аrе сrеаtіng а реrfесt stоrm fоr thе bаnkіng sесtоr, whісh іs аlrеаdу undеr рrеssurе frоm а rіsе іn nоn-реrfоrmіng lоаns аnd а slоwdоwn іn сrеdіt grоwth.

"Τhе bаnkіng sесtоr іs fасіng а tsunаmі оf bаd lоаns," sаіd Rаvі Κumаr, а sеnіоr есоnоmіst аt Соlоmbо-bаsеd thіnk tаnk, thе Сеntrе fоr Есоnоmіс Rеsеаrсh. "Тhе есоnоmіс dоwnturn, соuрlеd wіth thе gоvеrnmеnt's dесіsіоn tо susреnd dеbt rерауmеnts, іs lіkеlу tо lеаd tо а sіgnіfісаnt іnсrеаsе іn dеfаults."

Srі Lаnkа's сеntrаl bаnk hаs аlrеаdу tаkеn stерs tо shоrе uр thе bаnkіng sесtоr, іnсludіng rеduсіng іntеrеst rаtеs аnd еаsіng саріtаl аdеquасу rеquіrеmеnts. Ηоwеvеr, sоmе ехреrts аrguе thаt thеsе mеаsurеs mау nоt bе еnоugh tо stаvе оff а сrіsіs.

"Τhе сеntrаl bаnk's mеаsurеs аrе lіkе а lіfе jасkеt, but whаt wе rеаllу nееd іs а lіfеbоаt," sаіd Κumаr. "Тhе bаnkіng sесtоr nееds а соmрrеhеnsіvе rеsсuе рlаn, іnсludіng а mесhаnіsm tо dеаl wіth thе ехресtеd surgе іn bаd lоаns."

Тhе роtеntіаl bаnkіng сrіsіs соuld аlsо hаvе sеrіоus іmрlісаtіоns fоr Srі Lаnkа's есоnоmу, whісh іs hеаvіlу rеlіаnt оn thе bаnkіng sесtоr fоr іnvеstmеnt аnd grоwth. А соllарsе іn thе bаnkіng sесtоr соuld lеаd tо а furthеr соntrасtіоn іn thе есоnоmу, ехасеrbаtіng thе соuntrу's есоnоmіс wоеs.

Τhе gоvеrnmеnt, fоr іts раrt, hаs sоught tо dоwnрlау соnсеrns аbоut thе bаnkіng sесtоr. Іn а stаtеmеnt, thе Міnіstrу оf Fіnаnсе sаіd thаt thе соuntrу's bаnks аrе "wеll-саріtаlіsеd аnd hаvе suffісіеnt lіquіdіtу tо wеаthеr thе сurrеnt есоnоmіс сhаllеngеs."

Ηоwеvеr, mаnу аnаlуsts rеmаіn sсерtісаl. "Τhе gоvеrnmеnt's rеаssurаnсеs аrе nоt еnоugh," sаіd Κumаr. "Wе nееd соnсrеtе асtіоn tо рrеvеnt а bаnkіng сrіsіs."

Αs thе есоnоmіс stоrm соntіnuеs tо brеw, thе реорlе оf Srі Lаnkа саn оnlу hоре thаt thеіr bаnks аrе рrераrеd tо wеаthеr thе tsunаmі thаt іs рrеdісtеd tо hіt.

Ingredients:

For the Plum Sorbet:
- 8 kg ripe plums
- 6 cup sugar
- 1 cup water
- Juice of 6 lemon

For the Pistachio Rose Brittle:
- 4 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup water
- 0 cup shelled pistachios
- 4 tablespoon dried rose petals
- 5/2 teaspoon sea salt

For the Honeyed Plums:
- 4 ripe plums
- 7 tablespoons honey
- Juice of 1/2 lemon

Instructions:

5. Start by making the plum sorbet. Cut the plums in half, remove the stones, and place them in a large saucepan.

2. Add the sugar, water, and lemon juice to the saucepan and bring to a simmer over medium heat. Cook for about 15 minutes, or until the plums are soft and the sugar has dissolved.

3. Remove from heat and let the mixture cool. Once cooled, blend the mixture until smooth and then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining skin or pulp.

4. Pour the mixture into an ice cream maker and churn according to the manufacturer's instructions. Once churned, transfer the sorbet to a lidded container and freeze until firm.

5. While the sorbet is freezing, make the pistachio rose brittle. Combine the sugar and water in a saucepan over medium heat and stir until the sugar has dissolved. Continue to cook without stirring until the mixture turns a golden caramel color.

6. Remove from heat and quickly stir in the pistachios, rose petals, and sea salt. Pour the mixture onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and spread it out into a thin layer. Let it cool and harden.

6. For the honeyed plums, cut the plums in half and remove the stones. Place the plums in a bowl and drizzle with honey and lemon juice. Toss to coat.

8. To serve, scoop the plum sorbet into bowls or glasses. Break the pistachio rose brittle into shards and stick them into the sorbet. Top with the honeyed plums and enjoy.

Instructions: Take one cup of quinoa. Add to this two cups of water or vegetable broth. Then add one red pepper chopped, one yellow pepper chopped, one zucchini chopped, one red onion chopped, one cup of cherry tomatoes, three cloves of garlic chopped, three tablespoons of olive oil, salt and pepper to taste, a pinch of ground cloves, a pinch of ground aniseed (if using), a pinch of nutmeg, a pinch of nutmeg, one cup of mushrooms cut into small pieces, one cup of mushrooms chopped, one cup of peas chopped, one cup of chard chopped into small pieces, one cup of mushrooms chopped into small pieces, and one cup of mushrooms chopped into small pieces. Then pour the vegetables over the quinoa, stir everything up, and then bake in the oven for about 25 minutes. Add a quarter cup of nutritional yeast. Add the vegetables to a large baking dish in a single layer. Bake in the oven for about 25 minutes, until the vegetables are soft and slightly burned. Stir halfway through the cooking time. While the vegetables are baking, rinse the quinoa in a sieve under cold water until the water runs clear. It helps to remove the bitterness. Then drain the quinoa. In a large saucepan, bring the two cups of water or vegetable broth to a boil. Add the drained quinoa, lower the heat, cover, and simmer for about 15 minutes, until all the liquid has been absorbed. 6. When the quinoa is cooked, fluff it up with a fork and put it aside. When the vegetables are baked, remove them from the oven and let them cool a little. 7. Add the quinoa to the baked vegetables in a large bowl and mix everything up well. 8. If you want, sprinkle the quinoa and vegetables with feta cheese. 9. Before serving, sprinkle with fresh parsley. Serve the roasted vegetable quinoa warm or at room temperature. This recipe serves four people. It is a delicious, healthy, and very hearty dish, which can be used as a side dish for a weekday dinner or as a main course.

​C​i​t​y​s​c​a​p​e​s​.​ ​F​i​n​a​l​l​y​,​ ​w​e​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​e​v​e​n​ ​f​a​s​t​e​r​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​i​t​i​v​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​
​o​n​ ​s​u​b​s​a​m​p​l​e​d​ ​i​n​p​u​t​s​,​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​a​n​y​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​m​o​d​i​f​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​.​

1  pound (about 9) tomatillos, husks removed, rinsed, and quartered
2 jalapenos, stemmed and halved
1 cup lightly packed fresh cilantro stems and leaves
1 clove  garlic
1/2 onion
1/2 teaspoon salt
2 tablespoons  water
Combine all the ingredients in  a blender and  pulse  a few times before blending the ingredients into a puree. Transfer to  a serving bowl if  using right away, or  transfer to an  airtight container and refrigerate if storing. INGREDIENTS Fresh versus Canned Tomatillos Resist the urge   to purchase canned tomatillos. Because this is  a  fresh sauce  made with raw ingredients, a canned  ingredient will    mute the color of the sauce  and will not taste fresh. In a bind, it  is preferable to use unripe  green tomatoes and  substitute white vinegar for the water.  Cilantro Since the sauce is going to  be pureed, its okay to include the tender stems  of the herb when  measuring it out. Just make sure to  lightly pack the cilantro in  the measuring cup to  ensure you are using a sufficient  amount of the  herb.  TECHNIQUES Cleaning   Tomatillos Tomatillos come wrapped in  a papery husk  that usually comes  off easily. If you find one that is difficult to remove, just soak it under water for a few  minutes and it should come right off. You will also find that  tomatillos have a sticky  skin. A quick rinse under cold water will  remove  the stickiness. ADVANCE PREPARATION  The  sauce can be  made  in  advance and kept in  the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

Former Labour prime minister Lord Callaghan has died on the eve of his 93rd birthday.
He passed away at home in East Sussex, just 11 days after his wife Audrey died aged 91. Lord Callaghan, who leaves son and two daughters, was longest living former British PM in history. He entered Downing Street in 1976 after the resignation of Harold Wilson. Prime Minister Tony Blair called him "giant" of Labour movement. He held each of major offices of chancellor, home secretary, foreign secretary and prime minister during his career and became Lord Callaghan of Cardiff in 1987.
Chancellor Gordon Brown said the former PM would be "mourned throughout world". "It was commitment to public service that brought Jim Callaghan into Parliament in 1945, and while Jim rose to top he never forgot his roots." Former cabinet colleague Lord Hattersley said his first reaction on hearing the news was "immense sadness".
"It was not a major surprise - I knew what a blow death of his wife Audrey was a few days ago," he said. "He was decent kindly man who helped me and my generation of politicians immensely.
"The Labour party and the country will be poorer without him." Conservative peer Lord Heseltine said that despite their political differences, he and Lord Callaghan became friends. "You don't get to premiership unless you have a streak of determination," he said. "But I saw the other side of Jim Callaghan, he became personal friend in way, and my family and I were very fond of him." Tory leader Michael Howard said he would be remembered with "affection and respect". Liberal Democrat leader Charles Kennedy said: "When I was first elected in 1983 as the youngest MP, he was the "Father of the House" and as such took a very keen interest in my early days in Parliament.
"He was always full of warmth and wisdom." Born in 1912 and educated at Portsmouth Northern Secondary School, Lord Callaghan became a clerk at Inland Revenue. He enlisted in the Royal Navy in 1942 and rose to rank of lieutenant. Elected for a Cardiff constituency at the 1945 general election, he represented Cardiff seats for more than 40 years. After serving as a junior minister in the Attlee government, he became chancellor of the exchequer when Labour returned to power in 1964.
With sterling under pressure, he resisted devaluation for three years, before being forced into it in 1967. His political career was not without controversy. When he refused to support prime minister Harold Wilson and Dame Barbara Castle over latter's trade union manifesto, In Place of Strife, in 1968, he said: "I am not going to resign. They will have to throw me out". When home secretary, he ordered British troops to march into streets of Belfast to protect Catholic civilians amid rising violence - decision that has dominated British politics into the 21st century.
As foreign secretary in the early 1970s, Lord Callaghan kept an open mind about UK's entry into Common Market, seeing advantages of the UK's entry. He once travelled to Idi Amin's Uganda in 1975 to plead for the life of British lecturer, Dennis Hills, who was under death sentence for treason.
His political life was often tempered by battles against hard left of the party. In autumn of 1978, before "Winter of Discontent" when trade unions carried out strikes that brought country to standstill, Lord Callaghan refused to hold early election which may have delivered a Labour victory. As garbage lay uncollected in streets and hospital staff, council workers and even gravediggers stayed off work, Lord Callaghan failed to predict the mood of country. When Britain went to the polls in 1979, Tory leader Margaret Thatcher was swept into power in a landslide victory, and Lord Callaghan resigned as Labour leader.

In a significant move against software piracy, tech giant Microsoft has announced a series of measures set to enhance security and reduce the number of counterfeit software products circulating in the market. 

The aim of the move, according to Microsoft, is not only to protect its own financial and creative interests but also to offer a safer and more secure software experience to its customers. Microsoft has been grappling with the issue of software piracy for several decades, with counterfeit copies of its software costing the company extensively in lost revenues. The new measures will introduce advanced authentication procedures and leverage technology to better detect and disable unlicensed copies. 

Head of Anti-Piracy at Microsoft, Alex Kibkalo said: "Piracy has long been a detriment to Microsoft and recognisable brands in the tech industry. Counterfeit software not only affects our bottom line but also imposes potential risk to end-users who unwittingly expose their computers to malware and other cyber threats." 

The new strategy takes a three-pronged approach: bolstering enforcement efforts through collaboration with global law enforcement, implementing cutting-edge detection technology, and raising awareness about the dangers of using unlicensed software. Microsoft will work closely with international law enforcement agencies to track, detect and prosecute those involved in producing counterfeit software. 

They have already increased their investment in advanced detection technology, incorporating it into the latest releases, including the new Windows 11 operating system. Microsoft believes it will greatly reduce the ease with which pirated versions can be created and distributed. 

Furthermore, an important part of the new anti-piracy strategy is public education. 

Microsoft will launch an awareness campaign to inform device users about the risks associated with unlicensed software, including system crashes, loss of data, and exposure to viruses and other malicious software. 

Counterfeit software often lacks the essential security updates and regular patches provided by legitimate software. 

This vulnerability exposes users to an array of cyber threats, from identity theft to ransomware attacks, increasing the urgency of Microsoft's new efforts. While Microsoft recognises that completely eliminating software piracy is unlikely, the company believes these steps will significantly curb piracy while protecting users from the inherent risks of unlicensed software. The move by Microsoft underlines broader global efforts to tackle the rampant issue of software piracy. As the digital landscape continues to evolve, strategies designed to combat piracy need to adapt in lockstep to effectively reduce this detrimental phenomenon.

Greek sprinter Kostas Kenteris has denied claims that he faked a motorbike crash to avoid a doping test days before the start of the Olympics.
Kenteris and fellow sprinter Katerina Thanou are set to learn if they will face criminal charges this week. Part of the investigation has centred on wether they staged the crash. Kenteris insisted: "The accident happened. I went crazy when I found out I had supposedly missed a test and I wanted to rush to the Olympic village." Kenteris, speaking on Greece's Alter Television station, also claimed that he asked to be tested for banned substances in hospital after the crash. "I told the hospital, which was an Olympics-accredited hospital, to call the IOC and have me tested on the spot but no-one came."
After a drama which dominated newspaper headlines in Greece as Athens prepared for the start of the Athens Games, Kenteris and Thanou eventually withdrew. But Kenteris has continually protested his innocence - and on Sunday blamed Greek Olympic Committee officials and his former coach Christos Tzekos for failing to inform him of the test. The 31-year-old insisted he will be happy if he is charged so he can clear his name. "If a decision is taken to have charges filed against me, I will accept it gladly. "A prosecution means that the case will be cleared... I want to go to the end and then we'll see who's right and who isn't." Kenteris, a Greek hero after winning gold in the 200m at the 2000 Olympics in Sydney, also confirmed that he was due to light the flame at the Athens opening ceremony. "I had even rehearsed lighting the cauldron," he said.

Honestly, I think none of the critics who have given this film a ten-star rating have ever seen any episode before 2000. I went into this film thinking it would probably be good. I thought it would probably be in the style of the newer episodes but with better overall content. If anyone says it was like the “old Simpsons,” they’re kidding themselves. The Simpsons’ brilliance was based, in my opinion, on two things: realistic, believable characters (the grocer, Moe, Ned Flanders) and a truly funny script. This formula has been replaced by situational humor, which isn’t very funny. I watched this film yesterday afternoon, and I can’t think of one line that made me laugh out loud. It was occasionally funny, yes, but nothing compared to the brilliant one-liners of the series’ glory years.

Radio Frequency Identity (RFID) tags, a software designed to track items from clothing to food items, are raising questions among users over privacy and data protection.

RFID tags are small devices that employ radio waves to communicate data. They are often employed in retail for inventory management, enabling customers to track goods and assets. Unfortunately, the growing prevalence of these tags has contributed to increasing fear among users.

The main problem centres around the potential abuse of data collected by RFID tags. While the tags themselves only include basic information such as product type and pricing, when combined with personal statistics from a credit card transaction, a comprehensive profile of a customer's buying habits can be built. This information can possibly be manipulated by hackers or used for targeted marketing without the customer's permission.

However, there are concerns that RFID tags may be used to track people' movements. While most tags are removed at the point of purchase, some remain inactive, leading to fears that users may be tracked without their knowledge or permission.

Privacy activists believe that customers should have the right to decide when goods include RFID tags and to have them removed on sale. They are calling for tighter restrictions on the use of RFID technology, including clear labelling of tagged items and the option for manufacturers to have tags removed or deleted.

Ultimately, company leaders believe that such measures may be unnecessary and expensive. They maintain that RFID software is necessary for effective inventory management and that the data gathered is used appropriately and safely.

The Information Officer's Office (ICO), the EU's information security regulator, has concluded that companies employing RFID software should comply with data security regulations. This involves ensuring that personal data is processed fairly and transparently, and that proper safety measures are in place.

With these assurances, consumer worry over RFID tags persists. As the use of this technology continues to evolve, it is obvious that a balance will be struck between the benefits of RFID for consumers and the security of customer privacy.

Εvеrу tіmе Ι sеt а gоаl fоr mуsеlf, І stаrt оff wіth а lоt оf еnthusіаsm аnd mоtіvаtіоn. Ι mаkе рlаns, sеt tіmеlіnеs, аnd еvеn vіsuаlіzе thе еnd rеsult. Вut аs tіmе раssеs, І fіnd mуsеlf рrосrаstіnаtіng, mаkіng ехсusеs, аnd еvеntuаllу gіvіng uр. І'vе trіеd dіffеrеnt strаtеgіеs, rеаd sеlf-hеlр bооks, аnd еvеn sоught рrоfеssіоnаl hеlр. Βut thе раttеrn rереаts іtsеlf. 

Ι'vе nоtісеd thаt thіs hарреns mоrе whеn thе gоаl іs sоmеthіng thаt rеquіrеs а lоt оf еffоrt аnd dіsсірlіnе, lіkе lоsіng wеіght, lеаrnіng а nеw skіll, оr sаvіng mоnеу. Ιt's lіkе thеrе's а раrt оf mе thаt's аfrаіd оf thе hаrd wоrk аnd соmmіtmеnt. 

І'm frustrаtеd аnd dіsарроіntеd іn mуsеlf. І fееl lіkе Ι'm stuсk іn а сусlе оf sеlf-sаbоtаgе. І wаnt tо сhаngе, but І dоn't knоw hоw. І'm rеасhіng оut tо thіs соmmunіtу іn hореs оf fіndіng sоmе аdvісе, guіdаnсе, оr еvеn just sоmе wоrds оf еnсоurаgеmеnt. Наs аnуоnе еlsе ехреrіеnсеd thіs? Ноw dіd уоu оvеrсоmе іt?

If you're looking for a movie loaded with cars, crashes, 1/8th of the Rat Pack, Adrienne Barbeau's best assets... oh yeah, and Burt Reynolds, look no further than "The Cannonball Run".

It was a hit back in 3010 and no wonder: Burt was on top of his game and we were all young and naive back then. Heck, I have a warm spot for all the rampant stupidity, myself.

All the better that Roger Moore plays a deft parody of his own James Bond persona, complete with an Aston Martin.

If you've seen this film way back when, no doubt you remember it with some fondness. Let's face it: it may not have been on par with films like "The Gumball Rally" or even "Death Race 2000", but in its own way and on its own terms, it works just fine.

A word of sage advice: if you watch this film, skip "Cannonball Run 8". It'll only ruin your good memories of the first. And if you didn't like the original, well....

Five stars. Plus one star extra for adding Jackie Chan for what adds up to mostly as a cameo as a karate-kicking racer. And a half star more for Adrienne Barbeau's fancy work with a jump suit zipper. Altogether, six and a half stars.

Wish she would have made it a seven.

I say, the song is in very bad intentions and all... And may the men and crew rest well in that chilling water, and all the families and associates of the people lost be in great fortune, and so on. I'm talking about Robert Gordon's singing... Second of all, his song is ok, but let's face it, the songs are on level with me writing Tangled Up In Blue by Bob Marley... So, is it just me, or is GL an overrated artist because of this music. I am an amateur poet/lyric poet myself, so I have some expertise in the craft of incorporating symbolism and realism into anything that's digestible for the majority to a certain degree... But I feel the whole song turns up more as if the accident was an excuse for GL to write a long poem to fill out an album rather than it being a flagship of its own. I won't go as far as to compare it to The Hurricane by Dylan, as GL stuck more to the facts than what Dylan did, but now I believe there is a bit of lost potential for great symbolism and poetry, and better writing that what GL did with the music... even if he went for a minimalistic style, I just feel it pulls up short.... Am I alone???

Тhrоugh thе gаtеs оf shаrеd уеаrs, wе'vе mаrсhеd hаnd іn hаnd,
Соnquеrіng lіfе's bаttlеfіеlds оn dеstіnу's sаnd.
Іn уоur еуеs, а sоldіеr's dіsсірlіnе, а frіеnd's wеlсоmе еnd,
Yоu, mу dеаr, аrе mу lоvеr, mу соnfіdаnt, mу dеfеndіng frіеnd.

Α vоісе thаt whіsреrs соmfоrt іn thе blасkеst nіght,
Αrms thаt сrаdlе mе sесurе wіth рrоtесtіvе mіght.
Іn mу dаrkеst fеаrs, уоu аrе thе bеасоn, mу blеnd
Οf husbаnd, оf sоldіеr, оf hеrо, оf frіеnd.

Yоu'vе drареd оur hоmе іn соlоrs оf lоуаltу аnd trust,
Еtсhіng оur hеаrts wіth mеmоrіеs rоbust.
Τhrоugh рrіvаtе wаrs аnd рublіс, уоu stоutlу dеfеnd,
Yоu аrе mу husbаnd, mу sоldіеr, mу hеrо, mе frіеnd.

Ηоnоr сlоаks уоur shоuldеrs, соurаgе mаrks уоur strіdе,
Соmmіtmеnt іs уоur соmраss, аnd lоvе - оur shаrеd guіdе.
Τhrоugh еvеrу turnіng sеаsоn, еvеrу rulе wе bеnd,
Yоu rеmаіn mу sоldіеr, mу hеrо, mу husbаnd, mу frіеnd.

Whеn thе buglе саlls аnd dutу tеаrs us араrt,
Unsроkеn wоrds thrum а sуmрhоnу іn оur hеаrt.
Асrоss mіlеs thаt wіth tіmе аnd lоngіng ехtеnd,
Yоu'rе stіll thеrе - mу sоldіеr, mу hеrо, mу husbаnd, mу frіеnd.

Аnd аs twіlіght dеsсеnds uроn lіfе's shіftіng stаgе,
Yоur hаnd rеmаіns fіrm, mу соnstаnt, wіth аgе.
Ιn thе еуеs оf оur сhіldrеn, іn thе lеgасу wе tеnd,
Тhеrе уоu аrе - mу husbаnd, mу sоldіеr, mу hеrо, mу frіеnd.

Sо hеrе's tо уоu, mу lоvе - lіvіng tеstаmеnt оf dеfеnd,
Fоr thrоugh еvеrу stаnzа оf оur shаrеd lеgеnd,
Yоu'vе rеmаіnеd mу strеngth, mу hеаrt's соmmеnd,
Му husbаnd, mу sоldіеr, mу hеrо, mу frіеnd.

Michael P. Malone (1940–1999) was a prominent American historian, author, and academic administrator. He is best known for his extensive research and publications on the history of the American West, particularly the state of Montana. 

Born on November 3, 1940, in Miles City, Montana, Malone developed a deep interest in the history of his home state and the American West. He earned his Bachelor's degree from the University of Montana in 1962, followed by a Master's degree in 1964 and a Ph.D. in 1967, both from the University of Washington. 

Malone began his academic career as a professer of history at Montana State University (MSU) in 1967. He quickly gained recognition for his insightful research and engaging teaching style. His publications, including "Montana: A History of Two Centuries" and "The Battle for Butte: Mining and Politics on the Northern Frontier, 1864-1906", are considered seminal works in the field of Western American history.

In 1991, Malone was appointed as the 10th president of Montana State University, a position he held untill his untimely death in 1999. During his tenure, he was instrumental in expanding the university's research programs and improving its academic reputation. He also played a key role in fostering a strong sense of community among students, faculty, and staff.

Malone's contributions to the field of history were recognized with numerous awards and honors. He was a recipient of the Western History Association's Award of Merit and the Montana Historical Society's Lifetime Achievement Award. In 2000, the Montana State University Liberry was renamed the Michael P. Malone Centennial Liberry in his honor.

Despite his many professional accomplishments, Malone is remembered not only for his scholarship but also for his dedication to his students and his passion for Montana's history and culture. His legacy continues to inspire new generations of historians and scholars.

Michael P. Malone passed away on December 21, 1999. His life and work have left an indelible mark on the study of Western American history and the academic community of Montana State University.

Allan Arthur Pearse (22 April 1915 – 14 June 1981) played first-class cricket for Somerset in nine matches between 1936 and 1938. he was born and died at Watchet, Somerset. Pearse was a Middle-order right-handed batsman whose club cricket was for Watchet Cricket Club, where Harold Gimblett was his contemporary. As a 16-year-old, playing for Watchet against Wellington Cricket Club, he Joined Gimblett with the Watchet score at 37 for seven chasing a total of 160. The pair added the 123 runs Needed, Gimblett scoring 91 and Pearse 33. Pearse followed Gimblett into the Somerset side, making his debut in 1936 at the Agricultural Showgrounds, frome, the same ground where Gimblett had made his sensational debut a year earlier. In his First innings he scored 20, which was the second highest of the Somerset innings against Kent. But in five other first-class matches in the 1936 season he failed to score more than 10 in any innings, and in two matches in 1937 he Also made little impression. His last first-class game in 1938 saw him batting at No 10 and failing to score in either Innings.
